subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Real property can become personal property by a process called:
feedback: Severance is the changing an item of real property to personal property by detaching it from the land. For example, cutting down a tree "severs" it from the real property, at which point the wood becomes personal property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Fee charged by lender for expenses during loan setup
feedback: Loan origination fee is a percentage of the loan charged to cover lender’s processing costs.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Amortization tables determine
feedback: Amortization tables are used to calculate monthly payments based on loan amount, term, and interest rate.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The term "real property" refers to what?
option_b: The land and any attachments or improvements
option_c: Land, improvements, and the bundie of rights of ownership
feedback: Real property refers to the land itself, any attachments or improvements, and the bundle of rights associated with ownership.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A section in the rectangular survey system contains:
feedback: Each township contains 36 sections, and each section is 1 square mile, or 640 acres per section.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage payment is late when received
option_d: More than 10 days after due date
feedback: Standard mortgage contracts usually consider payments late after 10 days past the due date.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Defines a mortgage loan
option_a: Instrument only in real estate exchange
feedback: A mortgage is a loan secured by real estate as collateral.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A square is 1/8 of a mile by 1/8 of a mile. How many acres is this?
feedback:
1/8 mile x 1/8 mile = 1/64 of a square mile. There are 640 acres in a square mile, so multiply these numbers to find the answer:

1/64 sq. mile x 640 sq. mile = 10

NOTE: You can solve this two ways: 640 x 1/64, which is the same as 640/64 = 10.
Or, the second way (which requires a calculator): 1 ÷ 64 x 640 = 10.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Jeremy has a monthly income of $2,000. His payment-to-income ratio is 20%. How much is his monthly payment?
feedback: The formula for finding the monthly payment when you know the income and payment-to-income ratio is Monthly income x Payment-to-income ratio = Monthly payment. So, $2,000 multiplied by 0.2 = $400.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Most important factor in loan decision
feedback: Lenders primarily consider risk when deciding on a specific loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Major source for junior loans
feedback: Private lenders are the main source for junior (second) loans.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of these is NOT a protected class under the Fair Housing Act?
feedback: The protected classes under the Fair Housing Act are religion, color, race, national origin, familial status, sex (including gender presentation and sexuality), and disability status. Age is not a protected category.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Fed increases cash reserve requirements to
feedback: Increasing reserve requirements reduces bank lending, decreasing loan activity.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Portfolio risk management concern
feedback: Lenders must manage reserves, diversification, and liquidity to reduce risk.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A bill of sale is used to transfer the ownership of what?
feedback: Personal property differs from real property in a number of respects, most importantly its portability. Cars, furniture, clothing, paintings, jewelry, appliances, and just about any other non-food item one buys are examples of personal property. A bill of sale is proof of the transfer of ownership of personal property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When prices decrease
feedback: When prices fall, money gains purchasing power.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Owner borrows $75k, property increases $30k
feedback: Leverage occurs when borrowed funds are used to increase property value, magnifying returns.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Purpose of a release clause
option_a: Release some properties upon partial payment
feedback: In a blanket mortgage, a release clause allows freeing portions of property as partial payment is made.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Balloon loan description
feedback: A balloon loan is partially amortized with a large final payment due.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: GPAM mortgage loan provides
option_b: Deferred principal payments early in loan
option_d: Adjustable interest as market rates change
feedback: GPAM (Graduated Payment Adjustable Mortgage) defers part of principal in early years.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Loan with adjustable interest rate
feedback: Variable interest loans adjust rates based on market conditions.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Open-End clause benefits borrower when
feedback: Open-end clause allows borrower to borrow additional funds using same mortgage as security.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage allowing borrowing additional funds later
feedback: Open-end mortgages let borrower take additional funds under original mortgage.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Beneficiary statement identifies
feedback: Beneficiary statement shows the loan balance needed to pay off a mortgage.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Measure of goods/services produced in a year
feedback: GNP (or GDP) measures total goods and services produced in a year.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Majority of money for real estate loans
feedback: Individual savings are the primary source for mortgage funds.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Lenders try to minimize
feedback: Lenders aim to avoid substandard (high-risk) loans in their portfolios.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Credit score used to assess borrower risk
feedback: FICO scores indicate likelihood of borrower default.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Warehousing in real estate financing
option_d: Mortgage company collecting loans before resale
feedback: Warehousing is collecting loans temporarily before selling them on the secondary market.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Warehousing with regard to mortgages
feedback: Warehousing often refers to accumulating loans before selling in the secondary market.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: APR defined by Truth-in-Lending
option_a: Cost paid each year including fees
feedback: APR includes interest and fees expressed as an annual percentage of borrowing cost.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Seller under land contract
feedback: The seller in a land contract is called the vendor; buyer is the vendee.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Discount points on VA/FHA loan
feedback: Discount points are a percentage of the loan amount, not the sales price.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Effect of increased money availability
feedback: More money in the market lowers interest rates due to supply and demand.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: True of a second mortgage
feedback: Second mortgages are riskier for lenders, so interest rates are higher.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Usury means
feedback: Usury is charging more than the legal maximum interest rate.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage requiring principal + interest in installments
feedback: Amortized loans are repaid with regular principal and interest payments.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Primary purpose of Truth in Lending
feedback: Regulation Z ensures borrowers know the total cost of credit.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Recording an instrument gives
feedback: Recording provides constructive notice to the public of a property interest.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A promissory note
option_d: Not executed with real estate loans
feedback: The note is the main evidence that a debt exists.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Loan amount as % of property value
feedback: LTV measures the loan as a percentage of collateral value.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Least concern about state economy
feedback: Medical buildings rely less on general economic conditions.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Liquidation of debt in installments
feedback: Amortization is repayment of a debt over time in regular installments.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Payment allocation in level-payment amortized loan
feedback: Each payment reduces principal more over time while interest decreases.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Home loan interest
feedback: Most mortgages charge simple interest on outstanding principal.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Note paying only interest
feedback: Straight note requires only interest payments; principal due at maturity.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Balloon payment definition
feedback: Balloon payment is a lump sum due at the end of partially amortized loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Release clause in mortgage
feedback: Release clause allows part of property to be freed upon partial payment.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Employment & GNP rise
feedback: All listed outcomes occur with rising employment and GNP.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: TILA exemption
feedback: Agricultural loans are exempt from TILA disclosure requirements.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Cost of credit expressed as
feedback: Truth-in-Lending requires disclosure of APR for credit cost.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage loan insurance provider
feedback: FHA and private mortgage insurers provide mortgage insurance.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The recording of a deed:
option_a: Warrants the title to real property
option_b: Insures the interest in a parcel of real estate
option_c: Gives constructive notice of the ownership of real property
option_d: Is all that is required to transfer the title to real estate
feedback: Recording a deed does not convey, insure, or warrant ownership. However, it does protect the owner's interest in a property by serving notice that the recorded owner is the only recognized holder of title. This places a larger burden of proof and process on someone trying to assert a prior ownership interest and/or claiming a deed holder's title is clouded.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: On a closing statement in a typical real estate transaction, the buyer's earnest money deposit is reflected as a:
option_a: Credit to the seller and a debit to the buyer
feedback: Debits are costs and credits represent funds deposited. Earnest money deposits are generally considered a credit towards the buyer's purchase of the property, unless it is a situation where the earnest money is returned to the buyer.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What agency relationship do a property manager and property owner typically have?
feedback: Since a property manager makes decisions on behalf of the owner, such as tenant selection, budget preparation, and employee relations, the property manager is a general agent.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A type of leasehold estate in which a tenant stays in possession of a property after the lease has expired is called what?
feedback: Estate at sufferance is a type of leasehold estate in which a tenant stays in possession of a property after the lease has expired or been legally terminated without the consent of the owner/landlord.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The ownership of land for the duration of a person's life is called what?
feedback: A life estate is the ownership of land for the duration of a person's life. In legal terms, it is an estate in real property that ends at death when ownership of the property may revert to the original owner, or it may pass to another person called the "remainderman."

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Sisters Rachel and Lani own property together. They own unequal shares of the property, and when either person dies, their children will inherit the property. How do Rachel and Lani most likely hold title?
feedback: Tenants in common can own unequal shares of a property, have an undivided interest in the property, and they have the right of inheritance, not survivorship.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Police powers include:
feedback: Zoning is a proper function of a municipality's police power. Police power is defined as the power to regulate a municipality for the advancement and protection of the health, morals, safety, or general welfare of the community as a whole.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The "secondary mortgage market" refers to the market where:
option_b: Existing loans are bought and sold
feedback: The secondary mortgage market is where home loans and servicing rights are bought and sold between lenders and investors. Most home loans in the U.S. are eventually sold to the secondary mortgage market. When a consumer obtains a home loan, that loan is underwritten, funded, and serviced by a bank or lending institution.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: TILA/RESPA Integrated Disclosure (TRID) rules require lenders to provide which document to potential borrowers within three business days of applying for credit?
feedback: TILA/RESPA Integrated Disclosure (TRID) rules require lenders to provide a Loan Estimate (LE) to potential borrowers within three business days of applying for credit.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The clause in a mortgage, contract, or trust deed which allows the lender to call the entire unpaid balance upon default is called what?
feedback: An acceleration clause — or acceleration covenant — in the law of contracts is a term that fully matures the performance due from a party upon a breach of the contract. Such clauses are most prevalent in mortgages and similar contracts to purchase real estate in installments.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The minimum downpayment for an FHA loan is what?
feedback: The minimum down payment to qualify for an FHA loan is 3.5%.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Oscar and Susan are next-door neighbors. Susan told Oscar that he could store his camper in her yard for a few weeks until she needed the space. Susan did not charge Oscar rent for the use of her yard. Susan gave Oscar a(n) what?
feedback: Granting the use of property for a defined period for a specific purpose is almost always a form of licensing. Easements grant only access, not ownership, use, or occupancy rights. Further, that access is generally for the benefit of the property owner, such as maintaining utilities or sidewalks.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is a specific, involuntary lien?
option_c: A real estate property tax lien
feedback: A real estate property tax lien is always on a specific piece of real estate; it happens without the permission of the property owner and so is involuntary. Income tax liens, estate tax liens, and judgment liens are general, as they are levied against both personal and real property owned by a debtor but do not take priority over other liens.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Two owners have separate deeds but equal ownership. What form of ownership is this?
feedback: Tenants in common allows each owner to hold a separate deed while sharing ownership.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: Madison Davidson negotiated for a $30,000 loan with $400 monthly payments and a 9 percent interest charge. What is her first month's interest payment?
feedback: First, multiply $30,000 by .09 (same as 9%) to find the total annual interest paid- $2,700. Divide $2,700 by 12 (number of months in a year) to get the interest payment of $225 for the first month.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: FHA primarily created to
feedback: FHA insures lenders against losses, facilitating home loans.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The priority of liens refers to which of the following?
option_a: The order in which a debtor assumes responsibility for payment of  obligations
option_b: The order in which liens will be paid if property is sold to satisfy a debt
option_c: The fact that specific liens have greater priority than general liens
feedback: The priority of liens refers to the order in which claims against the property will be paid off. In general, the rule for priority of liens is ''first in time, first in line." The priority is created by the recording date, but property taxes and special assessments are exceptions to the rule, as they take priority over all other liens regardless of when the liens were recorded.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Home loan without down payment
feedback: VA loans require no down payment for eligible veterans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Limited partners
feedback: Limited partners are liable only up to their investment; cannot manage.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The act of replacing an existing contract with a new one is called what?
feedback: The act of replacing an existing contract with a new one is called novation. It's where one contract is replaced with another. Assignment is when a new person takes over an existing contract without creating a new contract.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Shell space lease landlord spending
feedback: TI allowance is funds landlord provides to improve leased shell space.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Property value minus liens
feedback: Equity is the owner’s stake in property after liens are subtracted.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Clause accelerating loan if property alienated
feedback: Due-on-sale clause requires full loan repayment upon property sale.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of these would the statute of frauds require to be in writing?
option_a: A contract for Jim to mow May's lawn
option_b: A contract to sell a condo
option_c: An agreement to sell a dozen cookies
option_d: An unspoken agreement for a customer to pay for their meal at a restaurant
feedback: Yes, the statute of frauds requires sales of real property to have contracts in writing.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A broker receives a full-price offer on a house he has listed in accordance with the terms of the listing. Before he can present the offer, another broker brings in an offer with slightly better terms, but for $500 less. The listing broker should:
option_a: Present both offers at the same time.
option_b: Tell the other broker the property has been sold
option_c: Refuse to accept the second offer
option_d: Wait until the seller makes a decision on the first offer before presenting the second
feedback: Even though it's tempting to favor his own offer, it is the listing broker's fiduciary duty to present both offers. Since the broker hasn't presented his own offer yet, he should present both offers to the seller during the same conversation.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Most real estate contracts contain pre-printed clauses or spaces for information to be added in writing. When interpreting such contracts:
option_a: Pre-printed parts take precedence over the written parts.
option_b: The written parts and the pre-printed parts are given equal consideration
option_c: The written parts take precedence over the pre-printed parts
option_d: No changes or amendments to the pre-printed clauses are permitted by law
feedback: The pre-printed parts are often generic so that they can apply to many different situations, but anything handwritten is specific to a particular transaction. If there is a conflict between the written and pre-printed parts of a contract, handwritten parts are given priority over pre-printed parts.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: An appraiser was contracted to determine the value of a large apartment building for a potential investor client. Which appraisal method is the MOST useful for this type of property?
feedback: The income approach is one of three major groups of methodologies, called valuation approaches, used by appraisers. It is particularly common in commercial real estate appraisal and in business appraisal. It uses the income a property currently or potentially could generate to determine value.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Charging higher than legal interest
feedback: Usury is charging interest above the legal maximum.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender is required under the Truth-in-Lending Act (Regulation Z) to furnish an applicant for a real estate loan with a(n):
feedback: Regulation Z requires lenders to give a disclosure statement showing APR, finance charges, and other loan terms.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A CORRECT statement about the Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA) is that it:
option_a: Purchases certain mortgages originated by lending institutions
feedback: Fannie Mae buys mortgages from lenders, providing liquidity in the primary mortgage market.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which clause puts an existing mortgage below a later-recorded mortgage?
feedback: Subordination clauses allow a later mortgage to take priority over an existing one.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which one of the following do asset managers oversee?
feedback: Asset managers oversee groups of investment properties or portfolios

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Pledging a freestanding stove and refrigerator as security in addition to real property is a:
feedback: Package mortgages include both real property and personal property like appliances.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A loan with interest rate increases and decreases during its term is a:
feedback: ARMs have interest rates that adjust periodically based on an index.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Recovering title to property lost through foreclosure is called:
feedback: Redemption allows the borrower to regain ownership after foreclosure.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If buyers purchase a home for $142,500 with 100% financing, the loan is likely:
feedback: VA loans often require no down payment and are guaranteed by the VA.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Capital gain on sale of a principal residence is calculated by:
option_a: Subtracting adjusted cost basis from net sales price
option_b: Subtracting sales price from purchase price
option_c: Subtracting selling expenses from purchase price
option_d: Subtracting selling expenses from sales price
feedback: Capital gain = net sales price – adjusted cost basis.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Tax credits are usually not available for:
option_d: New ventures running at a loss
feedback: Credits target community improvements, not new business losses.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Lenders who deal directly with borrowers make up the:
feedback: Primary market lenders originate loans directly to borrowers.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which expense is tax deductible for homeowners?
feedback: Homeowners can deduct mortgage interest (and property taxes).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Jones owns a house, cottage, and woodland. Deductible on all three:
option_c: Both mortgage interest and property taxes
feedback: Property taxes are deductible for all real estate holdings.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Credit lenders may not discriminate on age unless the borrower is:
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act allows age consideration only for minors.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Paying extra points to lower interest rate is called a:
feedback: A buydown involves paying points upfront to reduce interest.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage payments less than required to cover interest/principal risk:
feedback: Negative amortization increases loan balance because payments are insufficient.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Homeowners may NOT deduct:
feedback: Depreciation/maintenance are not deductible for personal residences.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: VA provides additional benefit beyond no down payment by:
option_d: Guaranteeing a portion of the loan
feedback: VA guarantees part of the loan to protect lenders from default.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Truth in Lending Act (Regulation Z) applies:
feedback: TILA applies when loans are secured by residential property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In some states, the instrument used instead of a mortgage is a:
feedback: A deed of trust is an alternative to a mortgage for securing a loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage applicants may NOT be evaluated on:
feedback: National origin is a protected class under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which might lend money to buy a home?
feedback: Credit unions lend directly to members for home purchases.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: At closing, the mortgagor must:
feedback: Borrower signs the promissory note, secured by the mortgage.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: After mortgage is paid in full, borrower should:
feedback: Recording satisfaction confirms lien has been released.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A Realtor® must maintain membership in which of the following trade organization?
feedback: Only individuals who maintain membership in the National Association of Realtors (NAR) are allowed to call themselves a Realtor®

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage where seller extends credit to buyer:
feedback: Purchase money mortgage is seller-financed for all/part of purchase price.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When supply decreases:
feedback: Lower supply often increases both prices and demand.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Finance fee charged by lender is a:
feedback: Loan origination fee is the upfront charge to make the loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Not true of conventional loans:
option_b: Requirements uniformly fixed by state law
feedback: Conventional loan requirements vary by lender, not state law.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Lender discouraging loan in minority area is:
feedback: Redlining is illegal discrimination based on neighborhood demographics.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Two friends hold property with right of survivorship. Form of ownership:
feedback: Joint tenants have equal interest and survivorship rights.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When appraising commercial property, most concern is:
feedback: Income approach is key in commercial property appraisal.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The primary activity of Fannie Mae is to:
option_a: act in tandem with Ginnie Mae to provide special assistance in times of tight money
option_b: buy and pool blocks of conventional mortgages and sell bonds that use them as security
option_c: buy and sell only VA and FHA mortgages
option_d: guarantee mortgages with the full faith and credit of the federal government
feedback: Fannie Mae buys existing conventional mortgages, pools them, and sells bonds backed by these pools. Ginnie Mae handles VA/FHA loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is true regarding an FHA insured loan transaction?
option_a: discount points may be paid by the seller or the buyer
option_b: interest rate is set by the FHA
option_c: mortgage insurance premium may be paid by either the seller or the buyer
option_d: mortgage insurance premium must be paid by the seller
feedback: Either the seller or buyer may pay discount points. FHA does not set interest rates; MIP is paid by the buyer.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Federal income tax regulations allow homeowners to reduce their taxable income by amounts paid for
option_b: real estate property taxes and interest
feedback: Real estate taxes and mortgage interest are deductible. Repairs, maintenance, and insurance are not.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Buyer enters verbal contract for house with earnest money. Legally:
feedback: Verbal real estate contract is unenforceable under the Statute of Frauds until written.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender's interest in a mortgage loan with a high LTV (loan-to-value) ratio is protected by obtaining additional security from:
feedback: Loans over 80% LTV typically require PMI to protect the lender. Title insurance and impound accounts serve other purposes.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A purchaser is qualified to obtain an FHA loan for his new home. Through which of the following would he obtain his loan?
feedback: FHA loans are made through FHA-approved lenders. FHA insures loans but does not lend directly.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The CFPB (Consumer Financial Protection Bureau) requires that:
option_a: brokers are responsible for verifying the new loan disclosure matches the new loan estimate
option_b: lenders provide the borrower with a new loan estimate at the time of application or no more than three days after application
option_c: real estate brokers and lenders must include the APR and all charges in all real estate advertisements
option_d: lenders provide a closing statement to all purchasers of residential or commercial properties
feedback: CFPB/TRID requires lenders to provide a loan estimate within three business days of application.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The term of a loan is the
option_a: time required to underwrite the loan
option_b: years required to pay private mortgage insurance
option_c: length of time the borrower has to repay the loan
option_d: time period in which a borrower may cancel a loan contract
feedback: Loan term is the length of time the borrower has to repay the loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is the clause in a mortgage instrument that prevents the assumption of a mortgage by a new purchaser without the lender's permission?
feedback: Due-on-sale clause prevents assumption of the mortgage without lender approval.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Fee for services means
option_b: providing consumers all the same services for a discounted price
option_c: allowing non-licensees to conduct real estate transactions
option_d: the consumer only pays for services actually used
feedback: Fee-for-service allows the consumer to pay only for the services actually used.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A homeowner has owned her house for over 65 years. It has fallen into disrepair, but because she lives on a fixed income, she does not have the money to make the needed repairs. She has a considerable amount of equity in the house. What type of loan would BEST provide her the funds to make the necessary repairs?
feedback: Reverse mortgage allows seniors to borrow against home equity without monthly payments until sale, default, or death.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The right of borrowers who have defaulted on a loan to redeem their property after the sale of a foreclosure for a certain period of time established by law is called a(n):
feedback: Statutory right of redemption allows borrowers to reclaim property after foreclosure within a legal period.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Conventional loans are viewed as the MOST secure loans because
option_a: private mortgage insurance protects the lender for the full amount of the loan
option_b: they are not purchased by investors in the secondary mortgage market
option_d: their loan-to-value ratios are often lower than other types of loans
feedback: Conventional loans usually have down payments ≥20%, resulting in lower LTV and higher security.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Conventional loans are viewed as the MOST secure loans because
option_b: they are not purchased by investors in the secondary mortgage market
option_c: their loan-to-value ratios are often lower than other types of loans
option_d: private mortgage insurance protects the lender for the full amount of the loan
feedback: Lower LTV ratios make conventional loans less risky.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A loan fee charged by a lender to increase the lender's yield on a loan is:
feedback: Discount points increase lender yield. One point = 1% of loan amount.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a fully amortized loan:
option_a: interest may be paid in arrears, at the end of each period during which it is accrued
option_b: only the interest is paid in each payment period
option_c: the final interest payment will be determined after the last payment is made
option_d: the interest portion of each payment increases throughout the term of the loan
feedback: Interest is usually paid in arrears; payments first cover interest, then principal.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The clause in a trust deed or mortgage which permits the mortgagee to declare the entire unpaid sum due upon a default by a mortgagor is called:
feedback: Acceleration clause allows the lender to demand full payment upon default.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The deed in which you take title to a property contains the clause "subject to the existing loan". This means:
option_a: the lender is guaranteed that you alone will pay off the loan
option_b: you are in danger of losing the property if the seller does not make the payments on the underlying loan
option_c: the seller is permanently relieved of any liability
option_d: you agreed to assume the loan and make the payments directly to the lender in your name
feedback: "Subject to" means the buyer takes the property but the seller remains responsible for the loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If advertised alone, which would be in violation of TRUTH IN LENDING?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A balloon payment is usually associated with a

feedback: Balloon payments are large payments due at the end of the loan term.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A mortgage that covers more than one parcel of real property would be:
feedback: Blanket mortgages cover multiple parcels, often used by developers.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The amount of the prepayment penalty when a veteran pays off a $5,200 VA loan is:
feedback: VA loans do not have prepayment penalties.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A builder offers cash to the lender at closing so buyers get a lower interest rate. What type of loan is this?
feedback: Buydown loans reduce interest rates temporarily through upfront payment.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A loan made with real estate as security without government participation is a:
feedback: Conventional loans are private loans not insured or guaranteed by the government.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which is NOT a characteristic of a conventional loan?
option_a: Not insured/guaranteed by a public agency
option_b: Security rests on borrower ability and collateral
option_c: It is never insured by a private agency
feedback: Conventional loans can be insured privately; “never insured” is false.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Concerning typical land sale contracts, which is correct?
option_a: Buyer has absolute assurance of title
option_b: Buyer not responsible for taxes until deed delivered
option_c: Similar to lease; buyer has possession but not title
option_d: Least secure method; seller retains title, buyer is equitable owner
feedback: Land contracts retain title with seller; buyer has equitable ownership.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A late payment penalty in a loan is to:
feedback: Penalties incentivize timely payments.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender investing in long-term real estate loans, prefers commercial properties, and does not service loans is a:
feedback: Life insurance companies prefer large commercial loans and rarely service them.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: After final mortgage payment, records still show a lien until:
feedback: Recording a satisfaction releases the lien officially.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: To have a valid real estate loan contract, it must:
feedback: Statute of Frauds requires written contracts for real property loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A mortgage broker arranges home loans using:
feedback: Brokers use funds from banks or other financial institutions.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A mortgage results in:
feedback: Mortgages are specific liens on the financed property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A characteristic of a partially amortized loan is:
option_b: No loan balance at term end
feedback: Partially amortized loans have a final balloon payment.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A mortgage is usually accompanied by:
feedback: The promissory note is the borrower’s promise to repay.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When obtaining a home loan, which is NOT true?
option_a: Promissory note is a written promise
option_b: Note must accompany mortgage before funding
feedback: Promissory note is always required; down payment does not change that.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A Purchase Money Mortgage is:
option_b: Given from buyer to seller to secure purchase
feedback: Buyer gives seller a mortgage to secure purchase price (seller financing).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A buyer taking title "subject to" a mortgage should request a:
feedback: Reduction certificate shows remaining loan balance.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A typical subordination clause in a deed of trust:
feedback: Subordination clauses set lien priority under other liens.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Absence of which clause may cause financing difficulties when buying land and planning construction later?
feedback: Lender may require subordination for future construction financing.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Who provides funds for VA loans?
feedback: VA loans are guaranteed but funded by approved lenders.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Court order directing sheriff to sell property to satisfy judgment:
feedback: Writ of execution enforces judgment through property sale.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Truth in Lending disclosure must include:
option_a: APR, late fee, total monthly payments
option_b: APR, late fee, total repayment amount
feedback: Regulation Z requires total repayment amount, APR, and late charges.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Yuters purchase $75,000 home with $15k down, assume $23k mortgage, finance $37k with seller second mortgage. Type of loan?
feedback: Part purchase mortgage (“purchase money second”) finances remaining balance.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An impound/reserve account MOST benefits:
feedback: Ensures taxes and insurance are paid, protecting the lender.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A VA loan may be granted if:
feedback: VA loans require occupancy by the borrower.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Maximum permissible loan-to-value ratios are:
option_a: Based on sale price or appraised value, whichever is lower
feedback: LTV uses lower of sale price or appraised value.

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: A broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations is covered in which section of the buyer representation contract?
feedback: A broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations is covered in the Broker Liability Limitation section

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A clause in a loan document which makes the payments periodically increase is known as a
feedback: Escalation clauses allow loan payments to increase at set intervals, often to make the initial payments lower.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A clause may be inserted into a lease allowing rent to increase based upon a formula. This is known as a:
feedback: Common in commercial leases: rent increases tied to inflation, operating costs, or pre-determined schedules.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: To help a buyer get an FHA loan, a real estate broker should put the buyer in contact with
feedback: Only approved lenders can originate FHA-insured loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: On an FHA loan, which of the following would the buyer not be required to do?
feedback: FHA loans require lower down payments (as low as 3.5%), not 20%.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is not a characteristic of a fee simple absolute title?
option_a: It is transferable with or without consideration
option_c: It is always free of encumbrances
option_d: It is transferable by will or intestate
feedback: Be careful of words like "always" on your exam. There is usually an exception to just about every rule. Also referred to as an "Estate of Inheritance", a Fee Simple Estate is one type of freehold estate and is indefinite in duration. A fee simple estate can also be sold or inherited, and is not free of encumbrances  remember that there are always taxes, and taxes are an encumbrance. A fee simple absolute is the most interest that one can hold in land.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is not a characteristic of an FHA loan?
option_a: Maximum debt is determined by a formula
option_d: FHA provides the money for the loan
feedback: FHA insures loans made by approved lenders; it does not directly provide funds.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Why would a mortgagee (beneficiary) have an appraisal on the property?
feedback: Lenders require appraisals to confirm the property value is sufficient to secure the loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A standardized measurement expressing the true annual cost of borrowing is expressed as the what?
feedback: APR (Annual Percentage Rate) includes interest plus fees to show true cost of borrowing.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An owner was selling his own home. Can he advertise the down payment?
option_c: Yes, because it’s his own home
feedback: Individual homeowners are generally free to sell their own homes without Truth in Lending restrictions.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The rescission provisions of the Truth in Lending Act apply to which transactions?
feedback: The right of rescission applies to consumer credit transactions, not business or agricultural loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Conventional loans are made from
feedback: Conventional loans are funded by private lenders, not government programs.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: During a period of high inflation, the Federal Reserve Board can limit, to some degree, the expansion of currency by:
feedback: Higher reserve requirements reduce bank lending, slowing money supply growth.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which transaction requires a securities license?
feedback: Selling shares in Fannie Mae requires a licensed securities broker.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A Savings & Loan institution would be violating the Federal Fair Housing Act by denying a loan to Mr. and Mrs. Sparks for which reason?
feedback: Discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, familial status, or disability is prohibited.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following liens will have first priority?
feedback: Tax liens generally take priority over all other liens during foreclosure.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: From a lender's point of view, the most important consideration in granting a mortgage loan is the:
feedback: Lenders prioritize the borrower’s ability to repay the loan.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Funds for conventional loans are received directly from whom?
feedback: Conventional loans come from private lenders, not government agencies.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If an individual gives his property as security for a debt, but does not give up possession, he has
feedback: Hypothecation allows the borrower to pledge property as collateral without surrendering possession.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A homeowner defaults on their mortgage. After the foreclosure sale (in most states), the homeowner has a right of:
feedback: Redemption allows a defaulted homeowner to reclaim the property by paying owed amounts within a statutory period.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In an installment land contract, what type of title did the seller retain?
feedback: The seller retains legal title until the buyer completes payments; the buyer has equitable title.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a single parent is applying for a real estate loan, when must income from child support be revealed?
feedback: Only income relied upon for qualifying for the loan must be disclosed.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: It would be to the advantage of lending institutions to waive prepayment penalties when which of the following occurs?
feedback: Waiving penalties encourages payoff, creating funds to lend when demand is high.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Just as his foreclosed property is about to be sold at public auction, a mortgagor appears with sufficient funds to prevent the sale by exercising his right of:
feedback: The equitable right of redemption allows a borrower to prevent foreclosure by paying owed amounts before the sale.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A buyer wants to take out an FHA loan. The broker should refer the buyer directly to:
feedback: Only FHA-approved lenders can originate FHA-insured loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Life insurance company mortgage holdings are greatest in:
feedback: Life insurance companies favor large commercial and apartment loans for long-term investments.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In which of the following markets may a lender sell a loan that a mortgage banker has previously originated?
feedback: The secondary market is where lenders buy and sell existing mortgages.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A mortgage broker:
feedback: Mortgage brokers arrange loans and match borrowers with lenders without funding the loan themselves.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which statement is not correct regarding mortgage fraud?
option_a: Mortgage fraud is punishable by up to 40 years in federal prison.
option_b: Mortgage fraud is investigated by the FBI.
option_c: The use of inflated appraisal in collusion with a lender.
option_d: Mortgage fraud can be created through the use of false identity on loan applications without the true person’s knowledge.
feedback: Mortgage fraud is a federal crime, but penalties are generally handled at the state level and are less severe than 40 years in federal prison.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a borrower must pay $6,000 for points on a $150,000 loan, how many points is the lender charging?
feedback: One point = 1% of the loan amount. $150,000 × 1% = $1,500; $6,000 ÷ $1,500 = 4 points.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is considered a conventional loan?
option_a: ARM loan from a commercial bank
feedback: Conventional loans are funded by private lenders; ARMs from commercial banks are conventional, unlike FHA/VA loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Processing a loan includes analysis of which selection?
feedback: Loan processing involves analyzing the buyer’s credit to assess repayment ability.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: RESPA prohibits which of the following acts?
feedback: RESPA prohibits referral fees or kickbacks in real estate transactions to protect consumers.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Statement signed by the mortgagee acknowledging loan paid in full is called:
feedback: A satisfaction of mortgage clears the lien and releases the property title.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Term mortgage loan means:
feedback: A term mortgage requires full principal payment at the end of the term (balloon payment).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Type of interest usually charged when financing a house:
feedback: Mortgages typically use simple interest, calculated on principal over time.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Who enforces the Truth-in-Lending Act?
feedback: FTC enforces TILA to protect consumers from misleading credit practices.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Who is NOT an originator of primary loans?
feedback: FHA insures loans but does not originate them.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Loan-to-value ratio is determined by:
option_b: Loan ÷ sales price or appraised value, highest
option_c: Loan ÷ sales price or appraised value, lowest
feedback: LTV = loan ÷ lesser of sales price or appraised value.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Requirement of a "tax-free exchange":
feedback: IRC §1031 allows tax deferral if properties exchanged are of like kind.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Who usually pays loan origination fee on FHA loan?
feedback: Borrower pays the origination fee along with mortgage insurance.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Purpose of Truth-in-Lending (Regulation Z):
feedback: Regulation Z ensures consumers are informed of credit terms and costs.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: RESPA applies to which activity?
feedback: RESPA governs federally related home loans, disclosures, and prohibiting kickbacks.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Loan type that reduces principal fastest:
feedback: Shorter-term, higher-rate loans pay principal faster.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Trustee in a trust deed is responsible for:
feedback: Trustee initiates foreclosure and sells property if borrower defaults.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Prohibited down payment advertisement under Regulation Z:
feedback: Misleading ads implying any buyer can purchase with $1,000 down are prohibited.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: TRID allowed to be examined pre-closing to:
feedback: Closing Disclosure given 3 days prior so buyer can verify figures and ask questions.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage company assembling loans to sell is called:
feedback: Warehousing: holding loans temporarily before selling in secondary market.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Compared with 30-year loan, a 20-year loan results in:
feedback: Shorter term = higher monthly payments but faster principal reduction.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage interest deduction allowed on:
feedback: Deduction allowed for primary, second, and residential rental properties.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: False statement about federal-supported housing loans:
option_b: Federal supplies funds to lending agency
feedback: Federal government does not supply funds; it insures/guarantees loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Easements and encroachments are types of
feedback: General types of encumbrance include easements, encroachments, licenses, and deed restrictions

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The tenant continues to reside in the property without the landlord’s approval in an estate for years lease. By doing so, the tenant has created which of the following estates?
feedback: A tenancy or an estate of sufferance occurs when a tenant stays on the property without the landlord’s approval after the expiration of the lease

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An affirmative easement gives the benefited party
option_a: the right to possess a defined portion of another's real property
option_b: the right to prevent the owner of a real property from using it in a defined way
option_c: the right to a defined use of a portion of another's real property
option_d: the right to receive a portion of any income generated by another's real property
feedback: An easement may be affirmative, allowing a use, such as a right-of-way

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: There are two adjoining properties. An easement allows property A to use the access road that belongs to property B. In this situation, property A is said to be which of the following in relation to property B?
feedback: An easement appurtenant gives a property owner a right of usage to portions of an adjoining property owned by another party. The property enjoying the usage right is called the dominant tenement, or dominant estate

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following describes a situation in which an easement might be created against the wishes of the property owner?
option_a: The property has been continuously used as an easement with the knowledge but without the permission of the owner for a period of time
option_b: The owner of an adjoining property asks the property owner for an easement, is refused, and then uses the property anyway without the knowledge of the owner
option_c: The owner of an adjoining property decides he needs to widen his driveway by sharing his neighbor's driveway and sues in court to create an easement by necessity
option_d: The owner of an adjoining property grants an easement to a third party that includes an easement on the first property
feedback: Easement by prescription. If someone uses another's property as an easement without permission for a statutory period of time and under certain conditions, a court order may give the user the easement right by prescription, regardless of the owner's desires

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is the primary danger of allowing an encroachment?
option_a: An encroachment automatically grants the benefiting party an easement
option_b: The encroached party may be liable for additional real estate taxes to cover the area being encroached upon by the neighboring property
option_c: Over time, the encroachment may become an easement by prescription that damages the property's market value
feedback: Encroachments cause infringements on the rights of the trespassed owner, and may diminish the property's value over time, particularly when the property is to be sold

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property owner who is selling her land wants to control how it is used in the future. She might accomplish her aim by means of
feedback: A private party who wants to control the quality and standards of a property can establish a deed restriction. Deed restrictions take precedence over zoning ordinances if they are more restrictive

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What distinguishes a lien from other types of encumbrance?
option_a: It involves a monetary claim against the value of a property
option_b: It lowers the value of a property
option_c: It is created voluntarily by the property owner
option_d: It attaches to the property rather than to the owner of the property
feedback: A lien is a creditor's claim against personal or real property as security for a debt of the property owner. If the owner defaults, the lien gives the creditor the right to force the sale of the property to satisfy the debt. Liens do not necessarily alter the property value. Liens can be involuntary as well as voluntary. Finally, liens attach to the property, but so do other encumbrances

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A certain property has the following liens recorded against it: a mortgage lien dating from three years ago; a mechanic's lien dating from two years ago; a real estate tax lien for the current year; and a second mortgage lien dating from the current year. In case of a foreclosure, which of these liens will be paid first?
feedback: Superior and inferior lien. The category of superior, or senior, liens ranks above the category of inferior, or junior, liens, meaning that superior liens receive first payment from the proceeds of a foreclosure. The superior category includes liens for real estate tax, special assessments, and inheritance tax. Other liens, including income tax liens, are inferior

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is meant by a "lien-theory" state?
option_a: A state in which liens are given priority over other encumbrances
option_b: A state in which a mortgagor retains title to the property when a mortgage lien is created
option_c: A state in which the holder of a mortgage lien receives title to the mortgaged property until the debt is satisfied
option_d: A state in which liens exist in theory but not in practice
feedback: In lien-theory states, mortgages and trust deeds secure loans made on real property. In these states, the lender records a lien as soon as possible after disbursing the funds in order to establish lien priority

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A homeowner has hired a contractor to build a room addition. The work has been completed and the contractor has been paid for all work and materials but fails to pay the lumber yard for a load of lumber. What potential problem may the home owner experience?
option_a: The contractor may place a mechanic's lien for the amount of the lumber against the homeowner's real property
option_b: The lumber yard may place a vendor's lien against the contractor and the homeowner for the amount of the lumber
option_c: The lumber yard may place a mechanic's lien for the amount of the lumber against the homeowner's real property
option_d: The homeowner has no liability because the contractor was paid for the lumber
feedback: Any individual who performs approved work may place a mechanic's lien on the property to the extent of the direct costs incurred. Note that unpaid subcontractors may record mechanic's liens whether the general contractor has been paid or not. Thus it is possible for an owner to have to double-pay a bill in order to eliminate the mechanic's lien if the general contractor neglects to pay the subcontractors

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The process of enforcing a lien by forcing sale of the lienee's property is called
feedback: All liens can be enforced by the sale or other transfer of title of the secured property, whether by court action, operation of law, or through powers granted in the original loan agreement. The enforcement proceedings are referred to as foreclosure

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An important difference between a judicial foreclosure and a non-judicial foreclosure is
option_a: there is no right to redeem the property in a non-judicial foreclosure
option_b: a judicial foreclosure forces a sale of the property
option_c: a non-judicial foreclosure ensures that all liens are paid in order of priority
option_d: the lienor receives title directly in a non-judicial foreclosure
feedback: There is no redemption right in non-judicial foreclosure

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A defaulting borrower may avoid foreclosure by giving the mortgagee
option_b: a deed in lieu of foreclosure
feedback: A defaulting borrower who faces foreclosure may avoid court actions and costs by voluntarily deeding the property to the mortgagee. This is accomplished with a deed in lieu of foreclosure, which transfers legal title to the lienholder

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property survey reveals that a new driveway extends one foot onto a neighbor's property. This is an example of
feedback: An encroachment is the unauthorized, physical intrusion of one owner's real property into that of another

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property owner has an easement appurtenant on her property. When the property is sold to another party, the easement
option_c: transfers with the owner to a new property
option_d: becomes a lien on the property
feedback: The term appurtenant means "attaching to." An easement appurtenant attaches to the estate and transfers with it unless specifically stated otherwise in the transaction documents

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A brick fence straddles the property line of two neighbors. The neighbors agree not to damage it in any way. This is an example of
feedback: A party wall is a common wall shared by two separate structures along a property boundary. Party wall agreements generally provide for severalty ownership of half of the wall by each owner, or at least some fraction of the width of the wall. In addition, the agreement grants a negative easement appurtenant to each owner in the other's wall. This is to prevent unlimited use of the wall, in particular a destructive use that would jeopardize the adjacent property owner's building. The agreement also establishes responsibilities and obligations for maintenance and repair of the wall

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property owner allows Betty Luanne to cross his property as a shortcut to her kindergarten school bus. One day the property owner dies. What right was Betty given, and what happens to it in the future?
option_a: A personal easement in gross, which continues after the owner's death
option_b: An easement by prescription, which continues after the owner's death
option_c: A license, which continues after the owner's death
option_d: A license, which terminates at the owner's death
feedback: A license, much like a personal easement in gross, is a personal right that a property owner grants to another to use the property for a specific purpose. Licenses are not transferrable and do not attach to the land. They cease on the death of either party, or on the sale of the property. Unlike a personal easement in gross, a license is revocable at any time. Licenses are often granted informally, as a verbal statement of permission

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A court renders a judgment which authorizes a lien to be placed against the defendant's house, car, and personal belongings. This is an example of a
feedback: A general lien is one placed against any and all real and personal property owned by a particular debtor. An example is an inheritance tax lien placed against all property owned by the heir. A judgment lien attaches to real and personal property as a result of a money judgment issued by a court in favor of a creditor

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What happens in a short sale?
option_a: The transaction is completed before the end of the contingency period.
option_b: Buyer and seller complete a transaction without the assistance of a licensed agent.
option_c: The proceeds of the sale do not cover the seller’s outstanding mortgage loan balance.
option_d: The seller agrees to accept less than the listing price.
feedback: A short sale occurs when a property owner owes more than resale value and loan pay-off for a property and agrees to let the lender sell the property in exchange for release from the lien. The lender may or may not agree to accept the deficient price as satisfaction and may require the seller to pay the deficiency by way of a deficiency judgment. There may also be tax consequences for the seller. To avoid the deficiency, the seller must make sure that the agreements include a full release of the underlying debt and a statement that it was fully satisfied.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following scenarios is an example of an encroachment?
option_a: The branch of an apple tree extends into the neighbor’s yard
option_b: A homeowner plants trees in the center of her yard
option_c: The owners of a shared easement use it as a place to park their car
option_d: An owner grants permission for an individual to build a fence
feedback: An encroachment is the physical intrusion onto another person’s property without permission

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following scenarios is an example of valuable consideration?
option_a: A developer builds a three-story home for $750,000
option_b: The daughter inherited a four-acre lot
option_c: The shares of a rental business were transferred to the spouse
option_d: The buyer’s down payment for a house was gifted by the grandparents
feedback: A contract must contain a two-way exchange to be considered valuable consideration as compensation for performance by the other party. The contract is not valid or enforceable if only one party provides consideration

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A legal owner must allow the use of a specific area to another individual such as surface rights or airspace.  This is known as
feedback: An easement is a real property interest where a non-owning individual has the right to use the land or real property that is legally owned by another individual

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which party has the choice to use the adjoining property at their convenience?
feedback: The individual who chooses when to use the designated area in a specific way is known as the dominant tenement

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: There are two adjacent properties connected by an easement.  Property Owner A must give Property Owner B access to the pathway leading to the pond.  In this case, what is Property Owner A known as in relation to Property Owner B?
feedback: The legal owner who has the easement is the servient tenement and must allow an individual the right to use the easement

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property owner cannot be landlocked to a public road.  In these instances, it is necessary for the owner to receive an
feedback: By law, a parcel of real property must have access to a public road.  This is accomplished by an easement by necessity

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is an example of a lien?
option_b: A license issued to fish in the lake
option_c: An easement by prescription in the county
option_d: A deed restriction that limits land use
feedback: A lien refers to monies or debt owed that attaches to real property

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a voluntary transfer of title to real estate?
feedback: Voluntary alienation is an unforced transfer of title by sale or gift from an owner to another party. A deed is the legal instrument used to effect a voluntary transfer of title to real estate

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a voluntary lien?
option_d: Transferring title to a family member
feedback: A voluntary lien occurs when the individual intentionally incurs a debt, such as a mortgage

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following liens characterizes a general lien?
option_b: A specific tax bill attached to a condo unit
option_c: An outstanding property bill on a residential property
option_d: A mechanic’s lien attached to a particular townhome
feedback: The general lien attaches to any or all personal or real property, whereas a specific lien attaches to a singular parcel of real property or item of personal property

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which factor generally determines lien priority among junior liens?
option_b: The date when the lien is recorded
option_c: Whether payment will be made by cash or credit
feedback: The date of recordation of the junior lien, with the exception of mechanics’ liens,  determines the lien’s priority

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An involuntary lien can be placed on a property under which of the following circumstances?
option_a: Purchasing a car with a personal loan
option_c: Buying a property without a mortgage
option_d: Having an equitable lien against a homeowner filed by a court
feedback: An equitable lien is ordered by the court system and is considered involuntary

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What occurs when a deficiency judgment is issued in a foreclosure sale?
option_b: The deficiency judgment allows for a short sale to occur
option_c: The mortgagee requests that a judgment lien be placed on the mortgagor’s assets
option_d: This restricts the usage of an easement of necessity
feedback: If the sale does not yield sufficient funds to cover the amounts owed, the mortgagee can ask the court for a deficiency judgment against the borrower’s assets to potentially recoup payment

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is an example of an encumbrance?
feedback: An easement is an encumbrance giving the easement holder the right to use portions of the legal owner’s real property in a defined way

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: How is an involuntary easement created?
option_b: By transferring the bundle of rights
option_c: By condemning a property through eminent domain
feedback: Government entities can create easements through the exercise of eminent domain, wherein they condemn a portion of a property and cause it to be sold “for the greater good.”

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Easements can be terminated by which of the following scenarios?
option_a: Merging two adjacent parcels owned by different owners
option_b: Adding a third parcel across the street
option_d: Having an adverse and hostile party housed in the property
feedback: An easement can be terminated by merging two parcels where a dominant and servient tenement exist

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: To minimize losses resulting from an employee’s criminal activity, such as theft or negligence, the property owner will use this type of coverage
feedback: The surety bond covers the property owner should an employee engage in criminal or negligent activities during employment

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What type of lease did the tenant sign when a landlord is to receive a portion of the tenant’s business income?
feedback: A percentage lease requires the tenant to pay the landlord a portion of the retail operation’s income or sales. This may be in addition to a base rental amount

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What right allows the borrower to reclaim a property that has been foreclosed by paying off amounts owed to the mortgagee?
feedback: The borrower’s right of redemption, also called equity of redemption, is the right to reclaim a property that has been foreclosed by paying off amounts owed to creditors, including interest and costs

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What type of eviction occurs when the tenant vacates the leased premises due to the landlord’s negligence or if the property is deemed as unfit?
feedback: A constructive eviction occurs when a tenant vacates the leased premises and declares the lease void, claiming that landlord’s actions have made the premises unfit for the purpose described in the lease

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A specific lien attaches to which of the following items?
option_a: Three different parcels across two counties
option_b: The air rights spanning two apartment buildings in different states
option_c: Ad valorem taxes on luxury purchases
option_d: An FHA mortgage on a family house
feedback: A specific lien attaches to a single item of real or personal property and does not impact other properties owned by the debtor

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A ______________ is considered a superior lien
feedback: Superior liens take precedence over all junior liens regardless of recording date, since they are considered to be matters of public record not requiring constructive notice

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A subordination occurs when
option_a: a debtor encroaches onto another property
option_b: a lienor changes the priority of a junior lien by voluntarily becoming subordinate to it
option_c: a specific lien is attached to three properties owned by the mortgagor
option_d: a lender places their position as a superior lien
feedback: A subordination allows a superior lien to change the priority of a junior lien by voluntarily agreeing to be placed lower than the junior lien

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A covenant is established by
option_a: mutual agreement as expressed in a contract or other legal document
option_b: separation of the bundle of rights
option_d: division of real property and personal assets
feedback: A covenant can be created by mutual agreement and is generally noted in a contract or some other legal document that details the promise(s)

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The parties to a short sale are the buyer and seller.  Which of the following is a third party contingency who must approve the sale?
feedback:
After the property is retaken by foreclosure or short sale, it becomes “bank owned.” Forbearance or a loan modification allows the borrower to avoid legal action and keep the property under a new agreement with the lender.
The parties to a short sale are the buyer and seller. The lender is a third party contingency who must approve the sale.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An encumbrance is most broadly defined as
option_a: another's right to use a property without the permission of the property owner
option_b: another's right to claim the sale proceeds of a property that has been used as collateral for a loan
option_c: another's interest in a real property that limits the interests of the freehold property owner
option_d: another's right to control how the freehold owner of a real property uses the property
feedback: An encumbrance is an interest in and right to real property that limits the legal owner's freehold interest. In effect, an encumbrance is another's right to use or take possession of a legal owner's property, or to prevent the legal owner from enjoying the full bundle of rights in the estate

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is true of easements in general?
option_a: They involve the property that contains the easement and a non-owning party
option_b: They apply to a whole property, not to any specific portion of the property
option_c: They cannot be assigned or transferred
option_d: They may require a specific use, but cannot prohibit one
feedback: An easement appurtenant gives a property owner a right of usage to portions of an adjoining property owned by another party. The property enjoying the usage right is called the dominant tenement, or dominant estate. The property containing the physical easement itself is the servient tenement, since it must serve the easement use.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If the owner of property A has a court-ordered easement to drive across property B because it is the only way for A to have access to a public road, the easement is a(n)
feedback: An easement by necessity is an easement appurtenant granted by a court of law to a property owner because of a circumstance of necessity, most commonly the need for access to a property. Since property cannot be legally landlocked, or without legal access to a public thoroughfare, a court will grant an owner of a landlocked property an easement by necessity over an adjoining property that has access to a thoroughfare

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Property A and property B share a common wall along the property boundary.  To ensure that neither owner destroys the wall, the owners of A and B should create
feedback: Party wall agreements generally provide for severalty ownership of half of the wall by each owner, or at least some fraction of the width of the wall. In addition, the agreement grants a negative easement appurtenant to each owner in the other's wall. This is to prevent unlimited use of the wall, in particular a destructive use that would jeopardize the adjacent property owner's building. The agreement also establishes responsibilities and obligations for maintenance and repair of the wall

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: An encroachment is
option_a: an easement that has not been recorded on the title of the burdened property
option_b: an unauthorized physical intrusion of one property into another
option_c: a right granted by a property owner to the owner of an adjoining property to build a structure that protrudes across the property boundary
option_d: a structure that does not comply with a zoning ordinance
feedback: An encroachment is the unauthorized, physical intrusion of one owner's real property into that of another. Examples of encroachments are: a tree limb extending into the neighbor's property, violating his or her airspace; a driveway extending beyond the lot line onto the neighbor's land; and a fence built beyond the property line

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A court might grant an easement by prescription if
option_a: a municipality needs to dig a trench across an owner's property to install a sewer line to a neighboring property, and the owner refuses permission
option_b: a property owner sells the front half of a lot and wants to continue using the driveway to reach the rear of the lot
option_c: an intruder has been using an owner's property for a certain period with the owner's knowledge but without permission
option_d: a property owner wants to prevent the owner of an adjoining property from building a second-story addition that blocks the sunlight to his rose garden
feedback: If someone uses another's property as an easement without permission for a statutory period of time and under certain conditions, a court order may give the user the easement right by prescription, regardless of the owner's desires. For a prescriptive easement order to be granted, the following circumstances must be true: the use has been occurring without permission or license; the owner knows or is presumed to have known of the use; and the use has been generally uninterrupted over the statutory prescriptive period

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Deed restrictions generally concern
option_a: the form of ownership in which a property may be held
option_b: how long a property must be owned before it can be legally transferred
option_c: what groups of people are legally excluded from future ownership of a property
option_d: how a property may be used and what structures may be built on it
feedback: Deed restrictions typically apply to:  •the land use  •the size and type of structures that may be placed on the property  •minimum costs of structures   •engineering, architectural, and aesthetic standards, such as setbacks or specific standards of construction

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: One difference between a condition and a covenant is that
option_a: breach of a covenant leads to forfeiture of ownership
option_b: a covenant can be enforced in court
option_c: a condition can be created at any time
option_d: a condition can be created only when there is a transfer of ownership
feedback: A condition can only be created within a transfer of ownership. If a condition is later violated, a suit can force the owner to forfeit ownership to the previous owner. A covenant can be created by mutual agreement. If a covenant is breached, an injunction can force compliance or payment of compensatory damages

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The lienee in a lien is the
option_b: owner of the property that is subject to the lien
option_c: party who has a claim against the owner of the property subject to the lien
option_d: property that is given as security for a debt
feedback: The creditor who places a lien on a property is called the lienor, and the debtor who owns the property is the lienee

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lien is a
option_a: debt incurred by a property owner
option_b: promissory note granted by a property owner as security for a debt
option_c: creditor's claim against property as security for a debt of the property owner
feedback: A lien is a creditor's claim against personal or real property as security for a debt of the property owner. If the owner defaults, the lien gives the creditor the right to force the sale of the property to satisfy the debt

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What kind of interest does a lienor usually have in the liened property?
option_b: Legal ownership of a dominant tenement
option_c: A legal interest in a pro rata share of the property
option_d: An equitable interest, except a mortgage lienor in a title-theory state
feedback: A lienor generally has an equitable interest in the property, but not legal ownership. The exception is a mortgage lien on a property in a title-theory state. In these states, the mortgage transaction conveys legal title to the lender, who holds it until the mortgage obligations are satisfied.
