During the mortgage loan period, the borrower has equitable title to the property

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: How is a lien terminated?
option_a: Payment of the debt that is the subject of the lien and recording of the satisfaction
option_b: Transfer of the property that has the lien
option_c: Recording of another lien that is superior
option_d: Death of the lienor or lienee
feedback: A lien terminates on payment of the debt and recording of documents. Payment of the debt and recording of the appropriate satisfaction documents ordinarily terminate a lien. If a default occurs, a suit for judgment or foreclosure enforces the lien. These actions force the sale of the property

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A general lien is a lien
option_c: placed against an individual rather than against any particular property
option_d: placed against any and all property owned by an individual
feedback: A general lien is one placed against any and all real and personal property owned by a particular debtor. An example is an inheritance tax lien placed against all property owned by the heir

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The landlord’s rights and responsibilities include all the following EXCEPT
feedback: The landlord maintains the right to enter the premises, receive payments, retake on termination, and pursue remedies for tenant’s default

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A real estate tax lien, a federal income tax lien, a judgment lien, and a mortgage lien are recorded against a property. Which lien will be paid first when the property is sold?
feedback: The category of superior, or senior, liens ranks above the category of inferior, or junior, liens, meaning that superior liens receive first payment from the proceeds of a foreclosure. The superior category includes liens for real estate tax, special assessments, and inheritance tax. Other liens, including income tax liens, mortgage liens and judgment liens, are inferior

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lien holder can change the priority of a junior lien by agreeing to
option_b: lower the amount of the claim
feedback: A lienor can change the priority of a junior lien by voluntarily agreeing to subordinate, or lower, the lien's position in the hierarchy

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following tax liens is a junior lien?
feedback: Superior liens in rank order:  1. Real estate tax liens   2. Special assessment liens   3. Federal estate tax liens   4. State inheritance tax liens.  Junior liens: priority by date of recording:  •Federal income tax liens  •State corporate income tax liens  •State intangible tax liens  •Judgment liens  •Mortgage liens  •Vendor's liens  •Mechanic's liens (priority by date work was performed)

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Foreclosure is
option_a: a court-ordered acceleration of loan payments
option_b: the final step in a bankruptcy filing
option_c: a proceeding to enforce a lien by forcing sale or transfer of a secured property
option_d: a proceeding to take equitable title to a property that was liened as security for a mortgage loan
feedback: All liens can be enforced by the sale or other transfer of title of the secured property, whether by court action, operation of law, or through powers granted in the original loan agreement. The enforcement proceedings are referred to as foreclosure

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A property is secured by a mortgage that does not contain a "power of sale" clause.  To foreclose, the lien holder will have to
option_c: obtain a deed in lieu of foreclosure
option_d: obtain a writ to extinguish the lien
feedback: Judicial foreclosure occurs in states that use a two-party mortgage document (borrower and lender) that does not contain a "power of sale" provision. Lacking this provision, a lender must file a foreclosure suit and undertake a court proceeding to enforce the lien

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a strict foreclosure,
option_a: a lender takes title to the liened property directly
option_b: a lender receives the proceeds from the forced sale of the liened property
option_c: the defaulting owner does not have the opportunity to prevent the foreclosure by paying the amounts owed
feedback: A lender takes title to the liened property directly. If a borrower has failed to meet loan obligations in spite of proper notice and applicable grace periods, the lender can accelerate the loan, or declare that the loan balance and all other sums due on the loan are payable immediately

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A short sale may occur when
option_a: several buyers are engaged in a bidding war on the same property.
option_b: a property owner needs to sell at a price that is less than the loan pay-off amount.
option_c: a property seller prefers not to pay for the full services of a broker.
option_d: a contract to sell or buy does not include a contingency period for inspections and repairs.
feedback: A short sale occurs when a property owner owes more than resale value and loan pay-off for a property and agrees to let the lender sell the property in exchange for release from the lien. The lender may or may not agree to accept the deficient price as satisfaction and may require the seller to pay the deficiency by way of a deficiency judgment.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A homeowner borrows money from a lender and gives the lender a mortgage on the property as collateral for the loan. The homeowner retains title to the property. This is an example of
feedback: The process of securing a loan by pledging a property without giving up ownership of the property is called hypothecation

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a deed of trust transaction, which of the following occurs?
option_a: The beneficiary conveys title to a trustee in exchange for loan funds
option_b: The trustee conveys title to a beneficiary in exchange for loan funds
option_c: The trustor conveys title to a trustee in exchange for loan funds from the beneficiary
option_d: The trustee conveys title to a trustor in exchange for loan funds from the beneficiary
feedback: A deed of trust conveys title to the property in question from the borrower (trustor) to a trustee as security for the loan. The trustee is a third party fiduciary to the trust. While the loan is in place, the trustee holds the title on behalf of the lender, who is the beneficiary of the trust

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender who charges a rate of interest in excess of legal limits is guilty of
option_d: nothing; there are no legal limits to interest rates
feedback: Many states have laws against usury, which is the charging of excessive interest rates on loans. Such states have a maximum rate that is either a flat rate or a variable rate tied to an index such as the prime lending rate

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender is charging 3 points on a $500,000 loan. The borrower must therefore pay the lender an advance amount of
feedback: A discount point is one percent of the loan amount. Thus, one point on a $500,000 loan equals $5,000. 3 points would be $15,000. The lender charges this as pre-paid interest at closing by funding only the face amount of the loan minus the discount points. The borrower, however, must repay the full loan amount, along with interest calculated on the full amount

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The difference between a balloon loan and an amortized loan is
option_a: an amortized loan is paid off over the loan period
option_b: a balloon loan always has a shorter loan term
option_d: a balloon loan must be retired in five years
feedback: Amortized loans retire the principal balance over the loan period. If a loan does not do this, one must make a balloon payment at the end of the loan term to complete the loan payoff

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A distinctive feature of a promissory note is that
option_b: it must be accompanied by a mortgage
feedback: A promissory note is a negotiable instrument, which means the payee may assign it to a third party. The assignee would then have the right to receive the borrower's periodic payments

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When the terms of the mortgage loan are satisfied, the mortgagee
option_a: may retain any overage in the escrow account
option_b: may inspect the property before returning legal title
option_c: may be entitled to charge the borrower a small fee to close the loan
option_d: may be required to execute a release of mortgage document
feedback: Lenders may be required to release the mortgage or trust document to the borrower when the borrower has paid off the loan and all other sums secured by the document. The release clause, also known as a defeasance clause, may specify that the mortgagee will execute a satisfaction of mortgage (also known as release of mortgage and mortgage discharge) to the mortgagor

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In addition to income, credit, and employment data, a mortgage lender requires additional documentation, usually including
feedback: Loan underwriting is the process of assessing the lender's risk in giving a loan. Mortgage underwriting includes: evaluating the borrower's ability to repay the loan; appraising the value of the property offered as security; and determining the terms of the loan

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The three overriding considerations of a lender's mortgage loan decision are
option_a: points, interest rate, and loan term
option_b: the location of the mortgaged property, the borrower's cash, and the amount of the borrower's equity
option_c: the ability to re-pay, the value of the collateral, and the profitability of the loan
option_d: the amount of the loan, the borrower's income, and the down payment
feedback: A lender assesses risks by examining, or qualifying, both borrower and property. In qualifying a borrower, an underwriter weighs the ability of the borrower to repay the loan. In qualifying a property, an underwriter assesses the ability of the property value to cover potential losses. In this evaluation, a lender requires that the appraised value of the property be more than adequate to cover the contemplated loan and costs. Finally, the loan must make money for the lending organization as a basic business precept

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The major players in the secondary mortgage market are
option_a: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and Ginnie Mae
option_d: Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, and the Federal Reserve
feedback: As major players in the secondary market, the Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA, "Fannie Mae"), Government National Mortgage Association (GNMA, "Ginnie Mae), and Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (FHLMC, "Freddie Mac") tend to set the standards for the primary market

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The primary role of the Federal Housing Authority in the mortgage lending market is to
option_a: guarantee loans made by approved lenders
option_b: insure loans made by approved lenders
option_c: purchase loans made by approved lenders
option_d: originate loans made by approved lenders
feedback: The Federal Housing Administration (FHA) does not lend money, but insures permanent long-term loans made by others. The lender must be approved by the FHA, and the borrower must meet certain FHA qualifications

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A graduated payment loan is a mortgage loan where
option_a: loan funds are disbursed to the borrower on a graduated basis
option_b: the interest rate periodically increases in graduated phases
option_d: the loan payments gradually increase and the loan term gradually decreases
feedback: With a graduated payment mortgage, the payments at the beginning of the loan term are not sufficient to amortize the loan fully, and unpaid interest is added to the principal balance. Payments are later adjusted to a level that will fully amortize the loan's increased balance over the remaining loan term

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A buydown is a financing arrangement where
option_a: the lender lowers the interest rate on a loan in exchange for a prepayment of principal
option_b: the borrower pays additional interest at the onset in order to obtain a lower interest rate
option_c: the lender requires the borrower to buy down the price of the property by increasing the down payment
option_d: the borrower pays the lender additional funds to buy down the term of the loan
feedback: A buydown loan entails a prepayment of interest on a loan. The prepayment effectively lowers the interest rate and the periodic payments for the borrower

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The key feature of an adjustable mortgage loan is that
option_b: the monthly payment increases over the life of the loan
option_c: the principal balance does not amortize
option_d: the loan term can be shortened or lengthened
feedback: Adjustable rate mortgages (ARMs) allow the lender to change the interest rate at specified intervals and by a specified amount. Federal regulations place limits on incremental interest rate increases and on the total amount by which the rate may be increased over the loan term

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: One feature of a wraparound mortgage loan is that
option_a: the loan is a senior loan
option_b: the seller offering the buyer a wraparound can profit from a difference in interest rates
option_c: the underlying loan must be retired
option_d: the second mortgage borrower may make payments directly to the first mortgage lender
feedback: In a wraparound loan arrangement, the seller receives a junior mortgage from the buyer, and uses the buyer's payments to make the payments on the original first mortgage

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A builder is required to secure a loan with mortgages on three properties. This is an example of
feedback: A blanket mortgage is secured by more than one property, such as multiple parcels of real estate in a development

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is true of a loan with negative amortization?
option_a: The loan is an interest-only loan
option_b: Payments are not sufficient to retire the loan
option_c: The loan balance is diminishing, or going negative
option_d: Additional interest is being added to the monthly payment
feedback: Negative amortization causes the loan balance to increase over the term. This occurs if the borrower's periodic payment is insufficient to cover the interest owed for the period. The lender adds the amount of unpaid interest to the borrower's loan balance

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A bank is offering Henry a 30-year loan of $300,000 at a rate of 4.5%. The loan constant for this loan is 5.07. How much will Henry have to pay for monthly principal and interest?
feedback: 300 ($300,000 / 1,000) times the loan constant of 5.07 is $1,521 for PI each month.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A valid mortgage or trust deed is evidence of
option_a: collateral for the payment of monies due
option_c: transfer of title from owner to lender
option_d: borrower’s ability to repay the loan
feedback: A valid mortgage or trust deed financing arrangement requires a note as evidence of the debt

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The mortgagor in a mortgage financing transaction is the
option_c: representative of the title insurance company
option_d: previous owner of the subject property
feedback: A mortgage is a legal document stating the pledge of the borrower’s property as collateral security to the lender (the mortgagee).  The  borrower is the mortgagor.  (Remember both the words “lender” and “mortgagee” have two ‘e’s.)

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A deed of trust conveys title to the property
option_a: from the borrower to a trustee
option_b: from the lender to a trustee
option_d: for a period of one year
feedback: A deed of trust conveys title to the property in question from the borrower (trustor) to a trustee as security for the loan. The trustee is a third party fiduciary to the trust. While the loan is in place, the trustee holds the title on behalf of the lender, who is the beneficiary of the trust

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The principal amount of the loan is
option_a: the amount of money borrowed on which interest payments are calculated
option_b: the purchase price less the amount of the appraised value
option_c: the purchase price minus the down payment
option_d: the sum of the total amount borrowed plus the down payment
feedback: The capital amount borrowed, on which interest payments are calculated, is the original loan principal

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A discount point is
option_a: an amount deducted from loan origination fees as a thank-you for selecting that lender
option_b: an amount above which a discount is given to a qualified borrower
feedback: A discount point is one percent of the loan amount. Thus, one point on a $100,000 loan equals $1,000. The lender charges this as pre-paid interest at closing by funding only the face amount of the loan minus the discount points

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A promissory note sets forth the
option_a: loan amount, term of loan, and interest rate
option_b: loan amount, term of loan, interest rate and method of repayment
option_c: loan amount, term of loan, interest rate, method and timing for repayment
option_d: loan amount, term of loan, interest rate, method and timing for repayment, and borrower’s promise to repay
feedback: A borrower who executes a promissory note is the maker or payer of the note. The lender is the payee. To be properly executed, all parties who have an interest in the property should sign the note. The note sets forth: the loan amount, the term of the loan, the method and timing of repayment, the interest rate to be paid, the borrower's promise to pay

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: When applying for a loan, a borrower will need to provide documentation that might include which of the following?
option_a: A credit report and verification of income
option_c: The employment history for the past ten years
option_d: The home addresses for the past 12 years
feedback: The process of initiating a mortgage loan begins when a borrower completes a loan application and submits it to a lender for evaluation by the lender's underwriters. The lender will require supporting documents as part of the application process which will include a credit report and verification of income

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The financial components of a mortgage loan include
option_b: principal, interest & interest rate, points, term, and payments
option_c: term, amount of loan, and PMI
feedback: The financial components of a mortgage loan include the principal, interest and interest rate, term and payments

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Private mortgage insurance (PMI) may be required by the lender on loans that have a down payment of
feedback: A lender may seek further protection against risk by requiring the borrower to obtain private mortgage insurance. This is frequently the case with loans requiring a relatively small down payment, leading to a high loan-to-value ratio

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The process of securing a loan by pledging a property without giving up ownership of the property is called
feedback: The process of securing a loan by pledging a property without giving up ownership of the property is called hypothecation

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A state in which the laws consider the mortgage to be a lien against the property and where the lender receives equitable title rather than legal title to the property is known as
feedback: A state whose laws give a lender on a mortgaged property equitable title rather than legal title. The mortgagor in a lien theory state retains legal title

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A state in which the laws give the legal title of a mortgaged property to the lender until the borrower satisfies the terms and obligations of the loan is known as
feedback: A state whose laws give legal title of a mortgaged property to the mortgagee until the mortgagor satisfies the terms and obligations of the loan

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower who executes a mortgage is known as the
feedback: A mortgage is a legal document stating the pledge of the borrower (the mortgagor) to the lender (the mortgagee).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower who executes a promissory note is known as the
feedback: A borrower who executes a promissory note is the maker or payer of the note. The lender is the payee

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In states where a trust deed is used instead of a mortgage, the borrower is referred to as the
feedback: In the case of a deed of trust, the lender as beneficiary requests the trustee to execute a release deed or deed of reconveyance to the borrower as trustor

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a borrower pays more than the scheduled payment amount on a loan, the excess amount paid is
option_a: added to the escrow account for payment of taxes
option_b: credited to the principal balance of the loan
option_c: added to the amount of the loan principal
option_d: returned to the borrower within five business days
feedback: If a borrower pays more than the scheduled payment amount, the excess amount paid is credited to the principal balance of the loan

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If the actual title to the property is not given to the borrower until the loan is paid in full
option_a: the property is located in a lien theory state
option_b: the property is located in a title theory state
option_c: the borrower has not made all payments as agreed
option_d: the mortgage was not recorded as it should have been
feedback: States differ in their interpretation of who owns mortgaged property. Those that regard the mortgage as a lien held by the mortgagee (lender) against the property owned by the mortgagor (borrower) are called lien-theory states. Those that regard the mortgage document as a conveyance of ownership from the mortgagor to the mortgagee are called title-theory states

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is information that a lender will seek from borrowers to determine their ability to repay the loan?
feedback: The lender must rely on eight types of information to determine that the borrower has the ability to repay the loan: current income or assets (excluding the value of the mortgaged property), current employment status, credit history, monthly payment for the mortgage, monthly payments being made on other loans on the same property, monthly payments for other mortgage-related expenses, other debts, monthly debt payments compared to monthly income (debt-to-income ratio)

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Before issuing a loan commitment, the lender must qualify the potential borrower by requesting certain documentation and submitting it to the appropriate parties for review.  These parties are known as the
feedback: When a lender's underwriters have qualified an applicant and the lender has decided to offer the loan, the lender gives the applicant a written notice of the agreement to lend under specific terms

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Once the loan has been approved, the borrower will receive notice by way of a written promise called the loan
feedback: The lender gives the applicant a written notice of the agreement to lend under specific terms. This written promise is the loan commitment. The commitment may take a number of common forms, including a firm commitment, a lock-in commitment, a conditional commitment, and a take-out commitment

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following takes place prior to closing?
option_a: The lender deposits the funded amount with the closing or escrow agent
option_b: The lender authorizes the real estate broker to close the transaction
option_c: The borrower pre-pays all real estate taxes up to and including the time of closing
option_d: The real estate broker transfers the title to the mortgaged property to the buyer
feedback: At or before the real estate closing, the lender deposits the borrower’s loan proceeds with an escrow agent, along with instructions for disbursing the funds

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Under the terms of Regulation Z, which of the following statements is true?
option_a: The lender must disclose all finance charges as well as the APR in advance of closing
option_b: Willful violation of Regulation Z may be punishable by imprisonment of up to ten years
option_c: Financial cost disclosures do not need to be presented in writing
option_d: The borrower has a right to cancel within seven days of the completion of the transaction
feedback: Under Regulation Z, a lender must disclose all finance charges as well as the true annualized percentage rate (APR) in advance of closing. A lender does not have to show the total interest payable over the loan term or include in finance charges such settlement costs as fees for appraisal, title, credit report, survey, or legal work. Disclosure must be distinctly presented in writing

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) is a federal law that is designed to
option_a: account for monies disbursed at the closing of the transaction
option_b: give direction to the closing agent regarding the disbursement of funds
option_c: standardize settlement practices and ensure that borrowers understand settlement costs
option_d: give direction to the borrowers regarding the funds needed to close
feedback: The Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) is a federal law that aims to standardize settlement practices and ensure that buyers understand settlement costs. RESPA applies to purchases of residential real estate (one- to four-family homes) to be financed by "federally related" first mortgage loans

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: All deeds transferring the property from the seller to the buyer must include the
feedback: The full legal description of the property to be transferred must be included on the deed of transfer

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following would NOT be a part of the secondary mortgage market?
feedback: Secondary mortgage market organizations include: Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA, or Fannie Mae), Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (FHLMC, or Freddie Mac), Government National Mortgage Association (GNMA, or Ginnie Mae), investment firms that assemble loans into packages and sell securities based on the pooled mortgages, life insurance companies, pension funds, primary market institutions who also invest as secondary lenders

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A lender‘s closing requirements can include all of the following EXCEPT
option_d: a copy of the listing agreement
feedback: The lenders closing requirements can include the following:  survey, inspections, hazard insurance, title insurance, certificate of occupancy, reserves for taxes and insurance, private mortgage insurance

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The real estate agent‘s role during the time between the contract signing and the closing usually involves helping with which of the following pre-closing activities?
option_a: Arranging for inspections, surveys, appraisals, and repairs
option_b: Preparing the deed to transfer ownership of the real estate
option_c: Arranging and paying for the movers to make the seller's transition easier
option_d: Being available to represent the buyer or seller at the closing if there is no attorney
feedback: The real estate agent is not allowed to practice law, nor are they expected to be responsible for ordering services on behalf of someone else without their permission. Therefore, common duties fulfilled by the agent include arranging for inspections, surveys, appraisals, and repairs

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How is the pay-off amount to the seller’s lender shown on the closing statement?
option_c: Not included in the official closing statement
option_d: Prepaid by the seller before closing
feedback: When the seller has a mortgage, it must be paid off from the closing proceeds.  The pay-off amount is therefore shown as a debit on the seller side of the closing statement since those are funds due by the seller

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following organizations is part of the secondary mortgage market?
feedback: Secondary mortgage market organizations include: Federal National Mortgage Association (FNMA, or Fannie Mae), Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (FHLMC, or Freddie Mac), Government National Mortgage Association (GNMA, or Ginnie Mae), investment firms that assemble loans into packages and sell securities based on the pooled mortgages, life insurance companies, pension funds, primary market institutions who also invest as secondary lenders

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following statements is true when describing a Federal Housing Administration (FHA) mortgage?
option_a: There are no specific regulations regarding appraisals
option_b: If borrower defaults, FHA reimburses the lender for losses due to default by the borrower
option_d: There is no maximum length of the repayment period
feedback: The Federal Housing Administration (FHA) does not lend money but insures permanent long-term loans made by others. The FHA ensures that the lender will not suffer significant loss in the case of borrower default. To provide this security, FHA provides insurance and charges the borrower an insurance premium

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following statements is true when supply exceeds demand?
option_a: The real estate prices will remain stable
option_b: The real estate prices will begin to increase
feedback: The availability of certain properties interacts with the strength of the demand for those properties to establish prices. When supply exceeds demand, a condition of surplus exists, and real estate values decline

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is a requirement for eligibility for a VA loan?
option_a: A veteran must apply for a Certificate of Eligibility
option_b: The borrower must be on active duty
option_c: A down payment of 5% is required
feedback: A veteran must apply for a Certificate of Eligibility to find out how much the VA will guarantee in a particular situation

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A junior loan is defined as a loan that
option_a: has a loan amount less than the senior loan
option_b: is adjustable on an annual basis
option_c: is subordinate to a senior or first mortgage loan
option_d: was initiated subsequent to the primary mortgage
feedback: When there are multiple loans on a single property, there is an order of priority in the liens which the mortgages create. The first, or senior, loan generally has priority over any subsequent loans. Second loans are riskier than first loans because the senior lender will be satisfied first in case of default

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A negative amortization loan
option_a: results in the borrower owing less at the end of the loan term than with a conventional loan.
option_b: gives the borrower no flexibility in repaying the loan
option_c: results in the borrower potentially owing more at the end of the loan term
option_d: requires that the lender lessen the amount of the amortization on an annual basis
feedback: Negative amortization is a situation in which the loan balance of an amortizing loan increases because periodic payments are insufficient to pay all interest owed for the period. Unpaid interest is added to the principal balance

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Comparable has a feature not in subject property → value of that feature is:
feedback: Subtract from comparables to equalize with subject property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is known as a special purpose loan?
feedback: The following are special-purpose loans: home equity, package, construction, bridge, equity participation, take-out, reverse annuity, and blanket

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If the real estate taxes are $5,800 per year and the insurance is $200 per year, what is the amount that will be escrowed each month?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: First-time homebuyers want to purchase a home for $200,000.  They have $20,000 for a down payment. What is the principal amount of the loan?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: First-time homebuyers want to purchase a home for $200,000.  They have $20,000 for a down payment. What is the percentage of loan to value (LTV)?
feedback: $180,000 divided by $200,000=.9 which is 90%

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a ____________ mortgage, a homeowner pledges the equity in the home as security for a loan which is paid out in regular monthly amounts over the term of the loan
feedback: In a reverse annuity mortgage, a homeowner pledges the equity in the home as security for a loan which is paid out in regular monthly amounts over the term of the loan. The homeowner, in effect, is able to convert the equity to cash without losing ownership and possession

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a ____________ loan arrangement, the seller receives a junior mortgage from the buyer and uses the buyer's payments to make the payments on the original first mortgage
feedback: In a wraparound loan arrangement, the seller receives a junior mortgage from the buyer and uses the buyer's payments to make the payments on the original first mortgage

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The mortgage loan documents stipulate that which of the following conditions must be met during the term of the loan?
option_a: The borrower must keep the property insured as required by the lender
option_b: The borrower must maintain the property’s landscaping
option_c: The borrower must maintain the property in excellent operating condition
option_d: The borrower must seek the lender’s approval if there is a desire to sell the property
feedback: The borrower must keep the property insured as the lender requires. Insurance proceeds, in case of a claim, are applied first to restoring the property, or, if that is not feasible, to payment of the debt

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The minimum down payment required to obtain an FHA loan is
feedback: The minimum down payment for an FHA-backed loan is based on the lower of the appraised value or the sales price. The present requirement for single-family residential loans is 3.5%

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The minimum down payment required on a VA loan, if the borrower qualifies, is
feedback: The VA usually requires no down payment, although the lender may require one

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A Certificate of Eligibility is required to qualify for
feedback: A veteran must apply for a Certificate of Eligibility to find out how much the VA will guarantee in a particular situation

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The clause in the mortgage that could allow the lender to require the borrower to repay the loan before the scheduled date is called
feedback: The acceleration clause grants the lender the right to call all sums immediately due and payable on a loan should the borrower violate certain provisions of the loan agreement

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The underwriting process includes which of the following?
option_a: Appraising the value of personal property
option_b: Determining the loan terms based on the location of the property
option_c: Reviewing the borrower’s history of payment of loans or rent on previous properties
feedback: A lender assesses risks by examining, or qualifying both borrower and property. In qualifying a borrower, an underwriter weighs the ability of the borrower to repay the loan. This requires an analysis of whether the borrower's income, cash resources, creditworthiness, net worth, and employment stability meet the lender's standards

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Under the terms of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, a lender may NOT do which of the following?
option_a: Ask applicants any question about their age, sex, religion, race, or national origin except as the law may require
option_b: Require that borrowers disclose all income, debt, cash, and net worth
option_c: Evaluate a loan applicant based on the applicant’s income and credit rating
option_d: Require that a borrower demonstrate creditworthiness
feedback: The ECOA has prohibited a number of practices in mortgage loan underwriting. Including asking applicants any question about their age, sex, religion, race or national origin, except as the law may require

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is a ratio used by lenders to determine whether or not the borrower has adequate means to make the periodic payments?
feedback: The debt ratio considers all of the monthly obligations of the income ratio plus any additional monthly payments the applicant must make for other debts. The lender will look specifically at minimum monthly payments due on revolving credit debts and other consumer loans

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following statements is true under the general terms of a contract for deed?
option_a: The seller retains the title, and the buyer receives possession and equitable title while making payments
option_b: The buyer will receive the title to the property at the closing of the transaction
option_c: The buyer will receive the title to the property and possession of the property at the closing of the transaction
option_d: The seller will retain possession of the property until 50% of the loan amount is paid
feedback: Under a contract for deed arrangement, the seller retains title and the buyer receives possession and equitable title while making payments under the terms of the contract. The seller conveys title when the contract has been fully performed

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Julio just purchased a house for $650,000 with a $600,000 mortgage loan and $50,000 cash downpayment.  Which of the following will Julio's lender most likely require Julio purchase?
feedback: If a loan's LTV ratio is too high, a lender may require that the borrower purchase private mortgage insurance, commonly called PMI. This insurance secures whatever portion of the loan amount takes the LTV ratio above the typical ratio limit – which is usually 80% of the property's value. This protects the lender from the borrower's default – which can become more likely if the borrower has very little equity in the property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is a pre-calculated, unchanging number that relates the principal and interest payment amount of a loan to the amount of the loan at a given interest rate and term?
feedback: With the loan constant in hand, one can easily derive the amount of a loan from its principal and interest payment and the amount of the principal and interest payment from the loan amount.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is the secondary loan market for American agricultural businesses?
feedback: The Federal Agricultural Mortgage Corporation, or Farmer Mac. Farmer Mac (FAMC) is a federal agency whose central function is to increase the availability and affordability of credit for American agriculture and American rural communities.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage financing is the practice of
option_a: buying and selling mortgages as an investment vehicle
option_b: lending money to real estate investors to finance the purchase of mortgages
option_c: using borrowed funds secured by a mortgage or trust deed to purchase real estate
option_d: obtaining equitable title to real estate by buying promissory notes
feedback: When a borrower gives a note promising to repay the borrowed money and executes a mortgage on the real estate for which the money is being borrowed as security, the financing method is called mortgage financing. The term "mortgage financing" also applies to real estate loans secured by a deed of trust

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is a lien-theory state?
option_a: A state in which a lienor holds legal title to a secured property
option_b: A state in which a mortgage is considered to be a lien against a secured property
option_c: A state that allows a real estate owner's creditors to record liens against the owner's property
option_d: A state in which a lien is considered as a conveyance
feedback: States differ in their interpretation of who owns mortgaged property. Those that regard the mortgage as a lien held by the mortgagee (lender) against the property owned by the mortgagor (borrower) are called lien-theory states. Those that regard the mortgage document as a conveyance of ownership from the mortgagor to the mortgagee are called title-theory states. Some states interpret ownership of mortgaged property from a point of view that combines aspects of both title and lien theory

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is the function of a note in a mortgage or trust deed financing arrangement?
option_a: It is evidence of the lender's interest in the collateral property
option_b: It is evidence of ownership of the mortgage or trust deed
option_c: It contains the borrower's promise to maintain the value of the property given as collateral for a loan
option_d: It is evidence of the borrower's debt to the lender
feedback: A valid mortgage or trust deed financing arrangement requires a note as evidence of the debt

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The document that provides evidence that a certain property is pledged as collateral for a loan is the
feedback: A mortgage is a legal document stating the pledge of the borrower (the mortgagor) to the lender (the mortgagee). The mortgage document pledges the borrower's ownership interest in the real estate in question as collateral against performance of the debt obligation

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The borrower in a mortgage loan transaction is known as the
feedback: The mortgagor is the borrower and the mortgagee is the lender. As a memory aid, notice that "lender" and "mortgagee" both have two "e"s. "Mortgagor" and "borrower" both have two "o"s

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a borrower obtains an interest-only loan of $750,000 at an annual interest rate of 8%, what is the monthly interest payment?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a borrower's monthly interest payment on an interest-only loan at an annual interest rate of 9% is $3,750, how much was the loan amount?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower of a $950,000 interest-only loan makes annual interest payments of $83,125.  What interest rate is the borrower paying?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: How much is a discount point?
option_d: It depends on the interest rate and the loan amount
feedback: A discount point is one percent of the loan amount. Thus, one point on a $100,000 loan equals $1,000

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Within months of buying their home, the Jackson family learned that the roof leaked during heavy rains. When the house was listed with the Bankers Agency, the seller had told the Bankers broker that the roof leaked, but Bankers claims the Jacksons did not ask about the roof. What actions can be taken in this situation?
option_a: The buyer cannot do anything because this fact should have been discovered during home inspection
option_b: The buyer can press criminal charges against their agent
option_c: The buyer can sue the broker for nondisclosure
option_d: The buyer cannot sue the broker under the license law
feedback: Disclosure is required by license law, thus the Jacksons can sue the broker for nondisclosure. The broker is required by law to disclose any fact that would materially affect the buyer's decision to make an offer, such as a leaky roof.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following is true of an amortizing loan?
option_a: The amount of annual interest paid is the same for every year of the loan term
option_b: Part of each periodic payment is applied to repayment of the loan balance in advance and part is applied to payment of interest in arrears
option_c: Except for any points that may be paid, the interest on the loan balance is usually paid in advance
option_d: The interest rate is reduced each year to maintain equal payments even though the outstanding loan balance is smaller
feedback: In an amortizing loan, part of the principal is repaid periodically along with interest, so that the principal balance decreases over the life of the loan. The annual interest is never the same, since the principal balance to which the interest rate applies changes every year. Interest on a loan is always paid in arrears, not in advance

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: For a loan that is not backed by the Federal Housing Administration or Veterans Administration, and for which the borrower is making a down payment of less than 20%, the lender is likely require the borrower to obtain
feedback: The lender may require the borrower to obtain private mortgage insurance, or PMI. Mortgage insurance protects the lender against loss of a portion of the loan (typically 20-25%) in case of borrower default. Private mortgage insurance generally applies to loans that are not backed by the Federal Housing Administration (FHA) or Veterans Administration (VA) and that have a down payment of less than 20% of the property value

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of these advertisements for a loan would trigger a TILA disclosure?
feedback: "No down payment required" triggers the Truth in Lending disclosures because it is a specific statement about only one aspect of the financing. "Easy terms" does not trigger the regulation because it's non-specific.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: To whom must an agency disclosure be given when a broker represents a seller of property?
option_a: The purchaser, before the purchase contract is signed
option_b: The seller, at their first face to face meeting
option_c: The seller, at their first face to face meeting
option_d: The purchaser, at their first face-to-face meeting
feedback: The agency disclosure must be made at the beginning of the first personal meeting with the purchaser so that the buyer is aware of the agency representation options.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Creating improved real property from raw land involves
option_a: financing, construction contracting, and regulatory approvals
option_b: property managers, rental agreements, and asset managers
option_c: property portfolios, architects, and zoning regulations
option_d: real estate licensees, regulators, and state laws
feedback: The creation and improvement of properties are functions performed by real estate professionals. However, developing real property from raw land involves capital formation, financing, construction contracting, and regulatory approvals.  The key parties involved in this aspect of the business are generally the developer, the landowner, the mortgage lender and the leasing agent(s)

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The experts who manage the legal aspects of a development project are the
feedback: The legal aspects of the development project include real estate attorneys, title companies, surveyors, property insurance companies, and government regulatory officials. Real estate licensees should avoid handling any legal aspect of a real estate transaction and should refer such matters to the legal experts

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following are generally considered to be the key parties involved in the improvement of land?
option_c: Market analysts, real estate licensees, and buyers
feedback: The key parties involved in this aspect of the business are generally the developer, the landowner, and the mortgage lender. Also involved are market analysts, architects, engineers, space planners, interior designers, and construction subcontractors

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: State license laws are administered in each state by
feedback: State license laws are administered in each state by a Real Estate Commission or other similarly named government agency. The Commission is charged with administering and enforcing license laws. In addition, the Commission may pass regulations that further refine or clarify state statutes

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The real estate industry is regulated by which of the following?
feedback: The real estate industry is regulated by every level of government. Federal and state statutes, as well as a large body of court decisions, generally referred to as common law, determine how real estate can be developed, managed, and transferred

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Special purpose properties may include
feedback: Special purpose properties include publicly or privately-owned recreational facilities, government buildings, churches, and schools

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The principal types of transactions are in real estate brokerage are sales, leases and subleases, exchanges, and
feedback: The principal types of transactions are sales, leases and subleases, exchanges, and options

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The primary body of laws and regulations governing real estate brokerage practice is
feedback: State real estate license laws comprise the primary body of laws and regulations governing real estate brokerage practice. License laws in each state specify who must obtain a license to practice real estate and set the requirements for obtaining and maintaining the license

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Differences between comparables & subject are handled how?
feedback: Always adjust comparables to reflect the subject property.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: What is a loan-to-value ratio?
option_a: The percentage of a lender's portfolio that is composed of mortgage loans
option_b: The ratio of borrowed principal plus total interest to the appraised value of the collateral property
option_c: The ratio of a lender's return on a mortgage loan to the value of the collateral property
option_d: The fraction of the appraised value of the property offered as collateral which the lender is willing to lend
feedback: The relationship of the loan amount to the property value, expressed as a percentage, is called the loan-to-value ratio, or LTV. If the lender's loan to value ratio is 80%, the lender will lend only $80,000 on a home appraised at $100,000. The difference between what the lender will lend and what the borrower must pay for the property is the amount the borrower must provide in cash as a down payment

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following professional activities is a real estate broker or agent most likely to be directly involved in?
option_a: Holding real estate as an investment
feedback: Rights and interests in real estate can be bought, sold, assigned, leased, exchanged, inherited, or otherwise transferred from one owner to another. Real estate brokers and the brokers' salespeople are generally centrally involved in such transfers

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The distinguishing feature of commercial property is that
option_a: it is not occupied by an owner-user
option_b: it has the potential to generate income from its use by a business
option_c: it is held by its owners primarily for its investment value
feedback: Commercial property generally refers to retail and office properties, but may also include industrial real estate. The term "commercial" relates to the fact that the property can potentially generate income from a business's usage

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: An important reason for a real estate salesperson or broker in a large market to specialize in a geographical area is that
option_a: large market areas have usually been divided up geographically by competing brokers
option_b: customers generally identify brokers with the area immediately around their offices
option_c: geographical specialization is the only legal way to organize business
option_d: it is difficult to maintain adequate market knowledge on a large area
feedback: Brokers and agents must maintain current, accurate data on properties. It is not possible to keep track of every property in larger markets. Therefore, one must create an area of geographical specialization. One's area may be defined by natural barriers; by streets and highways; or by a certain set of subdivisions

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A real estate professional who oversees the day-to-day operation of a specific property on behalf of the owner is a(n)
feedback: Property managers and their staff oversee specific properties on behalf of the owners, making sure the condition of the property and its financial performance meet specific standards

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is a primary real estate brokerage activity?
option_a: Obtaining a zoning variance for a home owner
option_b: Appealing an assessed valuation for a client
option_c: Assisting a developer to subdivide a property
option_d: Locating a landlord for a tenant
feedback: Primary real estate brokerage activities involve performance of one or more of the following tasks:  •locating a buyer for a seller  • locating a seller for a buyer  •locating a tenant for a landlord  •locating a landlord for a tenant

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The real estate industry is regulated by federal, state and local laws, as well as by
option_a: its own professional codes of ethics
feedback: In addition to federal, state, and local laws and regulations, the real estate industry is, to a degree, self-regulated by the codes of ethical conduct propounded by the industry's many trade organizations

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The agency that administers and enforces license laws in each state is the
option_c: real estate commission, council, or similar board
option_d: U.S. Circuit Court or U.S. Court of Appeals
feedback: State license laws are administered in each state by a real estate commission. The commission is charged with administering and enforcing license laws. In addition, the commission may pass regulations that further refine or clarify state statutes

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: In addition to specifying who must obtain a real estate license and requirements for obtaining one, license laws also define
option_a: how licensees must handle escrow deposits, funds, and fees
option_b: the minimum commission split a broker can give a salesperson
option_c: how many salespeople in a broker's office can have active licenses at a given time
option_d: how many brokers may cooperate in a single transaction
feedback: License laws in each state specify who must obtain a license to practice real estate and set the requirements for obtaining and maintaining the license. License laws also define critical aspects of real estate brokerage, including:  •procedures for handling escrow deposits and fees. •procedures for advertising  •guidelines for dealing with clients and customers

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: An investment property is
option_a: any property that yields a profit to the owner when it is sold
option_b: any property that is used for a purpose other than habitation
option_c: any property that is held by its owners for investment purposes
option_d: any property that produces a positive cash flow for its owner
feedback: Investment property refers to any property that is held by its owners for investment purposes

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A residential property is one that
option_a: is occupied by no more than five families
option_c: is located in a residential zone
feedback: Residential property refers to property that is owned and used for habitation

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Property management and real estate asset management are both real estate management professions. The primary distinction between the two is that
option_a: property managers always report to an asset manager
option_b: asset managers have greater knowledge of a property's finances
option_c: property managers handle day-to-day operations while asset managers manage portfolios of properties
option_d: asset managers are primarily responsible for maintenance technicians
feedback: Asset managers oversee groups of properties, or portfolios. Their role is to achieve the investment objectives of the owners as opposed to managing day-to-day operations

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The difference between what a borrower has to pay to purchase a property and the amount a lender will lend on the property is the
feedback: Price less loan is the down payment. This is also the buyer's initial equity

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: People in the real estate business who primarily focus on creating new properties are
feedback: Developers are primarily focused on creating new properties

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The term "commercial property" generally refers to
feedback: Commercial property generally refers to retail and office properties, but may also include industrial real estate

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following professionals involved in the real estate business are most concerned about procuring buyers and sellers for clients?
feedback: The brokerage company, in its simplest form, consists of a broker and the broker's agents, who together work to locate buyers, sellers, tenants and landlords for the broker's clients

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following ways of specializing is common in the real estate brokerage business?
feedback: In the modern brokerage environment, brokers and agents specialize along the following lines:  • property type  • geographical area  •type of transaction  •type of client  •type of relationship

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What is an advisory service provider?
option_a: A broker of fee simple titles
option_c: A broker performing non-transactional services for a commission
option_d: A broker who renders real estate services for a fee
feedback: In the advisory relationship, the broker works on identified real estate tasks or projects in exchange for a fee, salary, or retainer

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The level of government which is most active in regulating real estate licensees is the
option_d: municipal government where the person resides
feedback: State real estate license laws comprise the primary body of laws and regulations governing real estate brokerage practice

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Under the SC Fair Housing Act of 1968 an aggrieved person may file a complaint directly with what agency?
feedback: Under the SC Fair Housing Act of 1968, complaints are filed with the South Carolina Human Affairs Commission, which enforces state-level fair housing laws.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Mortgage bankers typically:
option_a: Originate and sell loans to investors
feedback: Mortgage bankers originate many loans and sell them at a discount to investors in the secondary market.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: 25-year-old building in condition of 11-year-old → this is:
feedback: Effective age = age based on condition, not actual years.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits a lender from
option_a: refusing a loan because the property is located in a certain area
option_b: including income from self-employment in the borrower's qualifying income
option_c: requiring both spouses to sign the loan application form
option_d: refusing a loan because a borrower has a defective credit report
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) requires a lender to evaluate a loan applicant on the basis of that applicant's own income and credit rating, unless the applicant requests the inclusion of another's income and credit rating in the application. In addition, ECOA has prohibited a number of practices in mortgage loan underwriting, including refusing a loan based on an applicant’s demographic characteristics

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A loan applicant has an annual gross income of $36,000. How much will a lender allow the applicant to pay for monthly housing expense to qualify for a loan if the lender uses an income ratio of 28%?

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a lender discovers that an applicant for a mortgage loan has borrowed the down payment from a relative and has to repay that loan, the lender is likely to
option_b: adjust the applicant's debt ratio calculation and lower the loan amount
option_c: increase the loan amount to enable the borrower to pay off the loan to the relative
option_d: require the borrower to make payments to an escrow account for repayment of the relative's loan
feedback: If someone such as a relative is lending an applicant a portion of the down payment with a provision for repayment, a lender will consider this another debt obligation and adjust the debt ratio accordingly. This can lower the amount a lender is willing to lend

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The Federal Reserve's Regulation Z applies to which loans?
option_b: All loans secured by real estate
option_c: All loans secured by a residence
feedback: Regulation Z applies to all loans secured by a residence. It does not apply to commercial loans or to agricultural loans over $25,000. Its provisions cover the disclosure of costs, the right to rescind the credit transaction, advertising credit offers, and penalties for non-compliance with the act

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: You are asked to show a property, which you know to be in a high crime area. What should you do?
option_a: Refuse while giving statistics on the crime rate.
option_b: Offer to show a property in a safer atmosphere.
option_d: Show that property and the surrounding areas.
feedback: You must show the property as requested; refusing could be discriminatory. You can provide crime statistics only if asked and from objective sources.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When could an associate legally act as a broker-in-charge?
option_a: When a broker-in-charge leaves the company, and the Real Estate Commission allows them to for up to 6 months.
option_b: When the BIC dies, and the Commission appoints them for up to 6 months.
option_c: Within 90 days after passing the brokers exam.
option_d: When they are the owner of the office, and the broker-in-charge dies.
feedback: If the broker-in-charge dies, the Commission may appoint an associate to act as BIC for up to 6 months.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following cannot be depreciated for tax purposes?
feedback: Land is not subject to wear and tear, so it cannot be depreciated.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: You, the listing agent, receive an offer on a home you have listed which refers to the sewer connection. You know that the property has a septic tank. How should you proceed?
option_a: Refuse the offer based on the inaccurate information.
option_b: Recommend that the seller make this change in their counteroffer, initial, date it and send it back.
option_c: Give the offer to the seller and say nothing.
option_d: Give the offer to the seller who accepts the offer and says sewers are a matter of record and the other broker should have known.
feedback: Any inaccuracies in an offer should be corrected before presenting it, typically in a counteroffer that is initialed and dated.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When a property is sold with a recorded easement on that property, what happens to the easement?
option_a: Terminates since constructive notice is given
feedback: Easements generally “run with the land” and transfer to the new owner (the grantee).

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Under Title VIII of the Fair Housing Act, family is best defined as:
option_c: Person or persons with children under 18 or the expectation of same
feedback: Family under Title VIII includes any person(s) with children under 18 or expecting a child.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: It is important to distinguish between real property and personal property. Which of the following would not be real property?
option_a: Air space over the city airport
option_b: A decorative bush planted in the front yard
feedback: “Chattel real” refers to personal property interests in real estate, so it is not considered real property.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What is true of a voidable contract?
option_c: Automatically void to the injured party
option_d: Valid until action is taken to rescind
feedback: A voidable contract is valid until one party takes legal action to rescind it.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The landlord refused to keep the property in habitable condition and the tenant terminated the lease and moved. What is this action called?
feedback: Constructive eviction occurs when a landlord fails to maintain habitable conditions, forcing the tenant to leave.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What type of lien would be filed by someone who does work or furnishes supplies to a project and does not get paid?
feedback: A mechanic’s lien is filed by someone who supplied labor or materials to a property and was not paid.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When the brokerage is employed, they become the agent for whom?
feedback: In real estate agency, the brokerage represents the client (could be buyer or seller, depending on the agreement).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, a lender, or a real estate agent who assists a seller in qualifying a potential buyer, may not
option_a: tell a rejected loan applicant the reasons for the rejection
option_b: ask the buyer/borrower about his/her religion or national origin
option_c: ask the buyer/borrower to explain gaps in his/her employment history
option_d: use a credit report if the loan applicant disputes any information in the report
feedback: ECOA prohibits discrimination in extending credit based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or dependency upon public assistance. A creditor may not make any statements to discourage an applicant on the basis of such discrimination or ask any questions of an applicant concerning these discriminatory items. A real estate licensee who assists a seller in qualifying a potential buyer may fall within the reach of this prohibition

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A conventional mortgage loan is one that is
option_a: backed by the Federal National Mortgage Association
option_b: insured under Section 203(b) of the Federal Housing Administration loan program
option_c: guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association
feedback: A conventional mortgage loan is a permanent long-term loan that is not FHA-insured or VA-guaranteed. FNMA does not "back" loans; FHA only insures FHA loans; and the VA, not GNMA guarantees loans

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The assumability of an FHA-insured loan is
option_b: limited by when the loan was originated and by the type of property
option_d: prohibited on all existing loans under current regulations
feedback: Rules for assumability vary according to when the FHA-insured loan was originated and whether the original loan was for an investment property or an owner-occupied principal residence

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A broker created an unintended agency relationship by way of her conduct towards a buyer. This is known as
feedback: An agency relationship that develops from the actions, conduct, and representations of either the agent or principal

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The buyer normally has the right to select the attorney to close the property. This right is assured by what law?
feedback: In SC, the buyer’s right to choose the closing attorney is protected under the South Carolina Consumer Protection Code.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Broker Rob buys an option from seller Johnson that is good for 6 months. Concerning this option, Broker Rob is the:
feedback: The optionee is the person who holds the right to buy under an option contract.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would not be a purpose for which property would normally be condemned under the power of eminent domain?
feedback: Subdivision development is not a public use and would not justify condemnation under eminent domain.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A land use which was in existence prior to the current zoning ordinance, which is now in violation of the current zoning ordinance, is best described as:
feedback: A non-conforming use is a land use established before a new zoning law that no longer conforms to current zoning.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The interests in land, comprised of various combinations of legal rights, powers, privileges, and immunities, are best described as follows:
feedback: The “bundle of legal rights” refers to the collection of rights associated with real estate ownership.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The value of the property is less than the cost. This is an example of what economic principle?
feedback: If the value is less than the cost to replace, this illustrates functional obsolescence.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The right-of-way that has no dominant tenement involved is best known as what type of easement?
feedback: An easement in gross benefits a person or entity, not another parcel of land, so there is no dominant tenement.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What is the power a municipality has that allows it to regulate the use of property within a designated area by enacting zoning ordinances?
feedback: Police power allows municipalities to regulate property use via zoning and other ordinances.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Real property owned by public corporations, cities, counties, and school districts, is generally owned:
option_b: As tenants in common for the benefit of the citizens
option_c: As tenancy in partnership between elected officials and all citizens
option_d: As joint tenants for the benefit of all citizens
feedback: Public entities usually hold title in severalty (a single legal entity owning the property).

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following individuals would not need a license?
option_c: Broker-in-charge who manages a real estate office but makes no sales
feedback: Court-appointed receivers in bankruptcy are exempt from needing a real estate license.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When does title to real estate pass?
feedback: Title passes when the deed is delivered and accepted by the grantee.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When the broker-in-charge accepts the listing from the seller, what action must the broker-in-charge take?
option_c: Represent the buyer if he helps the buyer write the offer.
option_d: Disregard the interest of the buyer since the seller is being represented.
feedback: A broker-in-charge must treat all parties fairly, even while representing the seller.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: All of the following are essential to a valid contract except:
feedback: Earnest money is not required for a valid contract; essential elements are legal purpose, consideration, and offer/acceptance.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The actual amount of money a property sells for is known by what appraisal term?
feedback: Market price is the actual sale price; market value is an estimate of worth.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A VA certificate of eligibility determines
option_a: whether an individual is a veteran
option_b: the maximum loan amount an approved lender can give to veterans
option_c: how much of a loan the VA will guarantee
option_d: whether a lender is approved to issue VA-guaranteed loans
feedback: A veteran must apply for a Certificate of Eligibility to find out how much the VA will guarantee in a particular situation

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Bob has his home listed; however, he sells the home without the aid of the brokerage. He finds that he is still legally obligated to pay the listing brokerage a commission. What type of listing did Bob sign?
feedback: An exclusive right-to-sell listing entitles the broker to commission no matter who sells the property.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would terminate a listing?:
feedback: Renunciation (withdrawal by the principal or agent) terminates a listing.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: If a condo owner defaults on real property taxes, the following would occur:
option_a: The condo complex has a lien filed against it.
option_b: All owners of condos in that complex are in jeopardy of the complex being sold on the courthouse steps.
option_c: Condominium ownership would be in jeopardy for one specific unit.
option_d: Condo owners do not have real property ownership since they only own stock.
feedback: In a condo, tax delinquency puts only the specific unit in jeopardy, not the entire complex.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The age of majority in South Carolina is:
feedback: The age of majority in South Carolina is 18.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The seller backs out of the transaction for no legal reason. What happens to the earnest money?
option_c: Split between the brokerage and buyer
option_d: Split between the brokerage and seller
feedback: If a seller defaults without legal cause, the earnest money is returned to the buyer.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: In what covenant of a general warranty deed does the grantor warrant that the title is free of defects except as noted?
feedback: The covenant “against encumbrances” warrants the title is free from liens or other encumbrances unless stated.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What are the four criteria for determining if an item is a fixture?
option_a: Intent, method of attachment, adaptation, and agreement of the parties
feedback: The four fixture tests are intent, method of attachment, adaptation, and agreement of the parties.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: If a deed restriction is more restrictive than a zoning ordinance, which of the following is true?
option_c: The one that was recorded first prevails.
feedback: If a deed restriction is more restrictive than zoning, the deed restriction prevails.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When Rob neglected to pay for the newly-built dock on his waterfront property, the Ace Construction Company attempted to file a mechanics lien on Rob's 40-foot sailboat. Was Ace Construction correct in this action?
option_a: Yes, because the existence of the dock benefits the boat, thereby making the boat legally attachable.
option_b: Yes, because a mechanics lien covers all property in Rob's possession.
option_c: No, because a mechanics lien is specific only to the property being improved.
option_d: No, because Rob's property borders a public lake and the lien must be levied by an attachment lien that includes water.
feedback: A mechanics lien can only be attached to the property improved, not unrelated personal property like a boat.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A, B, and C are joints tenants. If C sells his interest to D, which of the following is true?
option_a: A and B remain joint tenants.
option_b: D stands as a tenant in common with A and B.
option_c: Should D die, his heirs will stand as tenants in common with A and B who remain joint tenants.
feedback: All statements are true: C’s sale makes D a tenant in common with A and B, who remain joint tenants.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Water use is sometimes determined by allocating water to users who hold benefits use permits. This type of water use privilege comes under the doctrine of:
feedback: Prior appropriation allocates water rights based on beneficial use permits.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A deed is often recorded. What is the purpose of recording?
feedback: Recording provides constructive notice to the public of the deed’s existence.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What could legally prevent Sam from building a nightclub on his property?
option_d: Any or all of the above
feedback: Any of the listed legal restrictions (deed restrictions, zoning, local laws) could prevent certain property uses.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which loan offers the most flexibility?
feedback: VA loans generally offer the most flexibility in terms of down payment and qualification.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which choice best defines land?
option_a: The surface of the earth downward to the center and upward to infinity
option_b: Minerals below the earth and all improvements
feedback: Land legally includes the surface, subsurface, and air rights to infinity.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: It is important to be able to distinguish between real and personal property. All the following are real property except which one?
option_a: Air space over a city airport
option_b: A building attached to the land
option_c: A decorative bush planted in the front yard
option_d: Gas pumps used by a tenant in a business
feedback: Gas pumps are personal property (trade fixtures) owned by the tenant.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which choice best describes the four criteria for determining if an item is a fixture?
option_c: Intent, method, annexation of article, agreement of parties
option_d: Intent, method of attachment, adaptation, agreement of parties
feedback: Fixture tests include intent, method of attachment, adaptation, and agreement of the parties.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following items is classified as chattel real?
feedback: Chattel real is personal property related to real estate, like leases or mortgages.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What type of legal description would be most likely to reference a recorded plat?
feedback: The rectangular survey system uses townships, ranges, and sections — but for plats, lot and block are common.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The “limitation of liability” clause in a listing agreement generally protects the
feedback: The “limitation of liability” clause requires the owner to indemnify the broker against liability resulting from casualty, loss, and owner misrepresentation during the listing period

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: All the following are true concerning improvements except which one?
option_b: An improvement to a property would ordinarily increase its value.
option_c: An improvement to one property may not improve an adjoining property.
option_d: The improvement of one property would almost always increase the value of adjoining properties.
feedback: Not all improvements benefit neighbors; some may have no effect or even reduce value.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Rights, benefits, and improvements that run with the land are known as:
feedback: Appurtenances are rights and privileges tied to the property, passing with ownership.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following describes a purchase money mortgage financing arrangement?
option_a: A bank gives a buyer a senior mortgage loan that fully covers the cost of purchasing the property
