option_a: Lender Disclosure of Settlement Costs and Borrower’s Receipt of Documents
option_b: Lender Financial Statement and Borrower’s Consent to Closing
option_c: Estimate of Loan Proceeds and Disclosure of Closing Charges
feedback: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) requires lenders to use two specific forms to disclose settlement costs to the buyer. A lender must provide a Loan Estimate (H-24) within three days of receiving the loan application and allow the buyer to see the Closing Disclosure (H-25) three days before loan consummation. A lender must also provide a buyer with a copy of the information booklet, “Your Home Loan Toolkit,” concerning mortgage loan, closing costs and closing procedures

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, lenders are prohibited from doing which of the following?
option_a: Refusing credit due to the applicant's FICO score
option_b: Refusing credit due to the condition of the property
option_c: Refusing credit due to down payment requirements
option_d: Refusing credit due to the gender of an applicant
feedback: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC), the nation's consumer protection agency, enforces the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA), which prohibits credit discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you get public assistance. Lenders are still allowed to refuse loans to any person for financial reasons that would disqualify anyone from getting that same loan.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) was given authority to
option_a: supervise and enforce compliance with RESPA and its implementing regulations
option_b: enforce all policies that are put into place by the local banking community
option_c: monitor all real estate agencies and corporations to ensure compliance with RESPA
option_d: prepare the appropriate closing documents when a property is sold
feedback: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) is authorized to supervise and enforce compliance with RESPA and its implementing regulations

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A written summary of the history of all conveyances and legal proceedings affecting a specific parcel of real estate is called a(n):
feedback: An abstract of title is a written summary that traces every change of ownership and claim against a property (such as mortgages, liens, and easements). In some cases, the abstract goes back to the last change of title, and sometimes it traces back to the first recorded owner. It is part of the title report required by virtually all lenders.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The closing statement is
option_a: a compilation of credits and debits for the buyers and the sellers
option_b: a legal document that will be recorded in county records
option_c: a legal document that proves that a transfer of title took place
option_d: an estimate of required repairs to the property
feedback: The closing statement includes all aspects of the financial part of the property transfer.  All fees, costs, charges, sales/purchase price, prorations, and amounts paid outside of closing that relate to the property transfer will be included on the closing statement

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: When applying for a loan, the lender must deliver to the potential borrower a loan estimate that will provide the borrower with
option_a: an itemization of estimated settlement costs
option_b: the estimated amount of money that the borrower will need to purchase the property
option_c: the estimated amount of funds that the lender is willing to lend the borrower
option_d: the exact interest rate that the lender will charge
feedback: The loan estimate is the document required to be delivered to a potential borrower when the loan application is made.  Borrowers may speak with several lenders in order to assess costs and procedures so that an informed decision can be made by them

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An easement passes over an Smiths land to allow Jones access to an adjacent parcel owned by Jones. What term would apply to Jones land?
feedback: The dominant estate benefits from the easement.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The Closing Disclosure is a form that is provided by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) for use at the closing to
option_a: report cash transactions to the IRS
option_b: disclose a material defect in the property
option_c: disclose settlement costs to the buyer
option_d: disclose settlement costs to the seller
feedback: Under Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) rules, a lender must use the Closing Disclosure (H-25) to disclose settlement costs to the buyer. This form covers all costs that the buyer will have to pay at closing, whether to the lender or to other parties

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which type of lease requires the lessee to pay a flat rent?
feedback: A gross lease is a lease where the lessee pays a fixed rent and the lessor pays the taxes, insurance, and other charges regularly incurred through ownership.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: RESPA involves which of the following types of purchase and sale transactions?
option_c: Residential properties that are financed by a purchase money mortgage
option_d: Residential properties that are financed by a federally related mortgage loan
feedback: RESPA is a federal law enacted in 1974 and later revised. It applies to residential properties (4 or less units) financed by a federally related mortgage loan (e.g. FHA, VA, HUD) or intended to be sold to Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac.  It applies to first mortgages only

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Business relationships and affiliations among real estate firms, mortgage brokers, and title companies involved in a transaction are closely scrutinized since RESPA prohibits:
option_a: the buyer’s agent from recommending a home inspector
option_b: the seller’s agent from recommending a title company
option_c: the buyer or seller agents from suggesting an attorney
option_d: the payment of referral fees by a lender to a real estate agent
feedback: RESPA prohibits kickbacks, fees, gifts, and other unearned items unless the payment of such is for work actually performed.  An example would be a payment by a lender to a real estate agent for referring a client to that lender

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The payment of real estate taxes that are owed but not yet due is expressed on a closing statement as a
option_a: credit to buyer and debit to seller
option_b: credit to seller and debit to buyer
feedback: Assuming the seller “owns” the day of closing, the seller owes real estate taxes up to and including the day of closing.  If the taxes have not yet been billed, the seller must give a credit to the buyer at closing so that the buyer will have the funds to pay those taxes on behalf of the seller when the bill is issued.  Therefore, the seller gives a credit to the buyer in the amount of taxes due by the seller.  A debit in the same amount will be shown on the seller side of the closing statement

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The document that will indicate the procedures for closing, and identify responsibility for certain closing costs is known as the
feedback: The sales contract is the document that sets forth the agreement between the buyers and sellers and indicates, among other information, purchase price, closing date, approximate loan terms, credits to be given, real estate tax information, and proration procedures

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The subject property is a condominium with monthly assessments of $400.00 due on the first day of each month.  The Seller is current in payment of the assessments as of June 1, and the closing is scheduled for June 15.  What is the amount of the credit or debit, if any is due, and how will it be reflected on the closing statement?
option_a: $200 credit to seller; $200 debit to buyer
option_b: $200 debit to seller; $200 credit to buyer
option_d: No credit or debit is due
feedback: The seller has paid the assessment for the entire month of June and is responsible for the assessments due up to and including the day of closing.  The closing is June 15.  Therefore, the seller is only responsible for 15 days of the $400 total monthly assessment.  In this case, there are 30 days in June.  15 days is ½ of 30, so the calculation would be $400 divided by ½ =$200.  It would show as a credit to the Seller (or a reimbursement for what they have already paid) and a debit to the buyer who is the party who owes the assessment for the time they are the owner

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: In order to provide assurance to the lender concerning the quality of the property’s financial position, the lender may require a
option_a: a letter from the seller’s lender that the property is free from liens
option_b: a survey, hazard insurance, title insurance, and escrow for taxes
option_c: a certificate of occupancy from the municipality
option_d: a signed statement from the appraiser guaranteeing value
feedback: The survey illustrates the size of the property and any improvements, easements, structures, or encroachments that affect title.  Hazard insurance protects the condition and liability of the property or property owner.  Title insurance insures title to the property.  A tax escrow provides the lender with the ability to pay the taxes when due to protect their lien

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Earnest Money deposits by the buyer count towards the total purchase price of the property.  Therefore, the closing statement would indicate the earnest money as a
option_c: debit to both seller and buyer
feedback: Since the buyer has paid earnest money, generally with the contract, they are given credit for the amount paid against the purchase price on the closing statement

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Lender fees are to be paid by the buyer and are indicated on the closing statement as a
feedback: Lender fees are the responsibility of the Buyer and will be added to the buyer costs

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The purchase price is noted on the closing statement as
option_c: the total of the purchase price minus earnest money as debit to seller
option_d: the total of the purchase price minus earnest money as debit to buyer
feedback: The purchase price is credited to the seller

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the seller has pre-paid the annual real estate taxes of $12,000, as well as the monthly $500 assessment through July 31, what would the total amount of the prorations be if the closing date is June 30?
feedback: Real Estate Taxes: $12,000 per year = $1,000 per month, Association Assessment = $500 per month paid through July 31, Closing is June 30, Real Estate Taxes July 1 – December 31 = $6,000, Assessment July 1 – July 31 = $500, Total proration amount is $6,500

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the seller has pre-paid the annual taxes, how would the prorations be shown on the closing statement?
option_a: As a credit to buyer and a debit to seller
option_b: As a credit to seller and a debit to buyer
option_c: The amount will not be prorated since it was prepaid
option_d: The amount will not be prorated since annual taxes are not prorated
feedback: Since Seller has already paid the amounts shown above, the buyer will give a credit to the seller by way of a debit to the buyer on the closing statement

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the Seller has paid the monthly homeowner association assessment of $620 for the month of July, what would be the amount of the credit due to the seller if the closing is July 1?
feedback: Total monthly assessment is $620.    $620/31 days = $20 per day, Seller has paid the July assessment.  Closing is July 1, so the seller owes only 1 day of assessments, Therefore, buyer will credit 30 days (31 days in July) of assessments (30 x $20) to Seller at closing.  $20 x 30 days = $600

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Using the 12 month/30 day/365 day method, calculate the proration credit for the current year’s real estate taxes that the buyer will receive at closing if the total tax amount for the year is $6,000 and the closing occurs on November 1.  The seller has paid the taxes through June 30
feedback: Total annual tax = $6,000. Closing November 1.  Taxes paid by seller through June 30 (6 months). Seller is responsible for taxes from January 1 through November 1. $6,000 divided by 365 days = $16.44/day. July 1 – November 1 = 124 days. 124 days x $16.44/day = $2,038.56 credit to Buyer

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the sales price of the property is $200,000 and the cost of the state transfer tax stamp is $1.00 per $1,000 of sales price, what is the amount of the transfer tax that is due at closing?

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the county transfer tax is $.50 per $1,000 of the sales price of the property being sold,  what is the amount of county transfer tax due at closing if the sales price is $250,000?

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The commission paid is shown on the closing statement as a debit to the seller since this will be paid out of the closing proceeds.  Calculate the amount of commission due if the sales price is $400,000 and the commission rate is 5.5%.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the annual taxes are $12,000, the closing is August 15, and the lender requires 6 months of taxes to be deposited into the escrow at closing, what is the amount of taxes that the lender will require?
feedback: $12,000 per year = $1,000 per month x 6 months = $6,000

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: There are certain restrictions placed on the lender when charging borrowers more than the amount on the original Loan Estimate form.  Certain charges that are not affected by the restrictions  include which of the following?
option_a: Property insurance premiums, prepaid interest, and amounts placed into an escrow
option_b: Recording fees, attorney fees, and credit report fees
option_c: Seller’s loan payoff and per diem interest
feedback: The TILA/RESPA Disclosure Rules allow limited restrictions for these charges to deviate from what was disclosed on the Loan Estimate. The requirement is that the information on the Loan Estimate was based upon the best information reasonably available to the lender at the time the Loan Estimate was provided

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The TILA RESPA Integrated Disclosure Rule does not apply to
option_c: loans or lines of credit to trusts for tax or estate planning
option_d: loans secured by vacant land or by 25+ acres
feedback: Home equity lines of credit, reverse mortgages and private creditor are types of loans that are not a part of the TRID requirements

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The information itemized on the Closing Disclosure would include the
option_a: list of occupants of the purchased property
option_b: debits and credits to buyers and sellers
feedback: All debits and credits to both the seller and buyer must be listed on the Closing Disclosure

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Charges for third-party services and recording fees paid by or imposed on the buyer or seller are grouped together on the Closing Disclosure and are subject to a 10% cumulative tolerance.  This means that the lender may charge the buyer or seller more than the amount disclosed on the Loan Estimate, as long as the total amount charged does not exceed 10%
option_a: more than the amount shown on the Loan Estimate
option_b: of the total charges in any one group
option_d: of the amounts dues to creditors
feedback: Charges for third-party services and recording fees paid by or imposed on the consumer are grouped together and subject to a 10% cumulative tolerance ("10% tolerance" charges). This means the creditor may charge the consumer more than the amount disclosed on the Loan Estimate for any of these charges so long as the total sum of the charges added together does not exceed the sum of all such charges disclosed on the Loan Estimate by more than 10%

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Closed transactions must be reported to the Internal Revenue Service, according to The Tax Reform Act of 1986.  The information must be reported on the IRS Form
feedback: The 1099-S is the form required by the IRS to report the names and addressed of the parties and information about the sale proceeds

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: The Tax Reform Act of 1986 also requires that persons designated by the act as “real estate brokerages” must report a closed real estate transaction to the IRS and provide information contained in the report to each party to the transaction.  The act defines “real estate broker” as which of the following?
option_b: The vice president of the mortgage company
option_c: The senior executive of the title company
option_d: The seller or buyer brokerage, settlement agents,  or mortgage lenders
feedback: The IRS defines the term “real estate broker” for the filing requirement as the seller or buyer brokerage, settlement agents, mortgage lenders

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the transaction involves a non-resident alien, the directive of such requirements would be found in the
option_a: Foreign Investment and Real Property Tax Act of 1980 (FIRPTA)
feedback: This is the Act that relates to whether or not a tax report must be filed for parties that are non-resident aliens

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Which of the following documents should be used to transfer title?
feedback: The Warranty Deed is one type of deed that transfers title

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Which of the following documents transfers the seller’s personal property?
feedback: The bill of sale is the document that itemizes the personal property that is to be sold and transferred to the buyer at the closing

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Which of the following does RESPA prohibit?
option_c: Brokers from earning a commission, as stated in the listing agreement
option_d: Brokers from engaging in dual agency
feedback: Because RESPA does not allow “kickbacks” from a 3rd party service provider in the transaction, the brokers must disclose to the parties whether or not they may or will be receiving any fees from outside service providers for work done on behalf of the closing and the clients, or if there is an affiliated business arrangement with a 3rd party provider

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: During the period between the signing of the sale contract and the closing date,
option_a: the seller may continue to accept offers on the property
option_b: the seller may not occupy or use the property
option_c: buyer and seller are expected to remove any contingencies that are stated in the contract
option_d: buyer and seller continue to negotiate the selling price if either party is dissatisfied
feedback: The time period between signing and closing is expected to be sufficient for the removal of any contingencies, such as the buyer's obtaining of financing, the performance of inspections, and the correction of identified physical defects. Failure of either buyer or seller to perform pre-closing actions specified in the contract can delay or terminate the transaction

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: When should a buyer undertake a "buyer's walk-through" of a property that is under contract?
option_a: Immediately after the offer is accepted
option_c: As shortly before the closing date as possible
option_d: As soon as all inspections have been completed
feedback: A final inspection, called a buyer's walk-through, should be conducted as close to the closing date as possible

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If a sale transaction is to occur in escrow,
option_a: the broker has no further involvement
option_b: an escrow agent holds funds and documents until all parties have satisfied the conditions necessary for closing
option_c: the seller's broker holds the sale documents until the buyer has satisfied the terms of the contract
option_d: the buyer's escrow agent and the seller's escrow agent complete the transaction
feedback: If the closing occurs "in escrow" rather than face-to-face, the principal parties deposit funds and documents with the appointed escrow agent, and the escrow agent disburses funds and releases documents to the appropriate parties when all the conditions of the escrow have been met.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A licensee’s choice of specialization is most likely influenced by
feedback: The licensee’s choice of specialization is influenced by competitive factors in the market and by perceived opportunities

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the conditions of an escrow agreement are not met and the transaction cannot be completed, the escrow agent
option_a: levies a fine against the defaulting party
option_b: assigns his or her fiduciary responsibilities to the seller's broker
option_d: cancels the sale contract and destroys the transaction documents
feedback: If for any reason the transaction cannot be completed, for instance if the buyer refuses the title as it is offered, or the buyer fails to produce the necessary cash, the escrow instructions usually provide a mechanism for reconveying title to the seller and funds to the buyer. In such a case, both parties return to their original status as if no sale had occurred

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A buyer and a seller have employed an escrow agent to handle a closing.  Which of the following statements is true?
option_a: Buyer and seller do not need to attend the closing
option_b: The seller receives the earnest money deposit from the escrow agent as soon as the buyer delivers it
option_c: The buyer receives legal title as soon as the seller accepts the down payment
option_d: The escrow agent certifies the buyer's earnest money check and provides the buyer with an opinion of abstract
feedback: If the closing occurs "in escrow" rather than face-to-face, the principal parties deposit funds and documents with the appointed escrow agent, and the escrow agent disburses funds and releases documents to the appropriate parties when all the conditions of the escrow have been met.The primary parties at the closing are normally buyer, seller, and a closing agent or escrow officer. Other parties who might be present include the title officer, attorneys, brokers or agents, and the lender's representative. It is not actually necessary for any of these parties to attend the meeting. The closing agent can complete the transaction, provided all documents have been duly executed in advance

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act applies to a transaction, the lender must
option_a: give the seller a booklet describing settlement costs and procedures
option_b: use a prescribed form to disclose settlement costs to the buyer
option_c: sign an agreement with seller and buyer to act as closing agent
option_d: allow the buyer to inspect the closing statement at least one week prior to the closing date
feedback: Effective October 3, 2015, a TILA/RESPA Integrated Disclosure Rule (TRID) integrates the disclosure requirements of RESPA and Truth-in-Lending, replacing the old Good Faith Estimate form and HUD-1 Uniform Settlement Statement a new Loan Estimate form and Closing Disclosure form, respectively

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: To avoid violating the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act, parties who are providing services to the buyer or seller in a transaction must
option_a: be paid before the closing date for any service they provide
option_b: inform the closing agent of the cost of their services at least one week before the closing date
option_c: receive payment only from the funds held in escrow, not directly from buyer or seller
option_d: disclose in writing any business relationships they have with other parties involved in the transaction
feedback: Business relationships and affiliations among real estate firms, mortgage brokers, title insurance firms and other such companies that are involved in a transaction are permitted, provided the relationships are disclosed in writing to the consumer, the consumer is free to go elsewhere for the relevant service, and the companies do not exchange fees for referrals

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: In the context of a closing, proration refers to
option_a: determining the amount of the commission that buyer and seller owe their respective agents at the closing
option_b: apportioning an amount paid, received, or due according to the period of time that a party is responsible for the item
option_c: determining which expenses the buyer and seller should pay unilaterally outside of the closing
feedback: Many of the items to be settled at the closing are partly the responsibility of the buyer and partly of the seller. Some are expense items that the seller has paid in advance, where the buyer owes the seller part of the expense. Some are income items that the seller received in advance, and the seller owes the buyer a part of the income. Others are items the buyer will have to pay in arrears, and the seller owes the buyer part of the expense. The method of dividing financial responsibility for such items is proration. With a prorated item, there is always a debit to one party and a corresponding credit for the same amount to the other party

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: If an item to be prorated affects buyer and seller, and no outside party, which of the following statements is true?
option_a: The item must be prorated and recorded as a debit to one party and a credit to the other party for the same amount
option_b: The item must be prorated and recorded as a debit to one party; the remainder is recorded as a credit to the other party
option_c: The party who is owed money receives a credit for the entire item, and the party who owes money receives a debit for the prorated amount
option_d: The party who owes money receives a debit for the portion owed and a credit for the portion that is not owed
feedback: With a prorated item, there is always a debit to one party and a corresponding credit for the same amount to the other party

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: An item is said to be paid in arrears if it is normally paid
option_a: on a monthly or yearly basis
option_b: at some time after the expense is incurred
feedback: Items paid in arrears are paid after the expense has been incurred

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Which of the following items is paid in arrears?
feedback: Typical items paid in arrears are real estate taxes, utility bills and interest on an assumed mortgage

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A seller received a rental payment of $100 in advance.  At closing, the seller has "earned" only $32 of this rent.  What should appear on the closing statement?
option_a: A debit to the seller and credit to the buyer for $32
option_b: A debit to the seller for $68 and a credit to the buyer for $32
option_c: A debit to the seller for $32 and a credit to the buyer for $100
option_d: A debit to the seller and credit to the buyer for $68
feedback: The buyer is owed $68 for their share of the rent so they are credited for $68

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A buyer will receive a water bill for an estimated $100 at the end of the month.  At closing, the seller has used an estimated $43 in water.  What should appear on the closing statement?
option_a: A debit to the seller and credit to the buyer for $57
option_b: A debit to the seller and credit to the buyer for $43
option_c: A debit to the buyer and credit to the seller for $57
option_d: A debit to the buyer and credit to the seller for $43
feedback: For an item the buyer will pay in arrears, the buyer receives a credit and the seller receives a debit. In effect, the seller has incurred some of the expenses that the buyer will be paying for after closing. Thus the seller must pay the buyer the used portion of the expense, or $43

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A sale transaction closes on April 1, the ninety-first day of the tax year.  The day of closing belongs to the seller.  Real estate taxes for the year, not yet billed, are expected to be $3,150.  According to the 365-day method, what is the seller's share of the tax bill?

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: What is the seller's share of the real estate taxes?
feedback: Assuming a 365 day year, the daily tax expense is ($3,300 ÷ 365), or $9.041. As taxes are paid in arrears, the buyer will be paying the annual bill. Thus, he will be owed a credit for the seller's share of the bill, which is $9.041 x 244 days, or $2,206.03

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: What is the buyer's share of the existing hazard insurance already paid in full?
feedback: The buyer's share is the unused portion, or (365 days – 244 days), or 121 days. The daily expense is ($700 ÷ 365), or $.9589. ($1.918 x 121) = $232.05. The seller receives this amount as a credit and the buyer a debit

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How much is the buyer's tax and insurance escrow?
feedback: The lender wants the hazard insurance and real estate taxes to be split.  Therefore, the total is $725. $700 x .5 = $350,  $3,300 x .5 = $1650,  $350 + $1650 = $2,000

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: What is the buyer's prepaid interest?
feedback: If the buyer pays $984.37 interest for 30 days, the daily expense is ($984.37 ÷ 30), or $32.812. If there are 29 days of pre-paid expense, the buyer's charge is ($32.812 x 29), or $951.56

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How much will the buyer pay for points?

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How much will the seller pay the broker?

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How much will the seller receive at closing?
feedback: The credits for the seller are $262,500 (sale price) and $232.05 for hazard insurance (total of $262,732.05).  The debits are a total of $18,687.03; $2,206.03 for real estate taxes, $15,750.00 for broker commissions, $250 for escrow fees and $481 for transfer fees.  $87,616.03 - $6,716.34 = $80,899.69

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: How much will the buyer have to pay at closing?
feedback: The total debits for the buyer are $270,933.61 ($262,500 for sale price, $4,725 for points, $951.56 for prepaid interest, $2,000 for tax/insurance escrow, $232.05 for hazard insurance, $250 for title insurance, $250 for escrow fee, and $25 for recording fees).  The total credits for the buyer are $242,056.03 ($3,600 for earnest money, $236,250 for loan amount, and $2,206.03 for real estate taxes). $270,933.61 - $242,056.03 = $28,877.58 so the buyer must pay $28,877.58 at closing

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A principal empowers an agent to conduct the ongoing activities of one of her business enterprises. This is an example of
feedback: In a general agency, the principal delegates to the agent ongoing tasks and duties within a particular business or enterprise. Such delegation may include the authority to enter into contracts

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A property seller empowers an agent to market and sell a property on his behalf. This is an example of
feedback: Special, or limited, agency. Under a special agency agreement, the principal delegates authority to conduct a specific activity, after which the agency relationship terminates. In most cases, the special agent may not bind the principal to a contract

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Implied agency arises when
option_a: an agent accepts an oral listing
option_b: a principal accepts an oral listing
option_c: a party creates an agency relationship outside of an express agreement
option_d: a principal agrees to all terms of a written listing agreement, whether express or implied
feedback: An agency relationship can arise by implication, intentionally or unintentionally. Implication means that the parties act as if there were an agreement. For example, if an agent promises a buyer to do everything possible to find a property at the lowest possible price, and the buyer accepts the proposition, there may be an implied agency relationship even though there is no specific agreement

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship may be involuntarily terminated for which of the following reasons?
option_a: Death or incapacity of the agent
feedback: An agency relationship may terminate contrary to the wishes of the parties by reason of death or incapacity of either party

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A principal discloses that she would sell a property for $375,000. During the listing period, the house is marketed for $425,000. No offers come in, and the listing expires. Two weeks later, the agent grumbles to a customer that the seller would have sold for less than the listed price. Which of the following is true?
option_a: The agent has violated the duty of confidentiality
option_b: The agent has fulfilled all fiduciary duties, including confidentiality, since the listing had expired
option_c: The agent is violating the duties owed this customer
option_d: The agent has created a dual agency situation with the customer
feedback: An agent must hold in confidence any personal or business information received from the client during the term of employment. An agent may not disclose any information that would harm the client's interests or bargaining position, or anything else the client wishes to keep secret

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A principal instructs an agent to market a property only to families on the north side of town. The agent refuses to comply. In this case,
option_a: the agent has violated fiduciary duty
option_b: the agent has not violated fiduciary duty
option_c: the agent is liable for breaching the listing terms
option_d: the agent should obey the instruction to salvage the listing
feedback: An agent must comply with the client's directions and instructions, provided they are legal. An agent who cannot obey a legal directive, for whatever reason, must withdraw from the relationship. If the directive is illegal, the agent must also immediately withdraw

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An owner's agent is showing a buyer an apartment building. The buyer notices water stains on the ceiling, and informs the agent. The agent's best course of action is to
option_a: immediately contract to paint the ceiling
option_b: immediately contract to repair the roof
option_c: suggest the buyer make a lower-price offer
feedback: An agent has the duty to inform the client of all material facts, reports, and rumors that might affect the client's interests in the property transaction

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent owes customers several duties. These may be best described as
feedback: In general, agents owe a third party:  •honesty and fair dealing  • reasonable care and skill  •proper disclosure

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent fails to discover flood marks on the walls in the basement of a property. The agent sells the property, and the buyer later sues the agent for failing to mention the problem. In this case, the agent
option_a: may be guilty of intentional misrepresentation
option_b: has an exposure to a charge of negligent misrepresentation
option_c: has little exposure, since the problem was not mentioned on the signed disclosure form
option_d: is not vulnerable, since the problem was not discovered
feedback: An agent can be held liable for failure to disclose facts the agent was not aware of if it can be demonstrated that the agent should have known such facts. For example, if it is a common standard that agents inspect property, then an agent can be held liable for failing to disclose a leaky roof that was not inspected

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent informs a buyer that a clause in a contract is standard language. After explaining the clause, the agent assures the buyer that the clause does not mean anything significant. If something goes wrong with the transaction, the agent could be liable for
feedback: A broker must exercise care not to offer advice outside of his or her field of expertise. Violations of this standard may expose the agent to liability for the unlicensed practice of a profession such as law, engineering, or accounting

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An outside broker locates a seller for a buyer representative's client. In this instance, the outside broker is acting as
feedback: In a subagency, a broker or licensed salesperson works as the agent of a broker who is the agent of a client. Subagents might include a cooperating licensed broker, that broker's licensed salespeople, and the listing broker's licensed salespeople, all of whom agree to work for the listing broker on behalf of the client

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Agent Bob, who works for Broker Bill, obtains an owner listing to lease a building. Bill's other agent, Sue, locates a tenant for Bob's listing. In the absence of any arrangement to the contrary, Broker Bill in this instance is
feedback: Dual agency occurs whenever a single broker represents both principal parties to a transaction. Here, since Bob and Sue work for Bill, Bill is the dual agent

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent is operating as a disclosed dual agent on a transaction. In this case, the agent
option_a: may not represent one party's interests to the detriment of the other
option_c: must be obedient and loyal to both parties
option_d: must require that the principals share equally in paying the commission
feedback: A dual agent's first duty is to disclose the agency relationship to both principal parties or to withdraw from one side of the duality. After disclosing, the agent must obtain the written consent of both parties. If both parties accept the dual agency, the agent owes all the fiduciary duties to both parties except full disclosure, undivided loyalty, and exclusive representation of one principal's interests

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The duties of an agent acting as a facilitator are most similar to those of a
feedback: A recent solution allows a broker to represent no one in a transaction. That is, the broker acts as a transaction broker, or facilitator, and is not an agent of either the buyer or seller. In this relationship, the facilitator does not advocate the interests of either party. This is most similar to dual agency

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The meaning and import of the agency relationship should be disclosed to the client
option_a: prior to completing a listing agreement
option_b: prior to or upon completion of an offer
option_c: upon the initial contact with the person
option_d: whenever a dual agency relationship is indicated
feedback: An agent who intends to represent a seller or owner must disclose the import of the proposed agency relationship in writing before the listing agreement is executed

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Owner agents must disclose their agency relationship to tenants or buyers
option_a: immediately prior to the initial contact
option_d: immediately following any offer executed by the customer
feedback: A buyer agent must disclose the agency relationship to the seller or seller's agent on first contact. Substantive contact is assumed

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A tenant representative should disclose his or her agency relationship to the owner's agent
option_a: immediately prior to the initial contact
option_d: immediately following any offer executed by the landlord
feedback: A buyer agent must disclose the agency relationship to the seller or seller's agent on first contact. Substantive contact is assumed

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Licensee Gretchen of the Jumbo Mountain Agency takes a listing to sell the home of John Hess. Prospective buyer Lucy Bingham asks licensee Calvin Bentley of the same agency to assist her in purchasing the Hess property. What can the broker of the Jumbo agency do to prevent Gretchen and Calvin from creating unwanted dual agency conflicts for Jumbo?
option_a: Appoint Gretchen and Calvin as designated agents of the seller and buyer, respectively.
option_b: Have Calvin sign an exclusive buyer agency agreement with the buyer.
option_c: Have Gretchen sign a non-exclusive subagency agreement with the seller.
option_d: Take over Gretchen’s listing and act directly as the seller’s agent.
feedback: A designated agent is a salesperson or an associate broker selected by the broker and client as the client's legal agent pursuant to a designated agency agreement. Under a designated agency agreement, the client has no agency relationship with any licensee other than those specified in the designated agency agreement. Absent such an agreement, the client has an agency relationship with the broker and all affiliated licensees in the brokerage firm. The designated agency agreement allows a broker to select a salesperson and associate brokers to act as the client’s agent.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The most primary of relationships in real estate brokerage is that between broker and client, the relationship known in law as the
feedback: The most primary of relationships in real estate brokerage is that between broker and client, the relationship known in law as the agency relationship. In every state, a body of law, generally called the law of agency, defines and regulates the legal roles of this relationship

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The essence of the agency relationship is
option_a: trust, confidence, and mutual good faith
feedback: The essence of the agency relationship is trust, confidence, and mutual good faith. The principal trusts the agent to exercise the utmost skill and care in fulfilling the authorized activity, and to promote the principal's best interests. The agent undertakes to strive in good faith to achieve the desired objective, and to fulfill the fiduciary duties

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The party who hires the agent is known as which of the following?
feedback: The principal, or client, is the party who hires the agent. The agent works for the client. The principal may be a seller, a buyer, a landlord, or a tenant

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a unique characteristic of portfolio lenders?
option_a: They originate loans to retain in their loan portfolios
option_b: They are not required to follow regulations of the primary and secondary mortgage markets
option_c: They may legally discriminate regarding who they will accept as borrowers
feedback: A primary mortgage market lender may or may not sell its loans into the secondary market. Many lenders originate loans for the purpose of retaining the investments in their own loan portfolio. These loans are referred to as portfolio loans, and lenders originating loans for their own portfolio are called portfolio lenders

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a strength of the cost approach?
option_a: Depreciation is difficult to measure, especially for older buildings
option_b: The cost to create improvements is not necessarily the same as market value
option_c: The cost approach is very accurate for a property with new improvements, which are the highest and best use of the property
option_d: The cost approach provides an upper limit for the comparables value based on the undepreciated cost of reproducing the improvements
feedback: The strengths of the cost approach are that it: provides an upper limit for the subject's value based on the undepreciated cost of reproducing the improvements, is very accurate for a property with new improvements which are the highest and best use of the property

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a universal agency relationship?
option_a: The principal delegates to the agent ongoing tasks and duties within a particular business or enterprise
option_b: The principal delegates authority to conduct a specific activity, after which the agency relationship terminates
option_c: The principal hires a licensed broker to procure a ready, willing, and able buyer or seller
option_d: The principal empowers the agent to perform any and all actions that may be legally delegated to an agency representative
feedback: In a universal agency relationship, the principal empowers the agent to perform any and all actions that may be legally delegated to an agency representative. The instrument of authorization is the power of attorney

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a distinguishing characteristic of a sole proprietor?
option_a: The business profits of a sole proprietor are not taxable
option_b: A sole proprietor is except from licensing requirements
option_c: A sole proprietor is protected against personal losses and lawsuits
option_d: A sole proprietor is personally liable for their own debts and may be sued personally
feedback: One of the distinguishing characteristics of sole proprietorships is liability: sole proprietors are personally liable for their own debts and actions and those of employees while performing business duties; proprietors may be sued personally

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a distinguishing feature of a general partnership?
option_a: All partners are protected against liability for debts
option_b: All partners bear full liability for debts and obligations
feedback: One of the distinguishing characteristics of a general partnership is liability: all partners bear full liability for debts and obligations jointly and severally

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship can arise by implication, intentionally or unintentionally. Which of the following illustrates implied agency?
option_a: An agent promises a buyer to do everything possible to find a property without entering into a specific agreement
option_b: An agent signs a listing agreement with a seller promising to do everything possible to find a buyer
option_c: An agent provides the buyer with a written disclosure statement indicating that the buyer should seek representation
option_d: An agent responds to an email answering general questions about the advertised property
feedback: An agency relationship can arise by implication, intentionally or unintentionally. Implication means that the parties act as if there were an agreement. For example, if an agent promises a buyer to do everything possible to find a property at the lowest possible price, and the buyer accepts the proposition, there may be an implied agency relationship even though there is no specific agreement

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Ownership of real estate is often described as a “bundle of legal rights.” These rights could include:
option_a: Mineral rights, air rights, water rights, right to make any improvements one desires
option_c: Mineral rights, air rights, water rights, possession, leasing, control, disposition
option_d: Emblements, chattel real, trade fixtures, personal property
feedback: The bundle includes possession, control, enjoyment, exclusion, and disposition rights.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship may terminate for all the following reasons EXCEPT
option_c: the revocation of the agent’s license
feedback: An agency relationship may terminate by reason of: death or incapacity of either party, abandonment by the agent, condemnation or destruction of the property, renunciation, breach, bankruptcy, revocation of the agent's license. An undisclosed property defect would not be grounds for terminating the agency relationship

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Adam grants a life estate to his mother. This life estate is based on the life of Adam’s father. When Adam’s father dies the estate goes back to Adam. While Adam’s father is alive, Adam has what type of interest in the property?
feedback: Adam has a reversionary interest because the property returns to him after the measuring life ends.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of an agent’s fiduciary duty of care?
option_a: An agent who offers advice outside of his or her field of expertise
option_b: An agent who does not offer advice outside of his or her field of expertise
option_c: An agent who withholds unrealistically low-priced offers from the seller
option_d: An agent who recommends the buyer make a 95% offer
feedback: The notion of care extends to observing the limited scope of authority granted to the agent. A conventional listing agreement does not authorize an agent to obligate the client to contracts, and it does not allow the agent to conceal offers to buy, sell, or lease coming from a customer or another agent. Further, since a client relies on a broker's representations, a broker must exercise care not to offer advice outside of his or her field of expertise

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which fiduciary duty requires the agent to place the interest of the client above those of all others?
feedback: The duty of loyalty requires the agent to place the interests of the client above those of all others, particularly the agent's own. This standard is particularly relevant whenever an agent discusses transaction terms with a prospect

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A property is advertised “with the use of the tennis courts.” The “tennis courts” are an example of:
feedback: Easements give legal rights of use; amenities refer to features enhancing property enjoyment.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which fiduciary duty extends beyond the termination of the listing?
feedback: The confidentiality standard is one of the duties that extends beyond the termination of the listing: at no time in the future may the agent disclose confidential information

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Critical areas of disclosure to prospective buyers in a transaction are
option_a: a neighborhood’s race, religion, and principal ethnicities
option_b: agency, property condition, and environmental hazards
feedback: Essential disclosures to buyers primarily concern agency, property condition, and environmental hazards

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: What type of lease could contain the following language? “Beginning on December 1, 2020, and ending on December 31, 2020 . . ..”
feedback: A tenancy for years has a defined start and end date.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent who intentionally fails to reveal a material fact about a property is
option_a: protecting the seller and fulfilling his or her fiduciary duty of confidentiality
option_b: defrauding a buyer and could be held liable for intentional misrepresentation
feedback: An agent may defraud a buyer by misrepresenting or concealing facts. While it is acceptable to promote the features of a property to a buyer or the virtues of a buyer to a seller, it is a fine line that divides promotion from misrepresentation. Silent misrepresentation, which is intentionally failing to reveal a material fact, is just as fraudulent as a false statement

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A breach of duty by a real estate licensee may result in all the following EXCEPT
option_c: disciplinary action by the state license law authorities
option_d: rescission of the sales contract between the seller and buyer
feedback: A breach of duty may result in: rescission of the listing agreement (causing a loss of a potential commission), forfeiture of any compensation that may have already been earned, disciplinary action by state license law authorities, including license suspension or revocation, suit for damages in court

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In a _____________________ role, the agent represents one party in the transaction
feedback: In single agency, the agent represents one party in a transaction. The client may be either seller or buyer

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In a subagency relationship, a broker or licensed salesperson works as
option_a: the agent of a seller who is also cooperating with other agents
option_b: the agent of a broker who is the agent of a client
option_c: the agent of a broker who is the agent of a customer
option_d: the transactional broker who is not representing either party
feedback: In a subagency, a broker or licensed salesperson works as the agent of a broker who is the agent of a client. Subagents might include a cooperating licensed broker, that broker's licensed salespeople, and the listing broker's licensed salespeople, all of whom agree to work for the listing broker on behalf of the client

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of dual agency?
option_a: An agent who represents two brokerage firms
option_b: An agent who represents buyers and tenants
option_c: An agent who represents sellers and landlords
option_d: An agent who represents both tenant and owner
feedback: Dual agency means representing both principal parties to a transaction. The agent represents both buyer and seller or tenant and owner

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The primary forms of an agency relationship between brokers and principals are all the following EXCEPT
feedback: The primary forms of agency relationship between brokers and principals are: single agency, dual agency, and subagency. In a fourth kind of relationship, referred to as transaction brokerage, no agency relationship exists in the transaction

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A broker who represents no one in a transaction is acting as a/an
feedback: A broker to represent no one in a transaction. That is, the broker acts as a transaction broker, or facilitator, and is not an agent of either the buyer or seller. In this relationship, the facilitator does not advocate the interests of either party

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Subject to variations in state regulation, "substantive contact" between listing agent and customer occurs whenever the agent is
option_a: showing the buyer more than three properties
option_b: holding an open house answering general questions about the location
option_c: responding to questions of fact regarding advertised properties
option_d: eliciting confidential information from a prospect regarding motivation
feedback: Subject to variations in state regulation, "substantive contact" between listing agent and customer occurs whenever the agent is: showing the prospect a property, eliciting confidential information from a prospect regarding needs, motivation, or financial qualification, executing a contractual offer to sell or lease

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent who desires to operate in a dual agency capacity must
option_a: obtain the informed written consent of all parties
option_b: obtain the written consent of the broker
feedback: An agent who desires to operate in a dual agency capacity must obtain the informed written consent of all parties. Subsequent contracts should confirm the disclosure. "Informed written consent" means both parties have read, understood, and signed an acceptable disclosure form

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A dual agent, unless expressly instructed by the relevant party, usually cannot disclose which of the following?
option_a: The motivation of any party concerning the transaction
option_c: The material defects of the property
feedback: An agency relationship can arise by implication, intentionally or unintentionally. Implication means that the parties act as if there were an agreement. For example, if an agent promises a buyer to do everything possible to find a property at the lowest possible price, and the buyer accepts the proposition, there may be an implied agency relationship even though there is no specific agreement

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following can occur if a broker or agent acts in any way that leads a customer to believe that the agent is representing the customer?
option_a: An implied single agency has occurred
option_b: An expressed and disclosed single agency has occurred
option_c: An implied and undisclosed dual agency can potentially be created
option_d: An expressed and undisclosed dual agency can potentially be created
feedback: If a broker or agent acts in any way that leads a customer to believe that the agent is representing the customer, a dual agency has potentially been created

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: It is cooperating brokers who form _____________ to facilitate the process of bringing together buyers and sellers
feedback: It is cooperating brokers who form multiple listing services to facilitate the process of bringing together buyers and sellers

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent of the seller is known as the
feedback: Real estate vernacular calls an agent of the seller or owner the listing broker. An agent who works for the listing broker and who obtained the listing is the listing agent. A broker who represents a buyer is the buyer's broker. One who does this as a specialization is a buyer broker. A broker or salesperson who represents tenants is a tenant representative

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A broker who represents lessees is a
feedback: Real estate vernacular calls an agent of the seller or owner the listing broker. An agent who works for the listing broker and who obtained the listing is the listing agent. A broker who represents a buyer is the buyer's broker. One who does this as a specialization is a buyer broker. A broker or salesperson who represents tenants is a tenant representative

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following does NOT terminate an agency agreement?
feedback: Full performance of all obligations by the parties terminates an agency relationship. In addition, the parties may terminate the relationship at any time by mutual agreement. Thirdly, the agency relationship automatically terminates on the expiration date, whether the obligations were performed or not

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The agency relationship is defined by
option_c: the law of real estate contracts
option_d: the agreement between a principal and an agent
feedback: In every state, a body of law, generally called the law of agency, defines and regulates the legal roles of this relationship. While the relationship is subject to contract law, agency law dictates how the relationship will achieve its purposes, regardless of what the listing contract states

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is true of the connection between compensation and the agency relationship?
option_a: An agreement to give and receive compensation creates an agency relationship
option_b: If an agency relationship exists, the principal must provide valuable consideration to the agent
option_c: The relationship is independent of any compensation arrangement
option_d: If an agency relationship exists, the agent is entitled to compensation
feedback: It is important to understand that the agency relationship does not require compensation or any form of consideration. Nor does compensation define an agency relationship: a party other than the principal may compensate the agent

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The defining feature of a special agency is that the agent has authority to perform
option_a: all actions legally delegated by a power of attorney
option_b: all actions necessary to conduct an enterprise on behalf of the principal
option_c: all actions necessary to complete a specific transaction on behalf of the principal
option_d: specific activities on behalf of the principal, as defined in the agency agreement
feedback: Under a special agency agreement, the principal delegates authority to conduct a specific activity, after which the agency relationship terminates

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The agent and principal may terminate the agency relationship by mutual consent
option_b: at any time until the agent has begun to perform the obligations of the agreement
option_c: only if the written agreement provides for cancellation
option_d: only if the principal and agent have a contract
feedback: The parties to an agency relationship may terminate the relationship at any time by mutual agreement

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: If an agency relationship terminates because one of the parties defaulted, which of the following is true?
option_b: Both parties must continue to perform all obligations of the agreement
option_c: The defaulting party may have a financial liability
option_d: The damaged party has no claim against the defaulting party
feedback: Involuntary termination of the relationship may create legal and financial liability for a party who defaults or cancels. For example, a client may renounce an agreement but then be held liable for the agent's expenses or commission

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Among the fiduciary duties imposed on a real estate agent is the requirement to
option_a: refuse offers the agent knows will be unacceptable to the principal
option_b: present all offers to the principal
option_c: advise the principal against accepting an offer that is below full price
option_d: advise a prospect that the principal will not accept the prospect's offer in order to elicit a better offer
feedback: A conventional listing agreement does not authorize an agent to obligate the client to contracts, and it does not allow the agent to conceal offers to buy, sell, or lease coming from a customer or another agent. Further, since a client relies on a broker's representations, a broker must exercise care not to offer advice outside of his or her field of expertise

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: One of the agent's fiduciary duties that continues even after a listing agreement expires is
feedback: The confidentiality standard is one of the duties that extends beyond the termination of the listing: at no time in the future may the agent disclose confidential information

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The level of competence that a principal has the right to expect from an agent is generally that which is
option_b: necessary to earn the promised compensation
option_d: comparable to that of other practitioners in the area
feedback: The agent is hired to do a job, and is therefore expected to do it with diligence and reasonable competence. Competence is generally defined as a "standard of practice" equal to the level of real estate marketing skills and knowledge of other practitioners in the area

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A title insurance company official would have known that an "abstract of title” is a:
option_a: Standard form that is used in the industry
option_b: Closing statement from the title insurance company
option_d: Written summary of documents relating to the title of the property
feedback: An abstract of title is a summary of the history of the ownership of a property, starting with the original grant, and including all subsequent encumbrances and conveyances.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: If a potential buyer discloses financial qualifications to a seller's agent, the agent is required to
option_b: disclose the information to the buyer's agent
option_c: disclose the information to the seller
option_d: verify the buyer's statements before disclosing them to the client
feedback: An agent has the duty to inform the client of all material facts, reports, and rumors that might affect the client's interests in the property transaction. This includes: the agent's opinion of the property's condition; information about the buyer's motivations and financial qualifications; discussions between agent and buyer regarding the possibility of the agent's representing the buyer in another transaction

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A buyer's broker owes the full set of fiduciary duties to
option_c: the party who will pay the commission
feedback: The agency relationship imposes fiduciary duties on the client and agent, but particularly on the agent. The buyer is a client of the buyer's broker, and the buyer's broker is an agent of the buyer

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A subagent is technically the agent of
option_c: a broker who has an agency relationship with a client
option_d: the client's and the customer's agents
feedback: In a subagency, a broker or licensed salesperson works as the agent of a broker who is the agent of a client. Subagents might include a cooperating licensed broker, that broker's licensed salespeople, and the listing broker's licensed salespeople, all of whom agree to work for the listing broker on behalf of the client

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is a dual agency situation?
option_a: Two agents share the exclusive right to represent the same client
option_b: One agent represents both sides in a transaction
option_c: A selling agent from one brokerage works with a listing agent from another brokerage to complete a transaction
option_d: One agent represents two sellers at the same time
feedback: Dual agency means representing both principal parties to a transaction. The agent represents both buyer and seller or tenant and owner

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following situations has the potential for creating an implied dual agency?
option_a: A listing agent's subagent learns that a potential buyer has made false claims about his financial qualifications and informs the listing agent
option_b: A listing agent's subagent learns that a potential buyer has made false claims about his financial qualifications and does not inform the listing agent
option_c: A buyer's agent notices a structural problem in a house the buyer is considering and informs the buyer
option_d: A buyer's agent notices a structural problem in an house the buyer is considering and informs the buyer and the seller's agent
feedback: If a broker or agent acts in any way that leads a customer to believe that the agent is representing the customer, a dual agency has potentially been created. For example, a buyer makes confidential disclosures to the agent who works for the seller and exhorts the agent to keep them confidential. The buyer wants the house but knows he is going to lose his job in a month and probably will not qualify for financing. If the agent agrees to keep the information confidential, the agent has not only created an agency relationship with the buyer, but is now in a dual agency situation. Moreover, if the agent fails to disclose the buyer's confidence to the seller, the agent has violated fiduciary duty to the seller

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Among the duties of a broker who is acting as a transaction broker, or facilitator, is
option_a: preserving the confidentiality of information received from either party by not telling the other party
option_b: helping the two parties to arrive at a deal that is closest to achieving the objectives of each party
option_c: disclosing material facts that affect the value of the property to both parties
option_d: choosing to obey the instructions of one party and informing the other party of the decision
feedback: In the role of facilitator, the broker's only fiduciary duties and standards of conduct are those of accounting; skill, care, and diligence; honesty and fair dealing; and disclosure to both parties of all material facts in residential sale transactions affecting the property's value. Some states do not allow for this form of relationship in a real estate transaction

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In which of the following contact situations would a seller's agent be expected to disclose his agency relationships?
option_a: The agent is showing the client's property to a prospective buyer
option_b: The agent tells an acquaintance at a party about the client's property
option_c: The agent answers questions about the client's property for a telephone caller responding to a newspaper ad
option_d: The agent is showing a potential buyer houses in a certain price range in the multiple listing book
feedback: Agency disclosure must occur upon "substantive contact" between the listing agent and a customer. Examples of substantive contact include: showing the prospect a property; eliciting confidential information from a prospect regarding needs, motivation, or financial qualification; and executing a contractual offer to sell or lease

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is TRUE of a designated agency relationship?
option_a: The client has an implied agency relationship with all licensed agents in the designated agent’s firm.
option_b: There is no client confidentiality that must be preserved.
option_c: The agency agreement must name all associate brokers who are authorized to act as supervisory brokers.
option_d: A designated agent may represent both parties in a single transaction without being a dual agent for either.
feedback: A designated agency agreement must contain the name of all associate brokers who are authorized to act as supervisory brokers. A listing agreement or a buyer's agency agreement may be amended to establish a designated agency relationship, to change a designated agent, or to change supervisory brokers at any time pursuant to a written addendum.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: To be valid, a listing agreement
feedback: A valid listing may be oral or written. However, many states consider only a written listing to be enforceable, particularly an exclusive right-to-sell listing

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The type of listing that assures a broker of compensation for procuring a customer, regardless of the procuring party, is a(n)
feedback: The exclusive right-to-sell agreement is the most widely used owner agreement. Under the terms of this listing, a seller contracts exclusively with a single broker to procure a buyer or effect a sale transaction. If a buyer is procured during the listing period, the broker is entitled to a commission, regardless of who is procuring cause

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An owner agrees to pay a broker for procuring a tenant unless it is the owner who finds the tenant. This is an example of a(n)
feedback: An exclusive agency listing authorizes a single broker to sell the property and earn a commission, but leaves the owner the right to sell the property without the broker's assistance, in which case no commission is owed

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A landlord promises to compensate a broker for procuring a tenant, provided the broker is the procuring cause. This is an example of a(n)
feedback: An open listing is a non-exclusive authorization to sell or lease a property. The owner may offer such agreements to any number of brokers in the marketplace. With an open listing, the broker who is the first to perform under the terms of the listing is the sole party entitled to a commission

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: On a closing statement in a typical real estate transaction, the buyer’s earnest money deposit is reflected as a:
option_b: credit to the seller and a debit to the buyer
feedback: Earnest money is applied toward the purchase price, so it appears as a credit to the buyer.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A property owner agrees to pay a broker a commission, provided the owner receives a minimum amount of proceeds from the sale at closing. This is an example of a(n)
feedback: A net listing is one in which an owner sets a minimum acceptable amount to be received from the transaction and allows the broker to have any amount received in excess as a commission

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The most significant difference between an owner representation agreement and a buyer representation agreement is
feedback: An owner listing authorizes a broker to represent an owner or landlord. A buyer agency or tenant representation agreement authorizes a broker to represent a buyer or tenant

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A multiple listing authorization gives a broker what authority?
option_a: To list the owner's property in a multiple listing service
option_b: To sell several properties for the owner at once
option_c: To sell or lease the property
option_d: To delegate the listing responsibilities to other agents
feedback: A multiple listing is not a distinct listing contract but rather a provision in an exclusive listing authorizing the broker to place the listing into a multiple listing service. A multiple listing service is an organization of member brokers who agree to cooperate in the sale of properties listed by other brokers in exchange for a share of the broker's resulting commission

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A broker is hired to procure a customer for a client. In order to earn compensation, the agent must procure a customer who
option_a: has seen the property and reviewed all documents
option_b: is ready, willing, and able to transact
option_c: has completed an acceptable sale or lease contract
option_d: will successfully complete the transaction at closing
feedback: Finding a customer means locating a party who is ready, willing, and able to transact under the client's terms

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: One of the most important actions an owner's agent is authorized to perform under an exclusive listing agreement is
option_b: assisting the customer in evaluating the relative merits of other properties
option_c: authorizing price adjustments in a timely manner
option_d: executing the sale or lease agreement
feedback: A listing agreement authorizes a broker to undertake actions relevant to achieving the performance objective, which includes showing or seeking property

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent's performance of due diligence concerning a listing can best be described as
option_a: initiating the marketing plan on or before a deadline
option_b: expending an amount of effort that is commensurate with the nature and size of the transaction
option_c: ascertaining the facts about the client and the property at the onset of the listing period
option_d: helping customers satisfy their needs to the extent they are entitled
feedback: Due diligence in the listing context refers to verifying the accuracy of the statements in the listing regarding the property, the owner, and the owner's representations

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The amount of a real estate broker's commission is
option_c: established by the Board of Realtors®
option_d: established by state real estate license law
feedback: The amount of a broker's commission is whatever amount the client and broker have agreed to. Compensation may be in the form of a percentage of the sale or lease price, or a flat fee

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A client suddenly decides to revoke an exclusive right-to-sell listing midway through the listing term. The reason stated: the client did not like the agent. In this case,
option_a: the client is criminally liable for discrimination on the basis of the cause for cancellation
option_b: the client may be liable for a commission and marketing expenses
option_c: the agent can sue the client for specific performance, even if no customer had been located
option_d: the agent has no recourse but to accept the revocation
feedback: Both principals to the listing agreement have the power to revoke the contract at any time. They do not, however, always have the right. That is, client or broker may cancel a listing but remain liable for damages to the other party. For an exclusive right-to-sell listing: if the property sells during the term of the revoked listing, the client is liable for the commission. If the property does not sell, the client is liable for the broker's actual costs

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A "protection period" clause in an exclusive listing provides that
option_a: the owner is protected from all liabilities arising from the agent's actions performed within the agent's scope of duties
option_b: the agent has a claim to a commission if the owner sells or leases to a party within a certain time following the listing's expiration
option_c: agents are entitled to extend a listing agreement's term if a transaction is imminent
option_d: an owner is not liable for a commission if a prospective customer delays in completing an acceptable offer
feedback: Many listings include a protection clause stating that, for a certain period after expiration, the owner is liable for the commission if the property sells to a party that the broker procured, unless the seller has since listed the property with another broker

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A seller wants a licensee to put her home in the MLS and conduct an openhouse but do nothing else to sell it for a reduced commission. The kind of listing agreement that would allow this arrangement is a(n)
feedback: Rather than provide the full services due a client under a standard listing agreement, a licensee may enter into an agreement to provide limited services selected by the client.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An owner promises to pay a commission fee for any broker who produces a ready, willing, and able buyer to purchase his house. However, the broker is not obligated to perform any services on behalf of the owner.  What type of agreement has the owner created?
feedback: An agreement in which only one party promises to perform, contingent on the other party’s performance of an option action

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An exclusive listing agreement requires, in writing,
feedback: Though the exclusive listing agreements vary from state to state, generally, a written listing agreement must include owner names, property address or legal description, listing price, expiration date, commission terms, and authority granted

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: When a buyer is amenable to the terms of the transaction and is capable of paying the price, the individual is deemed as
feedback: A ready, willing, and able customer is one who is amenable to the terms of the transaction (ready and willing); and financially capable of paying the price and legally capable of completing the transaction (able)

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An express agreement authorizes the broker to act on behalf of a client in procuring or selling a parcel of real property.  This agreement is referred to as a(n)
feedback: An express agreement, written or oral, authorizes the broker to pursue specific actions on behalf of the client

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A listing agreement may not be terminated due to
option_b: the incapacitation or death of either party
option_d: the expiration of the listing agreement
feedback: A listing agreement may terminate based on performance, infeasibility, mutual agreement, abandonment, breach, listing expiration, invalidity of contract, incapacitation or death of either party, involuntary title transfer, or destruction of the property

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An exclusive-right-to sell agreement contains a protection period clause that protects the
