A Special Warranty Deed assures the buyer that the seller has not done anything to encumber the property title during their ownership period. It is the most appropriate choice for a seller who has owned the property for a limited time and wishes to provide assurances solely for that duration. This deed guarantees that there are no liens or claims against the property arising during the time the seller owned it, but it does not protect against issues that may have existed before the seller's ownership. This specificity makes it an ideal choice for sellers who cannot or do not want to provide a full history of the property but are willing to stand behind their own period of ownership.
Comparatively, a General Warranty Deed offers the most comprehensive protection, guaranteeing the property's title against all claims, extending back to the property's origins. This deed would be excessive and potentially misleading in a scenario where the seller only wants to assure the period of their ownership. A Bargain and Sale Deed implies that the seller holds title to the property but makes no warranties regarding the quality of the title, which could leave the buyer exposed to potential title issues from the seller's ownership period. Lastly, a Quitclaim Deed provides no warranties whatsoever; it merely transfers whatever interest the seller has in the property, if any, without assuring that the title is clear of liens or encumbrances. This type of deed is typically used between family members or to clear up title issues rather than in traditional sales where the buyer seeks some level of protection.
Key Takeaway: A Special Warranty Deed is best suited for sellers who wish to provide title assurances only for the period during which they owned the property, offering a balance between guaranteeing a clear title during their ownership without assuming responsibility for the property's entire history.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Who is responsible for administering real estate license law in South Carolina?
option_a: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission
option_b: The Department of Housing and Urban Development
option_c: The South Carolina Human Affairs Commission
option_d: The South Carolina Association of REALTORS®
feedback: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission oversees administration of the real estate license law for the state, whereas the Association of REALTORS® is a trade association, the Human Affairs Commission responds to fair housing complaints, and the Department of Housing and Urban Development is a federal agency that oversees housing issues.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following comes under the authority of the South Carolina Real Estate Commission?
option_a: Administering the real estate licensing exams at established test centers
option_b: Creating the real estate licensing examinations for the state
option_c: Making and enforcing the rules to which real estate licensees are subject
option_d: Enacting the laws governing the actions of real estate licensees
feedback: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission only has the authority to make and enforce the rules that real estate licensees must follow. The legislature enacts law, a testing service writes exam questions (which the commission then reviews), and an independent testing service administers the examinations.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following reasons may NOT serve as the basis for the South Carolina Real Estate Commission to investigate a licensee?
option_a: Based on the director's reasonable concern that the licensee may not be fit to practice
option_c: Based on a written complaint regarding a cooperative commission arrangement
option_d: Based on a written complaint from a dissatisfied customer
feedback: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission may choose to investigate a licensee based on its own initiative, a complaint from a disgruntled customer, or a reasonable concern that the licensee may be unfit to practice; however, the Commission may not become embroiled in commission disputes.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: Which of the following items is paid in advance?
option_a: A special assessment for a sidewalk
option_b: A commission to a real estate broker
option_d: Interest on a home equity loan
feedback: Hazard and flood insurance premiums are typical items paid in advance

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following people needs to be licensed as a real estate broker in the state of South Carolina?
option_a: Sal Conti is an attorney-at-law who is acting under a power of attorney to convey real estate.
option_b: Jane Santi and Jack Winston are business partners who are selling an office building they own jointly.
option_d: Stephen Douglas employs fewer than three agents in his enterprise
feedback: Anyone who employs even one agent must be licensed, but an individual acting under power of attorney to sign a principal's name and partners selling their own real estate need not be licensed. Duplex owners are specifically exempt from the licensing requirement.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: An individual need NOT be licensed as a real estate agent to perform which of the following activities?
option_a: Employ licensees to perform real estate activities
option_c: Get paid for helping someone find a buyer for their home
option_d: To resell a personal manufactured home
feedback: Reselling a personal manufactured home may be accomplished without a license, but an individual must be licensed as a real estate agent or property manager in order to sell real estate in exchange for payment, employ licensees to perform real estate activities, and to negotiate listings.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following professionals does NOT need to hold a real estate license in order to perform real estate activities in the scope of their professional duties?
option_c: An attorney whose client requires a real estate transaction
feedback: An attorney may perform real estate activities without a license if the transaction is within the scope of duties for the client, but appraisers and auctioneers must have a real estate license to engage in real estate activities and a resident apartment manager must hold either a real estate or property manager's license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Vera, an office manager for Roadside Realty, performs the following activities on a routine basis: managing the flow of paperwork through the office, preparing forms and advertising copy, and hiring and supervising clerical staff. Which of the following is true in these circumstances?
option_a: Vera is exempt from real estate licensing requirements.
option_b: Vera is required to have a salesperson's license to perform such duties.
option_c: Vera is required to have a broker's license to perform such duties.
option_d: Vera is violating the license law.
feedback: Since Vera is performing non-real estate clerical and office management duties, she is exempt from licensing requirements.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is true regarding applications for any real estate license in South Carolina?
option_a: A completed application must be submitted before a candidate is eligible to sit for the written licensing exam
option_b: A photo of the applicant must be attached to the application
option_c: All applications must be received by May 31 of a calendar year in order to be considered during that year
option_d: The application must be accompanied by a sworn statement testifying to the applicant's character
feedback: A license application must be submitted before a candidate can take the written exam, which is given throughout the year. Neither a photo nor a sworn character statement needs to accompany the application, although a fingerprint-based criminal background check will be required during the application process.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: All of the following are requirements an applicant must meet for a real estate license in South Carolina EXCEPT:
option_b: Completion of at least two years of college
option_c: Proof of passing score on the license examination within the past calendar year
feedback: An applicant does not need to show evidence of any college work as a prerequisite for obtaining a real estate license but must be at least 18 years of age, show proof of a passing score on the licensing examination (which is valid for one year), and complete a criminal background check using the SC Real Estate Commission's online criminal background check service.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the term of a real estate salesperson's license in South Carolina?
option_a: A two-year cycle that ends on June 30 in the designated year
option_b: Two years, expiring on the date of the licensee's birthday in the appropriate year
option_c: Two years from the date of issuance
option_d: Two years, expiring on December 31 in the appropriate year
feedback: All licenses have been divided (by the commission) into a two-year cycle and expire on June 30 every two years.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Anne Conti's real estate license will expire on June 30 of next year. By what date must she renew in order to avoid a monetary penalty?
option_a: Within 1 calendar year of the date of expiration
option_b: By the date of expiration; there is no grace period
option_c: Within 30 days of the date of expiration
option_d: Within 10 business days of the date of expiration
feedback: There is no grace period for renewal without monetary penalty after the license expires, so Anne must renew her license by June 30 of next year to avoid having to pay a penalty.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true of the licensing and duties of personal real estate assistants in South Carolina?
option_a: Any personal real estate assistant may insert factual details into form contracts with the supervising broker's oversight and approval.
option_b: Any personal real estate assistant must be licensed to work with a supervising broker.
option_c: Any personal real estate assistant must be unlicensed because only salespeople and brokers can hold licenses.
option_d: Any personal real estate assistant may show houses for their supervising broker.
feedback: Personal real estate assistants may be either licensed or unlicensed, depending on the range of duties to be performed. While unlicensed individuals are limited to ministerial duties and licensed individuals may show houses and perform more services for their supervising brokers, all personal real estate assistants may insert factual information into form contracts as long as this is done under the supervision of the employing broker.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What paperwork is a nonresident licensee required to file with the South Carolina Real Estate Commission?
option_c: A copy of his or her birth certificate
feedback: A nonresident licensee must file an irrevocable consent to suit with the South Carolina Real Estate Commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If an on-site property manager for an apartment complex is responsible for negotiating and signing apartment leases, does the individual need to be licensed as a real estate salesperson?
option_a: No, all on-site managers are exempt from licensing requirements
option_b: No, as long as they work directly under the supervision of the owner
option_c: Yes, on-site property managers must hold a real estate salesperson license
option_d: Yes, or a real estate property manager license
feedback: An on-site manager who negotiates and signs leases must have a real estate salesperson license, broker license, or property manager license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following individuals is not exempt from licensing law or requirements while engaging in property management activity?
option_a: An innkeeper arranging transient lodging at his inn.
option_b: A limited partner in a limited partnership who is acting under a separate written property management agreement to rent the limited partnership's real property
option_c: A hotel representative in the business of arranging transient lodging in a hotel or inn.
option_d: A railroad company arranging transient lodging at a hotel or inn in the course of its business.
feedback: The arrangement of transient lodging is generally exempt from real estate licensing laws, though it can vary state-by-state. A limited partner operating under a written property management agreement would most likely require a real estate license (but could also vary state-by-state).

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following individuals is not exempt from the South Carolina License Act?
option_a: Todd leases apartments within a building he owns
option_b: Anne earns bonuses for recruiting prospective buyers and renters of real estate
option_c: Carol is a resident tenant who works part-time as a secretary
option_d: Selwyn is a resident maintenance worker and grounds person for a large apartment complex
feedback: Unless an individual engages in an activity that requires a license, such as procuring prospective renters or buyers for real estate, the individual need not be licensed and is exempt from the provisions of the Act. NOTE: This question is asking who is NOT EXEMPT (would need to be licensed). All incorrect answers would be EXEMPT from needing a license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Vivian decides to put her house on the market by herself. Is she required to have a real estate license?
option_a: No, as long as she is a licensed attorney, she may sell her house without a license.
option_b: No, she does not need a real estate license to sell her own property.
option_c: Yes, a temporary license is required in order to legally sell her own house.
option_d: Yes, anyone who sells real property in South Carolina must first have a valid real estate license.
feedback: Vivian does not need a real estate or attorney's license to sell her own property; individuals only need to be licensed as real estate salespersons or brokers if they intend to sell other people's property.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When do real estate licenses expire?
option_d: Never; real estate licenses in South Carolina are granted in perpetuity
feedback: South Carolina licenses expire every other year on June 30.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which activity requires licensure as a real estate salesperson or broker?
option_a: Acting as the trustee of an estate
feedback: Individuals may buy, sell, or construct their own homes or act as a trustee without a license, but licensure is required for anyone who engages in real estate activities, such as auctioning farm land.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Who is exempt from the continuing education requirements for license renewal in South Carolina?
option_a: Sue, a 65 year-old licensee who has been active in the real estate business for over 30 years.
option_b: Leslie, a licensee who works for a referral company.
option_c: Eric, who completed his post license requirements for his first renewal and is going to renew his license for the second time.
option_d: Andrew, an inactive licensee who wishes to change to active status.
feedback: Sue, when she reached the age of 65 and a minimum of 25 years of licensure, became exempt from having to complete continuing education requirements.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What limitations are placed on a licensee on inactive status?
option_a: The licensee may not conduct any real estate activities while on inactive status.
option_b: The licensee may work part time as an assistant showing houses but may not be directly involved in real estate sales.
option_c: The licensee may work as a leasing agent for a property management company but may not be directly involved in real estate sales.
option_d: The licensee may receive compensation for referrals but may not be directly involved in real estate sales.
feedback: An inactive licensee is not permitted to conduct any real estate activities. Since receiving compensation for referrals, working as a leasing agent at a property management company, and showing houses are all considered to be real estate activities, they may not be performed by someone who is on inactive status.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: George finds a buyer for a relative's property and earns a commission from the sale. While depositing his check, George realizes he forgot to renew his real estate license 3 months ago. What penalty is he likely to incur?
option_a: A five-year ban on applying for a real estate license
option_b: None, because there is a legal relationship between George and the seller
option_c: A maximum fine of $50,000, one year in prison, or both
option_d: A maximum fine of $500, six months in prison, or both
feedback: Per Section 40-57-780 of the license law, a real estate broker, salesperson, or property manager who fails to renew or register their license and continues to engage in real estate business is guilty of a misdemeanor and, upon conviction, must be fined not more than five hundred dollars or imprisoned not more than six months, or both.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: All of the following are requirements a broker-in-charge license applicant must meet in South Carolina EXCEPT:
option_a: Completion of education requirements dictated by the commission -- except as allowed by exemption
option_b: At least three years' experience as a licensed salesperson
option_c: Completion of all requirements for the property manager-in-charge license as a substitution for the broker-in-charge licensing requirements
feedback: The property manager-in-charge license is not identical to the broker-in-charge license, so requirements for the former cannot be substituted for the latter. Some exemptions for related education and experience are available for the broker-in-charge license, but applicants must generally be at least 21 years old and have been licensed for at least three years.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is true regarding a property manager licensee's supervisor?
option_a: The property manager may work under one property manager-in-charge during the business week and a separate property manager-in-charge on weekends.
option_b: The property manager may be licensed with either a broker-in-charge or a property manager-in-charge -- but not both at the same time.
option_c: The property manager may be licensed under both a broker-in-charge and a property manager-in-charge simultaneously.
option_d: The property manager may be licensed only under a property manager-in-charge.
feedback: The property manager may only be licensed with one person at a time -- either a broker-in-charge or a property manager-in-charge -- and that person must be the same supervisor throughout the course of the week and weekend.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A real estate office/company's policy in South Carolina must include which of the following elements?
feedback: Every office/company policy in South Carolina must include a policy on fair housing.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Regarding a broker-in-charge in South Carolina, what is true of his or her employment?
option_a: He or she is required to have at least a twenty-five percent interest in the company.
option_b: He or she may oversee more than one office only with commission approval.
option_c: He or she may oversee two offices within a 25-mile radius without commission approval.
option_d: He or she must have been licensed as a broker for at least 5 years.
feedback: Only with the commission's approval may a licensee be broker-in-charge of more than one office at a time, and there is no minimum experience requirement or specific ownership interest for a broker-in-charge.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The Seddon family bought the largest house… its value rose less. This is an example of:
feedback: The principle of regression is the reason people are cautioned against owning the most expensive house in the neighborhood. Lower priced homes pull down the value of higher-end properties.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If a broker-in-charge becomes mentally incapacitated, what effect does this have on his or her real estate company?
option_a: An associate licensee may serve as the broker-in-charge for up to six months with approval of the commission.
option_b: The company may not continue business until a new broker-in-charge is selected.
option_c: The company may not accept any new clients but may continue to work with existing clients.
option_d: An associate licensee may serve as the broker-in-charge for up to 90 days before being required to obtain a broker-in-charge license.
feedback: With the commission's approval, another salesperson may serve as the company's broker-in-charge for up to six months when the named broker-in-charge becomes mentally incapacitated.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How long is a broker-in-charge required to maintain leases and management agreements?
option_b: for a minimum of three years
option_d: for a minimum of five years
feedback: A broker-in-charge must maintain records of leases and management agreements for five years.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Vera paid the required renewal fee for her salesperson's license but has not completed the mandatory continuing education requirements. What action may the South Carolina Real Estate Commission take?
option_a: Renew the license and charge a $25/month fee until Vera completes the mandatory continuing education
option_b: Renew the license, giving Vera a grace period of 30 days to complete her continuing education
option_c: Return Vera's license renewal fee and refuse to renew the license
option_d: Place Vera's license on inactive status without penalty until she completes the continuing education requirement
feedback: Since Vera has paid the fee, the commission may place her license on inactive status, without penalty, until she fulfills the mandatory continuing education requirements.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Shaun is a real estate broker who wishes to become a property manager. Which of the following statements applies to Shaun?
option_a: He may manage property with his real estate broker's license
option_b: He must attend a 45-hour property manager course
option_c: He must be a broker-in-charge to manage property
option_d: He must obtain a property manager's license to reinforce his real estate license
feedback: Shaun may manage property with his valid real estate broker's license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How long does a broker-in-charge have to deposit cash received as earnest money?
feedback: Per Section 40-57-136 of South Carolina Real Estate License Law, "Trust funds received by a broker-in-charge in a real estate sales or exchange transaction must be deposited as follows in a separate real estate trust account: (i) cash or certified funds must be deposited within forty-eight hours of receipt (excluding Saturday, Sunday, and bank holidays); (ii) checks must be deposited within forty-eight hours after written acceptance of an offer by the parties to the transaction (excluding Saturday, Sunday, and bank holidays)."

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Bob, a broker for the Browns (the buyers), delivers an offer to purchase to the listing broker, Leslie. The seller is out of town on business and will not see the offer for a week. How soon must Leslie deposit the earnest money check into the escrow account?
option_b: Within 48 hours of ratifying the contract
option_d: Within 24 hours of ratifying the contract
feedback: Since the Browns' earnest money is in the form of a check, Leslie has until 48 hours after ratification of the contract to deposit the check into the escrow account.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following would NOT be grounds for revoking a broker's license in South Carolina?
option_a: Depositing earnest money into a personal checking account
option_d: Guaranteeing future profit from the resale of property
feedback: Since commission rates are always negotiable between the seller and the broker, a generous commission does not constitute grounds for revocation of a broker's license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When does the South Carolina Real Estate Commission have the power to revoke a salesperson's license?
option_a: When he/she commingles earnest money with personal funds
option_b: When he or she enters an exclusive-listing contract
option_c: When he/she receives the employer's consent to represent a real estate broker other than his or her employer
option_d: When he or she attempts to represent a buyer
feedback: Commingling earnest money with personal money is prohibited and can result in revocation of a salesperson's license. Representing another broker with consent, representing a buyer, and entering an exclusive-listing agreement are all acceptable practices.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following actions may a licensee legally perform?
option_a: Arrange for a buyer client to pay the commission on a sale
option_b: Encourage a seller to reject a buyer's offer based on the fact that the buyer is an Muslim
option_c: Delay the closing on a transaction until the terms of the commission are settled
option_d: Place a "For Sale" ad in a local circular before an anticipated listing is confirmed
feedback: Placing a "For Sale" ad without a confirmed listing, delaying a closing due to commission disputes, and encouraging a seller to reject an offer based on religious grounds are all actions in violation of the license law; however, arranging for a buyer to pay the commission on a sale is acceptable as long as the buyer agrees.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Henry, a broker, reports a property sales price to a lender that is above the actual sales price. Is Henry in violation of the license law?
option_a: No, as long as the inflated price is no greater than the estimated closing costs for the property; this will enable the buyers to have a loan that covers all their costs.
option_b: No, as long as he does so with the buyer's consent for the purpose of helping the buyer obtain a comprehensive loan.
option_c: Yes, because this constitutes false reporting of information.
option_d: Yes, if the inflated price is greater than ten percent of the actual sales price
feedback: Reporting a sales price to a lender that is above the actual sales price constitutes reporting false information and may result in a fine, imprisonment, and/or revocation of license for Henry.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What step must precede the placement of a "For Sale" sign on a listed property?
option_a: The broker must first get permission from the owner.
option_b: The broker must first get the consent of the multiple-listing service.
option_c: The broker must first get permission from the neighbors.
option_d: The broker must first sell the property.
feedback: Before placing a "For Sale" sign on a property, the broker must have the owner's consent and the confirmed listing.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What information must a real estate licensee include in the advertisement of real property?
option_a: The licensee's box number or street address
option_c: The name of the real estate company in whose name the ads are placed
option_d: A telephone number to call for more information
feedback: All advertising for real property must be done in the name of the employing real estate company; no other information, particularly the identification of the property owner, need be given.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best describes the relationship between price and value in the market system?
option_a: Value is based on the interaction of underlying economic factors; price is a quantification of value in a transaction
option_b: Value and price are identical at any given moment
option_c: Value by definition is the price one paid for an item, adjusted for time and costs paid in addition to price
option_d: Value is an estimate of the cost of an item; price is an estimate of what one will pay for the item
feedback: A price is the amount of money or other asset that a buyer has agreed to pay and a seller has agreed to accept to complete the exchange of a good or service. It is a quantification of the value of an item traded. Price is not something of value in itself. It is only a number that quantifies value. The economic issue underlying the interplay of supply and demand is, how do trading parties arrive at the value of a good or service as indicated by the price?

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best describes the legality of net listings?
option_a: Net listings are legal because they provide maximum profit on collectible commissions.
option_b: Net listings are legal only with the advance approval of the commission.
option_c: Net listings are illegal in South Carolina.
option_d: Net listings are only legal when the seller gives consent
feedback: A net listing is a listing which provides that the agent may retain, as compensation for his services all sums received over and above a net price to the owner. Net listings are illegal in South Carolina because they introduce a potential conflict of interest for the broker.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In addition to the influences of demand and the underlying determinants of value, real estate supply responds to
feedback: In addition to the influences of demand and the underlying determinants of value, real estate supply responds to the following: development costs, particularly labor, availability of financing, investment returns

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Andrew, a broker, accepted a listing from a seller who said that he wanted to clear $70,000 on the sale of his house. Andrew then sold the home for $180,000, gave $70,000 to the seller, and kept the remaining $110,000 as commission. Which of the following is true in this situation?
option_a: Andrew should have given the seller a better appraisal of the value of his property when accepting the listing.
option_b: Andrew accepted an illegal net listing.
option_c: As the seller's agent, Andrew had the duty to sell the property for the highest possible value.
option_d: Andrew's $110,000 commission exceeds the recommended guidelines.
feedback: Andrew accepted an illegal net listing which, in this case, interfered with his ability to work in the seller's best interests.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The primary forms of government influence on real estate markets are
option_c: national economic cycles and national trends
feedback: Governments at every level exert significant influence over local real estate markets. The primary forms of government influence are: local zoning power, local control and permitting of new development, local taxing power, federal influence on interest rates

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How are commissions governed in the state of South Carolina?
option_a: Acceptable guidelines are published by the South Carolina Real Estate Commission.
option_b: Commissions are always negotiable between the broker and the client
option_c: Commissions are determined by local groups of brokers in each congressional district.
option_d: Commissions are set by law based on a percentage of the sale price.
feedback: Commissions are always fully negotiable and are not set by law or local custom.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements about commissions is false?
option_a: Commissions may be paid by either the buyer, the seller, or both depending on the agreement reached
option_b: Commissions may not be shared with an unlicensed individual
option_c: Commissions are determined by mutual agreement between the broker and the principal
option_d: Commissions may be shared with any individual, licensed or unlicensed, who assisted in bringing the buyer and the seller together
feedback: Commissions may never be shared with an unlicensed individual who assisted in bringing the buyer and seller together, as this is a real estate act that requires a license to perform. However, commissions may be shared with unlicensed individuals in the form of client rebates. Commissions may be paid by either the buyer or the seller and are always fully negotiable between the broker and the principal. NOTE - This question asks "Which of the following statements... is false" which means it's looking for a false answer, and it's false that commissions may be shared with unlicensed individuals who assisted in bringing the buyer and seller together.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements defines “base employment”?
option_a: The percentage of persons employed in a given area
option_b: The percentage of persons employed in their field of study
option_c: The number of persons employed in the businesses that represent the economic foundation of the area
option_d: The unemployment rate in an economically deprived area
feedback: Base employment is the number of persons employed in the businesses that represent the economic foundation of the area. For example, the auto industry has traditionally been the primary base employer of the Detroit metropolitan area

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Real estate supply responds to
feedback: In addition to the influences of demand and the underlying determinants of value, real estate supply responds to: powers and regulation development costs, particularly labor, availability of financing, investment returns, a community's master plan, government police

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: In keeping with South Carolina mandates, a broker who has received earnest money from a buyer should take which of the following steps?
option_a: Establish an escrow account specifically for that transaction.
option_b: Secure the earnest money in a safe place at the broker's place of business until the closing.
option_c: Deposit the earnest money into the broker's personal account immediately (or by the next day) as partial payment of the commission.
option_d: Deposit the money into an existing escrow account established to hold earnest money deposits from all clients.
feedback: Earnest money must be deposited into an escrow account established for the purpose of holding earnest money from all buyers. Commingling funds is illegal.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is true of an account established to hold money belonging to others?
option_a: The account must be labeled as "escrow" or "trust" on all checks, deposits slips, and bank statements
option_b: All records of the account must be labeled both "trust" and escrow."
option_c: Each transaction must have a separate escrow account.
option_d: The account must be in a different bank than the broker's personal checking account.
feedback: The account may be labeled either "trust" or "escrow," and the broker may open the account in any bank as long as it is separate from all personal accounts.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Ralph Milton, a broker, manages three separate properties (owned by three individuals): The Landing, Berry Lane, and White River Complex. The Landing needs emergency repairs, but there is not enough money in The Landing's account to cover the costs. May Ralph temporarily borrow money from the escrow account of one of the other properties he manages?
option_a: Yes, since he is the managing agent for all three properties, he may transfer funds between escrow accounts on a temporary basis, as needed.
option_b: No, by doing so Ralph is violating regulations governing the proper handling of escrow funds.
option_c: No, Ralph must use personal funds if there is not enough money in the designated escrow account.
option_d: Yes, as long as there is a minimum of $1,000 left in the account and Ralph returns the funds borrowed within ten business days.
feedback: Ralph has violated state law by using one client's escrow account funds to cover the costs of repairs to another client's property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The amount of money or other assets that a buyer has agreed to pay and a seller has agreed to accept to complete the exchange of a good or service is defined as that good’s or service’s
feedback: A price is the amount of money or other asset that a buyer has agreed to pay and a seller has agreed to accept to complete the exchange of a good or service. It is a quantification of the value of an item traded

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What piece of information is NOT required in a listing agreement?
option_a: An adequate description of the property to be sold
option_b: Recommended sales price of the property
option_d: An automatic rollover clause that extends the length of the contract upon expiration (unless the seller provides written notice of cancellation to the agent)
feedback: A written listing agreement must include the time duration of the listing, a proposed sales price, and an adequate description of the property, but an automatic rollover provision that extends the duration of the listing is not permitted.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following disclosures need NOT be included in a listing contract according to South Carolina law?
option_a: An accounting of the agent's duties
option_b: The amount of compensation the agent will receive for the transaction
option_c: The net price the seller will receive at closing
option_d: When applicable, the compensation that will be paid to cooperating brokers
feedback: A listing agreement must detail the agent's duties, the compensation the agent will receive, and (when applicable) the compensation that will be paid to cooperating brokers; the seller's net return, however, is not included in the listing agreement.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What action must a broker or licensed salesperson take upon receiving a listing for a property?
option_a: Prepare a legible, signed, true, and correct copy of the listing for the person or persons signing it
option_b: Cooperate with other real estate offices that wish to market the property
option_c: Create a listing file with a unique number in compliance with the regulations established by the commission
option_d: Publicize the listing through advertisements in local newspapers
feedback: The one step a broker must take upon obtaining a listing is to provide the person or persons signing the listing with a legible, signed, true, and correct copy of the listing agreement.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When individuals believe they have been the targets of illegal discrimination, what legal redress do they have?
option_a: They may take their complaint to the Human Affairs Commission or HUD.
option_b: They may take their complaint to the Association of REALTORS®
option_c: They may file a complaint with the South Carolina Real Estate Commission.
option_d: They may file a complaint with the Attorney General of the state.
feedback: People who believe they may have the victims of illegal discrimination may take their complaints to the Human Affairs Commission or HUD.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Kyle listed his house with Latham Realty on June 15, and the termination date specified in the listing was August 15. After one month, Kyle decided to remove his house from the market. Which of the following best describes the situation?
option_a: Kyle has signed a listing agreement that legally requires him to leave his house on the market until the specified termination date in August.
option_b: Kyle has canceled the agreement and will not incur any penalties.
option_c: Kyle has withdrawn the realty's authority to sell the property and may be subject to a penalty.
option_d: Kyle's action will be reviewed by the South Carolina Real Estate Commission to determine if it is acceptable.
feedback: Since Kyle has canceled the listing prior to the agreed-upon termination date, he may be responsible for some of the realty's expenses in attempting to sell the property. The Real Estate Commission will not be involved in settling the matter, however.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: From whom may a South Carolina real estate salesperson legally collect compensation?
option_a: From a licensed real estate broker
option_b: From his or her employing broker
option_c: From any principal in a transaction
feedback: A licensed real estate salesperson may only accept lawful compensation from his or her employing broker.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Under which circumstances is it permissible for a licensed salesperson to hold a concurrent license with more than one South Carolina broker?
option_a: Concurrent licensure is permissible only with the written consent of the South Carolina Real Estate Commission.
option_c: Concurrent licensure is perfectly acceptable and requires no special permission.
option_d: Concurrent licensure is acceptable only with the written consent of the brokers being represented.
feedback: Salespersons may only be licensed under one employing broker.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Alice is a broker-in-charge whose license has been suspended for two years. Which of the following is true of the associate brokers and salespeople who are licensed under Alice?
option_a: Their licenses will be revoked, pending reinstatement of Alice's license.
option_b: Alice's suspension has no effect on the licenses of associate brokers and salespeople.
option_c: Their licenses will also be suspended for two years.
option_d: Their licenses must be returned to the real estate commission.
feedback: The licenses of all associate brokers and salespeople working under Alice must be returned to the commission and placed on inactive status until the licensees find a new qualified employing broker.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Andrew Belkin, the broker-in-charge of Belkin Downtown Realty, decides to open a branch office in an adjacent community. Andrew applies for a branch office license for the new Belkin Suburban Realty office and names one of his associate licensees as the branch office manager. Given these facts, will Andrew receive approval for the branch office?
option_a: Yes, as long as the real estate commission agrees to allow Andrew to be broker-in-charge of two offices.
option_b: No, because a broker-in-charge may only oversee one office at a time.
option_c: No, because brokers are not allowed to have branch offices in multiple municipalities.
option_d: Yes, because the branch office name clearly identifies its relationship with the main office.
feedback: Since each office must have a broker-in-charge, Andrew must demonstrate his ability to fulfill the duties and responsibilities of the position for two separate locations; then, if the commission grants approval, Andrew will be granted the branch office license.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Carol Powers is an associate broker who has decided to transfer from her present real estate company to a partnership with her best friend, who is a broker. What must she do before she can begin actively selling in the new partnership?
option_a: Carol's present employing broker must transfer Carol's associate broker's license to her new partner.
option_b: Carol must take her license to her best friend's brokerage and notify the real estate commission within three days of the transfer.
option_c: Carol's new partner must notify the real estate commission of the change and remit the proper forms and fees for the transfer.
option_d: Carol must notify her current Broker-in-Charge that she's leaving and fill out an online Transfer/Inactive Status Form.
feedback: Before Carol can start selling, she must notify her current Broker-in-Charge that she's leaving and fill out the online form provided by the Real Estate Commission. This notifies the new Broker-in-Charge via email and doesn't require them to sign anything. If a license can't be transferred online, Carol will need to fill out a Transfer/Inactive Status Application and have her new Broker-in-Charge sign it, then send it to the Real Estate Commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How long does a broker have to notify the commission of a change in place of business?
feedback: When a licensed broker changes their place of business, they must notify the South Carolina Real Estate Commission within 10 days per Section 40-57-135 of SC license law.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A broker is required to perform all of the following actions in any real estate transaction except which one?
option_a: Ensure that the closing statement is accurate and that a copy of it is delivered to all parties in a transaction
option_b: Give an opinion about the condition of the seller's title to the property
option_c: Maintain records of all relevant documents for five years after the closing
option_d: Verify that the written purchase and sale agreement contains all the terms of the parties' agreement
feedback: A broker may not give a personal opinion of the condition of the seller's title to the property because doing so may constitute an unauthorized practice of law.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following will not necessarily lead to the payment of a commission?
option_a: Binding contract between seller and buyer
option_c: Closing of the title by buyer
feedback: When a willing buyer and a willing seller enter a binding contract and close on a property, the salesperson or broker is generally entitled to a commission. Simply having a signed listing agreement, though, does not guarantee a commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is required of every South Carolina real estate office?
option_a: Employment of at least one licensee
option_b: Adequate signage at the office location
option_c: An archive of complete escrow and transaction records for five years
feedback: Every South Carolina real estate office is required to maintain files of escrow accounts and transaction records for five years.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When is an individual considered legally competent in South Carolina?
feedback: Age 18 is the age of legal competence in South Carolina.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What type of agreement need not be detailed in writing?
feedback: Leases for terms of less than a year need not be in writing to be enforceable.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Carl Lorraine apartment complex has an established policy prohibiting pets. Anne, a young woman with a visual impairment who owns a guide dog, wishes to rent a unit in Carl Lorraine. Must the landlord make a concession to the "no pets" policy for Anne?
option_a: According to the South Carolina Fair Housing Act, the landlord may not prohibit a guide dog but may impose restrictions on the weight of the dog if Anne is to rent the unit.
option_b: In this case, according to the nondiscrimination provision of the South Carolina Fair Housing Act, the landlord must make a concession for Anne's guide dog and rent the unit to Anne.
option_c: As long as the landlord applies the "no pets" policy uniformly and in a nondiscriminatory manner, the landlord may oppose the guide dog and refuse to rent the unit.
option_d: Guide dogs are not specifically addressed within the South Carolina Fair Housing Act, so the landlord need not rent the unit to Anne.
feedback: South Carolina Fair Housing Act specifically prohibits landlords from refusing to rent units to people with visual impairments on the basis of a "no pets" policy and also prohibits landlords from establishing a weight limit for guide dogs.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following groups is NOT a protected class under the South Carolina Fair Housing Law?
feedback: The Fair Housing Act mirrors federal legislation which does not include sexual orientation as a protected class.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: For which of the following actions can a licensee have their license revoked?
option_a: For failing to disclose information about a murder on the site of a property to a potential buyer
option_b: For neglecting to inform a tenant that their security deposit will be held by the property owner rather than the management company
option_c: For arrest for possession of a controlled substance
option_d: For awarding compensation to a clerk to type contracts
feedback: A licensee may have their license revoked if they fail to inform a tenant as to who will hold the security deposit or if they are convicted (not just arrested) of a drug offense.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Oscar sends his license renewal fee to the commission but does not have enough funds in his bank account to cover the fee. What action may the South Carolina Real Estate Commission take in this instance?
option_a: The commission must immediately cancel Oscar's license for failure to remit the appropriate renewal fee.
option_b: The commission may take disciplinary action against Oscar.
option_c: The commission must notify Oscar of the NSF and allow him an extension of 30 days to remit the appropriate fee by cashier's check or money order.
option_d: The commission may require Oscar's broker-in-charge to pay the renewal fee.
feedback: The commission may take disciplinary action against Oscar since his license renewal fee was returned for insufficient funds.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Who receives the interest that accrues on a buyer's earnest money deposit?
option_b: Earnest monies must be deposited in non-interest-bearing accounts.
option_c: If interest earned is greater than ten percent of the earnest money deposit, it is payable to the buyer; otherwise, the broker receives the interest.
option_d: The broker receives the interest if the buyer has agreed in writing.
feedback: When earnest monies are deposited in interest-bearing accounts, the broker may keep any interest that accrues as long as the buyer has agreed to this in writing.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What happens to earnest monies in the case of a dispute between the buyer and the seller?
option_a: The broker may not disburse the funds until the dispute is settled.
option_b: Earnest monies are always returned to the buyer.
option_c: Earnest monies are always disbursed to the seller.
option_d: The broker must send the monies in question to the South Carolina Real Estate Commission for disbursement.
feedback: In the case of a dispute over the entitlement of earnest money, the broker must hold the funds in escrow until the dispute is settled by agreement, mediation, or court order.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A certain house has, in addition to the main house, an unfinished but heated basement where the family’s adult son lives; a finished but unheated attic with sloped ceilings are seven feet in height at the ridge, that is used as a child’s playroom; a two-car garage that is attached to the main house but not heated; and a large interior central stairwell providing access to the second floor. Which of these spaces would be considered part of Gross Living Area under ANSI and Fannie Mae standards?
feedback: Both stair and closets areas are generally fully countable in gross living area.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following statements is true when demand for properties exceeds supply?
option_a: The real estate prices will remain stable
option_b: The real estate values will begin to decrease
feedback: The availability of certain properties interacts with the strength of the demand for those properties to establish prices. When demand for properties exceeds supply, a condition of scarcity exists, and real estate values rise

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When supply and demand are generally equivalent, the market is in
option_a: balance and real estate values stabilize
option_b: unbalanced and real estate values increase
option_c: balanced and real estate values increase
option_d: unbalanced and real estate values stabilize
feedback: When supply and demand are generally equivalent, the market is considered to be in balance, and real estate values stabilize

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: By which of the following means would earnest money disputes NOT be settled?
option_a: By a written agreement signed by all parties
feedback: In the case of a dispute about earnest monies, a broker is not allowed to arbitrate the resolution of the situation.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission requires a broker to maintain all of the following records for trust accounts holding other people's money EXCEPT which one?
option_a: A running balance accounting of each entry
option_b: A monthly reconciliation for each entry
option_c: An accounting of the name of payee for each deposit in the account
option_d: An accounting of the check number of each deposit received
feedback: In maintaining a trust account, a broker is required to keep a detailed accounting of the running balance of each entry, the check number of each deposit received, and the name of the payee for each deposit but is not required to keep a monthly reconciliation for each entry.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Carly, a broker, realizes that her escrow account has no current deposits. She receives a letter from the bank warning her that the account requires a minimum balance of $100 to stay open. Can Carly deposit personal funds in the escrow account to keep it open?
option_a: No, she must appeal to the bank for a waiver of the minimum balance requirement.
option_b: No, this is considered commingling of funds which is illegal.
option_c: Yes, she may deposit personal funds up to a maximum of $500.
option_d: Yes, but she may only deposit the minimum required to keep the account open.
feedback: Carly may deposit a minimum amount of personal funds in the escrow account to keep it open -- in this case, $100.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is required by the South Carolina Real Estate Commission?
option_a: A broker-in-charge must establish a central escrow account for multiple branch offices.
option_b: A broker-in-charge must train only associate licensees in the proper handling of escrow funds.
option_c: A broker-in-charge must deposit cash earnest money into an escrow account within 24 hours of receiving the funds.
option_d: A broker-in-charge must keep back-up copies of all computerized escrow account records.
feedback: A broker-in-charge is required to maintain back-up copies of computerized accounts, to instruct both employees and associate licensees in the proper handling of escrow funds, and to deposit cash earnest money within 48 hours of receipt.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When Billie Smith, a broker, receives monies for rent on a property, what is she required to do?
option_a: Deposit the monies in her operating account
option_b: Deposit the monies in an escrow account
option_c: Disburse the monies to the landlord within 10 days of receipt
option_d: Retain the monies in an escrow account for at least 30 days after the deposit clears
feedback: When Billie receives monies for rent on a property, she must deposit these funds into an escrow account and disburse them to the property owner within a reasonable time after the deposit clears.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The Noonans have signed a contract to purchase a property, but they fail to qualify for a loan. What happens to their earnest money?
option_b: The funds are awarded to the seller
option_c: The funds are returned to the Noonans.
option_d: None of the other options are correct
feedback: Since the Noonans failed to qualify for a loan, their earnest money will be returned to them.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When is a classified ad considered illegal?
option_a: When it doesn't include a phone number
option_b: When it contains only a phone number
option_c: When it omits the property's address
option_d: When it lists the name of the company but not the individual broker
feedback: An ad that doesn't contain the name of the company is considered illegal by the South Carolina Real Estate Commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the purpose of the mandatory continuing education required by the South Carolina Real Estate Commission?
option_a: To advance the real estate profession
option_b: To meet the requirements of the National Association of REALTORS®
option_d: To reduce insurance payouts for errors and omissions
feedback: The South Carolina Real Estate Commission requires licensees to complete mandatory continuing education in order to fully protect the public.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The first step in the process of appraising a property or a real property interest is to
option_a: define the appraisal problem and the purpose of the appraisal
option_b: collect, organize, and analyze relevant data about the subject property
option_c: analyze market conditions to identify the most profitable use for the subject property
option_d: estimate the land value of the subject
feedback: The first step in the process is to define the appraisal problem and the purpose of the appraisal. This involves: identifying the subject property by legal description, specifying the interest to be appraised, specifying the purpose of the appraisal, for example, to identify market value for a purchase, identify rental levels, or establish a value as collateral for a loan, specifying the date for which the appraisal is valid, identifying the type of value to be estimated

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following approaches serves as the basis for a broker's opinion of value?
feedback: The sales comparison approach, also known as the market data approach, is used for almost all properties. It also serves as the basis for a broker's opinion of value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The sales comparison approach is limited in which of the following ways?
option_c: It cannot be used for vacant land
option_d: It contradicts the principle of substitution
feedback: The sales comparison approach is limited in that every property is unique. As a result, it is difficult to find exact comparables, especially for special-purpose properties

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements describes a weakness of the cost approach?
option_a: The cost to create improvements is always the same as market value
option_b: The cost to create improvements is not necessarily the same as market value
option_c: The cost approach is very accurate for a property with new improvements, which are the highest and best use of the property
option_d: The cost approach provides an upper limit for the subject’s value based on the undepreciated cost of reproducing the improvements
feedback: The limitations of the cost approach are that: the cost to create improvements is not necessarily the same as market value, depreciation is difficult to measure, especially for older buildings

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following best describes the regulations governing escrow accounts for a real estate company that has a property management division?
option_a: The company's agents must place rents in one escrow account and security deposits in a separate escrow account.
option_b: The company's agents must see that all rents are paid directly to the landlords and all security deposits held in escrow.
option_c: The company must maintain separate escrow accounts for each of its divisions.
option_d: The company must maintain at least one escrow account but need not have separate accounts for its sales and rental divisions.
feedback: Although separate accounts for each division are recommended, only one escrow account is required by law.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When a seller counteroffers on a buyer's written offer, which of the following is true?
option_a: Both parties need to initial and date any changes to the offer
option_b: A new written offer must be drawn up and submitted to the seller
option_c: Any changes to original offer need to be initialed by the buyer
option_d: Since the original contract is in written form, further negotiations can be verbal as long as official notes are kept
feedback: Sometimes counteroffers are completely new offers, but often they are modifications of a previous offer. When any changes are made to an offer or counteroffer, all parties must initial and date the changes to signify acceptance. This makes the counteroffer a new offer with new terms.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: In South Carolina, which of the following is a false statement about offers on a property?
option_a: They must stipulate all terms except the closing date (which is negotiable)
option_b: Ratified copies of the contract must be provided to all parties
option_c: Any changes made during the negotiation phase must be initialed and dated by all parties
option_d: They must be made in writing
feedback: Offers to purchase a property must contain all terms and conditions, including the closing date.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The cost approach is most often used for recently built properties where the actual costs of development and construction are known. It is also used for
feedback: The cost approach is most often used for recently built properties where the actual costs of development and construction are known. It is also used for special-purpose buildings which cannot be valued by the other methods because of lack of comparable sales or income data

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The cost of constructing, at current prices and using current materials and methods is called
feedback: Replacement cost is the cost of constructing, at current prices and using current materials and methods, a functional equivalent of the subject improvements

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A cornerstone of the cost approach is the concept of
feedback: A cornerstone of the cost approach is the concept of depreciation. Depreciation is the loss of value in an improvement over time. Since land is assumed to retain its value indefinitely, depreciation only applies to the improved portion of real property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is an example of functional obsolescence?
option_a: A property with design features that are no longer desirable
option_b: A property that has curable structural deterioration
option_c: A property located in a deteriorating neighborhood
option_d: A property suffering from normal wear and tear
feedback: Functional obsolescence occurs when a property has outmoded physical or design features that are no longer desirable to current users

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When developing company guidelines, which of the following actions must a broker take regarding the commission's guidelines for the agency and agency disclosure form?
option_a: A broker must create an office policy that identifies a minimum of three types of agency relationships the company will practice.
option_b: A broker must require that each buyer receive a copy of the completed seller agency disclosure form.
option_c: A broker must set company guidelines that are at least as stringent as the commission's guidelines.
option_d: A broker must create an office policy that commits to participation in cooperative sales.
feedback: Participation in cooperative sales is elective but must be identified. A minimum of one type of agency—and a maximum of four types of agency relationships—must be defined. A broker can require, optionally, that each buyer receive a copy of the completed seller agency disclosure form. In all cases of establishing company policy, the broker must set guidelines that are at least as stringent as the guidelines set by the commission.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The income approach is based on the principle of
feedback: The income capitalization approach, or income approach, is used for income properties and sometimes for other properties in a rental market where the appraiser can find rental data. The approach is based on the principle of anticipation: the expected future income stream of a property underlies what an investor will pay for the property. It is also based on the principle of substitution: that an investor will pay no more for a subject property with a certain income stream than the investor would have to pay for another property with a similar income stream

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Kelly is a South Carolina licensee on inactive status. What must she do if she wishes to remain on inactive status?
option_a: She must pay the required fees every two years.
option_b: She may only remain inactive for two years; after that she is required to reactivate her license or license will be revoked.
option_c: She must complete eight hours of continuing education every two years.
option_d: She need not do anything until she converts her license to active status.
feedback: In order to maintain inactive status, licensee Kelly must remit the required fees every two years.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Net operating income is
option_a: effective gross income minus total operating expenses
option_b: an estimate of the rate of return an investor will demand on the investment
option_c: potential gross income minus an allowance for vacancy and credit losses
option_d: the scheduled rent of the subject plus income from miscellaneous sources
feedback: Net operating income is effective gross income minus total operating expenses

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The duties of a broker-in-charge encompass all of the following EXCEPT which one?
option_a: Establishing daily contact with associated licensees
option_b: Assisting in the resolution of license disputes for associated licensees
option_c: Training associated licensees in the proper management of trust funds
option_d: Reviewing all agency agreements created by associated licensees
feedback: A broker-in-charge must maintain "reasonable and regular" contact with associated licensees, but this need not include daily contact.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser may estimate potential gross rental income using
feedback: Total potential rent can be derived by multiplying current rental rates for each type of unit in a complex times the number of units of each type.  Other income (laundry machines, vending, etc.) can be added to that number to derive total potential income

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The capitalization rate is
option_a: an estimate of the rate of return an investment property will appreciate
option_b: an estimate of the rate of return an investor will demand on the investment
option_d: the interest rate a lender will charge an investor
feedback: The capitalization rate is an estimate of the rate of return an investor will demand on the investment of capital in a property such as the subject.  A property’s cap rate can also be derived by dividing net income by its value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser obtains an indication of value from the income capitalization method by
option_a: dividing the gross income for the subject by the capitalization rate
option_b: dividing the net operating income for the subject by the capitalization rate
option_c: multiplying the net operating income for the subject by the capitalization rate
option_d: subtracting the net operating income for the subject by the capitalization rate
feedback: An appraiser obtains an indication of value from the income capitalization method by dividing the estimated net operating income for the subject by the selected capitalization rate

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The gross rent multiplier (GRM) is used primarily for which types of properties?
feedback: The gross rent multiplier (GRM) and gross income multiplier (GIM) approaches are simplified income-based methods used primarily for properties that produce or might produce income but are not primarily income properties. Examples are single-family homes and duplexes

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The advantage of the gross income multiplier (GRM) is that
option_a: it offers a relatively quick indication of value using an informal methodology
option_b: it considers variables such as vacancies
option_c: it considers variables such as operating expenses
option_d: it is more reliable than other methods
feedback: The advantage of the income multiplier is that it offers a relatively quick indication of value using an informal methodology. However, the approach leaves many variables out of consideration, such as vacancies, credit losses, and operating expenses

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following describes the formula used to calculate the gross income multiplier (GIM)?
option_a: GIM x Subject gross annual income = estimated value
option_b: Price divided by Gross annual income = GIM
option_c: Price divided by Gross monthly income = GIM
option_d: Effective gross income minus total operating expenses
feedback: The gross income multiplier for a property is: Price divided by Gross annual income = GIM

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When a licensee violates a provision of the license law, to which of the following penalties may he or she be subject?
option_a: Minimum license suspension of one month
feedback: A licensee who violates a provision of the license law may incur the following penalties at the discretion of the commission: a fine of $500 to $5000 after a hearing in front of the commission, revocation of license for a minimum of one year, or a restraining order issued at the request of the commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Selwyn Short is a salesperson in North Carolina, and he referred a cooperative sale to Deborah, an associate broker in South Carolina. May Deborah pay Selwyn a referral fee in these circumstances?
option_a: Yes, as long as the fee is paid through Selwyn's broker-in-charge.
option_b: Yes, as long as the amount is less than $1,500.
option_c: No, because Selwyn did not conduct any of the negotiations in South Carolina.
option_d: No, because payment of a referral fee is prohibited under South Carolina's licensing laws.
feedback: Deborah may pay a referral fee to Selwyn as long as the following conditions are met for the cooperative sale: payment must go through Selwyn's broker-in-charge, and Selwyn may not conduct any part of the negotiations in South Carolina. No minimum or maximum for referral fees has been established.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Carol is a new licensee. She is negotiating her first sale for a seller. Which of the following must she keep in mind as she receives offers for the property?
option_a: She must continue to seek offers on the property until the date of closing.
option_b: She must make sure all parties agree to any changes or modifications by drafting a new document for each modification.
option_c: She must decide which offers are acceptable and may be presented to the seller.
option_d: She must deliver ratified copies of the contract to all parties as soon as possible.
feedback: Complete copies of a ratified contract must be delivered to involved parties promptly after the signing. Carol must also present all offers to the seller and have all parties initial and date any changes or modifications to the offer. Once a property is under contract, a broker need not seek additional offers; any that are made, though, must be presented to the seller and may be used as a "backup" offer if all parties agree to that provision.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Sunnybrook is a quiet suburban township primarily populated by retirees. Gerald Hamm, a listing broker, is aware of a long-range township plan to construct a parkway adjacent to the site of his listing. Is he obligated to share this information with a potential buyer?
option_a: No, because this information is public knowledge and discoverable by the buyer.
option_b: Yes, because this a material fact that requires full disclosure.
option_c: Yes, but only if a question about long-term planning is posed by the prospective buyer.
option_d: No, unless he has a signed and executed agency agreement with the buyer.
feedback: Since this information could have a material impact on the purchase of the property, Gerald must fully disclose all known discoverable information to a prospective buyer regardless of whether or not the buyer asks.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Stephanie is the broker-in-charge for Big Hill Realty. Her workload is heavy and she recently had some associated licensees leave her company. She has been so busy that she forgot to renew her license by June 30. If she continues to engage in real estate transactions with an expired license, what penalties may she incur?
option_a: $500 fine and/or six months in prison upon conviction of practicing without a license
option_b: None, because the license remains active as long as she pays the renewal fee and penalty fees within six months
option_c: None, as long as she can prove that nonrenewal was due to hardship rather than malicious intent
option_d: $1,000 fine and/or six months in prison after an automatic hearing with the commission
feedback: If Stephanie practices without a license, regardless of the circumstances, she is committing a misdemeanor which is punishable by a $500 fine and/or six months in prison. There is no grace period after the expiration of a license; it lapses immediately. Reference (Section 40-57-780): https://www.scstatehouse.gov/code/t40c057.php

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: At what point in proceedings is a client relationship between broker and buyer formalized?
option_a: When the buyer shares confidential information, such as financial portfolio details
option_b: When the buyer seeking representation signs an agency disclosure form
option_c: When the broker shows prospective properties to the buyer
option_d: When the buyer and broker both sign an agency agreement
feedback: Signing of the buyer agency agreement formalizes the client relationship between buyer and broker. Although signing the agency disclosure form does not formally establish the relationship, the form should be signed before the buyer shares any confidential information or sees any properties.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following must occur when a salesperson leaves a company?
option_a: The broker-in-charge must provide a forwarding address to the MLS service within ten days.
option_b: The broker-in-charge must notify the South Carolina Real Estate Commission immediately.
option_c: The broker-in-charge is required to terminate all listing agreements held by that salesperson.
option_d: The broker-in-charge has no specific obligations in these circumstances.
feedback: It is incumbent upon a broker-in-charge to immediately notify the South Carolina Real Estate Commission and provide a forwarding address when a salesperson leaves the company. Since listing agreements are ultimately between the seller and the broker-in-charge, the salesperson's departure has no effect on listing agreements.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the intent of the South Carolina agency disclosure form?
option_a: To create an agency relationship with a client
option_b: To provide proof of representation to the other party
option_c: To adhere to the nondiscrimination provisions of the South Carolina Fair Housing law
option_d: To acknowledge the nature of the relationship requested by the individual
feedback: The agency disclosure form is a means to acknowledge what type of relationship a buyer or seller has requested with a broker. Copies of the form need not be given to the other party in a transaction, and only a written agency agreement can formalize an agency relationship.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Lakeside Realty has agency agreements with both Sue (a buyer) and the Zuckermans (sellers). Sue would like to make an offer on the Zuckerman's property; under what circumstances, if any, is this permitted.
option_a: Dual agency is never allowable in the state of South Carolina.
option_b: Since both parties have signed agency agreements, this is allowable.
option_c: This is only allowable if the seller has agreed to pay the commission.
option_d: This is only allowable if both parties give their consent to the dual agency.
feedback: If both Sue and the Zuckermans give informed written consent to the dual agency, Lakeside Realty may represent both parties regardless of who pays the commission.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Mary Lou Williams, a buyer, contacts a real estate office in Charleston because she is interested in relocating to the area and would like some help finding a house. Which of the following actions requires an agency agreement between Mary Lou and the real estate office?
option_a: The real estate office explains the different types of agency relationships that can exist between buyer and broker
option_b: The real estate office helps Mary Lou find a lender that prequalifies her for a mortgage
option_c: The real estate office gives Mary Lou printouts of houses that meet her specifications
option_d: The real estate office provides Mary Lou with current rates and terms for mortgages
feedback: In order to prequalify Mary Lou, the real estate office must receive confidential information, so this action requires that an agency relationship be established. Providing other types of information, though, such as listing details and current mortgage rates, does not require an agency relationship.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: South Carolina recognizes four types of agency relationships. Choose the set which indicates the acceptable forms of agency relationships in South Carolina.
feedback: South Carolina's agency disclosure form recognizes buyer's agency, seller's agency, disclosed dual agency, and designated agency relationships.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Under which circumstances may a broker collect compensation from both the seller and the buyer in the same transaction?
option_a: If both parties are represented by attorneys
option_b: If both parties give informed consent to the dual compensation
