The plan will also include a budget and indications of what the manager intends to do with the property to meet the owner's objectives

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: An owner of a rental property decided to permanently seal, cement, and repurpose the swimming pool area into a garden. This strategy for managing risk is called
feedback: Avoidance includes refraining from an activity that carries risk. One can avoid the risks of being in an automobile accident by not riding in automobiles

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Experience shows that the most practical strategies for risk management in real estate practice are reduction and transference, with procedures focusing on all the following EXCEPT
feedback: Experience shows that the most practical strategies for risk management in real estate practice are reduction and transference, with procedures focusing on: Education, Disclosure, Documentation, Insurance

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A standard Errors & Omissions (E&O) policy provides coverage for
option_a: damages resulting from a negligent act
feedback: The primary method for transferring the professional liability risks of brokers, managers, and licensees is Errors & Omissions (E&O) insurance. A standard E&O policy provides coverage for “damages resulting from any negligent act, error or omission arising out of Professional Services.”

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following provides coverage for the business losses resulting from a disaster, such as loss of rent and other revenue, when the property cannot be used for business?
option_c: Consequential loss, use, and occupancy insurance
feedback: The consequential loss, use, and occupancy insurance policy is used for business losses resulting from a disaster, such as loss of rent and other revenue, when the property cannot be used for business

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following provides evidence of compliance with laws and regulations?
feedback: Documentation provides evidence of compliance with laws and regulations. It proves what clients and customers and licensees said and did in a transaction. Some documentation is required by law

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Firms are required to keep written records of all real estate transactions for a number of years (usually three to five) after closing. Required records typically include all the following EXCEPT
feedback: Required records typically include: listing agreements, offers, contracts, closing statements, agency agreements, disclosure documents, correspondence and other communication records, notes and any other relevant information

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Hospital and medical coverage for employees injured in the course of employment is covered by
feedback: Workers’ compensation insurance covers hospital and medical coverage for employees injured in the course of employment

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: When licensees breach agency relationships, the penalties include
option_c: prevention of errors and omission insurance
feedback: When licensees breach agency relationships, possible penalties include rescission of transaction, loss of compensation, fees and costs, punitive damages, ethics discipline, and license discipline

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The purpose of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) is
option_b: to outline all the lender costs
option_d: to outline closing costs related to the purchase transaction
feedback: The purpose of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) is to give parties at certain purchase transactions accurate information reflecting their respective closing costs

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP)?
option_a: USPAP is a set of standards, guidelines, and accepted practices for the appraisal industry
option_b: USPAP established the criteria for appraisal certification
option_c: USPAP, in addition to appraisers, also regulates real estate licensees
option_d: USPAP is also known as Title XI
feedback: The Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP) is a set of standards, guidelines, and provisions for the appraisal industry

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the metes and bounds system?
option_a: A metes and bounds description must return to the point of beginning (POB)
option_b: The term metes refer to monuments and landmarks
option_c: The surveyor is not allowed to use artificial markers as stakes
option_d: The metes and bounds method is predominantly used in the Midwest
feedback: A metes and bounds description identifies the boundaries of a parcel of real estate using reference points, distances, and angles. The description always identifies an enclosed area by starting at an origination point, called point of beginning, or POB, and returning to the POB at the end of the description. A metes and bounds description must return to the POB in order to be valid

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the bundle of rights?
option_a: An owner cannot transfer subsurface rights without transferring air rights
option_b: An owner can transfer subsurface rights without transferring air rights
option_c: An owner cannot relinquish the right to own the subsurface rights
option_d: An owner can never divide the bundle of rights.
feedback: The bundle of real property rights also applies separately to the individual components of real estate: the air, the surface, and the subsurface. An owner can, for example, transfer subsurface rights without transferring air rights

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding investment properties?
option_a: Residential properties are not considered investment properties
option_b: Only commercial properties are considered to be investment properties
option_c: All classifications of property may be investment properties
option_d: Only properties that produce a positive cash flow are considered to be investment properties
feedback: Investment property refers to any property that is held by its owners for investment purposes. All classifications of property may be investment properties since all types are capable of generating rental income and/or tax savings

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding individual specialization in real estate?
option_a: One’s choice of specialization is influenced by competitive factors in the market and by perceived opportunities
option_b: Specialization in certain areas of real estate requires mandatory membership in the appropriate organization
option_c: One’s choice of specialization is regulated by the National Association of Realtors®.
option_d: Specialization in commercial transactions requires special certification in addition to licensure
feedback: A licensee’s choice of specialization is influenced by competitive factors in the market and by perceived opportunities. Since different properties have different features and potential buyers, brokers commonly choose to specialize in a property type

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding conflicts of interest?
option_a: Conflicts of interest arise when an agent fails to put the best interests of a client ahead of those of everyone else
option_b: Conflicts of interest do not occur with transactions involving licensees buying their own properties or selling broker-owned properties
option_c: Ordinary everyday transactions do not carry any built-in risk of being a conflict of interest
option_d: Licensees are exempt from any self-interest disclosures, so there is never a concern for conflict of interest
feedback: Conflicts of interest arise when an agent forgets to put the best interests of a client ahead of those of everyone else. This can happen in situations involving undisclosed dual agencies, broker-owned listings, licensees buying for their own account, vendor referrals, and property management subcontracting of services, among many others

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the National Association of Realtors’® Code of Ethics?
option_a: The Code of Ethics is regulated by state laws
option_b: The Code of Ethics sets an even higher standard of performance for its members
option_c: The Code of Ethics must be adhered to by all licensees regardless of membership
option_d: The Code of Ethics does not apply to real estate licensees
feedback: The National Association of Realtors® Code of Ethics not only reflects the law but sets an even higher standard of performance for member brokers and agents

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the provisions of a living trust?
option_a: A living trust allows the trustor, during his or­­ her lifetime, to convey title to a trustee
option_b: A living trust allows the trustor to convey title upon the death of the trustor
option_c: The trustee receives all income and sales proceeds
option_d: A living trust is the same as a will
feedback: A living trust allows the trustor, during his or­­ her lifetime, to convey title to a trustee for the benefit of a third party. The trustor charges the trustee with all necessary responsibilities for managing the property, protecting its value, and securing whatever income it may produce

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the provisions of a land trust?
option_a: In a land trust, the beneficiary can never be trustor
option_b: In a land trust, the beneficiary must be the trustee
option_c: The land trust applies only to real property, not to personal property
option_d: The land trust applies only to personal property, not to real property
feedback: A land trust allows the trustor to convey the fee estate to the trustee and to name himself or herself the beneficiary. The land trust applies only to real property, not to personal property. The agreement, or deed in trust, grants the beneficiary the right to possess and use the property and to exercise control over the actions of the trustee

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the effect of recording a deed?
option_a: Recording is necessary to make a deed valid
option_b: Recording is not necessary to make a deed valid
option_c: Recording the deed gives actual notice of the grantees ownership
option_d: Recording a deed is not in the grantee's best interest
feedback: Recording is not necessary to make a deed valid. However, it is in the grantee's best interest to do so. Recording the deed gives the public constructive notice of the grantee's ownership

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the government's right of eminent domain?
option_a: To acquire a property, the public entity must first adopt a resolution of necessity
option_b: Tenants affected by the condemnation sale always receive compensation
option_c: The transfer of title does not terminate an existing lease
option_d: An eminent domain proceeding is generally a voluntary transfer acquisition
feedback: To acquire a property via eminent domain, the public entity must first adopt a formal resolution to acquire the property, variously called a resolution of necessity. The resolution must be adopted at a formal hearing where the owner may voice an opinion

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding scarcity?
option_b: Scarcity is determined by the consumer's ability to pay for the item
option_c: A distinguishing feature of scarcity is the product's ability to do the job
option_d: Scarcity is a product's availability in relation to demand
feedback: The third critical element of value is a product's availability in relation to demand. The air conditioner is quite valuable if there are only five units in the entire city and everyone is hot. On the other hand, the value of an air conditioner goes down if there are ten thousand units for sale in a 500-person market

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding market price?
option_a: The market price should theoretically be the same as market value
option_b: The market price should reflect the analysis of comparables
option_c: The market price is an opinion of what a willing seller and willing buyer would agree
option_d: The market price is the highest price a buyer would pay
feedback: The market price, as opposed to market value, is what a property actually sells for. Market price should theoretically be the same as market value if all the conditions essential for market value were present. Market price, however, may not reflect the analysis of comparables and of investment value that an estimate of market value includes

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: To minimize losses resulting from an employee’s criminal activity, such as theft or negligence, the property owner will use this type of coverage
feedback: The surety bond covers the property owner should an employee engage in criminal or negligent activities during employment

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: What type of lease did the tenant sign when a landlord is to receive a portion of the tenant’s business income?
feedback: A percentage lease requires the tenant to pay the landlord a portion of the retail operation’s income or sales. This may be in addition to a base rental amount

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: What type of eviction occurs when the tenant vacates the leased premises due to the landlord’s negligence or if the property is deemed as unfit?
feedback: A constructive eviction occurs when a tenant vacates the leased premises and declares the lease void, claiming that landlord’s actions have made the premises unfit for the purpose described in the lease

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the assignment of a contract?
option_a: Listing agreements are assignable since they are personal service agreements
option_b: Listing agreements are not assignable since they are personal service agreements
option_c: Sales contracts are not assignable because they involve the purchase of real property
option_d: Sales contracts are assignable because they involve a personal service
feedback: A real estate contract that is not a personal contract for services can be assigned to another party unless the terms of the agreement specifically prohibit assignment. Listing agreements, for example, are not assignable, since they are personal service agreements between agent and principal.  Sales contracts, however, are assignable, because they involve the purchase of real property rather than a personal service

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the laws that regulate the actions of real estate licensees?
option_a: State laws define the extent to which real estate licensees may legally prepare real estate contracts
option_b: State laws permit real estate licensees to engage in the practice of law if it is in the best interest of their clients
option_c: Real estate licensees are permitted to engage in all aspects of contracts providing that they get written permission
option_d: Contract laws do not regulate real estate licensees
feedback: State laws define the extent to which real estate brokers and agents may legally prepare real estate contracts. Such laws, referred to as "broker-lawyer accords," also define what types of contracts brokers and agents may prepare. In some states, brokers and agents may not draft contracts, but they may use standard promulgated forms and complete the blanks in the form

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding a contract for deed?
option_a: Institutional lenders offer attractive financing terms for a contract for deed purchases
option_b: In a contract for deed, the seller acts as the lender and finances the purchase
option_c: In a contract for deed, the buyer retains the option to purchase the property
option_d: A contract for deed signifies that this is a cash sale
feedback: Like an option, a contract for deed offers a means for a marginally qualified buyer to acquire property. In essence, the seller acts as lender, allowing the buyer to take possession and pay off the purchase price over time

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding earnest money?
option_a: The earnest money deposit does not fulfill the consideration requirements for a valid sales contract
option_b: The earnest money deposit is required and must always be included in a real estate sales contract
option_c: The earnest money deposit is the only form of consideration that satisfies the compensation requirement
option_d: The earnest money deposit provides potential compensation for damages to the seller if the buyer fails to perform
feedback: The buyer's earnest money deposit fulfills the consideration requirements for a valid sale contract. In addition, it provides potential compensation for damages to the seller if the buyer fails to perform. The amount of the deposit varies according to local custom

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: The tenant continues to reside in the property without the landlord’s approval in an estate for years lease. By doing so, the tenant has created which of the following estates?
feedback: A tenancy or an estate of sufferance occurs when a tenant stays on the property without the landlord’s approval after the expiration of the lease

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)?
option_a: The ECOA prohibits discrimination in lending and does not apply to property managers
option_b: The ECOA applies to how property managers evaluate tenants
option_c: The ECOA encourages the use of different rental forms for different applicants
option_d: The ECOA encourages managers to use different credit requirements for minorities
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act, which prohibits discrimination in lending, applies to how property managers evaluate potential tenants. The manager must be consistent in evaluating the creditworthiness of applicants. The same application forms and the same credit requirements should be used with all applicants

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)?
option_a: The ECOA prohibits discrimination in lending and does not apply to property managers
option_b: The ECOA applies to how property managers evaluate tenants
option_c: The ECOA encourages the use of different rental forms for different applicants
option_d: The ECOA encourages managers to use different credit requirements for minorities
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act, which prohibits discrimination in lending, applies to how property managers evaluate potential tenants. The manager must be consistent in evaluating the creditworthiness of applicants. The same application forms and the same credit requirements should be used with all applicants

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)?
option_c: The ECOA allows lenders to deny loans without explaining the reason for denial
option_d: The ECOA is designed to prohibit redlining
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) was enacted in 1974. The Act requires lenders to be fair and impartial in determining who qualifies for a loan

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding a requirement of the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)?
option_a: The ADA makes no exceptions and requires landlords to make all modifications requested by a disabled tenant
option_b: The ADA requires that existing barriers be removed regardless of the cost to the employer
option_c: The ADA requires that disabled employees be compensated for any special accommodations requested and not received
option_d: The ADA requires that disabled employees be treated as other employees who have no disabilities would be treated
feedback: The ADA requires that disabled employees be treated as other employees who have no disabilities would be treated

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the obligations of a principal in an agency relationship?
option_a: The principal must be available for showings
option_b: The principal must be available for decision-making
option_c: The principal must allow the agent to advertise the property
option_d: The principal must always pay compensation
feedback: One of the obligations of a principal in an agency relationship concerns availability. In a special agency, the power and decision-making authority of the agent are limited. Therefore, the principal must be available for consultation, direction, and decision-making. Otherwise the agent cannot complete the job

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the duties imposed on a transaction broker?
option_a: The broker is held to standards for dealing with clients as opposed to customers
option_b: The transaction broker has an obligation to inspect the property for the benefit of a party
option_c: There are no fiduciary duties binding the transaction broker
option_d: The transaction broker must verify the accuracy of statements made by a party
feedback: Since there are no fiduciary duties binding the transaction broker, the broker is held to standards for dealing with customers as opposed to clients. These include honesty, fair dealing, and reasonable care. The transaction broker is under no obligation to inspect the property for the benefit of a party or verify the accuracy of statements made by a party

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the use of social media by licensees to promote the sale of a listed property?
option_a: Social media is not recognized as an official form of advertising and, therefore not regulated by licensing laws
option_b: Social media is recognized as a form of advertising and is subject to real estate commission advertising regulations
option_c: Social media is a form of advertising that cannot be controlled or regulated by real estate commissions
option_d: Social media offers licensee the flexibility to advertise creatively without restrictions
feedback: Social media websites allow rapid exchange of information, documents, photos, messages and data with a select group of contacts. They also represent another form of advertising and are therefore subject to advertising regulations in most states

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding one’s liability in a limited partnership?
option_a: The general partners bear full liability for debts and obligations
option_b: The general partners bear no liability
option_c: The limited partners bear full liability
option_d: All the partners are protected from bearing any personal liability
feedback: All the general partners in a limited partnership bear full liability for debts and obligations. A limited partnership has two or more partners, one or more being general partners and the others limited partners. The general partners run the business and are liable for debts and obligations. The limited partners are liable only to the extent of their investment in the partnership

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding a licensees responsibility and liabilities related to environmental laws?
option_a: Licensees are not expected to have expert knowledge of environmental laws
option_b: Licensees are not expected to be aware of environmental issues
option_c: Licensees are expected to have expert knowledge of environmental laws
option_d: Licensees are only expected to disclose environmental issues related to lead
feedback: Licensees are expected to be aware of environmental issues and to know where to look for professional help. They are not expected to have expert knowledge of environmental law nor of physical conditions in a property. Rather, they must treat potential environmental hazards in the same way that they treat other material facts about a property: disclosure

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding deed restrictions?
option_a: A deed restriction can limit how a property may or may not be used
option_b: Commercial properties are exempt from deed restrictions
option_c: Deed restrictions may contain restrictions based on gender
option_d: Local municipalities and zoning ordinances impose deed restrictions
feedback: A restriction expressed in a conveyance of a residential, commercial, or industrial property places limits on the use of the property

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding a broker’s comparative market analysis (CMA)?
option_a: Brokers need to exercise caution in presenting a CMA as an appraisal, which it is not
option_b: The broker's CMA is a comprehensive analysis identical to an appraisal report
option_c: The broker’s CMA is an unbiased report
option_d: A CMA carries more credibility than an appraisal report
feedback: A broker or salesperson who is attempting to establish a listing price or range of prices for a property uses a scaled-down version of the appraiser's sales comparison approach called a comparative market analysis, or CMA (also called a competitive market analysis). While the CMA serves a useful purpose in setting general price ranges, brokers and agents need to exercise caution in presenting a CMA as an appraisal, which it is not

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The licensee, in accordance with the duty of due diligence, must verify the
option_a: accuracy of the statements in the listing
option_d: repairs done to the property in the past five years
feedback: The licensee, in accordance with the duty of due diligence, must verify the accuracy of the statements in the listing regarding the property, the owner, and the owner's representations

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: In preparing a Comparative Market Analysis, licensees should guard against using the term ____________, which is reserved for use by certified appraisers
feedback: In preparing a Comparative Market Analysis, licensees should guard against using the terms “appraisal” and “value,” which are reserved for the use of certified appraisers. Misuse of these terms could lead to a charge of misrepresenting oneself as an appraiser. In discussing listed properties with clients or customers, real estate licensees should be careful to use guarded terms such as “recommended listing price,” “recommended purchase price,’ and “recommended listing price range.”

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The license laws of most states list illegal advertising actions subject to discipline such as
option_c: using the term “Realtor” ® when not authorized to do so retention
option_d: marketing office listings on the internet
feedback: The license laws of most states list illegal advertising actions subject to discipline such as: making misleading or untruthful statements in any advertising, including using the term “Realtor” when not authorized to do so and using any other trade name, insignia or membership in a real estate organization when the licensee is not a member

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: The landlord’s rights and responsibilities include all the following EXCEPT
feedback: The landlord maintains the right to enter the premises, receive payments, retake on termination, and pursue remedies for tenant’s default

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A licensee may be punished for using any exclusive real estate listing agreement form, sales contract form, or offer to purchase form that lacks
feedback: A licensee may be punished for using any exclusive real estate listing agreement form, sales contract form, or offer to purchase form that lacks a definite termination date

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: It is illegal for real estate professionals who are not attorneys to do which of the following?
option_a: Fill in the blanks in a contract
option_c: Attach a pre-printed rider to a contract
feedback: It is also illegal for real estate licensees who are not lawyers to give legal advice or interpret contract language. Licensees, however, may express opinions

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following expressions used in an advertisement is a violation of the Fair Housing Act?
feedback: Even if a home appears “ideal for a young family," it is best not to advertise it as such. Such advertising would exclude other groups such as singles, the elderly, and older families and therefore would be a violation of the Fair Housing Act

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Brokers who band together to set a price on their services in listing and selling property are guilty of
feedback: Antitrust laws forbid brokers to band together to set a price on their services in listing and selling property. Even being overheard discussing commission rates or being present at such a conversation can lead to charges of price fixing

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The risks of unintentional misrepresentation are reduced if an agent
feedback: The risks of unintentional misrepresentation are reduced if an agent: learns to measure and calculate areas accurately, relies on measurements reported by others only with extreme caution and specific disclaimers, refrains from exaggeration, avoids stating opinions a consumer might take for expertise

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The major risk management technique is to shift the responsibility for choosing a vendor to the consumer. This can be done by
option_a: refusing to recommend vendors at all
option_b: recommending a reliable relative to do the work
option_c: hiring a vendor on behalf of the consumer
option_d: supervising the vendor that was chosen by the consumer
feedback: The major risk management technique is to shift the responsibility for choosing a vendor to the consumer. This can be done by refusing to recommend vendors at all; by presenting a broad range of choices and allowing the consumer to select; or by presenting a short list of thoroughly vetted vendors and allowing the consumer to make the decision

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: RESPA currently requires lenders to give borrower applicants which of the following?
option_a: A Loan Estimate of expected closing costs within seven days of receiving the application
option_b: A Consumer Financial Protection Bureau loan information booklet
option_c: The opportunity to review the final settlement statement at the closing
option_d: The Closing Disclosure form specifying the cost to be paid at the closing within seven days
feedback: RESPA currently requires lenders to give a copy of a Consumer Financial Protection Bureau loan information booklet to the applicant. The booklet explains RESPA provisions, general settlement costs, and the required Closing Disclosure form

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: RESPA specifically prohibits any fee or kickback paid to a party for a service when the party has not actually rendered the service. For example,
option_a: it is prohibited for an insurance company to pay a real estate agent for referring a client
option_b: it is prohibited for a real estate agent to pay a fee to an advertising agency
option_c: it is prohibited for a real estate agent to collect a commission for a transaction that fails to close
option_d: it is prohibited for a real estate agent to receive a commission from a party that he or she did not represent
feedback: RESPA specifically prohibits any fee or kickback paid to a party for a service when the party has not actually rendered the service. For example, it is prohibited for an insurance company to pay a real estate agent or a lender for referring a client

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Typical trust fund handling requirements include
option_a: the broker named as trustee of the account
option_d: the broker named as trustee of the account
feedback: Typical trust fund handling requirements include: the broker named as trustee of the account, a federally-insured bank or recognized depository located in the state, an account that is not interest-bearing if the financial institution ever requires prior written notice for withdrawals, maintenance of records in a particular accounting format, separate records kept for each beneficiary, property, or transaction, records of funds received and paid out regularly reconciled with bank statements, withdrawals only by the broker-trustee or other specifically authorized person

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The general task of a property manager is to
option_a: produce the highest net return for the owner while safeguarding the property
option_b: find and retain tenants at the least expense to the owner
option_c: relieve the owner of all responsibility for operating and maintaining the property
option_d: negotiate rents and regulations between the tenants and the landlord
feedback: A manager has a fiduciary relationship with the principal and, in general, is charged with producing the greatest possible net return on the owner's investment while safeguarding the value of the investment for the owner/investor

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: To rehabilitate real property usually means:
option_a: the property will be restored to a good condition.
option_b: the property must be replaced to obtain its highest and best use potential.
option_c: design changes must be made to the property.
option_d: the use of the property will be changed.
feedback: Rehabilitation of property means to restore real property to an improved state. The restoration is usually needed because the property's condition has worsened.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which lien has priority in a foreclosure sale?
option_d: Mechanics or materialmen who file a timely notice after commencing work on the property
feedback: Tax liens take priority over all other liens, including liens recorded prior to a tax lien.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: When real estate under an estate for years agreement is sold, what happens to the lease?
option_d: It is subject to termination with proper notice
feedback: Tenancy for years is the common form of rental agreements, and it generally binds all future owners of the property for the term of the lease.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Price is best described as
option_a: what suppliers charge for goods and services
option_b: the amount of money consumers are willing to pay for a product or service
option_c: the amount of money a buyer and seller agree to exchange to complete a transaction
option_d: a control placed on prices by the federal government
feedback: A price is the amount of money or other asset that a buyer has agreed to pay and a seller has agreed to accept to complete the exchange of a good or service. It is a quantification of the value of an item traded

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Stephen, his nephew, and his niece are joint tenants. Stephen sells his interest to his sister, and then his nephew dies. As a result, which of these statements is TRUE?
option_a: The nephew’s heirs are joint tenants with Stephen and his sister
option_b: Stephen’s niece and sister are tenants in common
option_c: Stephen's niece and sister are joint tenants
option_d: The nephew’s heirs and Stephen are joint tenants, but the sister is a tenant in common
feedback: Tenancy in Common is ownership of real property by two or more parties that hold an interest in the property- they share only possession, not necessarily time, title, or interest equality. Joint Tenancy is an interest in property taken by two or more joint tenants with equal interest, sharing the same title and possession, and beginning at the same time. Upon the death of a joint tenant, the interest passes to the surviving joint tenants rather than to the heirs of the deceased. NOTE - When Stephen's nephew dies, his share passes to the niece. But because the sister's interest didn't begin at the same time as the niece's, they can't be Joint Tenants- so their common ownership reverts to Tenancy in Common.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Joe and Susan own property as joint tenants. Susan then sells her interest to Carol. What is the relationship between Joe and Carol regarding the property?
option_b: No relationship exists because Susan cannot sell her joint tenancy interest
feedback: If one joint tenant sells or transfers their interest to a third party, the joint tenancy is typically converted into a tenancy in common, as the right of survivorship only applies to the original joint tenants.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: A purchaser obtains financing from a local credit union to purchase a condominium unit. In this situation, which of these BEST describes the purchaser?
feedback: The mortgagor is the borrower in a mortgage, typically a homeowner. The borrower gives the lender a mortgage, which can be considered a "pledge" to pay the borrowed money back, so the lender is the "mortgagee."

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: If someone has a license to cross private property, which of the following would cause the license to terminate?
option_a: The person with the license had not used it for six months
option_c: The property had a tax lien filed against it
option_d: The person with the license had not used it for one year
feedback: If a property was sold, it would terminate a license for crossing a private property because the issuer of the license (the previous property owner) would no longer be involved. The new owner would need to grant a new license to the person who wants to cross the private property.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Financial reporting is
option_a: a duty imposed on a property owner by the Internal Revenue Service
option_b: a function usually outsourced by the owner to a Certified Public Accountant
option_c: a fundamental responsibility of the property manager
option_d: a property management technique used to ensure that tenants pay the correct amount of rent
feedback: Financial reporting to the principal is a fundamental responsibility of the property manager

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A certain building has a vacancy rate above that of the general market. The most likely reason for this is
option_d: lack of incentives from the municipality
feedback: There are many possible reasons for vacancies in a building:  •rent too high or too low  •ineffective marketing  •management quality  •poor tenant-retention program  •image and appearance problems  •high market vacancy rate. If vacancy rates in the managed property are too high, the manager may have to lower rates or identify problems in the property or its management that are contributing to vacancy level

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: One of the principal concerns for a manager in selecting a commercial tenant is
option_a: compatibility of the tenant’s business with that of other tenants
option_c: the current cost of borrowed funds for build-outs
option_d: the location of the tenant’s corporate headquarters
feedback: To ensure that the property produces the desired level of income from rent, it is essential to find the most suitable tenants. For commercial space, the manager must determine that the tenant's business is compatible with that of other tenants

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The term "boot" would probably be considered in connection with a(n):
feedback: The term "boot" is not used in the Internal Revenue Code or the Regulations, but is commonly used in discussing the tax consequences of a Section 1031 tax-deferred exchange. For example, if a property worth $150,000 is exchanged for a property worth $145,000 plus $5000 cash, the cash is considered the boot. Receipt of boot doesn't disqualify an exchange, but it may result in a taxable gain.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is a true statement about the Americans with Disabilities Act?
option_a: It applies only to residential properties
option_b: It applies only to commercial properties
option_c: It requires landlords and managers in some cases to make changes to their facilities
option_d: It requires property managers to hire the disabled
feedback: The Americans with Disabilities Act requires managers to ensure that disabled employees and members of the public have the same level of access to facilities as is provided for those who are not disabled. Employers with at least fifteen employees must follow nondiscriminatory employment and hiring practices. Reasonable accommodations must be made to enable disabled employees to perform essential functions of their jobs.Modifications to the physical components of the building may be necessary to provide the required access to tenants and their customers, such as widening doorways, changing door hardware, changing how doors open, installing ramps, lowering wall-mounted telephones and keypads, supplying Braille signage, and providing auditory signals

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is an illustration of a risk avoidance procedure that a property manager might take?
option_a: The manager hires a firm to provide security guards
option_b: The manager increases the reserves for replacing the carpeting
option_c: The manager closes a stairwell that is in need of repair
option_d: The manager adds a rider to the building’s hazard insurance policy
feedback: A manager may choose to remove the source of the risk, such as by closing off a dangerous area of the building

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: It is important for tenants to understand that
option_a: there are no insurance policies that specifically cover tenant property
option_b: their landlord is required to provide insurance coverage for their personal belongings
option_c: they do not need  any insurance coverage other than the standard fire and flood policy
option_d: their personal belongings are not covered by their landlord’s insurance policies
feedback: Owner's policies do not cover what is owned by the tenant. Tenants should obtain their own renter's or tenant's insurance to cover personal belongings

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: For the proper handling of client and owner monies, a property manager is generally required to
option_a: deposit all funds every month in the management firm’s central operating account
option_b: employ a notary to witness and record every deposit or payment received
option_c: maintain a special trust account in a qualified financial depository
option_d: disburse all funds to their legal owners on a weekly basis
feedback: State laws, usually incorporated into real estate commission rules and the state's real estate law, specify how a property manager is to manage trust funds. In general, the agent is to maintain a separate bank account for these funds, with special accounting, in a qualified depository institution

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A management agreement establishes a(n)
feedback: The management agreement establishes an agency agreement between manager and owner as well as specifying such essentials as the manager's scope of authority, responsibilities, objectives, compensation, and the term of the agreement

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the follow statements about management compensation is false?
option_a: It may be a flat fee based on square footage
option_b: It may be a commission based on rent
option_c: It must be negotiated between the agent and the principal
option_d: It is a standardized amount established by the Universal Landlord and Tenant Act
feedback: Compensation--the management fee or other means of compensation to the manager; there may be a flat fee based on square footage, a rental commission based on a percentage of annual rent, a combination of these, or some other arrangement; in compliance with anti-trust laws, management fees are not standardized but must be negotiated by agent and principal

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following describes a net lease?
option_a: The tenant pays a base rent plus some or all of the operating expenses
option_b: The tenant pays a fixed rent, and the landlord pays all operating expenses
option_c: The tenant pays a base rent plus an amount based on income generated in the leased space
option_d: The tenant pays a rent that increases at specified times over the lease term
feedback: Net leases have the tenant paying rent plus some or all of the operating expenses attributable to the rented space. This arrangement is commonly used in office and industrial leases

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If an apartment contains a washer and drier that are included in the lease,
option_a: the lessee is required to maintain them at the lessee’s expense
option_b: the property manager is responsible for  maintaining them
option_c: the landlord retains the right to use them at any time
option_d: the lessee has the right to remove them
feedback: The lease should set forth items that are excluded or included in the leased property. For instance, a residential lease may include built-in appliances such as dishwashers but exclude freestanding ones, such as refrigerators. If an item is included, it does must be maintained by the property manager

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How does an actual eviction occur?
option_a: A landlord obtains a court order to force the tenant to vacate the leased premises
option_b: The landlord’s agents forcibly remove the tenant from the premises
option_c: A tenant declares a landlord in default and vacates the leased premises
option_d: A landlord declares a tenant in default and takes possession of the leased premises
feedback: An actual eviction follows a procedure prescribed in state law and stated in the lease contract. The landlord must serve notice on the tenant a specified number of days before beginning the eviction suit. A court issues a judgment for possession, which requires the tenant to vacate. A court officer, such as a sheriff, may forcibly remove the tenant and possessions if the tenant refuses to vacate. The landlord can then enter and take possession

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The Universal Residential Landlord-Tenant Act
option_a: provides a standard lease form that is required in all 50 states
option_b: establishes a national board that hears complaints from tenants and landlords
option_c: aims to clarify imprecise language in residential leases
option_d: makes it illegal for landlords to demand more than two weeks’ rent as a security deposit
feedback: The Uniform Residential Landlord-Tenant Act is model legislation that has been adopted to a greater or lessor extent in many states. In addition to addressing fair and equitable remedies for breaches by both landlord and tenant, the act aims to clarify imprecise language in residential leases that can lead to confusion or exploitation

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: The cost of title insurance is based primarily on:
option_b: any number of covenants in the warranty deed.
feedback: Title insurance protects a lender or borrower from financial loss due to defects in a property's title, for example if a deed or deed of trust is fraudulent, lost, or incorrectly filed. The cost of title insurance varies mostly based on a property's value.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following provides a buyer with the best assurance of clear, marketable title?
feedback: Of the answers provided, title insurance provides the best assurance of marketable title.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the purpose of the Consumer Protection Code?
option_a: To govern the dollar exchange rate
option_b: To govern the protection rights of consumers in the state
option_c: To govern the interstate transfer of goods in the state
option_d: To govern the export of goods to foreign nations
feedback: The purpose of the Consumer Protection Code is to govern the protection rights of consumers in the state

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: If a buyer agrees to purchase a home "as is":
option_a: None of the other options are correct.
option_b: neither the seller nor the agent is required to reveal known defects.
option_c: the agent must reveal known defects to the buyer.
option_d: known defects are irrelevant to an "as is" purchase.
feedback: "As is" means the home is being sold in its present condition, but it does not mean that the buyer must agree to buy the home without investigating any potential problems. Generally, states require material fact property disclosures be provided to buyers, regardless of the type of sale agreement.

subject: A1 Real Property
question_text: In a settlement statement, how will a proration of prepaid water, gas, and electric charges be reflected?
option_a: As a debit to the buyer only
option_b: As a debit to the buyer and a credit to the seller
option_c: As a credit to the seller only
option_d: As a debit to the seller and a credit to the buyer
feedback: In this situation, prepaid utility charges would show as a credit to the seller (because the seller has already paid for them) and a debit to the buyer (because the buyer needs to pay the seller back for them).

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines the practice of commingling?
option_a: Depositing escrow money in a business trust account
option_b: Establishing a business trust account at the institution where a personal account is being held
option_c: Depositing funds in a business trust account for administrative maintenance purposes
option_d: Depositing business account trust funds in a licensee’s personal account
feedback: Commingling means mixing or mingling business and personal funds

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines the practice of conversion?
option_a: Adding personal funds to a business trust account to make-up for shortfalls in the balance
option_b: Using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held
option_c: Returning trust account funds when a transaction has ended
option_d: Depositing trust fund monies in an interest-bearing account
feedback: Conversion means using trust funds for a purpose other than the purpose for which they are held. Conversion is a violation of South Carolina real estate license law

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines a real estate team?
option_a: Five or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge
option_b: Two or more supervised licensees working together, but located in different branch offices
option_c: Two or more supervised licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge
option_d: Five or more supervised licensees working in a single office under the supervision of a broker-in-charge
feedback: A team is defined as two or more associated licensees working together as a single unit within a real estate office and supervised by a broker-in-charge. Teams have become more popular in brokerage recently

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines the action of assigning designated agents?
option_a: A broker-in-charge assigns a transactional agent to represent  the buyer exclusively
option_b: A broker-in-charge assigns a transactional agent to represent the seller exclusively
option_c: A broker-in-charge assigns the same affiliated licensee as a designated agent to represent both clients in the same transaction
option_d: A broker-in-charge assigns two affiliated licensees as designated agents, each one exclusively representing the different clients in the same transaction
feedback: The broker may specifically designate one or more associated licensees to act as the agent of the buyer or seller to the exclusion of all other associated licensees. Buyers and sellers must give informed consent to enter into designated agency relationships

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A financing contingency clause indicates the
option_b: terms of the financing sought by the buyer
option_c: buyer’s right to cancel the contract after the loan has been approved
option_d: seller’s right to cancel the contract at any time prior to the buyer’s loan approval
feedback: A financing contingency clause states under what conditions the buyer can cancel the contract without default and receive a refund of the earnest money. If the buyer cannot secure the stated financing by the deadline, the parties may agree to extend the contingency by signing next to the changed dates

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines a nontraditional mortgage product?
option_a: A mortgage product that exceeds the current APR by at least 2 points
option_b: A mortgage product that is not underwritten by a state-chartered lending institution
option_c: A mortgage product other than a thirty-year fixed-rate mortgage loan
option_d: A mortgage product with no originator's fee
feedback: A nontraditional mortgage product is a mortgage product other than a thirty-year fixed-rate mortgage loan.  Common nontraditional mortgages include balloon loans, interest-only, & 15-year loans

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Closing costs customarily paid by the seller include
option_a: real estate taxes due by the seller for the time they owned the property
option_b: the buyer’s appraisal and attorney fees
option_d: annual insurance cost for the calendar year after the closing
feedback: The contract identifies which closing costs each party will pay. Customarily, the seller pays title and property-related costs, and the buyer pays financing-related costs. Annual costs such as taxes are prorated between the parties. Note that who pays any particular closing cost is an item for negotiation. It is the seller’s responsibility to pay for the real estate taxes due up to and including the date of closing

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements best defines abandonment?
option_a: The unexplained absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of five days after default in the payment of rent
option_b: The unexplained absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of fifteen days after default in the payment of rent
option_c: The absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of at least 30 consecutive days
option_d: The absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of at least 31 consecutive days
feedback: Abandonment is considered to be the unexplained absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of fifteen days after default in the payment of rent

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If a landlord fails to properly return a tenant's security deposit, the tenant may recover the money in an amount equal to
option_a: three times the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees
option_b: the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees
option_c: the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees not to exceed $250
option_d: four times the amount wrongfully withheld
feedback: If the landlord fails to return to the tenant any prepaid rent or security/rental deposit with the required written notice, the tenant may recover the property and money in an amount equal to three times the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What action can a tenant take if the landlord is negligent in providing essential services after the tenant has given adequate written notice?
option_a: Procure the required services and have the charges sent to the landlord
option_b: Make the repairs him or herself and deduct the cost of the repairs from rent
option_c: Procure the required services and deduct the cost from the rent
option_d: Place a mechanic's lien on the property
feedback: If the landlord is negligent or willful in failing to provide essential services, the tenant may give written notice to the landlord specifying the breach and may either procure reasonable amounts of the required essential services during the period of the landlord's noncompliance and deduct their actual and reasonable cost from the rent or recover damages based upon the reduction in the fair-market rental value of the dwelling unit and reasonable attorney's fees

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If rent is unpaid when due, how many days after proper notice is a tenant allowed for payment before a landlord can terminate the rental agreement?
feedback: If rent is unpaid when due, a tenant is allowed 5 days after proper notice to pay before a landlord can terminate the rental agreement

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What procedure, if any, does the law stipulate regarding the replacement of batteries in battery-powered smoke detectors?
option_a: The tenant is required to provide and install batteries for battery-powered detectors
option_b: The owner is obligated to provide batteries for battery-powered detectors
option_c: The owner is obligated to provide and install batteries for battery-powered detectors
option_d: There are no rulings for which party is required to supply and install batteries in battery-powered detectors
feedback: The owner is responsible for supplying and installing the smoke detectors in rental units and housing and must give the tenant written or verbal instructions, or both, for testing the detectors and replacing batteries in battery-powered detectors. The tenant has the responsibility to notify the owner in writing of any deficiencies in the performance of the smoke detectors. Once notified by the tenant, the owner must repair or replace the detector within fifteen days

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What are the three major types of timeshare periods?
option_a: Fixed weeks, bubble weeks, and flex time
option_b: Fixed weeks, blanket weeks, and flex time
option_c: Fixed weeks, variable weeks, and flex time
option_d: Fixed weeks, floating weeks, and flex time
feedback: The three major types of timeshare periods are fixed weeks, floating weeks, and flex time

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A Vacation Time Sharing Lease Plan
option_a: must extend for a period of at least  five years
option_b: cannot extend more than three years
option_c: must extend for a period of more than three years
option_d: allows the purchaser to receive an ownership interest in real property
feedback: A Vacation Time Sharing Lease Plan must extend for a period of more than three years

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following descriptions best defines an Estuarine Sanctuary?
option_a: A research area designated by the Secretary of Commerce
option_b: A pool of brackish water with no connection to the open sea
option_c: A coastal body exposed during low tide
option_d: A pool of fresh water with a river connection to the open sea
feedback: An estuarine sanctuary is a research area designated as an estuarine sanctuary by the Secretary of Commerce

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The South Carolina Human Affairs Commission is comprised of
option_b: six elected and six appointed members
option_c: a member from each congressional district and three members-at-large
option_d: a member from each congressional district and two members-at-large
feedback: The South Carolina Human Affairs Commission is comprised of a member from each congressional district and two members-at-large

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The millage rate is
option_a: 5% on the fair market value of the property
option_b: the same across all taxing districts
option_c: equivalent to the tax per $1,000 of assessed value
option_d: equivalent to the tax per $10,000 of assessed value
feedback: The millage rate is equivalent to the tax per $1,000 of assessed value

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Under the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act, which of the following must be prepared in traditional paper and ink format?
option_a: Documents relating to real estate transactions
option_b: Documents relating to adoption and divorce
option_d: Documents relating to the transport of unlicensed motor vehicles
feedback: Under the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act, the documents relating to adoption and divorce must be prepared in traditional paper and ink format

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How do lenders classify mortgage loans with regard to a potential borrower's credit?
option_b: Class L-1, L-2, L-3, and L-4
option_d: A, B, C, and D paper
feedback: Lenders classify mortgage loans with regard to a potential borrower’s credit as A, B, C, and D paper. When a qualified borrower is approved for a mortgage loan, the loan is referred to as “A” paper. A borrower with some credit problems may have to take a loan at a higher rate of interest, known as “B” paper. Serious credit problems might lead to “C” paper. There is even a category of “D” paper for those with enormous credit problems. Borrowers who fall into any of the “below A” categories are referred to as sub-prime borrowers

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the practice called when lenders encourage a borrower to refinance a loan so that the lender can charge high points and fees for the new loan?
feedback: Loan flipping is when a lender encourages a borrower to refinance a loan so that the lender can charge high points and fees for the new loan. Sometimes the borrower also pays a higher interest rate than with the original loan

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What document must contain specific mortgage loan data elements such as the credit score of the borrower, term of the loan, and the annual percentage rate of the loan?
feedback: The Mortgage Log contains specific mortgage loan data elements such as the credit score of the borrower, terms of the loan, and the annual percentage rate of the loan

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A landlord must give the tenant at least
option_a: thirty-six hours’ notice of the intent to enter and may enter only at reasonable times
option_b: twenty-four hours’ notice of the intent to enter and may enter only at reasonable times
option_c: twenty-four hours’ notice of the intent to enter and may enter only at a pre-defined hour
option_d: twelve hours’ notice of the intent to enter and may enter only at reasonable times
feedback: A landlord must give the tenant at least twenty-four hours’ notice of the intent to enter and may enter only at reasonable times

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: A mortgage product other than a thirty-year fixed rate mortgage loan is called a(n)
feedback: A nontraditional mortgage product is a mortgage product other than a thirty-year fixed rate mortgage loan

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Under what act are consumers able to receive a free copy of their consumer report from each credit reporting agency once a year?
feedback: Under the Fair Credit Reporting Act, consumers are able to receive a free copy of their consumer report from each credit reporting agency once a year

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Failure to properly return a tenant's security deposit may result in the tenant recovering
option_a: two times the amount wrongfully withheld
option_b: all security monies, plus $500 in penalties
option_c: the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees not to exceed $250
option_d: three times the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney's fees
feedback: Failure to properly return a tenant’s security deposit may result in the tenant recovering three times the amount wrongfully withheld along with reasonable attorney’s fees

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Under what circumstances can a tenant procure maintenance services and then deduct the cost from the rent?
option_a: If a landlord is negligent in providing essential services after the tenant has given adequate written notice
option_b: If a landlord is negligent in providing essential services after three months
option_d: If a landlord has ignored an official health and safety notice
feedback: A tenant can procure maintenance services and deduct the cost from the rent if a landlord is negligent in providing essential services after the tenant has given adequate written notice

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Tenant Jan has stopped paying rent as of March 1st. When can the landlord terminate the rental agreement?
feedback: If rent is unpaid when due and the tenant fails to pay rent within five days from the due date, the landlord may terminate the rental agreement provided the landlord must give the tenant written notice of nonpayment and the intention to terminate the rental agreement

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The unexplained absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of fifteen days after default in the payment of rent is called
feedback: Abandonment is considered to be the unexplained absence of a tenant from a dwelling unit for a period of fifteen days after default in the payment of rent

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the process for the replacement of batteries in battery-powered smoke detectors?
option_a: The owner provides batteries and the tenant installs them
option_b: The tenant provides and installs all batteries
option_c: The owner provides and installs batteries
option_d: The tenant provides and the owner installs them
feedback: The tenant provides and installs all batteries in battery-powered smoke detectors

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the minimum time allowed for a Vacation Time Sharing Lease Plan?
option_a: It must extend for a period of at least five years
option_b: It must extend for a period of more than three years
option_c: It must extend for a period of more than two years
option_d: It must extend for a period of at least one year
feedback: The minimum time allowed for a Vacation Time Sharing Lease Plan is three years

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What automated system provides community notifications and automatic updates to the National Sex Offender public website?
feedback: SORT provides community notifications and automatic updates to the National Sex Offender public website

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: An estuarine sanctuary is a
option_a: pool of brackish water with a connection to the open sea
option_b: pool of brackish water with a connection to a river
option_c: coastal inlet covered by high tides
option_d: coastal body sanctuary designated by the Secretary of Commerce
feedback: An estuarine sanctuary is a coastal body sanctuary designated by the Secretary of Commerce

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Who appoints members to the South Carolina Human Affairs Commission?
feedback: The Governor appoints members to the South Carolina Human Affairs Commission

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: How is real property tax determined?
option_a: The millage rate is multiplied times the fair market value
option_b: The assessment rate is multiplied by the assessed value
option_c: The assessed value is multiplied by the millage rate
option_d: The fair market value is divided by the assessed value
feedback: Real property tax is determined by multiplying the assessed value by the millage rate

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What documents require homeowners to abide by association rules?
feedback: Covenants, conditions, and restrictions require homeowners to abide by the homeowners’ association rules

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following risk classifications reflects the most credit-worthy rating?
feedback: The “A” paper risk classification reflects the most credit-worthy rating

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: One purpose of the ESIGN Act is to
option_a: Identify which contracts in real estate brokerage may use electronic signatures and which may not
option_b: Require acknowledgement to effect the validity of electronic signatures
option_c: Eliminate the necessity of hard-copy signatures in real property contracting
option_d: Assure the legal and binding effect of the electronic form of signatures
feedback: One purpose of the ESIGN Act is to assure the legal and binding effect of the electronic form of signatures

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What federal law lays out the guidelines for using electronic signatures in interstate commerce?
option_c: The Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (ESIGN)
feedback: The federal law that lays out guidelines for using electronic signatures in interstate commerce is the Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (ESIGN)

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: In accordance with the SC Residential Landlord and Tenant Act, a tenant may
option_a: change locks at any time on a unit without the landlord’s permission
option_b: not change locks on a dwelling unit without the landlord’s permission
option_c: change locks at any time on a unit and take credit on the next rental payment for the expenses without the landlord’s permission
option_d: re-key locks during occupancy and then restore locks to original state upon termination of the lease, without the landlord's knowledge
feedback: In accordance with the SC Residential Landlord and Tenant Act, a tenant may not change locks on a dwelling unit without the landlord’s permission

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In accordance with the Statute of Frauds,
option_a: leases in excess of one year must be recorded to be enforceable
option_c: a five-year lease must be in writing to be enforceable
feedback: Generally, a lease for a period exceeding one year cannot be oral but must be in writing to be enforceable because of the statute of frauds

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An owner leases a property to a business in exchange for rent.  The tenant is required to pay all operating expenses as well.  This is an example of a
feedback: A net lease requires a tenant to pay for utilities, internal repairs, and a proportionate share of taxes, insurance, and operating expenses in addition to rent

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The percentage lease is most often used by
feedback: Percentage leases are used only for retail properties

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Residential leases differ from commercial and other types of leases in that
option_a: residential lease terms are longer than commercial lease terms
option_b: residential lease terms are shorter than commercial lease terms
option_c: residential leases are generally negotiable, and commercial leases are standard
option_d: residential leases are typically percentage leases, and commercial leases are not
feedback: lease terms are shorter, typically one or two years, lease clauses are fairly standard from one property to the next, in order to reflect compliance with local landlord-tenant relations laws, lease clauses are generally not negotiable, particularly in larger apartment complexes where owners want uniform leases for all residents

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In an instance where the landlord's negligence or disruptive action has rendered the property unoccupiable, a tenant may vacate the premises and declare that the lease is canceled by default. This action is called
feedback: The most common form of landlord default is failure to provide services and maintain the property condition. When a landlord defaults on the terms of the lease, tenants may sue for damages. In an instance where the landlord's negligence or disruptive action has rendered the property unoccupiable, a tenant may vacate the premises and declare that the lease is canceled by default. This action is called constructive eviction

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act (URLTA) aims to protect the
option_d: transient occupant in a hotel or motel rental
feedback: The Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act (URLTA) is a model law enacted as a blueprint for state laws to regulate leasing and management practices of landlords with residential properties. The act aims to: standardize rights of landlord and tenant, protect tenants from unethical practices, prevent unfair, complex leases and their enforceability

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Under the Uniform Residential Landlord and Tenant Act (URLTA), a landlord is obligated to do which of the following?
option_a: bargain in good faith with the tenant
option_b: maintain the rental unit free and clear of debris
option_c: provide the tenant with a signed lease agreement
feedback: The landlord's obligations. under URLTA, includes the following: bargain in good faith with the tenant, provide required maintenance, make repairs, comply with local building codes, provide access and safety services: elevator; fire escapes, etc., provide a procedure for delivery of official notices

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An individual who receives a proprietary lease has a
feedback: A proprietary lease conveys a leasehold interest to an owner of a cooperative

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A seller received an offer from a buyer for $350,000. The seller counter-offered with a different sales price of $375,000. Which statement is true regarding the status of the buyer’s original offer?
feedback: A void contract is an agreement that does not meet the tests for validity and therefore is no contract at all. If a contract is void, neither party can enforce it. A void agreement that is absent of competent parties, mutual consent, valuable consideration, legal purpose, or voluntary act of good will. By changing any of the terms of an offer, the offeree creates a counteroffer, and the original offer is void

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A seller induces a buyer to sign a sales contract for the purchase of a house based on the assurance that the property is less than five years old.  The buyer wants to rescind the agreement after learning that the building permit proves that the property is over twelve years old.  This contract is
option_a: voidable because the buyer’s action did not exbibit good faith
option_b: voidable because the seller’s action did not exhibit good faith
option_c: valid because the buyer should have done due diligence before signing the contract
option_d: void because the contract lacked mutual consent
feedback: The parties must create the contract in good faith, as a free and voluntary act. A contract is thus voidable if one party acted under duress, coercion, fraud, or misrepresentation

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The buyer submitted an offer to purchase a three-bedroom home below the asking price. The seller counter-offered with a different purchase price. When the seller gave a counteroffer, the seller became the
feedback: The offeror proposes contract terms in a contract to an offeree.  When an offeree accepts all terms without amendment, the offer becomes a contract. When an offeree creates a counteroffer, the original offer is void, and that party becomes the offeror

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A breach of contract is a failure to perform according to the terms of the agreement. This is called
feedback: A breach of contract is a failure to perform according to the terms of the agreement. Also called default, a breach of contract gives the damaged party the right to take legal action

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If the parties wish to create a new contract to replace a canceled contract, they must comply with the validity requirements for the new contract. Such substitution is called
feedback: Parties to a contract can agree to terminate, or renounce, the contract. If the parties wish to create a new contract to replace the canceled contract, they must comply with the validity requirements for the new contract. Such substitution is called novation

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If a seller changes the expiration date of a buyer's offer by one day, signs the offer and returns it to the buyer, which of the following statements is true?
option_a: The single amendment extinguishes the buyer's offer
option_b: A single amendment by the seller does not influence the status of the offer
option_c: The buyer is legally bound to the agreement
option_d: The counteroffer creates a voidable contract
feedback: By changing any of the terms of an offer, the offeree creates a counteroffer, and the original offer is void. At this point, the offeree becomes the offeror, and the new offeree gains the right of acceptance. If accepted, the counteroffer becomes a valid contract provided all other requirements are met

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An oral contract to sell mineral rights is performed by both parties.  Six months later, the seller has second thoughts and wants to reclaim the rights under the Statue of Frauds.  What is the likelihood that the seller will succeed?
option_a: Nil, because the Statute of Frauds is irrelevant to a contract that has been performed
option_b: Good, because an oral contract to convey a real estate interest is unenforceable under the Statute of Frauds
option_c: Good, because the contract is voidable
option_d: Nil, because the buyer committed no fraud
feedback: Once the parties to a valid oral contract have executed and performed it, even if the contract was unenforceable, a party cannot use the Statute of Frauds to rescind the contract

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If a borrower pays $1,650 interest per quarter on a straight note of $60,000, the interest rate would be:
feedback:
$1,650 = interest for 3 months
 $1,650 X 4 = $6,600 interest for one year
 $6,600 divided by $60,000 = 0.11 or 11%
 
 NOTE - 1 quarter of a year is equal to 3 months.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent has the duty to inform the client of
