question_text: What part of a non-navigable waterway does the owner of an abutting property own?
option_b: To the middle of the waterway
feedback: If the property abuts a non-navigable stream, the owner enjoys unrestricted use of the water and owns the land beneath the stream to the stream's midpoint

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is the "Doctrine of Prior Appropriation?"
option_b: The right of government to confiscate land and improvements
option_c: A doctrine that gives the state control of water use and the water supply
option_d: A real estate tax applied to owners of water rights
feedback: Since water is a resource necessary for survival, some states -- particularly those where water is scarce -- have taken the legal position that the state owns and controls all bodies of water. Called the Doctrine of Prior Appropriation, this position requires that property owners obtain permits for use of water

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The overriding test of whether an item is a fixture or personal property is
option_a: how long it has been attached to the real property
option_b: its definition as one or the other in a sale or lease contract
option_c: how essential it is to the functioning of the property
option_d: how it was treated in previous transactions
feedback: If a person intended an article to be a fixture, even though the item is easily removable

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is an emblement?
option_a: A piece of equipment affixed to the earth
option_b: A limited right to use personal property
option_c: A sign indicating a property boundary
option_d: A plant or crop that is considered personal property
feedback: Plants and crops requiring human intervention and labor are called emblements. Emblements, despite their attachment to land, are considered personal property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Four principal determinants of value underlying the price for a product are
option_b: desire, utility, scarcity, and purchasing power
feedback: The value of something is based on the answers to four questions:  •How much do I desire it?  •How useful is it?  • How scarce is it?  •Am I able to pay for it?

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An item can be converted from real to personal property and vice versa by means of which processes?
feedback: The classification may be changed by the process of conversion. Severance is the conversion of real property to personal property by detaching it from the real estate, such as by cutting down a tree, detaching a door from a shed, or removing an antenna from a roof. Affixing, or attachment, is the act of converting personal property to real property by attaching it to the real estate, such as by assembling a pile of bricks into a barbecue pit, or constructing a boat dock from wood planks

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The major sources of real estate law are legislation at federal, state and local level, and
feedback: Regulation takes the form of federal and state laws and regulations; county and local ordinances and codes; and court decisions in the judicial system

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is the primary thrust of federal involvement in real estate law?
feedback: In regulating real property rights, the federal government is primarily concerned with broad standards of real property usage, natural disaster, land description, and discrimination

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which level of government controls zoning laws?
feedback: County and local governments have the power to zone land

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A grocer temporarily installs special fruit and vegetable coolers in a leased grocery store in order to prevent spoilage. The coolers would be considered which of the following?
option_a: Trade fixtures that are real property
option_b: Trade fixtures that are personal property
option_c: Permanent fixtures that are real property
option_d: Permanent fixtures that are personal property
feedback: Trade fixtures are items of a tenant's personal property that the tenant has temporarily affixed to a landlord's real property in order to conduct business. Trade fixtures may be detached and removed before or upon surrender of the leased premises. Should the tenant fail to remove a trade fixture, it may become the property of the landlord through accession. Thereafter, the fixture is considered real property

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Under the doctrine of littoral rights, an owner claims ownership of all of the land underlying a lake where there are three other abutting property owners.  Which of the following is true?
option_a: The owner's claim is invalid, because the state owns the underlying land
option_b: The owner's claim is invalid, because the underlying land is shared equally with the other owners
option_c: The owner's claim is invalid, because he may only own underlying land to the middle of the lake
option_d: The owner's claim is valid, because the lake is navigable
feedback: The owner's claim is invalid, because the state owns the underlying land

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following represents the highest form of estate ownership?
feedback: Properties conveyed as Fee Simple Absolute estates are considered the highest form of ownership because the heirs can dispose of, enjoy, exclude, control, and possess the property generally without restriction

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following represents the highest form of estate ownership?
feedback: Properties conveyed as Fee Simple Absolute estates are considered the highest form of ownership because the heirs can dispose of, enjoy, exclude, control, and possess the property generally without restriction

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following distinguishes a determinable estate from a condition subsequent estate?
option_a: A determinable estate focuses on the use of the estate
option_b: The dispute of a determinable estate is settled in court
option_c: The recipient of a determinable estate determines the use of the estate
option_d: The remainderman of the determinable estate inherits the property
feedback: In a fee simple defeasible estate, the use of the property is established by the owner through a determinable estate. Should the property not be used according to the owner’s instructions, the property will automatically revert back to the grantor

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following distinguishes a determinable estate from a condition subsequent estate?
option_a: A determinable estate focuses on the use of the estate
option_b: The dispute of a determinable estate is settled in court
option_c: The recipient of a determinable estate determines the use of the estate
option_d: The remainderman of the determinable estate inherits the property
feedback: In a fee simple defeasible estate, the use of the property is established by the owner through a determinable estate. Should the property not be used according to the owners instructions, the property will automatically revert back to the grantor

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following reflects an estate restricted by specific dates or duration?
feedback: A leasehold estate refers to rental properties, whereby a lessor transfers possession of a premises to a lessee for a definitive period of time

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following reflects an estate restricted by specific dates or duration?
feedback: A leasehold estate refers to rental properties, whereby a lessor transfers possession of a premises to a lessee for a definitive period of time

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is a right provided to a life tenant?
feedback: The life tenant holds the rights of possession during a life estate and reverts to the original owner or to another designated individual upon death

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What type of leasehold estate does not have a definitive end date?
feedback: An estate at will continues indefinitely and terminates when either party gives sufficient notice or upon death

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What type of leasehold estate does not have a definitive end date?
feedback: An estate at will continues indefinitely and terminates when either party gives sufficient notice or upon death

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: How do fee simple estates differ from life estates?
option_b: Life estates transfer assets only between family members

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: On Monday, a woman offers to sell her vacant lot to a man for $35,000. On Tuesday, the man counteroffers to buy the lot for $25,500. On Friday, the man withdraws his counteroffer and accepts the woman’s original price of $35,000. Are they under contract for the lot? Why or why not?
option_a: No, because the counteroffer invalided the original offer.
option_b: Yes, because the man accepted the woman’s offer exactly as it was made, regardless of the fact that it was not accepted immediately.
option_c: Yes, because the man accepted before the woman advised him that the offer was withdrawn.
option_d: No, because the woman’s offer was not accepted within 72 hours of its having been made.
feedback: Making a counteroffer is effectively refusing the original offer and making a new one. The original offer is "dead" and cannot be accepted after making a counteroffer. In this case, if both parties actually were willing to agree to the $35k, the man would have to withdraw his counter and make a new counter at $35k (which she would then have to accept) or the woman would have to counter at $35k and he would have to accept.

The act also requires lender to inform prospective borrowers who are being denied credit of the reasons for the denial

subject: B2 Agency

The wholesaler finds a willing seller, puts the property under contract, and finds a third-party buyer willing to buy at a higher price than what the wholesaler has agreed to pay the original seller. How are some states fighting the practice of real estate wholesaling?
option_a: By requiring the wholesaler to have a real estate license
option_b: By requiring any property to be fully owned by any particular owner for 30 days
option_c: By requiring any property to be fully owned by any particular owner for 60 days

feedback: Some states require the wholesaler in such transactions to hold a real estate license.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A home seller tells the listing agent that he will not consider any offers from minority buyers.  A cooperating broker wants to show the home to a minority buyer, but learns from the listing agent of the seller's instructions.  The cooperating broker then informs the buyer that he cannot show that home to him.  The buyer subsequently asks to be shown only houses that are in minority neighborhoods.  Which parties in this scenario are probably liable for violating fair housing laws?
option_b: The seller and the listing agent only
option_c: The seller, the listing agent, and the cooperating broker
feedback: Fair housing laws apply to home sellers as well as to agents, with the exception of the exemptions. If an agent goes along with a client's discriminatory act, the agent is equally liable for violation of fair housing laws. It is thus imperative to avoid complicity with client discrimination. Further, an agent should withdraw from any relationship where client discrimination occurs

subject: B2 Agency

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Two people own a house, each having an undivided equal interest.  Which of the following best describes what they own?
option_a: Each owns fifty percent of the physical house and the land it rests on
option_b: Each owns one hundred percent of the physical house and the land it rests on
option_c: Each owns fifty percent of the estate represented by the real property, but no physical portion of the real property
option_d: Each owns one hundred percent of the estate represented by the real property and fifty percent of the physical house and the land it rests on
feedback: An undivided interest is an owner's interest in a property in which two or more parties share ownership. The terms "undivided" and "indivisible" signify that the owner's interest is in a fractional part of the entire estate, not in a physical portion of the real property itself. If two co-owners have an undivided equal interest, one owner may not lay claim to the northern half of the property for his or her exclusive use

question_text: An estate from period-to-period will continue as long as
option_a: the tenant makes, and landlord accepts, regular rent payments
option_b: the term specified in the lease
option_c: the period is less than a year
option_d: the landlord has not given notice of termination
feedback: In an estate from period-to-period, also called a periodic tenancy, the tenancy period automatically renews as long as the tenant pays rent in a timely manner and the landlord accepts it. At the end of a tenancy period, if the landlord accepts another regular payment of rent, the leasehold is considered to be renewed for another period. A conveyance of leased property does not cancel a leasehold interest

At the end of a tenancy period, if the landlord accepts another regular payment of rent, the leasehold is considered to be renewed for another period. subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An estate at will
option_b: is terminated only if so stated in the lessee's last will and testament
option_c: terminates on the death of lessor or lessee
option_d: terminates on the date specified in the lease agreement
feedback: The estate at will, also called a tenancy at will, has no definite expiration date and hence no "renewal" cycle. The landlord and tenant agree that the tenancy will have no specified termination date, provided rent is paid on time and other lease conditions are met. For example, a son leases a house to his father and mother "forever," or until they want to move. The estate at will is terminated by proper notice, or by the death of either party

feedback: In the role of transaction broker, the broker's duties and standards of conduct are to: account for all money and property received or handled, exercise reasonable skill and care, provide honesty and fair dealing, present all offers in a timely fashion, assist the parties in closing the transaction, keep the parties fully informed, advise the parties to obtain expert advice or counsel, disclose to both parties in residential sale transactions all material facts affecting the property's value, protect the confidences of both parties in matters that would materially disadvantage one party over the other

option_d: notify prospective customers that they have no choice in how they are represented
feedback: Disclosure removes confusion about who an agent is working for. It may obviate complaints arising from customers and clients who feel they have been deceived. Specifically, the requirement to disclose aims to: notify clients and customers about whom the agent represents, inform clients and customers of the fiduciary duties and standards of care the agent owes them, inform prospective clients and customers that they have a choice in how they are represented, obtain acknowledgement and acceptance of the disclosure from the principal parties

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A listing agent must disclose in writing to a buyer or tenant that the agent represents the owner in the transaction. This disclosure must occur
option_a: after accepting an offer from the prospective buyer
option_b: after the closing of the transaction
option_c: before the seller accepts the offer from the prospective buyer
option_d: before or at the first substantive contact with the customer prospect
feedback: A listing agent must disclose in writing to a buyer or tenant that the agent represents the owner in the transaction. This disclosure must occur before or at the first "substantive contact" with the customer prospect. The disclosure must also be confirmed in any subsequent sale or lease contract

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Agents have certain obligations to customers, even though they do not represent them. In general, they owe a third party
feedback: Agents do have certain obligations to customers, even though they do not represent them. In general, they owe a third party: honesty and fair dealing, reasonable care and skill, proper disclosure

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following agreements allow a broker to select salespersons and/or associate brokers to act as the client’s agent?
feedback: The designated agency agreement allows a broker to select a salesperson and associate brokers to act as the client’s agent.  A designated agency agreement must contain the name of all associate brokers who are authorized to act as supervisory brokers.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The owner must pay the listing broker a commission fee regardless of who is the procuring cause during the listing period. Which type of agreement did the owner sign?
feedback: Under the terms of this listing agreement, a seller contracts the exclusive services with a single broker to procure a buyer or effect a sale transaction. Regardless of who is the procuring cause, the broker is entitled to a commission if the property sells

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The owner retains the right to sell the property without the broker’s assistance; however, authorizes a single broker to sell the property and earn a commission.  The owner agreed to
feedback: An exclusive agency listing authorizes a single broker to sell the property and earn a commission but leaves the owner the right to sell the property without the broker’s assistance. Thus, the property owner who procures a buyer without the broker’s assistance, is not obligated to pay a commission to the broker

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The owner sets a minimum acceptable amount to be received from the transaction and allows the broker to retain any amounts in excess as the commission. The owner has agreed to
feedback: In a net listing, the seller sets a minimum acceptable amount to be received from the transaction and allows the broker to retain any amount above the minimum acceptable amount as the commission. Generally, net listings are regarded as unprofessional or illegal since it creates a conflict of interest for the broker

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An organization comprised of member brokers who agree to cooperate in the sale of properties listed by other brokers in exchange for a share of the broker’s earned commission is referred to as
feedback: A multiple listing service is an organization of member brokers who agree to cooperate in the sale of properties listed by other brokers in exchange for a share of the broker’s resulting commission

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Due diligence refers to verifying the accuracy of statements regarding
feedback: Failure to perform a reasonable degree of due diligence may increase an agent’s exposure to liability in the event that the property’s condition is not as represented or that the client cannot perform as promised related to property condition, ownership status, and the client’s authority to act

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The broker’s commission amount is based on
option_b: negotiations between the principal and the broker
option_c: rates established by local Realtor® boards
feedback: The amount of a broker’s commission is the agreed negotiated amount between the client and the broker

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is information NOT required in an exclusive agency listing agreement?
option_a: The legal description of the listed property
feedback: Exclusive listing agreements generally include owner names, property address or legal description, listing price, expiration date, commission terms, and authority granted

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: The term “cooperating with other agents” in a listing agreement refers to the seller agreeing or refusing to work with
feedback: The cooperation clause within a listing agreement requires the seller to agree or refuse to cooperate with subagents or buyer agents in selling the property, under what terms, and whether the seller agrees to compensate these parties. In addition, agreements may require subagents and buyer agents to disclose their relationships to the buyer upon initial contact and subsequently in writing a contract

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In addition to creating contractual obligations, a listing agreement creates
option_a: an agency relationship with fiduciary obligations
option_b: a general partnership with financial obligations
option_d: a temporary business entity subject to corporation regulations
feedback: A listing agreement establishes an agency relationship between agent and client that commits the agent to the full complement of fiduciary duties to the client in fulfilling the agreement

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following occurs when a valued product becomes increasingly scarce?
feedback: If a valued product becomes increasingly scarce, its value and price go up as consumers compete for the limited supply. If there is an overabundance of a product, the price falls, as demand is oversaturated

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Three brokers from different firms are having lunch together after a downtown business club meeting. In their conversation, a fourth broker from another firm is mentioned. The three brokers agree that they don’t like this other broker, and decide they will no longer show her listings to potential buyers. If they act on this decision, they may be guilty of an illegal act of
feedback: Group boycotting is the illegal activity of a number of competitors in a given market "ganging up" on a competitor in that market to limit the boycotted party's access to customers and patrons of the competitor's business. For instance, a group of brokers might collectively refuse to show a boycotted broker's listings to potential buyers. Over time, the boycotted company is essentially "starved" out of the market.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: A broker's compensation for effecting a transaction is usually
option_a: a negotiated percentage of the purchase price
option_b: established by the state’s licensing laws
option_d: a fixed percentage reflecting the going rate
feedback: A broker's compensation for effecting a transaction is usually a negotiated percentage of the purchase price

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statement is true regarding the use of the term Realtor®?
option_a: Only members of the organization may use the term to refer to themselves
option_b: Only brokers who specialize in residential sales may use the term
option_c: Any individual who passes the state licensing exam may immediately begin to use the term
option_d: Any individual who maintains an active broker’s license may use the term
feedback: Numerous trade organizations serve the real estate brokerage industry and all of its areas of specialization. The largest is the National Association of Realtors® (NAR). One should note that the term Realtor® is a protected trade name of NAR. Only members of the organization may use the term to refer to themselves

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: An agent’s commission schedule is
feedback: An agent's commission schedule is a comprehensive summary of commission splits under various circumstances and can vary widely

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: When a buyer or tenant makes an offer, the agent must
option_a: present it to the seller or landlord at the earliest possible moment
option_b: present the highest or best offer to the seller or landlord
option_c: present it to the seller or landlord after the contingencies have been satisfied
option_d: immediately accept it on behalf of the seller or landlord
feedback: When a buyer or tenant makes an offer, the agent must present it to the seller or landlord at the earliest possible moment. If the terms of the offer are unacceptable, the agent may assist the seller in developing a counteroffer, which the agent would subsequently submit to the customer or customer's agent

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding an agent’s compensation?
option_a: An agent’s commission schedule may vary widely
option_b: An agent’s commission split usually is 40%
option_c: The broker's commission portion usually is higher than the agent’s share
option_d: The commission splits between the broker and the agent is always 50/50
feedback: An agent's commission schedule can vary widely. In almost all cases, broker and agent negotiate the schedule of commissions in the environment of competitive market conditions

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: During the pre-closing period, an agent’s duties and responsibilities may include all the following EXCEPT
option_a: assisting the buyer in obtaining financing
option_b: assisting the seller in clearing the title
option_c: assisting the seller in completing property repairs
option_d: assisting the appraiser in determining market value
feedback: As dictated by custom and the circumstances of a transaction, an agent has a range of duties and responsibilities during the pre-closing period. Some of the possible responsibilities are: assisting the buyer in obtaining financing, assisting the seller in clearing title, assisting the seller in completing property repairs, recommending inspectors, appraisers, attorneys, and title companies, assisting in communications between principals, assisting in the exchange of transaction documents. Note that only an appraiser can identify a defensible estimate of market value

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Which of the following Acts requires “express prior authorization”?
feedback: The CAN-SPAM Act (Controlling the Assault of Non-Solicited Pornography and Marketing Act of 2003) supplements the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA). It: bans sending unwanted email 'commercial messages' to wireless devices, requires express prior authorization, requires giving an 'opt out' choice to terminate the sender's messages

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In general, brokerage advertising is subject to which of the following regulations and restrictions?
option_a: The broker is responsible for the content of advertising placed by agents
option_b: The broker is not liable for the content of advertising if paid for by agents
option_c: The seller is responsible for the content of advertising done by the brokerage firm
option_d: The seller must approve all advertisements before publication
feedback: Advertising is subject to regulation and restrictions. In general, state laws and regulations require that: advertising must not be misleading, the broker is responsible for the content of advertising done by agents, all advertising must reveal the identity of the broker; licensed brokers and agents may not use blind ads that conceal their identities, brokers selling their own property through the brokerage must disclose the brokerage identity, salespersons must include the broker's business identity in any advertising; they may not advertise in their own name solely (unless selling their own property through channels other than the agency)

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: Antitrust laws are designed to
option_c: prohibit the practice of brokers competing for the same listing
option_d: prohibit the practice of overly intensive competitive activity
feedback: Brokerage companies, like other businesses, are subject to anti-trust laws designed to prevent monopolies and unfair trade practices

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: In the village of Panther Ridge, there are five brokers in total, Schneika, Charlie, Nhien, Meily and Candy.  Brokers Schneika, Charlie, Nhien and Meily all collectively decide not to show buyers any properties listed by Candy.  This would be considered an example of
feedback: Group boycotting is the illegal activity of a number of competitors in a given market "ganging up" on a competitor in that market to limit the boycotted party's access to customers and patrons of the competitor's business.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the underlining determinants of supply and demand?
option_a: Market conditions are usually in a balanced state
option_b: Market conditions are not responsive to purchasing powers
option_c: Market demand operates independently from  supply
feedback: Since the underlying determinants of supply and demand (scarcity, desire, utility, purchasing power and costs) are constantly changing, a market is usually in some phase of imbalance

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Retail users are generally concerned with
feedback: Retail users are concerned with the following: sufficient trade area population and income, the level of trade area competition, sales volume per square foot of rented area, consumer spending patterns, growth patterns in the trade area

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Monies, labor, supplies given by landlord to tenant for occupancy:
feedback: Tenant improvement allowance is funds provided to customize or improve leased space.

subject: B3 Agency
question_text: What offense is broker Smith committing if she fails to deposit earnest money into escrow in a timely manner?
feedback: Commingling is the act of mixing the broker's personal or business funds with escrow funds. To avoid committing a violation, a broker must not deposit any money belonging to others in the broker's business or personal account. Also, a broker must not deposit operating funds into any trust account.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Industrial users are primarily concerned with which of the following economic and operational factors?
feedback: Industrial users are concerned with the following: functionality, the availability and proximity of the labor pool, compliance with environmental regulations, permissible zoning, health and safety of the workers, access to suppliers and distribution channels

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Albert coerced into signing contract with threats. Contract is:
feedback: Contracts signed under duress are voidable by the coerced party.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An estate at will
option_b: is terminated only if so stated in the lessee's last will and testament
option_c: terminates on the death of lessor or lessee
option_d: terminates on the date specified in the lease agreement
feedback: The estate at will, also called a tenancy at will, has no definite expiration date and hence no "renewal" cycle. The landlord and tenant agree that the tenancy will have no specified termination date, provided rent is paid on time and other lease conditions are met. For example, a son leases a house to his father and mother "forever," or until they want to move. The estate at will is terminated by proper notice, or by the death of either party

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An interest in real estate is best defined as ownership of
option_a: the full bundle of rights to real property
option_c: one or more of the bundle of rights to real property
option_d: the right to possession and use of real property
feedback: An interest in real estate is ownership of any combination of the bundle of sticks, including the rights to possess, use, transfer, encumber and exclude

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An interest in real estate is best defined as ownership of
option_a: the full bundle of rights to real property
option_c: one or more of the bundle of rights to real property
option_d: the right to possession and use of real property
feedback: An interest in real estate is ownership of any combination of the bundle of sticks, including the rights to possess, use, transfer, encumber and exclude

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Encumbrances and police powers are
option_a: interests that do not include possession
feedback: An encumbrance enables a non-owning party to restrict the owner's bundle of rights. Government entities also have nonpossessory interests in real estate which act to control land use for the public good within the entity's jurisdiction. The prime example of public interest is police power

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Encumbrances and police powers are
option_a: interests that do not include possession
feedback: An encumbrance enables a non-owning party to restrict the owner's bundle of rights. Government entities also have nonpossessory interests in real estate which act to control land use for the public good within the entity's jurisdiction. The prime example of public interest is police power

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What distinguishes a freehold estate from a leasehold estate?
option_a: A freehold includes the right to dispose or use
option_b: A leasehold endures only for a specific period of time
option_d: A leasehold is subject to government restrictions
feedback: Freehold estates are ownership estates, and their duration cannot be determined. A leasehold endures only for a specific period of time

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Exclusive agency listing; owner sells independently. Broker receives:
feedback: Exclusive agency: broker earns commission only if they procure the buyer.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What distinguishes a freehold estate from a leasehold estate?
option_a: A freehold includes the right to dispose or use
option_b: A leasehold endures only for a specific period of time
option_d: A leasehold is subject to government restrictions
feedback: Freehold estates are ownership estates, and their duration cannot be determined. A leasehold endures only for a specific period of time

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The highest form of ownership interest one can acquire in real estate is the
feedback: The fee simple freehold estate is the highest form of ownership interest one can acquire in real estate. It includes the complete bundle of rights, and the tenancy is unlimited, with certain exceptions. The fee simple absolute estate is a perpetual estate that is not conditioned by stipulated or restricted uses

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The highest form of ownership interest one can acquire in real estate is the
feedback: The fee simple freehold estate is the highest form of ownership interest one can acquire in real estate. It includes the complete bundle of rights, and the tenancy is unlimited, with certain exceptions. The fee simple absolute estate is a perpetual estate that is not conditioned by stipulated or restricted uses

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The distinguishing feature of a defeasible fee simple estate is that
option_a: it can be passed on to heirs
option_b: it has no restrictions on use
option_c: the estate may revert to a grantor or heirs if the prescribed use changes
feedback: The defeasible fee estate is perpetual, provided the usage conforms to stated conditions. Essential characteristics are: the property must be used for a certain purpose or under certain conditions; and, if the use changes or if prohibited conditions are present, the estate reverts to the previous grantor of the estate

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Listing where owner must pay commission even if self-sells:
feedback: Exclusive right-to-sell guarantees broker commission regardless of who sells.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The distinguishing feature of a defeasible fee simple estate is that
option_a: it can be passed on to heirs
option_b: it has no restrictions on use
option_c: the estate may revert to a grantor or heirs if the prescribed use changes
feedback: The defeasible fee estate is perpetual, provided the usage conforms to stated conditions. Essential characteristics are: the property must be used for a certain purpose or under certain conditions; and, if the use changes or if prohibited conditions are present, the estate reverts to the previous grantor of the estate

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Upon the death of the owner, a life estate passes to
option_a: the original owner or other named person
feedback: Upon the death of the owner or other named individual, the estate passes to the original owner or another named party

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: By signing exclusive right-to-sell listing, seller contracts with:
feedback: Listing is a bilateral employment contract between seller and broker.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: How is a conventional life estate created?
option_a: It happens automatically when title transfers unless a fee simple is specifically claimed
option_b: A fee simple owner grants the life estate to a life tenant
option_c: It is created by judicial action
option_d: It is created by a statutory period of adverse possession
feedback: A conventional life estate is created by grant from a fee simple property owner to the grantee, the life tenant

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: How is a conventional life estate created?
option_a: It happens automatically when title transfers unless a fee simple is specifically claimed
option_b: A fee simple owner grants the life estate to a life tenant
option_c: It is created by judicial action
option_d: It is created by a statutory period of adverse possession
feedback: A conventional life estate is created by grant from a fee simple property owner to the grantee, the life tenant

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Buyer withdraws offer before acceptance; what happens?
option_a: Seller entitled to half earnest money
option_c: Seller may sue for specific performance
feedback: No contract exists until acceptance; earnest money returned.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What distinguishes a pur autre vie life estate from an ordinary life estate?
option_a: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of the grantor
option_b: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of the grantee
option_c: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of a person other than the grantee
option_d: The pur autre vie estate cannot revert to the grantor
feedback: A pur autre vie life estate endures over the lifetime of a third person, after which the property passes from the tenant holder to the original grantor (reversion) or a third party (remainderman)

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What distinguishes a pur autre vie life estate from an ordinary life estate?
option_a: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of the grantor
option_b: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of the grantee
option_c: The pur autre vie estate endures only for the lifetime of a person other than the grantee
option_d: The pur autre vie estate cannot revert to the grantor
feedback: A pur autre vie life estate endures over the lifetime of a third person, after which the property passes from the tenant holder to the original grantor (reversion) or a third party (remainderman)

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following life estates is created by someone other than the owner?
feedback: A legal life estate is created by state law as opposed to being created by a property owner's agreement

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is true of a homestead?
option_a: A homestead interest cannot be conveyed by one spouse
option_b: A homestead interest cannot be passed to the children of the head of household
option_c: A homestead interest is a form of conventional life estate
option_d: A homestead is a primary or secondary residence occupied by a family
feedback: A homestead is one's principal residence. Homestead laws protect family members against losing their homes to general creditors attempting to collect on debts. Homestead laws generally provide that: the homestead interest cannot be conveyed by one spouse; both spouses must sign the deed conveying homestead property

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Buyer dies before notified of seller acceptance. Effect?
feedback: Offer terminates upon death before acceptance is communicated.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is true of a homestead?
option_a: A homestead interest cannot be conveyed by one spouse
option_b: A homestead interest cannot be passed to the children of the head of household
option_c: A homestead interest is a form of conventional life estate
option_d: A homestead is a primary or secondary residence occupied by a family
feedback: A homestead is one's principal residence. Homestead laws protect family members against losing their homes to general creditors attempting to collect on debts. Homestead laws generally provide that: the homestead interest cannot be conveyed by one spouse; both spouses must sign the deed conveying homestead property

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An executed contract is:
feedback: Fully performed by all parties.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A voidable contract remains binding until:
feedback: Voidable contracts are valid until the affected party rescinds.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Dower refers to
option_a: joint tenancy of husband and wife
option_b: a wife's life estate interest in her husband's property
option_d: a child's life estate interest in his or her parents' homestead
feedback: Dower refers to a wife's life estate interest in the husband's property. When the husband dies, the wife can make a claim to portions of the decedent's property. Curtesy is the identical right enjoyed by the husband in a deceased wife's property. Property acquired under dower laws is owned by the surviving spouse for the duration of his or her lifetime

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Dower refers to
option_a: joint tenancy of husband and wife
option_b: a wife's life estate interest in her husband's property
option_d: a child's life estate interest in his or her parents' homestead
feedback: Dower refers to a wife's life estate interest in the husband's property. When the husband dies, the wife can make a claim to portions of the decedent's property. Curtesy is the identical right enjoyed by the husband in a deceased wife's property. Property acquired under dower laws is owned by the surviving spouse for the duration of his or her lifetime

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is an illustration of the legal concept of elective share?
option_a: A surviving spouse places a lien on a debtor's property
option_b: A widow who was excluded from a will makes a claim to a portion of the couple's principal residence
option_c: A spouse who loses her home because of her husband's gambling debt sues in court for exemption from the debt
option_d: A widower whose spouse died without a will sues to change the provisions of the will
feedback: Elective share is a state-level statute enabling a surviving spouse to make a minimum claim to the deceased spouse's real and personal property in place of the provisions for such property in the decedent's will

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is an illustration of the legal concept of elective share?
option_a: A surviving spouse places a lien on a debtor's property
option_b: A widow who was excluded from a will makes a claim to a portion of the couple's principal residence
option_c: A spouse who loses her home because of her husband's gambling debt sues in court for exemption from the debt
option_d: A widower whose spouse died without a will sues to change the provisions of the will
feedback: Elective share is a state-level statute enabling a surviving spouse to make a minimum claim to the deceased spouse's real and personal property in place of the provisions for such property in the decedent's will

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A one-year lease on a house has expired, but the tenant continues sending monthly rent checks to the owner, and the owner accepts them. What kind of leasehold estate exists?
feedback: In an estate from period-to-period, also called a periodic tenancy, the tenancy period automatically renews for an indefinite period of time, subject to timely payment of rent. At the end of a tenancy period, if the landlord accepts another regular payment of rent, the leasehold is considered to be renewed for another period

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: Property managers will employ which of the following strategies as part of their risk management plan
feedback: The property manager and owner will employ one or several of these risk management strategies depending on the nature of the risk, the size of the potential loss, the likelihood of the occurrence, and costs

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A one-year lease on a house has expired, but the tenant continues sending monthly rent checks to the owner, and the owner accepts them. What kind of leasehold estate exists?
feedback: In an estate from period-to-period, also called a periodic tenancy, the tenancy period automatically renews for an indefinite period of time, subject to timely payment of rent. At the end of a tenancy period, if the landlord accepts another regular payment of rent, the leasehold is considered to be renewed for another period

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: If a lien covers all properties of the debtor in that county where it is recorded, it is known as:
feedback: General Liens applies to and burdens all of the owner's real property, but only in the county of recording. [Examples include tax liens or liens resulting from a court judgment.]. Therefore a judgment lien is not the answer because it is one of many other types of general liens.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Generally, construction onto the property of another, like a wall fence, building, etc. is known as what?
feedback: When you think of the word encroachment you think of somebody stepping over a line from their side on to yours. An encroachment is the physical intrusion of a structure or improvement on the land of another. Examples can also include a fence or driveway over the property line. Remember your property line extends upwards to the sky therefore a tree hanging over on to your property is encroachment as well. An encroachment can be found by a survey. It is a form of trespass, therefore when an encroachment occurs you may be able to sue your neighbor for trespassing.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would be considered real property?
feedback: Real property is that category of property that is immovable by law. Examples include load bearing walls, stock in a mutual water company, and minerals.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Option given for purchase of property is:
feedback: Option keeps the offer open; seller cannot revoke.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Personal property can be:
option_a: turned into real property, alienated, and/or hypothecated.
feedback: Personal property are items that are generally moveable and do not go with the real estate. Personal property can be turned into real property (such as in a fixture) it can be alienated (you can get rid of it) and it can be hypothecated (pledged as collateral to secure a loan).

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would likely be defined as a fixture?
option_b: A built-in stove in a mobile home
feedback: Fixtures are objects attached to real property or incorporated into the land which are now considered real property. To determine whether or not an item of Personal Property has become a Fixture, use the Maria test: Method, Adaptability, Relationship, Intention, Agreement. The size, cost and time of attachment are NOT factors. Mobile homes are generally not considered real property because they're not attached to the land, so a stove in a mobile home is not usually a fixture.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would be most likely found in a business office?
option_a: None of the other options are correct
feedback: Trade Fixtures are items that are attached to the property, but they are not considered real property because they are in a business office. They can leave with the person as they do not stay with the property. Trade fixtures are not appurtenant to the land because they are personal property. Always keep in mind to choose the best answer.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Zena granted the property to Ungrilda, but because she knew that Ungrilda had a drinking problem she told her that there was a condition. The condition was that the property would be forfeited if alcohol is sold on the premise. Ungrilda would have which of the following estates?
feedback: A Fee Simple Defeasible puts conditions on the use of a property. For example, an owner could lose title if the condition that no alcohol is sold on the property is violated.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The holder of a life estate, based on her own life, may do all of the following, except:
feedback: A Life Estate is a type of Freehold Estate which has a duration measured by the length of someone's life, after which the estate passes to a reversionary or remainder interest-holder. A life estate measured by the holder's life cannot be devised, but it can be borrowed against, leased or sold (though the purchaser will lose any rights when the life-estate holder dies and the property passes to the reversionary or remainder interest-holder).

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Placing two or more contiguous units under common ownership, for the purpose of increasing the value per unit, is known as:
feedback: Plottage is the common ownership of two or more units or lots next to each other which makes the value of the new combined property greater than the values of each individual lot added together. Assemblage is the process by which plottage occurs.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A situation in which the tenant's lease has expired, the tenant is no longer paying rent and is living on the property without permission is known as:
feedback: In an estate at sufferance, the tenancy is by a lessee who lawfully occupies the landlord's property, but then continues to occupy the property improperly after his rights have expired and without permission of the landlord. Think of it this way: The landlord and the estate are SUFFERING because rent is not being paid. The other types of estates mentioned here are actual lease agreements, but this is a situation of one party being unwilling to abide by the terms of any agreements previously reached.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The right-of-way to access telephone lines is a common example of:
feedback: An easement in gross grants a company or individual limited rights to access another's property for a specific purpose. In this case installing, maintaining and repairing telephone lines.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Title held by a husband and wife may include all of the following forms of ownership EXCEPT:
feedback: Severalty means sole ownership and, other than forming a corporation, spouses can only hold property in common through some form of joint ownership.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Mary owns one parcel of land and is trying to show that exceptional circumstances exist that justify using the land in a way that is prohibited by current zoning laws. Her intended use would not be detrimental to the public. She would probably petition the planning commission for a:
feedback: A petition for variance is a method by which a landowner can try to show that unique circumstances exist and that his land should be allowed an exception to present zoning laws. Variance is done lot by lot.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The difference between a judgment lien and a mechanic's lien is:
option_a: mechanic's liens are created by statute.
option_b: mechanic's liens may take priority earlier than the date they are recorded.
option_d: judgment liens are not enforceable until recorded.
feedback: Mechanic's liens take priority as of the first day of construction, and may even take priority earlier than their date of recording.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Unless otherwise specified, the rights to minerals which lie beneath the surface of land:
option_a: Cannot be conveyed apart from the real estate surrounding the minerals.
option_b: Are kept by the original grantor;
option_c: Are automatically transferred with the sale of the real property;
feedback: Appurtenances are said to "Run with the Land" and they require no separate conveyance. Appurtenances include Easements, Stock in a Mutual Water Company, Covenants, and Minerals. They are considered real property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Real estate brokers can have difficulty with personal property because ownership rights and identification are often difficult to determine. This is because personal property can:
option_a: be alienated, hypothecated and can become real property.
feedback: Personal Property, also known as "chattel", is a category of property that is generally movable and can be hypothecated (pledged as collateral), alienated (transfer of ownership) and become real property (as a fixture).

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: "Of indefinite duration" is a characteristic which is particularly descriptive of an:
feedback: Fee Simple Estate: Also referred to as an "Estate of Inheritance", a Fee Simple Estate is one type of Freehold Estate, and as such, is indefinite in duration. A Fee Simple Estate can also be sold or inherited, and is not free of Encumbrances. A Fee Simple Absolute is the most interest that one can hold in land.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A freehold estate consists of:
option_c: An estate from period to period;
feedback: Freehold estate is an estate in which ownership is for an indeterminate length of time, that would be "fee estate"; the other options are all examples of less than freehold estates.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The deed restrictions on a subdivision said that the lots must contain a minimum of 15,000 square feet structure. The zoning restrictions said that the lots must contain a minimum of 10,000 square feet structure. Which would prevail in this situation?
feedback: The higher standard of restrictions will usually prevail. For example, if deed restrictions in a subdivision required that each lot contain a minimum 15,000 square feet structure, and the applicable zoning laws required that each lot contain a minimum 10,000 square foot structure, the deed restrictions would prevail. With things like this, you usually go with the option that is more restrictive. NOTE - This question asks about the MINIMUM square footage of a structure, so the more restrictive number is 15,000 sq. ft. If the question asked about the MAXIMUM square footage of a structure, the more restrictive number would be 10,000 sq. ft.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: When the rent charged is based on the gross sales of a business, the lease is correctly termed:
feedback: Percentage Leases: In this type of lease, the amount of rent paid by the lessee is determined by calculating a percentage based on the gross receipts of the lessee's business (i.e., a commercial parking lot).

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: True concerning an option contract:
feedback: Optionor cannot revoke; optionee not obligated; rights follow rules stated.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A family rents a vacation home from July 5, 2020 to January 31, 2021. Their tenancy would properly be classified as:
feedback: An estate for years is a lease for any fixed period of time, for example a lease that commences on July 5th, 2020 and terminates on January 31, 2021. An estate for years already has a fixed end date when the lease begins  this separates it from a periodic tenancy, in which the lease automatically renews at the end of each agreed-upon period (usually from 1 week to 1 year) until either the tenant or landlord gives notice of termination.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: As used in real property law, "tenancy" most nearly means:
option_a: The mode or method of holding title to real property by an owner.
option_b: A tenant who has acted in excess of their rights.
option_c: The obstinancy of a holdover tenant.
feedback: Title vesting, also referred to as "Tenancy", simply refers to the method or mode of holding title to real property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Mr. Smith owns real property in severalty. This term is used to legally describe ownership by:
feedback: Ownership in severalty is a type of ownership, also known as separate ownership by one individual (or one corporation).

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: If five people own property as joint tenants, which of the following is true?
option_a: Each co-owner has a separate legal title to his undivided interest
option_b: They do not need to own equal interest in the property
option_c: If one of the owner dies, his heirs become tenants in common with the surviving co-owners
option_d: There is still only one title to the whole property
feedback: Joint Tenancy can be summarized with the word unity: unity of Time, Title, Interest and Possession. The unity of Title means that no matter how many joint tenants there are, there is still only one title to the entire property. The unity of Time means that all co-owners must have received their interest in the property at the same time, the unity of Interest means that all co-owners must have an equal interest (5 owners would mean each owner has 20% interest) and the unity of Possession means that each owner has an equal right to possess the property as the other owners.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: When the preliminary title report reveals the existence of an easement on the property, it indicates that the easement is a(n):
feedback: Anything that limits a person's use of a property is an encumbrance. Easements limit use in that they generally prohibit any kind of permanent structure on the area in question. For example, if a homeowner wanted to build a swimming pool in an area of his back yard and the local sewer company had an easement for pipes running under that area, he would have to find another location for his pool, even if it was not as desirable.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: All of the following are examples of police power EXCEPT:
feedback: Restrictive covenants are private matters established and enforced by residents on a voluntary basis. Police powers, on the other hand, are imposed by governments and impact all affected persons.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In determining whether an item is a fixture, which of the following would not be considered?
option_b: intention of the parties when the fixture was installed
feedback: Please be aware of words like "not" or "except" in the question. Fixtures are objects that are attached to real property or incorporated into the land which are now considered real property; in determining whether or not an item of Personal Property has become a Fixture. Use the Maria test (Method Adaptability Relationship Intention Agreement)  the size, cost and time of attachment are NOT factors.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An item which was personal property but has been attached to and become part of the real property is known as:
feedback: A fixture is personal property that is now considered real property. For example, a new cooktop is personal property until it is installed, at which point it becomes a fixture. The way to test if something is a fixture is to use the acronym MARIA. MARIA stands for Method, Adaptability, Relationship, Intention and Agreement.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is not required for obtaining an easement by prescription?
option_a: A hostile use which is adverse to the true owner
option_b: Open notorious use which is continuous
feedback: Easement by Prescription obtained by open, notorious, hostile, and continuous use. Confrontation with the owner is not a factor. Easement by prescription can be terminated for non-use.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Any claim or liability attached to a property is called:
feedback: Remember that an encumbrance is any claim against a property. Restrictions, liens, and buyer's claims (also known as a vendee's lien) are all FORMS of encumbrances.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In an appurtenant easement, the party that benefits is known as the:
feedback: The person whose land is benefited by the easement, and is the DOMINATING PARTY, is considered the dominant tenement. The servient tenement is the opposite of this. Leaser and lessee involve lease agreements, and have nothing to do with easements.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The rights of a landowner to use waters of an adjacent stream or river are known as:
feedback: While both riparian and littoral rights have to do with water, remember that riparian rights have to do with FLOWING bodies of water, such as rivers. (River and Riparian both start with 'r'.) Zoning and seisin deal with land control.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following methods of creating an easement would result in an easement which could most easily be terminated for non-use?
option_b: A valid quitclaim deed from the current owner
feedback: Easement by Prescription (requires open, notorious, hostile, and continuous use). Confrontation with the owner is not a factor. Easement by prescription can be terminated for non-use.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: The property owner will use this type of insurance coverage to safeguard against theft, vandalism, burglary, illness and accident, and machinery damage
feedback: Many types of insurance allows the shifting of liability away from the owner. Casualty coverage applies for specific risks, such as theft, vandalism, burglary, illness and accident, and machinery damage

subject: null
question_text: null
feedback: null

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Dan sold his land which had an easement appurtenant for a road. The deed which the buyer received contained an adequate description of the land, but made no reference to the easement. The buyer:
option_a: Loses the easement to the servient tenement
option_b: Has the same right to the easement as the seller did
option_d: Takes title to property which is landlocked
feedback: An appurtenant easement is an easement that is annexed to the ownership of one parcel and allows the owner the use of the neighbor's land. The buyer has the same rights as the seller.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Private restrictions on real property can be written either as a condition or a covenant. Which of the following is most correct concerning such restrictions?
option_a: Violation of covenants can result in penalties which are more severe and can result in loss of money only.
option_b: Violation of a condition can result in penalties which are more severe and can result in loss of title.
option_c: They become public restrictions upon recording.
option_d: Violations of such penalties are a criminal offense.
feedback: When private restrictions take the form of conditions in written agreements, any breach of such conditions can result in more severe penalties than if they were in the form of a covenant. Often violations result in a loss of title, such as with a fee simple defeasible estate.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Following proper action by the authorities, the zoning on a vacant lot was changed from "commercial" to "residential." Such an action is called:
feedback: Down zoning refers to zoning changes that convert commercially zoned properties into ones zoned for residential use.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following would be defined as a fixture in the law?
feedback: Fixtures are objects attached to real property or incorporated into the land which are now considered real property. Use MARIA: Method, Adaptability, Relationship, Intention, Agreement.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Re-zoning a section of town and changing its lawful use for new development from commercial to residential use is known as:
feedback: Down zoning usually reduces the economic value of the property involved.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: An estate in real property:
option_a: Will always run on into perpetuity.
option_b: Must only carry the right to immediate possession.
option_c: May exist along with other estates in the same property.
option_d: Can come into being only through a grant.
feedback: An estate in real property may exist along with other estates in the same property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A tenant rented an apartment, signing a 15-month lease. After the lease expired, the tenant stayed in the apartment without the landlord's permission. What kind of leasehold does the tenant have?
feedback: Holdover tenancy is a tenancy whereby a lessee retains possession of leased property after the lease has expired without the landlord's permission.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following terms BEST describes personal property that, by its attachment to real property, is regarded as real estate?
feedback: Fixtures are objects attached to real property or incorporated into the land which are now considered real property. In determining whether or not an item of Personal Property has become a Fixture, use the Maria test (Method Adaptability Relationship Intention Agreement). The size, cost and time of attachment are not factors. Examples of fixtures would be garage doors, a pool covering or a chandelier.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following terms BEST describes personal property that, by its attachment to real property, is regarded as real estate?
feedback: Fixtures are objects attached to real property or incorporated into the land and which are now considered real property. Use MARIA.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Keith and Ralph bought a house as tenants in common. If Keith dies, which of the following statements about ownership of the house is CORRECT?
option_a: Ralph holds life estate ownership in Keith's share of the property.
option_b: Ralph holds fee simple ownership in Keith's share of the property.
option_c: Ralph retains his interest in the property and Keith's share goes to his heirs.
option_d: It automatically becomes tenancy in severalty.
feedback: There is no right of survivorship when property is owned as tenants in common. Keith's share passes to his heirs.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: When a husband and wife take title to a property as tenants in common, they have elected a form of ownership that allows for:
option_a: the option of unequal shares of ownership
option_c: everything to be equal as per ownership of the property
feedback: Tenancy in common allows unequal shares. Unity of Possession is required; no automatic right of survivorship.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In a condominium apartment project, the BEST term to describe swimming pools and laundry rooms for the use of unit owners is:
feedback: Common elements are portions of a condominium development owned in common by unit owners.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Nelson owned two acres of land. He sold one acre to Andy and reserved for himself an appurtenant easement over Andy's land for ingress and egress. Andy's land:
option_a: can be cleared of the easement when Nelson sells the withheld acre to a third party.
option_b: is subject to an easement in gross.
feedback: Andy's land interest is the one burdened by the easement, making it the servient property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Ginny owns 50 acres of land with 500 feet of frontage on a desirable recreational lake. She wishes to subdivide the parcel into sellable lots, but she wants to retain control over the lake frontage while allowing lot owners to have access to the lake. Which of the following types of access rights would provide the greatest protection for a prospective purchaser?
feedback: Appurtenant easements afford the most protection since they pass with the property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Many states determine the order of water rights according to which users of the water hold a recorded beneficial use permit. This allocation of water rights is determined by:
feedback: In water-scarce states, the doctrine of prior appropriation applies: first in time, first in right.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The type of lease that is for an indefinite amount of time and may be cancelled by the landlord or tenant at any time is known as:
option_d: an estate from period to period.
feedback: An estate at will continues indefinitely and may be terminated by either party.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The local utility company dug up Bob's garden to install a natural gas line. The company claimed it had a valid easement and proved it through the county records. Bob claimed the easement was not valid because he did not know about it. The easement:
option_a: Was an appurtenant easement owned by the utility company.
option_b: Was not valid because Bob was not informed of its existence when he purchased the property.
option_c: Was valid even though the owner did not know about it.
option_d: Was not valid because it had not been used during the entire time that Bob owned the property.
feedback: Easements grant access and attach to the property even if the owner is unaware.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Peter owned two acres of land. He sold one acre to Chaim and reserved for himself an appurtenant easement over Chaim's land for ingress and egress. Chaim's land:
option_a: Can be cleared of the easement when Peter sells the withheld acre to a third party.
option_b: Is subject to an easement in gross.
feedback: Chaim's land interest is burdened by the easement; it is the servient property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Leslie and Vivian bought a store building and took title as joint tenants. Vivian died testate. Leslie now owns the store:
option_b: as a joint tenant with rights of survivorship.
option_d: as a tenant in common with Vivian's heirs.
feedback: Joint tenancy means that upon death, the surviving tenant owns the property in severalty (sole ownership).

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Andrew lives in an apartment building. The land and structures are owned by a corporation, with one mortgage loan covering the entire property. Like the other residents, Andrew owns stock in the corporation and has a lease on his apartment. This type of ownership is called a(n):
feedback: Cooperative ownership involves stock in a corporation that entitles the holder to lease a unit.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In a gift of a parcel of real estate, one of the two owners was given an undivided 60 percent interest and the other received an undivided 40 percent interest. The two owners hold their interests as what?
feedback: Because the interests were acquired as a gift and not simultaneously with survivorship intent, they are tenants in common.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following liens does not need to be recorded to be valid?
feedback: Liens by taxing authorities do not need to be recorded.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A tenant's lease has expired, but the tenant did not vacate the premises or negotiate a renewal lease. The landlord has declared that he does not want the tenant to remain in the building. This type of occupancy is referred to as an estate:
feedback: Estate at sufferance occurs when a tenant stays beyond the lease term without permission.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The right to control one's property includes all of the following EXCEPT:
option_a: the right to enjoy pride of ownership.
option_b: the right to invite people on the property for a political fund-raiser.
option_c: the right to erect "no trespassing" signs.
option_d: the right to exclude the utilities meter reader.
feedback: Utility companies are granted easements to enter property; owners cannot exclude them.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is/are considered to be personal property?
feedback: Furniture, rugs, lamps, and other portable items that are not attached constitute personal property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The owner of a condominium unit learns that a neighbor has failed to pay his real estate taxes. If this neighbor does not pay the taxes:
option_a: a lien can be filed against the condominium, including all of the units.
option_b: a lien can be filed against all the common areas of the condominium.
option_c: a lien can be filed against the neighbor's unit and his percentage of the common elements.
option_d: the taxing authority can order the condominium to be dissolved.
feedback: A lien is filed against the individual unit and that owner's share of the common elements.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is an encumbrance?
option_b: All of the other options are correct
feedback: Encumbrances include anything affecting or limiting title: mortgages, liens, easements, leases, restrictions.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Needing to complete a route for the new highway the state tries to buy Jones' ranch. He refuses to sell though. The state may go to court and ask that his ranch be condemned, allowing the land to be purchased. This would be an example of the state using its right of:
feedback: Eminent domain is the state's right to take land for public use. The government's right of eminent domain is enforced through condemnation, which results in just compensation being paid to the landowner whose land is being taken.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: One example of an easement in gross is the right of:
option_a: Utility companies to access a property in order to maintain wires or pipes
option_b: Owners to use the neighbor's backyard for their own recreation
option_c: Owners to cut across their neighbor's property
option_d: A roofing company working on repairs to store some of their equipment in the neighbor's yard
feedback: Public utility companies are given access over properties for the purpose of maintenance and repair of necessary lines, pipes, etc.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The holder of a life estate can do all of the following EXCEPT:
feedback: A life estate only exists for the life of its owner or the life of some other designated person. NOTE: A life estate is OWNERSHIP that is based on the life of someone. They hold the full bundle of rights for as long as they are alive. If this were a life estate that was based on the life of another, it would have been called a life estate pur autre vie.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Bill owns an easement in Joe's property/land. Bill is not a property owner. What kind of easement does Bill have?
feedback: An easement in gross is the limited right of one person (or party) to use another's land (servient estate) when such right is not created for the benefit of any land owned by the owner of the easement. In this case there is no dominant estate because the easement attaches personally to the owner, not a parcel of land.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Freehold estate includes:
feedback: A freehold estate is an estate in which you have exclusive rights to enjoy the possession of a property for an undefined length of time. In contrast, a less than freehold estate is held for a fixed, defined period. One type of freehold estate is a life estate. The other options are all examples of a less than freehold estate.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A, B, and C are co-owners of real property in joint tenancy. C sells his interest to D and, shortly thereafter, C dies. If C had also executed a will, leaving all of his property to his sole heir (whom we shall call E), title to the property would vest as:
option_a: A and B as joint tenants, together with E as a tenant in common
option_b: A and B as joint tenants, together with D as a tenant in common
option_c: A, B and E as joint tenants
option_d: A, B, and D as tenants in common
feedback: It is the right of any joint tenant to sell his share during ownership. In the example, the sale created a tenancy in common between D and the other joint tenants. The death of C had no effect as he died after the transfer, and E is irrelevant because C cannot will a joint tenancy share.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following does not "go with the land"?
feedback: An easement in gross is held by a person or entity, not a property, so it does not "go with the land". Typically an easement is used to go to another property. With an easement in gross, there is no other property to go to. Therefore it would not go with the land because there is no land.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The four unities of joint tenancy are
feedback: The four unities to a joint tenancy are: Time, Title, Interest and Possession. "TTIP"

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: All of the following statements are true about zoning EXCEPT that:
option_a: zoning laws are concerned with regulating property values.
option_c: violations of zoning ordinances may render title unmarketable.
option_d: zoning laws are generally enforced through building permits.
feedback: Zoning laws are NOT retroactive. This means that if zoning changes, any existing properties are generally "grandfathered" in under a non-conforming use permit.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A judgment will be most effective in the county where the:
feedback: A judgment has no legal effect until recorded, and then only to the extent it is recorded in a county in which the defendant owns real property.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What does the term "short rate" refer to?
feedback: A higher periodic rate charged for a shorter term than that originally contracted. The increased premium charged by an insurance company on early cancellation of a policy to compensate the insurer for the fact that the original rate charged was calculated on the full period of the policy.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: a recorded deed:
option_b: all of the other options are correct
feedback: Delivery presumes acknowledgement and gives constructive notice. Recording establishes presumption of delivery.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The four unities of joint tenancy are:
feedback: The four unities to a joint tenancy are: Time, Title, Interest and Possession. "TTIP" is an easy acronym to remember for these.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: If one uses the words "of definite duration" to describe a lease, it is most likely a(n):
feedback: An estate for years always will have a definite termination date which makes it a definite duration time. For example, when you see two dates such as January 5 to March 4th, that is an estate for years.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Two men are co-owners in fee simple of a small office building. One of the men dies intestate and leaves nothing to be distributed to his heirs.
