question_text: Title to a certain real property is being held in a trust as collateral for a loan. What kind of deed will the trustee use to convey the title to the borrower when the loan terms are satisfied?
feedback: On satisfaction of the loan terms, the trustee uses a reconveyance deed to convey the property back to the borrower

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is a documentary stamp tax?
option_a: A transfer tax based on the price of the property being conveyed
option_b: A tax a title company must pay in order to examine title records in the recorder's office
option_c: A tax collected by attorneys and paid to the state when transfer documents are prepared
option_d: A tax on stamps used to certify the authenticity of a conveyance
feedback: State law usually requires payment of a documentary stamp tax on a conveyance of real property. The tax is based on the actual price of the property conveyed, thus enabling taxing authorities to ascertain current market value for ad valorem tax purposes. Payment of the tax is evidenced on the deed

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The property transferred by a will is called the
feedback: The property transferred by the will is the devise

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is true of a nuncupative will?
option_a: It is in the testator's handwriting
option_b: Generally, it does not need to be signed in order to be valid
option_c: It is valid for the transfer of real property, even if it is not witnessed
option_d: Generally, it is not valid for the transfer of real property
feedback: A nuncupative will is generally not valid for the transfer of real property

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The court proceeding that generally settles a decedent's estate is called
feedback: A court proceeding called probate generally settles a decedent's estate, whether the person has died testate (having left a valid will) or intestate (having failed to do so)

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: If an owner of real property dies without leaving a will and with no legal heirs, what will generally happen to the property?
option_a: It will revert to the state or county by escheat
option_b: It will transfer to the decedent's executor
option_c: It will be divided equally among adjoining property owners
option_d: It will become a public easement
feedback: In the absence of heirs, title transfers to the state or county by escheat

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A municipality wants to build a water treatment facility, and requires some privately owned land to do so.  What procedure enables the municipality to buy the property, even against the owner's wishes?
feedback: The government uses eminent domain to condemn and purchase all properties abutting the thoroughfare in order to complete the construction project

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: One of the conditions an adverse possessor must meet in order to obtain legal title to a property is
option_a: openly possessing and claiming the property without the owner's consent
option_b: occupying the property without an occupancy permit
option_c: using the property intermittently and without permission over a period of years
option_d: building a permanent structure on the property
feedback: To claim legal title, the adverse possessor must maintain a consistent claim of hostile possession, which is a claim to ownership and possession regardless of the owner's claims or consent

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A buyer has signed a contract to purchase a property, but is uncertain of the condition of the title.  Who is legally responsible for knowing the condition of the title?
feedback: The buyer is legally responsible for knowing the condition of title, since it is a matter of public record

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A break in the chain of title to a property results in
feedback: Chain of title refers to the succession of property owners of record dating back to the original grant of title from the state to a private party. If there is a missing link in the chronology of owners, or if there was a defective conveyance, the chain is said to be broken, resulting in a clouded title to the property

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A lender's title insurance policy generally protects
option_a: the seller against liability for conveying unmarketable title
option_b: the buyer against loss of equity through fraud or a disputed title
option_c: the lender against the possibility that the lender's lien cannot be enforced
option_d: the lender against liability for certifying that the title records have been searched
feedback: A common title insurance policy is the lender's policy, which protect the lender's interests in the property. Thus, a lender who holds an $80,000 mortgage on a property will obtain protection worth $80,000 against the possibility that the lender's lien cannot be enforced

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A standard owner's title insurance policy generally protects
option_a: the owner against loss of title in a foreclosure suit
option_b: the owner against liabilities and losses resulting from title defects
option_c: lienholders against loss of their collateral
option_d: the seller against liability for conveying title with unrecorded defects
feedback: Standard owner's title insurance policy coverage protects against title defects such as incompetent grantors, invalid deeds, fraudulent transaction documents, and defects in the chain of title

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A central goal of public land use planning is to
option_a: balance individual property rights with the community's welfare
option_b: develop an accord between property owners and tenants
option_c: impede development by for-profit developers and construction contractors
option_d: subordinate private interests to the public good
feedback: Good land use planning has as its goals the preservation of property values, promotion of the highest and best us of property, a balance between individual property rights and public good, to keep growth within infrastructure capabilities, and incorporation of a community consensus into regulation and planning

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The best definition of a master plan is
option_a: an annual review of all land use permits and zones
option_b: a comprehensive analysis of existing land use patterns in a market
option_c: a state or regional land use law requiring compliance on a county-by-county basis
option_d: a fusion of land use laws and local land use objectives and strategies
feedback: The master plan fuses state and regional land use laws with local land use objectives that correspond to the municipality's social and economic conditions

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The principal mechanism for implementing a master plan is
feedback: Zoning is the primary tool by which cities and counties regulate land use and implement their respective master plans

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Zoning, building codes, and environmental restrictions are forms of local land use control known as
feedback: At the local level, county and city governments control land use through the authority known as police power. The most common expressions of police power are county and municipal zoning

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: If a municipality exerts its power of eminent domain against a certain property owner, what happens?
option_a: The owner must pay higher property taxes or give up the property
option_b: The owner must cede an easement without receiving any compensation
option_d: The owner must sell the property or grant an easement to the municipality for just compensation
feedback: Where necessary, the local government or municipality may force property owners to sell their property through the power of eminent domain

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In most jurisdictions, the master plan is managed by
feedback: In most jurisdictions, a planning commission or board comprised of officials appointed by the government's legislative entity handles the planning function

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: The lease used when the tenant agrees to pay a portion or all of the operating expenses is known as a
feedback: Under a net lease, the tenant pays rent plus some or all of the operating expenses attributable to the rented space. This arrangement is commonly used in office and industrial leases

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Counties and municipalities have the legal right to control land use due to
option_b: delegation of authority by state-level enabling acts
option_d: consensus of the local community through referendum
feedback: The process of land use planning begins when the state legislature enacts laws requiring all counties and municipalities to adopt a land use plan. The state enforces its planning mandates by giving state agencies the power to approve county and local plans

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: To be valid, a local zoning ordinance must
option_a: reasonably promote community health, safety and welfare
option_d: be published periodically in the local newspaper
feedback: Zoning ordinances must be clear in import, apply to all parties equally, and promote health, safety, and welfare of the community in a reasonable manner

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is the fundamental purpose of a building permit?
option_a: To restrict the number of new development projects
option_b: To establish the basis for an inspection
option_d: To ensure that improvements comply with codes
feedback: Local governments enforce zoning ordinances by issuing building permits to those who want to improve, repair, or refurbish a property. To receive a permit, the project must comply with all relevant ordinances and codes

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A primary objective of residential zoning is to
option_a: control the value ranges of homes in a neighborhood
option_c: ensure that only a limited amount of commercial and industrial activity is permitted in a particular residential zone
feedback: Residential zoning regulates density, by limiting the number and size of dwelling units and lots in an area

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A non-profit organization wants to erect a much-needed daycare center in a residential zone. Given other favorable circumstances, the local authorities grant permission by allowing
feedback: A special exception grant authorizes a use that is not consistent with the zoning ordinance in a literal sense, yet is clearly beneficial or essential to the public welfare and does not materially impair other uses in the zone

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A property that conformed with zoning ordinances when it was developed but does not conform to new ordinances is said to be
feedback: An illegal nonconforming use is one that conflicts with ordinances that were in place before the use commenced. For instance, if a motel that was allowed as a legal nonconforming use is sold, and the new owner continues to operate the property as a motel, the motel is now an illegal, nonconforming use

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: One situation in which a zoning board might permit a variance is when
option_a: it would cause the property owner unreasonable hardship to bring the property into compliance with zoning ordinances
option_b: the property owner is the one who brings the variance to the attention of the zoning board
option_c: the variance was caused by a contractor rather than by the property owner
option_d: the property is in conflict with no more than one zoning ordinance
feedback: A zoning variance allows a use that differs from the applicable ordinance for a variety of justifiable reasons, including that compliance will cause unreasonable hardship for the property owner to bring the property in compliance with the zoning regulations

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The approval process for development of multiple properties in an area includes submission of
feedback: The developer of multiple properties in a subdivision must submit a plat of subdivision containing surveyed plat maps and comprehensive building specifications

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A county or municipal authority usually grants a certificate of occupancy for new construction only after
option_a: all contractors have been paid for services
option_b: all work has been completed for at least thirty days
option_c: the construction complies with building codes
option_d: the tax assessor has valued the improvement
feedback: Building inspectors inspect a new development or improvement for code compliance. If the work complies, the municipality or county issues a certificate of occupancy which officially clears the property for occupation and use

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In addition to government entities, organizations that may be able to condemn property under the power of eminent domain include
feedback: Public entities that have the power of eminent domain include public districts, utilities, service corporations, and housing and redevelopment agencies

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A property owner is precluded by deed restriction from developing a thirty foot boat dock. The limitation prompts the owner to sell to another party. The new owner
option_a: is free to build the dock since the next door neighbor built a similar dock two weeks later
option_b: takes title subject to the same restriction
option_c: can build the dock with special permission from the zoning board
option_d: may build, since the restriction is extinguished by the sale
feedback: Deed restrictions attach to the title of the property. Therefore, the restriction passes to the new buyer

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A declaration of restriction in a planned unit development is unlike a deed restriction in that
option_a: it applies only to aesthetic standards of property use
option_b: it attaches to rights rather than interests
option_c: it cannot be terminated by a single individual
option_d: it takes effect only when approved by a homeowners' association
feedback: The declaration of a subdivision, Planned Unit Development, condominium, and commercial or industrial park contains private use restrictions. These have the same legal effect as a deed restriction, as the declaration attaches to the rights in the property. A private party cannot, however, extinguish a declaration restriction by agreement or quitclaim deed

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A deed restriction or declaration of restriction may be enforced by means of a
feedback: A private usage restriction can be enforced by filing for a court injunction

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A distinguishing feature of a deed condition is that
option_a: it gives the grantor the right to re-possess the property if the grantee violates the condition
option_b: it ceases to apply if a violation is allowed to continue for a certain period of time
option_c: it can be filed at any time after title has been transferred
option_d: it restricts who may own the property
feedback: A deed condition may restrict certain uses of a property, much like a deed restriction. However, violation of a deed condition gives the grantor the right to re-take possession of the property and file suit for legal title

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Contract between seller and licensee for commission if buyer procured:
feedback: Both parties promise performance; contract is bilateral and executory until completed.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Public land use planning incorporates long-term usage strategies and growth policies in a
feedback: Public land use planning incorporates long-term usage strategies and growth policies in a land use plan, or master plan. In many states, the process of land use planning begins when the state legislature enacts laws requiring all counties and municipalities to adopt a land use plan

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following identifies the primary objectives of a master plan?
option_a: To support local government and taxation
option_b: To police and enforce zoning ordinances and occupancy rates
option_c: To create job opportunities and business growth within a community
option_d: To control and accommodate social and economic growth
feedback: The primary objectives of a master plan are generally to control and accommodate social and economic growth

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: To formulate a growth strategy, a plan initially forecasts growth trends, then estimates how well the municipality can keep pace with the growth forecast. For example, a growth plan considers
option_c: compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act
option_d: attracting diversity and compliance with the Fair Housing Act
feedback: To formulate a growth strategy, a plan initially forecasts growth trends, then estimates how well the municipality can keep pace with the growth forecast. The outcome is a policy position that limits building permits and development projects to desired growth parameters. A growth plan considers: nature, location and extent of permitted uses, availability of sanitation facilities, adequacy of drainage, waste collection, and potable water systems, adequacy of utilities companies, adequacy and patterns of thoroughfares, housing availability, conservation of natural resources, adequacy of recreational facilities, ability and willingness of the community to absorb new taxes, bond issues, and assessments

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Some areas create ____________ to separate residential areas from commercial and industrial zones
feedback: Residential zoning regulates: density, by limiting the number and size of dwelling units and lots in an area, values and aesthetics, by limiting the type of residences allowed. Some areas adopt buffer zones to separate residential areas from commercial and industrial zones

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Commercial zoning regulates all the following EXCEPT
feedback: Commercial zoning regulates: intensity of usage, by limiting the area of store or office per site area. Intensity regulation is further achieved by minimum parking requirements, setbacks, and building height restrictions

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is the result of a zoning change that leaves existing properties in violation of the new ordinance?
feedback: A nonconforming use is one that clearly differs from current zoning. Usually, nonconforming uses result when a zoning change leaves existing properties in violation of the new ordinance

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following allows use that differs from the applicable ordinance?
feedback: A zoning variance allows a use that differs from the applicable ordinance for a variety of justifiable reasons, including that: compliance will cause unreasonable hardship, the use will not change the essential character of the area, the use does not conflict with the general intent of the ordinance

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following areas are addressed by building codes?
feedback: Building codes typically address: architectural and engineering standards, construction materials standards, building support systems such as life safety, electrical, mechanical, and utility systems

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Building inspectors inspect a new development or improvement for code compliance. If the work complies, the municipality or county issues a
feedback: Building inspectors inspect a new development or improvement for code compliance. If the work complies, the municipality or county issues a certificate of occupancy which officially clears the property for occupation and use

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following describes when a government entity is allowed to acquire privately owned real property for the public good and public use, regardless of the owner's desire to sell or otherwise transfer any interest?
feedback: Eminent domain allows a government entity to purchase a fee, leasehold, or easement interest in privately owned real property for the public good and for public use, regardless of the owner's desire to sell or otherwise transfer any interest. In exchange for the interest, the government must pay the owner "just compensation."

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Property owners in the private sector can regulate land use to some extent through which of the following?
feedback: Property owners in the private sector can regulate land use to some extent through deed restrictions and deed conditions

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A private usage restriction can be enforced by
feedback: A private usage restriction can be enforced by filing for a court injunction. A court can order the violator to cease and desist, or to correct the infraction

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A powdery mineral once commonly used as a fireproof insulating material around pipes is known as
feedback: Asbestos is a powdery mineral once commonly used as a fireproof insulating material around pipes, in floor tiles and linoleum, in siding and roofing, in wallboard, joint compound, and many other applications

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A colorless, odorless, poisonous gas that may result from faulty heating equipment is known as
feedback: Carbon monoxide is a colorless, odorless, poisonous gas that may result from faulty heating equipment

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The cost of constructing a functional equivalent of a subject property is known as
feedback: Reproduction cost is the cost of constructing, at current prices, a precise duplicate of the subject improvements. Replacement cost is the cost of constructing, at current prices and using current materials and methods, a functional equivalent of the subject improvements

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Identify the act that created the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the Council for Environmental Quality, giving them a mandate to establish environmental standards for land use planning
option_c: The Residential Lead-based Paint Hazard Reduction Act (1992, 1996)
feedback: The National Environmental Policy Act (1969) created the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the Council for Environmental Quality, giving them a mandate to establish environmental standards for land use planning

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Identify the act that authorized the EPA to establish air quality standards for industrial land uses as well as for automobile and airplane emissions
feedback: The Clean Air Amendment (1970) authorized the EPA to establish air quality standards for industrial land uses as well as for automobile and airplane emissions

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The Solid Waste Disposal Act regulates which of the following areas?
feedback: The Solid Waste Disposal Act regulates landfills

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act (CERCLA) regulates which of the following areas?
feedback: Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act (CERCLA) regulates hazardous waste disposal

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The Lead-based Paint Act of 1992 requires
option_a: a tenant or tenants agent to test for lead in all rental properties built before 1978
option_b: a seller or sellers agent to disclose known lead problems in properties built before 1978
option_c: a seller to mitigate any known lead problems in properties built before 1978
option_d: a sellers agent to test for lead in all properties
feedback: The Lead-based Paint Act of 1992 requires a seller or sellers agent to disclose known lead problems in properties built before 1978. Further, the 1996 lead-based paint regulation requires sellers or lessors of almost all residential properties built before 1978 to disclose known lead-based paint hazards and provide any relevant records available

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following environmental audits involves remediation?
feedback: A Phase III audit (ESA) involves remediation. Intensive testing, sampling, monitoring, and modeling are applied to design plans for remediation, cleanup, and follow-up monitoring

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: In the implementation of land use controls, why should a community be concerned about the effects on individual property values?
option_a: Preservation of property values is the sole purpose of public land use control
option_b: Sudden increases in property values place a burden on municipal services
option_c: In most communities, taxes based on property values support needed government services
option_d: If property is controlled, values will decline
feedback: The community must keep in mind the effect of government actions on individual property values, since local government is largely supported by taxes based on the value of property

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is the relationship between master planning and zoning ordinances?
option_a: The compilation of zoning ordinances is the master plan
option_b: A master plan eliminates the need for zoning ordinances
option_c: Master planning is a state-level function; zoning is limited to the county level
option_d: Zoning ordinances are a primary means of keeping land use in line with the master plan
feedback: Zoning is the primary tool by which cities and counties regulate land use and implement their respective master plans

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: One of the major considerations in planning where to allow growth is
option_a: the number of developers asking for permits
option_b: the effect of development on traffic patterns
option_c: the median age of the community
option_d: the number of housing starts reported for neighboring communities
feedback: In addition to the quantity of growth, a master plan also defines what type of growth will occur, and where. Major considerations include effect of new developments on traffic patterns and thoroughfares

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: The basic intent of zoning ordinances is to
option_a: establish the basis for public ownership of land for the common good
option_c: specify usage for every parcel within the zoning authority's jurisdiction
feedback: The intent of zoning ordinances is to specify land usage for every parcel within the jurisdiction

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Why do zoning authorities create different types of zones?
option_a: To ensure that a variety of construction types is available in the community
option_b: To separate different types of uses so that they do not interfere with each other
option_c: To preserve low density land uses
option_d: To encourage industrial and commercial users to relocate
feedback: One of the primary applications of zoning power is the separation of residential properties from commercial and industrial uses. Proper design of land use in this manner preserves the aesthetics and value of neighborhoods and promotes the success of commercial enterprises through intelligently located zones

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is a "buffer zone?"
option_a: A transitional zone between a residential zone and a commercial zone
option_b: An area zoned as a public park
option_c: A temporary zone created to allow users to come into compliance with a change in zoning
option_d: A zone around the boundaries of a municipality
feedback: Some areas adopt buffer zones to separate residential areas from commercial and industrial zones

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is meant by "intensity of usage?"
option_a: The number of building lots per acre
option_b: The number of people per square mile
option_c: The number of building permits issued per year within a zoning jurisdiction
option_d: The area of a commercial or industrial facility in relation to the size of the site
feedback: Commercial zoning regulates intensity, or how much commercial activity is permitted in relation to size of the site

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: The “Terms” section of a listing contract shows the
feedback: The “Terms” section of a listing contract shows the compensation the broker will receive

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: How does Planned Unit Development zoning differ from ordinary zoning?
option_a: It applies only to industrial and office parks
option_b: It incorporates a number of different zones within a single property boundary
option_c: It requires that multiple tracts of land be developed according to a single design for efficient use of space
option_d: It requires developers to obtain a separate building permit for every structure
feedback: PUD zoning designed to regulate use of whole tracts of land with a singular design

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following situations is most likely to represent an illegal nonconforming use?
option_a: A homeowner in a residential zone converts her residence to rental offices
option_b: A homeowner builds an attached garage in a neighborhood where all the garages are detached
option_c: A storeowner remodels a storefront in accordance with regulations, and then the zoning is changed to residential
option_d: A new zoning ordinance outlaws two-story additions after a homeowner completes an addition
feedback: An illegal nonconforming use is one that conflicts with ordinances that were in place before the use commenced. Turning an existing building zoned for residential use into a building that does not conform to residential use would be an illegal nonconforming use

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: What is the difference between a variance and a nonconforming use?
option_a: A variance, once granted, is unconditional and permanent
option_b: A variance is granted by the zoning board if the owner has a justifiable reason
option_c: A nonconforming use is allowed if the owner requests it in advance of building
option_d: A nonconforming use violates current zoning, but a variance does not
feedback: A zoning variance allows a use that differs from the applicable ordinance for a variety of justifiable reasons. A nonconforming use is one that clearly differs from current zoning

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: To obtain a change of the zoning for a particular property, for example, from residential to commercial, a property owner would ask the zoning board for a(n)
feedback: In an amendment, a current or potential property owner may petition the zoning board for an outright change in the zoning of a particular property

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Federal Truth-in-Lending Law (3-day right of rescission)
option_a: A loan secured by a second deed of trust on owner-occupied single-family residence after purchase
option_b: FHA or VA loan to purchase a single-family owner-occupied residence
option_d: Conventional purchase-money loan on residential property
feedback: The 3-day right of rescission applies to refinances or second mortgages on owner-occupied single-family homes, not purchase-money loans.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Value placed on property for tax purposes
feedback: Assessed value is used by tax authorities to calculate property taxes.

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: As evidence that building inspectors have found that a structure complies with building codes and is ready for use, the municipality or county issues a(n)
feedback: Building inspectors inspect a new development or improvement for code compliance. If the work complies, the municipality or county issues a certificate of occupancy which officially clears the property for occupation and use

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Which of the following is true of a successful eminent domain proceeding?
option_a: It cancels the property owner's mortgage loan balance
option_b: It leaves the property owner with equitable title in place of legal title
option_c: It extinguishes leases, liens, and other encumbrances on the property taken
option_d: It eliminates the "chain of title" by canceling the original grant
feedback: Transfer by eminent domain of title extinguishes all existing leases, liens, and other encumbrances on the property

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: Among other provisions, the Superfund Act (CERCLA) and Superfund Amendment and Reauthorization Act of 1986 provided that
option_a: large development projects undergo an environmental impact survey immediately after completion of construction
option_b: industrial users of real estate comply with air quality standards
option_c: the EPA would give financial help to homeowners to remedy any hazardous situation resulting from radon, asbestos, or lead-based paint
option_d: parties responsible for improper disposal of hazardous waste could be charged for the cleanup costs
feedback: The Superfund act provided money for hazardous waste disposal and the authority to charge cleanup costs to responsible parties

subject: A2 Real Property
question_text: A property owner acquired a property by means of a deed that included a deed restriction against building any kind of unattached structures on the property.  The owner now wants to build a small office/art studio in a separate structure.  What can the owner do to overcome the restriction?
option_a: Apply to the Zoning Board of Appeals for a special permit
option_b: Have the necessary parties execute a quitclaim deed
option_c: Sue in court to have the deed declared invalid
option_d: Obtain written permission to violate the restriction from the original grantor
feedback: A restriction expressed in a conveyance (deed or lease) of a residential, commercial, or industrial property places limits on the use of the property. Such restrictions are also referred to as "covenants, conditions, and restrictions," or CCRs. A quitclaim deed can terminate a private deed restriction

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: "Let the buyer beware"
feedback: Caveat emptor is a Latin term meaning "let the buyer beware."

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In a fixed-rate, fully amortized loan:
option_a: each mortgage payment reduces the principal by the same amount
option_b: each mortgage payment amount is the same
option_c: the principal amount in each payment is greater than the interest amount
option_d: a balloon payment will be made at the end of the loan
feedback: Fully amortized loans have equal payments. Interest portion declines, principal portion grows over time.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A purchaser negotiates a mortgage loan in which she will make equal monthly payments over a period of 30 years, with the balance of the loan being zero at the end of that term. The purchaser has negotiated a(n)
feedback: Fully-amortized loans have equal payments resulting in a zero balance at the end of the term.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower takes out a mortgage loan with monthly payments of $875.70 for 20 years, and a final payment of $24,095. This is what type of loan?
feedback: A balloon loan has a large final payment not fully amortized over the term.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Clause allowing lender to call entire unpaid balance due upon default:
feedback: Acceleration clause allows lender to demand full payment upon default.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: To have loan due if property is resold, which clause is required?
feedback: Alienation clause (“due-on-sale”) triggers repayment if property changes hands.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Under TRID, a lender must extend the closing how many business days if the APR is increased by more than .125% before closing?
feedback: TRID requires a new three-business-day waiting period if the APR changes by more than 0.125% for fixed-rate loans (0.25% for adjustable-rate loans).

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Fed action that relieves tight money market:
feedback: Lowering reserve requirements increases bank lending, easing tight money.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: 3 days before closing, CD must be provided under:
feedback: TILA/RESPA Integrated Disclosure (TRID) rule requires Closing Disclosure 3 days before closing.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An office building lacks sufficient cooling capability to accommodate modern computer equipment. This is an example of
feedback: Functional obsolescence occurs when a property has outmoded physical or design features which are no longer desirable to current users

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Truth-in-Lending laws apply to:
feedback: TILA governs consumer residential mortgage transactions, not commercial deals.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Constant amortized payment leaving balance at end is:
feedback: Balloon payment: fixed periodic payments with full principal due at loan end.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The lien priority of junior liens can be changed by a lienor's agreement to
feedback: A lienor can change the priority of a junior lien by voluntarily agreeing to subordinate, or lower, the lien's position in the hierarchy

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Among junior liens, the order of priority is generally established according to
feedback: Among junior liens, date of recording determines priority. The rule is: the earlier the recording date of the lien, the higher its priority

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A town has a rapidly growing population, but there are no longer any vacant lots around the lake to build more houses. In this case, it is likely that the price of existing homes on the lake
option_a: will stabilize, since the population must stabilize
option_c: will decline, since no further building can take place
option_d: will not show any predictable movement
feedback: The third critical element of value is a product's availability in relation to demand. The air conditioner is quite valuable if there are only five units in the entire city and everyone is hot. On the other hand, the value of an air conditioner goes down if there are ten thousand units for sale in a 500-person market

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following scenarios describes a license?
option_a: The utility company is granted an easement in gross
option_b: A homeowner drills into her yard in search of mineral rights
option_c: The owner of an easement allows a landlocked neighbor to access the public road
option_d: A new homeowner grants permission for an individual to fish in his pond
feedback: In granting a license, the property owner gives permission to an individual to use the property for a specific and personal use that is non-transferrable and does not attach to the land

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Contract allowing agent commission if anyone (even owner) sells:
feedback: Exclusive right-to-sell: broker earns commission regardless of who sells.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following describes a situation when a special assessment lien is issued?
option_a: When a franchise fails to pay its taxes
option_b: When a deficiency judgment is forgiven
option_c: When an increase in ad valorem taxes is scheduled
option_d: When local improvement projects are funded to build new sewers, roads, or power lines
feedback: The local government imposes taxes to fund projects that benefit that specific municipality

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A deed restriction typically focuses on
option_a: the permitted uses allowed by the property’s zoning
option_b: tax assessment and special assessment limitations
option_c: how the land will be used
feedback: A deed restriction is a limitation imposed on a buyer’s use of a property by stipulation in the deed of conveyance or recorded subdivision plat

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: How does a judicial foreclosure differ from a strict foreclosure?
option_a: The mortgagee must file a foreclosure suit with the court in a judicial foreclosure
option_b: The mortgagee issues the lien request in a strict foreclosure state
option_c: The mortgagor files the lien with the court system in a judicial foreclosure state
option_d: The mortgagee requests the legal title from the mortgagor
feedback: If the mortgage document does not contain a “power of sale” provision, the lender must file a foreclosure suit with the court to enforce the lien

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following serves as public notice that a mortgaged property may be in the process of litigation?
feedback: Lis pendens gives public notice that the mortgaged property may soon have a judgment issued against it

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: In what kind of easement is there a dominant tenement and a servient tenement?
feedback: An easement appurtenant gives a property owner a right of usage to portions of an adjoining property owned by another party. The property enjoying the usage right is called the dominant tenement, or dominant estate. The property containing the physical easement itself is the servient tenement, since it must serve the easement use

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Which provision addresses the buyer’s requirement for compliance with South Carolina laws regarding state income tax withholding requirements if the seller is a non-resident or has not filed SC state income tax returns?
option_a: SC income tax on residential gain
option_b: SC income tax on a non-resident owner’s gain
option_c: SC property tax due by foreign investors
feedback: According to the provision about SC income tax on a non-resident owner’s gain, the buyer must comply with SC laws regarding state income tax withholding requirements if the seller is a non-resident or has not filled SC state income tax returns

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which of the following best expresses the mechanics of a mortgage loan transaction?
option_a: The borrower gives the lender a note and a mortgage in exchange for loan funds
option_b: The lender gives the borrower a mortgage and receives a note in exchange for loan funds
option_c: The borrower receives a note in exchange for a mortgage from the lender
option_d: The lender gives the borrower a note, loan funds and a mortgage
feedback: Mortgagor gives note & mortgage to mortgagee, mortgagee gives loan amount to mortgagor

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If there is a significant undersupply of homes in a market, construction will tend to increase. This is an example of
feedback: A market tends toward a state of equilibrium in which supply equals demand, and price, cost, and value are identical. Thus if supply is scarce, construction will increase to stabilize the imbalance

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender lends money to a homeowner and takes legal title to the property as collateral during the payoff period. They are in a
feedback: States that regard the mortgage document as a conveyance of ownership from the mortgagor to the mortgagee are called title-theory states

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The loan-to-value ratio is an important underwriting criterion, for the primary reason that
option_a: borrowers with no equity will default and abandon the property
option_b: the lender wants to ensure the loan is fully collateralized
option_c: a borrower can only afford to borrow a portion of the entire purchase price
option_d: a fair amount of borrower equity demonstrates good faith
feedback: In qualifying a property, an underwriter assesses the ability of the property value to cover potential losses. In this evaluation, a lender requires that the appraised value of the property be more than adequate to cover the contemplated loan and costs. To protect further against loss, a lender will usually lend only a portion of the property's value. The relationship of the loan amount to the property value, expressed as a percentage, is called the loan-to-value ratio, or LTV

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) requires lenders to
option_a: extend equal credit to all prospective borrowers
option_b: consider the income of a spouse in evaluating a family's creditworthiness
option_c: discount the income of a person involved in child-rearing or child-bearing
option_d: specialize lending activity by geographical area for improved customer service
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA) requires a lender to evaluate a loan applicant on the basis of that applicant's own income and credit rating, unless the applicant requests the inclusion of another's income and credit rating in the application. In such a case, a lender may not discount or disregard income from part-time work, a spouse, child support, alimony, or separate maintenance

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The purpose of an income ratio in qualifying a borrower is to
option_b: compare one's earnings to one's short-term debt
option_c: identify the highest possible interest rate that the borrower can afford
option_d: quantify the borrower's assets to the fullest extent
feedback: Lenders want to be assured that the borrower has adequate means to make all necessary periodic payments on the loan in addition to other housing expenses and debts such as credit card payments and car payments. Most lenders use two ratios to estimate an applicant's ability to fulfill a loan obligation: an income ratio, or housing ratio, and a debt ratio, or housing plus debt ratio. The income ratio, or housing expense ratio, establishes borrowing capacity by limiting the percent of gross income a borrower may spend on housing costs

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower's debt ratio is derived by
option_a: dividing one's total debt by one's debt payments
option_b: dividing one's gross income by one's assets
option_c: dividing one's gross income by one's debts
option_d: dividing one's debts by one's gross income
feedback: The debt ratio formula is:  (monthly housing expense + monthly debt obligations) ÷ monthly gross income = debt ratio

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A lender's commitment to lend funds to a borrower in order to retire another outstanding loan is called a
feedback: A take-out commitment offers to make a loan that will "take out" another lender's loan, i.e., pay it off and replace it. The take-out loan is most often used to retire a construction loan. The take-out lender agrees to pay off the short-term construction loan by issuing a long-term permanent loan

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: What is the maximum period of time that any licensee may choose to place a license on inactive status?
feedback: A licensee can place a license on inactive status for an indefinite period of time

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: A nonresident licensee is
option_a: required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina, if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
option_b: required to maintain a place of business in both South Carolina and the state of residency, if the nonresident expects to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted within South Carolina
option_c: not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina, if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence
option_d: not allowed to receive any compensation or referral fees from transactions conducted in South Carolina, unless reciprocity is acknowledge by the both states
feedback: A nonresident licensee is not required to maintain a place of business in South Carolina if the nonresident maintains an active place of business in the state of residence. This is a common practice for towns near the border of the state

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Which of the following statements is true?
option_a: All licensees must renew in odd numbered years
option_b: All licensees must renew in even numbered years
option_c: Approximately one half of the licensees must renew in even numbered years and the remainder in odd numbered years
option_d: All licensees must renew on their initial license application anniversary date
feedback: About one half of South Carolina licensees must renew in even numbered years and the other half in odd numbered years

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Every broker-in-charge must maintain transaction documents for a minimum of
feedback: Every broker-in-charge must maintain transaction documents for a minimum of 5 years

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Trust funds received by a supervised licensee must be delivered to the supervising licensee
option_a: no later than the following business day
feedback: Trust funds received by an associated licensee must be delivered to the supervising licensee no later than the following business day

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: What action can be taken by the Commission to stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions?
option_b: Apply to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order
option_d: Request any state law enforcement entity to seek a bench warrant against the violator
feedback: The Commission can apply to an administrative law judge for a temporary restraining order to stop an unlicensed individual from practicing real estate transactions.  This is done in order to protect the public

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: How long must trust fund monies received in connection with a lease remain in a trust account?
option_a: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed or transferred after six months
option_b: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed or transferred after twelve months
option_c: Lease transaction trust fund monies must remain in the account until the lease expires or is terminated
option_d: Lease transaction trust fund monies can be removed if requested by the lessor
feedback: Lease transaction trust fund monies must remain in the account until the lease expires or is terminated.  They must not be converted or commingled with other funds

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: South Carolina would prohibit using which of the following account titles for the deposit of escrow/trust monies?
feedback: The account must be designated and titled to include the word “trust” or the word “escrow” in the name of the respective broker-in-charge’s or property manager-in-charge’s licensed real estate firm

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Which contract provision explains the limits of the broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, misrepresentations?
feedback: The Broker Liability Limitation contract provision explains the limits of the broker’s liability to the buyer with regard to acts of omission, negligence, or misrepresentations

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: Which of the following is a “substantive contact”?
option_a: A customer asks about sales trends
option_b: A customer wants to talk about finances
option_c: A customer attends an open house
option_d: A For Sale by Owner seller places a classified sales ad
feedback: Substantive contact is contact in which a discussion or dialogue between the consumer and the associated licensee moves from casual introductory talk to a meaningful conversation regarding the selling or buying motives or objectives of the seller or buyer, financial qualifications, and other confidential information that if disclosed could harm the consumer’s bargaining position

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If commercial real estate rental prices are falling in a market, it is likely that
option_a: demand has outstripped supply of space
feedback: Falling prices indicate an oversupply of commercial properties in relation to demand. In this case, construction of new supply will also slow down

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: At the closing of a mortgage loan
option_a: the borrower pays off the note and receives clear title
option_b: the lender issues a firm loan commitment
option_c: the parties complete all loan origination documents and the loan is funded
option_d: the borrower's loan application is complete and the file closed
feedback: Closing of a mortgage loan normally occurs with the closing of the real estate transaction. At the real estate closing, the lender typically has deposited the funded amount with an escrow agent, along with instructions for disbursing the funds. The borrower deposits necessary funds with the escrow agent, executes final documents, and receives signed copies of all relevant documents

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which laws or regulations require mortgage lenders to disclose financing costs and annual percentage rate to a borrower before funding a loan?
option_c: The Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act
feedback: Truth-in-Lending Laws, implemented by the Federal Reserve's Regulation Z, regulate the disclosure of costs, the right to rescind the credit transaction, advertising credit offers, and penalties for non-compliance with the act

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which laws or regulations prevent mortgage lenders from discriminating in extending credit to potential borrowers based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, and dependency on public assistance?
option_c: The Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act
feedback: The Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits discrimination in extending credit based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or dependency upon public assistance

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is an important economic characteristic of real estate?
option_a: The demand must literally come to the supply
option_b: Real estate is a highly liquid product
option_c: The product is quick to adapt to market changes
feedback: Since real property cannot be moved, real property investors and users must come to the supply. This creates risk, because if demand drops, the supply cannot be transported to a higher demand market

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The foremost factor contributing to commercial and residential demand in a market is
feedback: The engine that drives demand for real estate of all types in a market is employment. Base employment is the number of persons employed in the businesses that represent the economic foundation of the area

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to
feedback: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to the buyer

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A construction boom in a market is an indication that prices
feedback: A rise in prices is a market signal that there is an undersupply of product in relation to demand. As the market moves toward equilibrium, builders construct more buildings to meet the unmet demand

subject: B5 South Carolina Real Estate
question_text: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to
feedback: According to South Carolina law, interest earned on an earnest money deposit belongs to the buyer

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A local government could stimulate the real estate market by
option_a: increasing labor costs and curbing the money supply
feedback: Since the real estate market is local by definition, local factors weigh heavily in local real estate market conditions. This includes the capacity of the municipality's infrastructure to handle growth

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: Which laws or regulations require mortgage lenders to provide an estimate of closing costs to a borrower and forbid them to pay kickbacks for referrals?
option_c: the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act
feedback: RESPA is a federal law which aims to standardize settlement practices and ensure that buyers understand settlement costs. RESPA applies to purchases of residential real estate (one- to four-family homes) to be financed by "federally related" first mortgage loans. In addition to imposing settlement procedures, RESPA provisions prohibit lenders from paying kickbacks and unearned fees to parties who may have helped the lender obtain the borrower's business

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Two important concerns of retail property users are
option_a: trade area population and spending patterns
option_b: quality of life and dwelling amenities
option_c: costs of occupancy and building efficiency
option_d: environmental regulations and access by suppliers
feedback: Retail users are concerned with:  •sufficient trade area population and income  • the level of trade area competition  •sales volume per square foot of rented area  •consumer spending patterns  •growth patterns in the trade area

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: The Federal Reserve System regulates the money supply in which of the following ways?
option_a: Selling securities, printing money, and controlling lending underwriting requirements
option_b: Buying securities, changing the discount rate, and controlling banking reserves
option_c: Printing money, changing interest rates, and selling T-bills
option_d: Controlling the prime rate, trading securities, and purchasing loans
feedback: The Federal Reserve System regulates the money supply by means of three methods:  •selling or re-purchasing government securities, primarily Treasury bills  •changing the reserve requirement for member banks  •changing the interest rate, or discount rate, the system charges member institutions for borrowing funds from the Federal Reserve System central banks

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: One of the primary purposes for the secondary mortgage market is to
option_a: cycle funds back to primary lenders so they can make more loans
option_b: issue second mortgages and sell them in the home equity market
option_c: lend funds to banks so they can make more loans
option_d: pay off defaulted loans made by primary mortgage lenders
feedback: By purchasing loans from primary lenders, the secondary market returns funds to the primary lenders, thereby enabling the primary lender to originate more mortgage loans

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A principal role of FNMA is to
feedback: Federal National Mortgage Association, or Fannie Mae, buys conventional, FHA-backed and VA-backed loans

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: If a loan amount is $200,000 and the borrower is paying 2 points, the cost of those points is

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A pizza parlor rents space in a retail shopping center. The business owner bolts a large iron pizza oven to the floor to cook the pizzas. The oven is a(n):
feedback: The iron oven is a trade fixture used for a specific business purpose. It is personal property even though it is attached to the rented space. The owner must remove the oven on or before the last day of the lease. A fixture is an item permanently attached to the property and is considered real property. If the restaurant owner assembles and builds an oven in the space that cannot easily be removed, its removal may cause severe damage to the space. If not removed, the oven becomes a permanent fixture, real property belonging to the landlord.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A borrower obtains a 30-year, fully amortizing mortgage loan of $300,000 at 8%.  What is the principal balance at the end of the loan term?
feedback: In a fully amortizing mortgage, the principal balance is zero at the end of the term

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A house with one bathroom serving five bedrooms is suffering from:
feedback: A house with one bathroom serving five bedrooms is an example of an outdated design feature that lessens the value of a property. The loss of value may be curable if the anticipated increased value of the property would offset the cost of adding more bathrooms.

subject: A3 Financing
question_text: A house sold for $350,000.  The seller paid a brokerage commission of six percent, legal fees of $600, and had other closing costs of $3,000.  What are the net proceeds from the sale?
feedback: Amount realized. The amount realized, also known as net proceeds from sale, is expressed by the formula:  sale price - cost of sale = amount realize. Sale price: $350,000. Cost of sale: 6% (350,000 x 0.06 = $21,000) + $600 + $3,000 = $24,600. Net proceeds of sale: $325,400

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When land is surveyed, the description should contain:
option_a: the area contained within the described boundaries expressed in accepted units of measure.
option_b: a specific length and direction of the sides of the property.
option_c: all of the items listed in the other answers.
feedback: All of the options should be contained in a survey. A land survey is a measurement of a specific parcel of land to ascertain area, corners, boundaries, topography, and divisions with distances and directions of such parcel.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is the best definition of a "commercial acre":
option_a: An acre minus property that has been sold
option_b: The building portion of an acre after deductions for streets, curbs and sidewalks.
option_c: An acre minus all residential property
feedback: Commercial Acre: Also referred to as a Buildable Acre, this is the portion of land left that can be built minus the streets, sidewalks, curbs, etc.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser is appraising a single-family residence, and notices an abandoned gas station next door. The appraiser would logically recommend which of the following:
option_a: A soils report by a civil engineer
option_d: A toxic waste report from the EPA
feedback: A toxic waste report may be demanded in instances where a gas station has been abandoned nearby. If the results are depreciative, this is Economic Obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An expensive home that later became surrounded by cheaper homes would suffer a loss in property value attributable to:
feedback: Economic obsolescence is depreciation caused by factors outside the property lines, like surrounding cheaper homes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Economic obsolescence might be attributed to each of the following except:
option_a: no air conditioning in a commercial building in a warm climate.
option_d: an expensive home that later became surrounded by cheaper homes.
feedback: Lack of air conditioning is not economic obsolescence, but physical or functional obsolescence. The other options are external/economic factors.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following types of depreciation would be the most difficult to cure?
feedback: Economic obsolescence is most difficult to cure because it is due to external causes, outside of the property owner’s control.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When an appraiser is considering the economic life and the physical life of a building, the economic life is usually:
feedback: Economic life is the profitable life span of a property, usually shorter than physical life.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A "loss in value from any cause" is a common definition of:
feedback: Depreciation = loss of value due to physical, functional, or economic factors.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the capitalization method of appraising, the most difficult step is to:
feedback: Selecting the cap rate is most difficult because it involves risk, return, and market factors.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is not a proper charge in determining net income for capitalization purposes:
feedback: Mortgage interest is not deducted when calculating net income for capitalization.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following approaches converts income into value?
feedback: Capitalization approach converts income into value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the Methods could be used by Appraisers for estimating the cost of Construction:
option_a: All of the other options are correct
feedback: All listed methods are used: Unit-in-Place, Square Footage, and Quantity Survey.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which method would an appraiser be most likely to use when appraising a newly built (1-year-old) apartment building with no rental history?
feedback: Cost approach is used when no income history exists, like new properties.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If an appraiser is appraising a special purpose property he would probably use the:
feedback: Special purpose properties like schools/libraries use cost approach.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The effectiveness of the market data approach would be limited most by:
option_a: Types of property that are regularly sold.
option_b: The differences in the comparable property.
option_c: The financing terms of comparable property.
feedback: Market data is least reliable during rapid economic changes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When is the market data approach considered unreliable?
option_b: When there is nothing similar to the subject property
option_c: During times of rapid economic change
option_d: All reasons provided make this approach unreliable
feedback: The approach becomes unreliable under all these conditions.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is the basis of the market data approach to appraisal?
option_a: The principle of supply and demand
option_d: The principle of highest and best use
feedback: Market data is based on substitution: buyers choose the lowest-priced comparable property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Appreciation would be best defined as an:
feedback: Appreciation = increase in value over time.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Jane saw a property at $900,000, then found similar for $600,000. This would be the principle of:
feedback: Substitution principle: no one pays more if a similar alternative costs less.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What would be most important to an appraiser in assessing the value of a vacant lot?
option_c: How much the land has depreciated
feedback: Highest and best use is the primary consideration for vacant land.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is not one of the four important elements of value:
feedback: The four essential elements are: Scarcity, Transferability, Utility, Demand (STUD).

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The general term "value" means:
option_a: The average use and function of an object to all people
option_d: The worth, usefulness, or utility of an object to someone for some purpose
feedback: Value = worth, usefulness, or utility to someone for some purpose.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Definitions of market value are least concerned with:
feedback: Market value concerns open market, value in exchange, objective value. Market cost is least relevant.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following factors contributes most to the maintenance of value in a well-planned residential community:
option_b: Variances for highest and best use
option_d: Conformity to proper land use objectives
feedback: Conformity to proper land use objectives maintains community value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Where in a standard narrative appraisal report would one likely find the type of value being estimated:
feedback: The type of value is found in the statement of purpose.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Property should be valued at its highest and best use. If an appraiser is appraising a parcel with a useless building, he should:
feedback: Deduct demolition costs when the building is no longer valuable.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The most accurate determination of the age of a house can be made by inspecting the:
feedback: Tax assessor’s records provide the most accurate build date.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser is usually paid:
option_a: a fee agreed upon after completion
option_b: a standard fee set by association
option_c: a fee based on % of value
option_d: a fee based on time and effort
feedback: Appraisers are paid based on time and effort, not value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The appraisal approach most likely to be used in valuing a public library building would be:
feedback: Cost approach is used for special purpose properties like libraries.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Usefulness of the cost approach would be limited when:
option_a: an appraiser is appraising a new structure appropriate to its site and use.
option_b: there exists an unusually inactive real estate market.
option_c: appraising an old structure with many functional deficiencies.
feedback: The cost approach is most appropriate for new buildings, not old. Limited usefulness applies to older structures with deficiencies.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser who uses improper methods to appraise a property for a government backed loan may be guilty of:
feedback: The Appraiser must utilize proper methodology on government-backed loans or be guilty of a felony.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser's primary concern when analyzing residential property is:
feedback: Primary concern is marketability and acceptability of a property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The most comprehensive and complete appraisal report is the:
feedback: Narrative report is the most comprehensive, containing methods and conclusions.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is a fee appraiser:
option_a: An appraiser that works for a federally insured lender
option_b: An independent appraiser who works on a fee basis
option_c: A certified appraiser who works on salary
option_d: An appraiser who works for a lending institution
feedback: A Fee Appraiser is independent and charges per appraisal.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Pool effect in Madison vs Yuma (greater in Yuma) is an example of:
option_a: Principle of highest and best use
feedback: Contribution: value depends on how much it adds to net income.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Property values increase due to population increase is:
feedback: Unearned increment: value increase from external forces like population growth.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Unit of comparison in market data approach for a single-family residence is:
feedback: Market Data Approach uses entire property as the unit of comparison.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Most commonly used appraisal approach by licensees:
feedback: Licensees most often use Market Data for homes and lots.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Approach that sets the upper limit of value:
feedback: Cost replacement sets upper value limit.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Replacement cost refers to:
feedback: Replacement cost = same utility, not identical replica.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Approach most used in restaurant appraisal:
feedback: Restaurants are income-producing, so income approach applies.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Not a cause of obsolescence:
feedback: Wear and tear = physical deterioration, not obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Economic obsolescence occurs because of:
feedback: Economic obsolescence = caused by external factors.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Change in flight patterns causing value loss =
feedback: Flight path change is external → economic obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Example of economic obsolescence:
feedback: External oversupply depresses value = economic obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Major cause of loss in real property value:
feedback: Obsolescence is the primary cause of depreciation.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Approach most concerned with present worth of future benefits:
feedback: Income approach emphasizes present worth of future income.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Buyer offering $500k for 10-unit apartments like others is operating on:
feedback: Substitution = won’t pay more than comparable alternatives.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: "Effective age" refers to:
feedback: Effective age = based on condition, not chronology.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Adjustments in comparison approach apply to:
feedback: Adjust comparables, not the subject property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Income capitalization approach most important for:
feedback: Office buildings are income-producing, so income approach applies.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: 1920s garage too small by modern standards =
feedback: Functional obsolescence = outdated by current standards.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Why buyers purchase using market data approach:
feedback: Buyers compare recent sales of similar homes = market data approach.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which principle states that "a person will not pay more for one property than another equally desirable?"
feedback: Substitution is the basis of pricing; no one pays more if a substitute exists.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A real estate appraisal is limited to what period of time?
feedback: Appraisal value is valid as of a specific date; lenders usually accept 2–6 months.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A section:
option_b: has a perimeter of 5,280 sq ft.
feedback: A section = 1 square mile = 640 acres.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A board-foot is:
feedback: A board-foot = 12 × 12 × 1 = 144 cubic inches.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Market data approach effectiveness is limited most by:
feedback: Market data is unreliable during rapid economic change.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which basic economic characteristic best explains why real estate has value?
feedback: Value = highest and best use, maximizing resources.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Unearned increment in value most likely comes from:
feedback: Unearned increment = value rise without owner’s effort, e.g., population growth.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A bearing wall in a home:
option_c: All of the other options are correct
option_d: May be at any angle to a doorway
feedback: Bearing walls support loads, remain in remodeling, and can be angled.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A "real estate appraisal" is an estimate of value:
option_a: Based on analysis of fact as of a specific date
option_c: From average tax assessments over 5 years
feedback: Appraisal = estimate as of a specific date, based on facts gathered.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Building position on site relative to surroundings is called:
feedback: Orientation = relationship of building to site and surroundings.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Expressing anticipated future benefits in $ and discounting to present worth is:
feedback: Capitalization converts future income into present value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Most useful question for determining economic obsolescence in commercial property:
feedback: Prosperity of surrounding tenants shows external economic conditions.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Loss in value due to economic obsolescence is best shown by:
feedback: Economic obsolescence comes from external factors like zoning changes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Factor NOT contributing to obsolescence:
feedback: Wear and tear = physical deterioration, not obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Soil truckloads for backfill most likely used to:
feedback: Backfill = soil used to fill around foundation trenches.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Price at which willing buyer and seller agree is:
feedback: Market value = fair price in an open, informed market.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Legal description outlining boundaries with directions/distances from point of beginning:
feedback: Metes & bounds uses directions/distances from point of beginning.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Income approach most important for valuing:
feedback: Income approach applies to income-producing properties like offices.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Direct sales comparison approach most appropriate for:
feedback: Market data approach best for recent single-family sales.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Cost approach to estimate residential dwelling value:
option_c: Adjust price if economic changes occur
feedback: Cost = replacement cost – depreciation.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Land gained from river deposits is acquired by:
feedback: Accretion = gradual land buildup by water action.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Home with termite infestation despite maintenance is:
feedback: Termite damage = curable if repair cost < benefit.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Metes and bounds legal description must:
option_b: Begin and end at same point
feedback: Must begin & end at same identifiable point.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Physical characteristics of land include all EXCEPT:
feedback: Scarcity = economic concept, not physical characteristic.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Factor NOT directly affecting supply:
feedback: Population affects demand, not supply.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An acre contains approximately:
feedback: 1 acre = 43,560 sq ft.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which is economic obsolescence?
feedback: Zoning changes = external factor, hence economic obsolescence.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Land value is estimated on the basis of:
feedback:
The Market Data Approach to appraisal, also know as the Comparison approach, is the process of estimating the value of a property through the examination and comparison of actual sales of comparable properties. This Approach is best used to find values of residential houses, vacant lots and land.

When comparing two properties, it's important to deduct for features of the comparable- for example, if one property has a pool and the other does not, the appraiser needs to account for the value of the pool.
