This is the most difficult aspect of this approach. Adjustments are necessary because, while some properties are mostly similar, two properties are rarely similar concerning everything.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser was hired by Joe to appraise Bill's property. With whom should the appraiser discuss his findings?
feedback: The results of an appraisal are confidential. The appraiser is working for the entity that orders the appraisal (usually the lender). In this case, Joe ordered the appraisal, so that's who gets the final appraisal.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A four-bedroom house with a one-car garage would be an example of:
feedback: The correct answer is functional obsolescence, which is a loss in the value of a property resulting from a deficiency in the floor plan of a house. A one-car garage would be inadequate for the four bedroom house.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In an appraisal, the term "utility value" would most nearly be described as:
feedback: Utility value is the value in use to an owner-user. A property that has no utility has no value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is the best way to appraise the land value of a property?
feedback: The allocation method (also called the abstraction or extraction method) is an appraisal method whereby the appraiser estimates the land value of any improved property by deducting or abstracting the value of any site improvements from the overall sales price of the property. The amount remaining is the estimated sales price or indicated value of the land.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: You are preparing a comparative market analysis on a vacant lot that you hope to list for sale. Which of the following approaches to value should be used in the development of the estimated value?
feedback: The sales comparison approach is most applicable to the appraisal of vacant land. The cost approach is most applicable to the appraisal of special purpose properties such as a church. The gross rent multiplier is used as a substitute for the income approach in the valuation of a single-family home. The income approach is used in the appraisal of an income-producing property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following comparables would be the most helpful in appraising a home?
option_a: A similar home sold by a motivated seller to a motivated buyer in the past three months
option_b: A similar property sold under duress
option_c: A similar home sold by a motivated seller to a motivated buyer two years ago
option_d: A dissimilar home sold for nonpayment of taxes.
feedback: The most useful comps are similar properties, sold recently, for market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following comparables would be the most helpful in appraising a home?
option_a: A similar home sold by a motivated seller to a motivated buyer.
option_b: A similar home sold for nonpayment of taxes.
option_c: A similar property sold under duress.
option_d: A similar home sold by an unwilling seller to an unsure buyer.
feedback: This relates to the "willing seller, willing buyer" concept of value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The hardest and yet most important part of the market data method of appraising a property is:
option_a: analyzing the volume of sales data.
option_b: adjusting for differences between the comparable and the subject property.
option_d: collecting a sufficient volume of sales data.
feedback: The most difficult part of the market data method of appraising is adjusting for differences in the properties.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In order to accurately calculate a gross rent multiplier, what data would an appraiser obtain from comparable properties?
option_a: Annual rent and the selling price
option_b: Original cost and the annual income
option_d: Net income and the capitalization rate
feedback: The gross rent multiplier is arrived at by dividing the monthly or annual gross rent into the selling price of the property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If an appraiser is estimating the value of a property using the cost approach, which of these describes what the appraiser should do?
option_a: Review the sales prices of comparable properties
option_b: Determine the income that the property could produce
option_c: Estimate the replacement cost of the improvements
option_d: Adjust the subject property's value in comparison to a similar property
feedback: In real estate appraisal, the cost approach is one of three basic valuation methods. Using this method, an appraiser will estimate the cost to replace the building using current material prices (so they know the value in today's dollars), minus depreciation.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A homeowner builds a $200,000 house among $100,000 homes. A loss in value suffered by the expensive home would be best attributed to:
feedback: Otherwise known as Social Obsolescence, economic obsolescence is when the value of a property decreases due to factors outside of the property in the neighborhood or immediate area. The $100,000 homes are outside (external) of the $200,000 house and cause the more expensive home to lose value- so the answer is economic obsolescence. Functional obsolescence occurs inside the property lines such as poor design, while physical deterioration refers to deferred maintenance that results in property starting to fall apart, and not be useable.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The period over which a property may be profitably utilized is called its:
feedback: Economic life is the estimated period over which it is anticipated that a property may be profitably utilized.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The relationship between a property and a prospective purchaser is known as:
feedback: Value can also be described as the relationship between a property and a prospective purchaser. The most probable price a property should bring in a competitive and open market under all conditions requisite to a fair sale (market value).

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is a "fee appraiser?"
option_a: An appraiser who works for a lending institution.
option_b: An independent appraiser who works on a fee basis.
option_c: An appraiser who works for a federally insured lender.
option_d: A certified appraiser who works on salary.
feedback: A fee appraiser is a non-salaried professional who furnishes appraisal services for a fee.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The system of legal description that defines a parcel of land by tracing its perimeter is known as:
feedback: A metes-and-bounds description traces a parcel's perimeter by starting at a POB (point of beginning) and always ending back at the POB, so that the described tract is completely enclosed. The rectangular survey system divides land into rectangles and describes those rectangles with principal meridians and base lines. The lot and block system uses lot and block numbers in a plat map to describe property and is often used in urban areas. A street address is not a legal description.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A metes-and-bounds legal description:
option_a: can be made only in areas excluded from the rectangular survey system.
option_b: is not acceptable in court in most jurisdictions.
option_c: is used to complete areas omitted from recorded subdivision plats.
option_d: uses descriptions moving in a clockwise direction.
feedback: A metes-and-bounds description starts at a POB (point of beginning) and moves clockwise around the boundaries always ending back at the POB, so that the described tract is completely enclosed. The metes-and-bounds, government survey, and lot and block are all accepted in courts. The method may be used in areas included in the rectangular survey system. The metes-and-bounds method is often used to survey large parcels of land before the parcels are broken into subdivision plats.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A title search for a property under contract has revealed several outstanding liens against the property. Which of the following liens has highest priority?
option_a: A special assessment for the current year
option_b: A property tax lien for the current year
option_c: A judgment lien rendered and recorded last month
option_d: An outstanding first mortgage lien dated and recorded one year ago
feedback: Tax liens have priority over previously recorded liens and over a special assessment, which will also take priority over previously recorded liens. The priority of the other liens will be based on the dates they were recorded. Liens recorded first will have a first right to payment or priority.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A title search for a property under contract has revealed several outstanding liens against the property. Which of the following liens has highest priority?
option_a: An outstanding first mortgage lien dated and recorded one year ago
option_b: A special assessment for the current year
option_c: A judgment lien rendered and recorded last month
option_d: A property tax lien for the current year
feedback: The answer is a property tax lien for the current year. Tax liens have priority over previously recorded liens and over a special assessment, which will also take priority over previously recorded liens. The priority of the other liens will be based on the dates they were recorded. Liens recorded first will have a first right to payment or priority.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: How many acres are in a lot that is 1/4 of a mile wide by 1/4 of a mile long?
feedback:
Find the square mileage in the land by multiplying the sides: 1/4 mi. x 1/4 mi. = 1/16 sq. mi.
Multiply the square mileage in the land by the number of acres in a square mile (640) to find the answer: 1/16 x 640 = 40.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If there is a discrepancy between a residential lot size quoted in a listing contract and the actual lot size, the actual lot size that will prevail in a sales transaction will be found in the
feedback: The accuracy of the lot size is essential for both residential and commercial properties. If there is a discrepancy between the lot size quoted in a listing contract or deed and the actual lot size, the actual lot size found by a survey will prevail. Most MLS services will note that any square footage listed is only an estimate.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A township contains only:
feedback: Each section is 1 square mile. Each township is 6 miles square and contains 36 square miles, so a township contains 36 sections. Each section is 1 square mile, or 640 acres. The 36 square miles of a township are 23,040 acres.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A developer grants a local power company the right to install necessary transmission lines. The developer has granted the power company a(n):
feedback: An easement in gross one party's right to use another's land. An easement in gross belongs to its owner regardless of whether the owner has ownership or possession of any specific land. A license is a personal privilege to enter the land of another for a specific purpose. A license can be terminated or canceled. An easement by prescription is acquired when a person makes continuous and visible use of another's land for a certain period of time without the owner's permission. A conditional use permit is granted by a municipality to a property owner to allow a special nonconforming use of property in a residential district.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A condominium form of ownership is officially established when the
option_a: developer records a declaration in the public record.
option_b: construction of the improvements is completed.
option_c: unit owners all file their documents in the public record.
feedback: When a developer declares in the public record the existence of a condominium, the condominium is officially established. The declaration includes survey, legal description, bylaws, restrictive covenants, and architect drawings. Construction of improvements, the establishment of an owners' association, and sale of individual units follow the establishment of the condominium.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A landowner divided much of her land into smaller parcels and has recently sold a tract adjacent to a nature preserve. The preserve and the tract that was sold are landlocked and cannot be entered except through one of the other tracts still belonging to the landowner. The buyer of the tract will probably be granted what type of easement by a court action?
feedback: An easement by necessity arises when there is no ingress or egress (entry or exit) from one piece of property without crossing over a parcel of land owned by another. An easement in gross permits a right-of-way for an individual or company to use another's property. An easement by prescription is acquired when a person makes continuous and visible use of another's land for a certain period of time without the owner's permission. A conditional use permit is granted by a municipality to a property owner to allow a special nonconforming use of property in a residential district.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A buyer purchased two parcels of land. One parcel was one square mile and the other contained 10 acres. If the land cost $2,500 an acre, what was the cost of the land?
feedback: One square mile is 640 acres, so the two parcels together contain 650 acres (640 + 10 = 650). To find the cost, multiply the cost per acre by the total acres: 650 �- $2,500 = $1,625,000.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The BEST way to ensure that there are no encroachments to a property and to properly verify the boundaries of a parcel of land is to
feedback: Encroachments are found through actual notice or visual inspection by the buyer or someone working for the buyer. A survey shows the location of all improvements on a property and whether any improvements extend over the property lines. A legal description is a description of a specific real estate parcel complete enough for an independent surveyor to locate and identify the parcel. Monuments are fixed objects or markers to identify corners or places where boundary lines change directions, but they will not identify encroachments. Benchmarks are permanent reference points established for use in surveys to measure differences in elevation, but they will not identify encroachments.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Rising mortgage rates are an example of:
feedback: Factors outside the property boundary lines that affect a property's value are examples of external obsolescence (also known as economic obsolescence). Rising mortgage rates are an example of external obsolescence as it is not related to anything inside of the property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: One part of the process used in the income approach to determine value is to:
feedback: In the income approach to value, the capitalization rate is applied to the property's annual net operating income to arrive at the estimate of the property's value. Calculating reproduction costs, replacement costs, and depreciation are procedures used in the cost approach to value, not the income approach.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which appraisal method uses a rate of investment return in estimating a property's value?
feedback:
The income approach uses the rate of investment return, also known as the capitalization rate, to estimate a property's value. The value is determined by dividing the annual net income by the capitalization rate (NOI ÷ Rate = Value).

The capitalization rate is not used in the cost approach or the sales comparison approach. The gross rent multiplier is a factor used in the income approach to estimate the value of five or more residential units, commercial, or industrial properties.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Rents have gone up in the area. The principle that BEST describes this increase is
feedback: Under the concept of supply and demand, if demand increases and supply remains the same, the supply becomes more valuable. Fewer rental properties will produce higher rents. The principle of substitution says that the maximum value of a property tends to be how much it would cost to purchase an equally desirable substitute property. The principle of contribution is used to determine if improvements will increase or decrease the value of the real property. Conformity is the appraisal principle that holds that the greater the similarity among properties in an area, the better the properties will hold their value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser is responsible for
feedback: The appraiser does not set the value. After the appraiser completes the analysis and considers all the data, an estimate of value will be provided. An appraisal is an official estimate of value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The physical characteristic of real estate that means that every parcel of land is different is known as
feedback: Uniqueness is the concept that no two parcels of property are exactly the same or in the same location- you might also see this described as non-homogeneity or heterogeneity. An individual parcel has no substitute because each is unique. Immobility means that the geographic location of a parcel of land can never be changed. Indestructibility means that land is durable and cannot be destroyed. Scarcity is an economic characteristic of land, meaning that when the supply of land is limited, the price of the land increases.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: All of the following terms are associated with the economic characteristics of real estate EXCEPT
feedback: A license is the permission to use another's property and is not an economic characteristic of property. Permanence of the investment refers to the labor and capital used for an investment. Area preference refers to the location of the property. Improvements are additions to a property to enhance its value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In general, when the supply of a certain commodity increases:
option_a: demand for it tends to rise.
option_d: demand for it tends to drop.
feedback: According to the principle of supply and demand, if demand is constant and supply increases, sellers of a particular commodity compete with each other by lowering prices. Prices tend to rise if the demand for the commodity increases while the supply remains the same.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The principle of appraising, which states that the maximum value is achieved when the property is in harmony with its surroundings, is the principle of
feedback: The principle of conformity means that maximum value is realized if the use of land conforms to existing neighborhood standards. The principle of competition is the interaction of supply and demand, as excess profits tend to attract competition. The principle of contribution states that the value of any part of a property is measured by the effect of that part on the value of the entire property. The highest and best use of a property is the use that will give the property its greatest current value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A developer experiences success and good profits in a recent real estate project. What principle of value indicates that her experience will attract others to engage in similar projects in the same area?
feedback: Competition is the interaction of supply and demand, meaning that the good profits achieved by a real estate project will tend to attract new activity and competition from other developers or investors. According to the principle of anticipation, a property's value is affected by the expectation of future benefits or detriments. The principle of conformity means that the maximum value of a property is created when the property is in harmony with its neighborhood and surroundings. The highest and best use of a property is the use that will give the property its greatest current value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following would be classified as external depreciation?
option_a: Convenient access to schools and recreational facilities
option_b: A poorly designed floor plan that could be modified
option_c: A leaking roof that needs to be completely replaced
option_d: Poorly maintained properties in the neighborhood
feedback: External depreciation is caused by factors not on the subject property, such as poorly maintained properties in the neighborhood. A leaky roof is an example of physical deterioration. A poorly designed floor plan is an example of functional obsolescence. Convenient access to schools and recreational facilities are examples of good features that may add to the desirability of a neighborhood.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An example of functional obsolescence is
option_a: a five-bedroom home with one bathroom.
option_b: a decrease in the area's population.
option_d: chipping paint and other deferred maintenance.
feedback: Obsolescence is a loss of value. Functional obsolescence results from outdated or unacceptable physical features in a property. A five-bedroom home with only one bath is an example of functional obsolescence. Chipping paint and other deferred maintenance are examples of physical deterioration, which may be repaired or cured by repainting or other maintenance. Unattractive curb appeal and a decrease in the area's population are examples of external obsolescence, items which will not be easily cured by spending more on the property itself.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The market value of a parcel of real estate is
option_a: an estimate of its future benefits.
option_b: the amount of money paid for the property.
option_d: the most probable price it should bring.
feedback: An appraisal estimates the market value�"the price a property would most probably bring. The amount of money actually paid for a property is the market price, which may or may not equal the market value. An estimate of a property's future benefits may be used by an appraiser as part of the income approach to value. The value of a property without improvements consists of the value of the land itself.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser using the GRM (gross rent multiplier) would consider which of the following?
option_a: Market price of comparables and adjustments to the subject
option_b: Potential rent income and market value
option_c: Actual rental income and market value
feedback: The GRM (gross rent multiplier) times the rental income will determine the properties market value or anticipated sales price. Comparables and adjustments are used in the sales comparison approach. Market value is what the GRM is determining, not market price which is the actual sale price. Capitalization and net operating income are used in the income approach.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Change, contribution, and substitution are some of the basic principles that affect what aspect of real estate?
feedback: Change, contribution, and substitution all affect value�"the most probable price a property will bring. Change relates to the reality that nothing is constant and variations in various aspects of a property will affect its value. Contribution means that the value of any part of property is measured by how it affects the value of the whole property. Substitution says that the maximum value of a property tends to be determined by how much it would cost to purchase an equally desirable substitute property. Depreciation is a loss of value for any reason. Under the principle of supply and demand, when supply increases the value decreases and when demand increases, the value increases.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The term reconciliation refers to which of the following?
option_a: Analyzing and weighing the findings obtained by the different approaches to value to arrive at a final estimate of value
option_b: The process by which an appraiser determines the highest and best use for a parcel of land
option_c: Separating the value of the land from the total value of the property to compute depreciation
option_d: Loss of value due to any cause
feedback: The three approaches to value typically produce three different values. An in-depth analysis of these values is required to determine the most valid, logical, and reliable approach to be used to provide the final value estimate. Determining depreciation, a loss of value due to any cause, is part of the process of the cost approach. The highest and best use of a property is the single most profitable use for that property. Determining highest and best use is only one of the factors considered in an appraisal prior to choosing an approach to value and reconciliation.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: What is the cubic footage of a driveway that measures 60 feet long, 8 feet wide, and is 3 inches deep?
feedback:
To find out the total cubic footage, multiply the length, width and depth. Keep in mind that 3 inches is the same as 1/4 of a foot:
 60 ft. x 8 ft. x 1/4 ft. = 120 cubic feet
 
 On a calculator, this looks like:
 60 x 8 ÷ 4 = 120 cubic feet

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A homeowner constructs a five-bedroom, brick house with a tennis court and a pool in a neighborhood of modest two-bedroom and three-bedroom homes on narrow lots. The value of the five-bedroom house is MOST likely to be affected by the principle of:
feedback: The principle of regression suggests that the value of the more expensive, larger, and amenity-rich property will be negatively affected by the surrounding less valuable properties. In a neighborhood of smaller, modest homes, the presence of a larger, more upscale home does not increase its value proportionally because the general neighborhood standards and expectations set by the smaller homes influence how much a buyer is willing to pay for a property in that area. This principle highlights that a property's value is impacted by the value of surrounding properties.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: All of the following would require an appraisal EXCEPT:
feedback: Appraisals are required for all government-backed loans and loans that conventional lenders; only a seller-carry (seller-financed) loan would not meet this requirement. It would still be recommended that the seller get an appraisal, as good business practice.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The value of a swimming pool or other property amenities is determined by the principle of:
feedback: The principle of contribution is used to determine value of amenities such as a pool. Substitution underlies all approaches to value and states the market must be used to verify pricing. Conformity is used to determine if property conforms to the area and uses progression and regression.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Capitalization is a mathematical process by which annual net operating income is used to
feedback: Capitalization converts the net operating income (NOI) into an indication of value in the income approach to determine the current or present market value. An appraiser estimates the return on investment an investor would demand for the property being appraised. An appraiser will determine replacement costs, reproduction costs, and depreciation for a property when using the cost approach to value. The effective gross income is estimated by deducting an appropriate amount for vacancy and rent loss in an income-producing property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The characteristics of value include which of the following?
feedback: The characteristics of value (STUD) are: scarcity, transferability, utility and demand. Scarcity means that there is a limited supply of similar properties. Competition is the interaction of supply and demand causing prices for property to rise or fall. The economic principle of anticipation states that value is created by the expectation that certain events will occur affecting the value of a property. Balance refers to the amount remaining after certain mathematical calculations have been completed.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The listing or asking price of a property in the marketplace should be its:
feedback: Market value is what a property should sell for in an arm's-length transaction between informed parties, neither of which is under undue pressure, and the property has been exposed to the market for a reasonable length of time. The intrinsic value of a property is the result of a person's individual preferences for a given geographic area over other less desirable areas. The subjective value is what a person perceives the value to be based on the benefits to that person. The book value, used for income tax purposes, refers to the value of the property after factoring in the depreciation on the property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser conducting an appraisal of a single-family residence notes that the subject home does not have an attached garage. Three of the comparables chosen by the appraiser include attached, finished garages. To account for the garage that is present in each comparable, but not in the subject property, the appraiser will:
option_a: subtract the value of an attached garage from the subject property.
option_b: subtract the value of an attached garage from the sales price for each comparable.
option_c: add the value of an attached garage to the subject property.
option_d: add the value of an attached garage to the sales price for each comparable.
feedback: Adjustments are made only to comparable properties- never the subject. If the comparable is better than the subject or has a feature that is present in a comparable but not in the subject, the value of the amenity is subtracted from the sales price of the comparable. If the subject is better than the comparable, the value of the amenity is added.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A smaller home is surrounded by larger, more expensive homes. The value of the smaller home will most likely
option_a: be anticipated to increase for the first five years.
option_b: increase because of the larger, more expensive properties.
option_c: decrease because of the larger surrounding properties.
option_d: not be affected by surrounding properties.
feedback: Under the principle of conformity progression shows the value of a modest, less expensive home will increase if it is located among larger, fancier, more expensive properties. Under regression, a structure that is larger or more luxurious would tend to be valued in the same range of less lavish homes in a neighborhood of modest homes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the cost approach, an appraiser uses which of the following?
option_b: The property's assessed value as used for tax purposes
option_c: An estimate of the building's replacement cost
option_d: The owner's original cost of construction
feedback: The building's replacement cost is an essential component of the cost approach, along with depreciation and land value. The owner's original cost of construction is not considered in the cost approach. The sales prices of similar properties are considered in the sales comparison approach. The property's assessed value is not used to determine its fair market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When estimating the value of property using the cost approach, all of the following are considered by an appraiser EXCEPT:
option_a: quality of materials and workmanship in the original structure.
option_b: loss of value due to uncollected delinquent rent.
option_c: estimated loss attributable to an outdated heating system.
option_d: excessive amount of traffic noise outside the property.
feedback: Loss of value due to uncollected delinquent rent is a factor in determining a property's effective gross income in the income approach to value, not the cost approach as this question asks. An outdated heating system, the quality of original materials and workmanship, and traffic noise are considered in the cost approach in determining the depreciation of a property due to deterioration or obsolescence.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If the assessed valuation of a property is $20,000, with a tax rate of $4.00 per $100, what is the annual tax?
feedback: Remember that the $4.00 per $100 means that the $20,000 must be divided by 100, giving a total of $200. That, multiplied by $4.00 gives the total tax of $800.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: All of the following are characteristics of value EXCEPT:
feedback: To have value, a property must have monetary worth based on the desirability of the property. Obsolescence is not a desirable trait and, therefore, takes away from the desirability of the property and the demand for the property. Demand, transferability, scarcity, and utility are all characteristics of a property's value (remembered by the acronym STUD or DUST). Demand is the need or desire for ownership supported by the financial means to satisfy the need. Transferability refers to the relative ease with which ownership rights are transferred from one owner to another. Scarcity means that there is a limited supply of property. Utility refers to the property's usefulness for its intended purposes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser is assigned to appraise a house in an area with very few sales over the past year. One of the comparable properties she chooses is a home similar to the subject property. However, the comparable house recently sold as a foreclosure property. When writing her appraisal report, what action will the appraiser take as a result of the foreclosed sale?
option_a: Make a positive adjustment to the sales price of the foreclosed home
option_b: Make no adjustment to the foreclosed home
option_c: Make a positive adjustment to the value of the subject property
option_d: Make a negative adjustment to the sales price of the foreclosed home
feedback: A foreclosed home usually will sell for less than fair market value. The appraiser will make a positive, upward adjustment to the sales price of the foreclosed home to compensate for the sale at less than market value. In the sales comparison approach, adjustments are made to the sales prices of comparable properties, never to the subject property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the cost approach to value, the appraiser makes use of the
option_a: sales prices of similar buildings in the area.
option_b: owner's original cost of the building.
option_d: estimated replacement cost of the building.
feedback: The cost approach is used to estimate the replacement or reproduction cost of the building. Estimated replacement or reproduction cost less accrued depreciation plus estimated land value equals the estimated value by the cost approach. The owner's original purchase cost is not used in any approach to value since the process is determining current value. The sale of similar buildings is used in the sales comparison approach. Assessed value is used only by taxing authorities, but never in determining market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser determines that the cost approach is the most reliable approach in determining the value of an older government building. In estimating the value, the appraiser will MOST likely start by determining the:
feedback: The replacement cost is the cost to construct an improvement similar to the subject property, using current construction methods and materials, but not necessarily an exact duplicate. Replacement cost new is more frequently used in appraising older structures because it eliminates obsolete features and takes advantage of current construction materials and techniques. Reproduction cost is the construction cost at current prices of an exact duplicate of the improvement, including both the benefits and the drawbacks of the property. The gross rent multiplier and gross effective income are used in the income approach to value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser has been hired to prepare an appraisal on a rented property that includes an elegant old mansion that is now used entirely as an insurance company office. Which approach to value would the appraiser MOST rely on?
feedback: The income approach is the most appropriate approach for income producing properties such as an office building, even if the building was once a residence. The gross rent multiplier is not an approach to value. It is a process of estimating the market value of income-producing residential property. The sales comparison approach and the replacement cost approach may be used by an appraiser to assist in determining an estimate of value, but these approaches are not as useful or precise as the income approach to that property. Their findings will be weighed less than those of the income approach in the appraiser's final reconciliation.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: To find the value of a property, if the net operating income and the capitalization rate are known, an appraiser using the income approach to value would
option_a: multiply the effective gross income by the capitalization rate.
option_b: multiply the net operating income by the capitalization rate.
option_c: divide the net operating income by the capitalization rate.
option_d: divide the capitalization rate by the net operating income.
feedback: To find the value, the net operating income (NOI) is divided by the capitalization rate. Remember the IRV formula: Income divided by Rate or Value. In the income approach, I ( NOI) ÷ R (capitalization rate) = Value. If an appraiser knows the NOI and the value of the property, the capitalization rate may be found: NOI ÷ value = capitalization rate. If the appraiser knows the capitalization rate and the value of the property, the NOI can be calculated: value x rate = NOI.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Reconciliation is an appraisal term used to describe
option_a: the method used to determine the most appropriate capitalization rate for a property.
option_b: an average of real estate values for properties similar to the subject property.
option_c: the appraiser's determination of a property's highest value.
option_d: the appraiser's analysis and weighing of the findings of all three appraisal approaches
feedback: Reconciliation is the process used by the appraiser in determining how much weight should be given to each of the three approaches to value, if any. After considering all the data, the appraiser will estimate a final indication of value. Reconciliation does not mean taking the average of the estimates of value from the approaches; averaging is not used in any part of the appraisal process. It does not determine a highest value, but the fair market value of the property. Determining the capitalization rate is involved in the income approach to value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Calculating a dollar value for depreciation should be done when using which of the following?
option_c: The sales comparison approach to value
feedback: One of the components of the cost approach is the calculation of depreciation caused by wear and tear, functionality of items, or outside influences. The calculation is not used in the sales comparison or income approaches to value. A gross rent multiplier is used in the income approach to value for one-to-four residential rental properties.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: South Carolina uses a sex offender management application called
feedback: South Carolina uses a sex offender management application called SORT

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser asked to estimate the value of an existing strip shopping center would probably give the MOST weight to which approach to value?
feedback: The income approach will have the most weight in the analysis of income-producing property. The sales comparison approach will have the most weight in the analysis of single-family residential property. The cost approach may be used as one approach to appraising income property, but the income approach will be given the most weight by the appraiser. The gross rent multiplier method is used in the income approach but to determine value of residential income property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the income approach, all of the following are considered when calculating net operating income EXCEPT
feedback: Debt service (mortgage payments of principal and interest) is not included in calculating net operating income. Management fees, real estate taxes, cost of utilities, insurance, and other costs to maintain the property are all considered in determining a property's operating expenses.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In MOST market areas, rents are determined by:
feedback: The amount of properties and vacancy determine rent values. If the supply is low and demand is high rents go up. If the supply is high and demand is low rents go down. HUD, tenants' unions, and apartment owner associations do not play a major factor in determining rents.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser conducting an appraisal of a single-family home discovers that the subject home has one more bedroom than any of the three comparable properties the appraiser wants to use in the appraisal. To account for the extra bedroom, the appraiser will:
option_a: subtract the value of the bedroom from the sales price of each comparable.
option_b: add the value of the bedroom to the sales price of each comparable.
option_c: subtract the value of the bedroom from the subject property.
option_d: add the value of the bedroom to the subject property.
feedback: Adjustments are made only to comparable properties, never the subject. If the comparable is better than the subject or has a feature that is present in a comparable but not in the subject, the value of the amenity is subtracted from the sales price of the comparable. If the subject is better than the comparable, as in this situation, the value of the amenity is added to the comparable sales price.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The owners of the smaller model of houses in a neighborhood with larger homes may find that the values of their homes are affected by what principle?
feedback: Under the principle of progression, the smaller homes in an area with larger homes will have their value increased. The principle of regression is the opposite of progression and states that the owners of larger homes in an area with smaller homes may find the values of their homes decrease. Competition is the interaction of supply and demand causing prices for property to rise or fall. Under the principle of contribution the law of increasing returns applies when money spent on improvements to a property produce an increase in income or value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: All of the following are steps in the appraisal process EXCEPT
option_a: reconciling the data collected by the approaches used for the subject property.
option_b: considering the seller's estimate of the property's value.
option_c: gathering specific data on the subject property.
option_d: gathering general data for the area of the subject property.
feedback: The opinions of a seller as to the value of the property are often not an accurate estimate of value, as the seller's opinion could be based on emotion rather than objective reasoning. In estimating the property's value, the appraiser will gather data on the area of the property and data on the subject property. The appraiser will apply the approach or approaches to value appropriate for the type of property being appraised and will reconcile the data from the approaches to find a final estimate of value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser has been employed to estimate the market value of a parcel of vacant land. The resulting appraisal report would NOT include reference to the
option_a: highest and best use of the parcel.
option_d: most probable price the parcel will bring.
feedback: The listed price of the parcel is the price that the owner wants for the property. It may or may not be a realistic price or reflect the fair market value of the property. Highest and best use, the most probable price the parcel will bring, and the physical dimensions of the parcel are all applicable to the analysis by the appraiser in determining the market value of the parcel.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which approach to value would require the value of the land to be calculated separately from the value of the improvements?
feedback: The cost approach requires a separate estimated value for the land. Estimated replacement or reproduction cost less accrued depreciation plus estimated land value equals the estimated value by the cost approach. Neither the income approach nor the sales comparison approach calculates the value of land separately from the value of a property's improvements.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When used by an appraiser, the income approach to valuation makes use of which of the following?
feedback: Capitalization is a method of estimating today the future value of an income stream. Depreciation is the loss of value for any reason. Appreciation is an increase of value. Equalization is used by tax assessors and is a process of adjusting the assessed rate in a taxing district to achieve more conformity with other tax districts.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Economic, environmental, and external obsolescence refer to
option_a: lack of maintenance and ordinary wear and tear.
option_b: factors outside of the property lines that affect the property's value.
option_c: obsolescence that is reasonable and economically feasible to correct.
option_d: outdated items and changes in technology.
feedback: Lack of maintenance and ordinary wear and tear are marks of physical deterioration. Outdated items and outdated technology indicate functional obsolescence in a property. Obsolescence that is economically feasible to correct is curable obsolescence.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following documents must be transacted using the traditional paper and ink format?
option_a: Documents relating to new car sales
option_b: Documents relating to real estate transactions
option_c: Documents relating to installment type purchases
option_d: Documents relating to adoption and divorce
feedback: Documents relating to adoption and divorce must be transacted using the traditional paper and ink format

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What is the primary goal of homeowners’ associations?
feedback: The primary goal of homeowners’ associations is to increase property values

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Doug agreed to help his elderly neighbor, Mrs. Noonan, sell her house… Can Doug sue for payment?
option_a: Yes, although the statute of frauds does not permit recovery on an oral agreement for real estate transactions, South Carolina law allows enforcement of an oral commission contract under these circumstances.
option_b: No, South Carolina law requires an individual to have a valid real estate license at the time of closing in order to collect a commission from a seller.
option_c: No, South Carolina law disallows lawsuits to collect commissions unless the injured party is a licensed salesperson whose license was in effect before the oral agreement was made.
option_d: Yes, because Doug was scheduled to begin his formal real estate licensing coursework within 30 days of making the agreement with Mrs. Noonan, the commission agreement is binding.
feedback: Doug cannot sue Mrs. Noonan for commission because he did not have an active license at the time the commission agreement was reached.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The three major types of timeshare periods are fixed weeks, floating weeks and
feedback: The three major types of timeshare periods are fixed weeks, floating weeks and flex time

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Property owners may file an appeal of the assessed valuation of their property as often as every year.  Information required to be included with the appeal is
option_b: a certified copy of the property owner’s deed to the property evidencing ownership
option_c: a list of properties that are similar to the subject property showing lower assessed values
option_d: the owner’s title insurance policy issued by a local title insurance company
feedback: According to local law, a property owner must present evidence that the assessor made an error to a review board or appeal board. Typical evidence would include market data on comparable properties that sold recently, or evidence that neighboring properties had less appreciation or even declined in assessed value in comparison with the protestor's property

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: There are several types of exemptions that can be applied to the property’s assessed valuation.  For example, an exemption may include
option_c: private school educator or elementary school teacher
feedback: Miscellaneous exemptions may be granted to classes of property owner, such as: senior citizens, widows, and disabled individuals

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Risk management is described as
option_a: a way to address the expectations of investors
option_b: a structured approach to dealing with uncertainties and consequences of risk
option_c: a systematic approach to shifting financial liability to others
option_d: a structured approach to choosing wise investments
feedback: The purpose of risk management is to develop a structured approach when dealing with uncertainties and understand the consequences of risk

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If a taxing entity initiates an assessment, it becomes a lien on the property.  This type of lien is called
feedback: If a taxing entity initiates an assessment, the assessment creates an involuntary tax lien. If property owners initiate the assessment by requesting the local government to provide the improvement, the assessment creates a voluntary tax lien

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: One of the property manager’s fundamental responsibilities is
option_a: obtaining construction loans for the principal
option_c: finding a buyer for the property
option_d: maintaining good standing in a managers’ professional association
feedback: Financial reporting to the principal is a fundamental responsibility of the property manager. Reports may be required monthly, quarterly, and annually. Required reports typically include operating budget, cash flow, profit and loss, and budget comparison statements

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: If a property’s vacancy rate is significantly lower than market rates, it may be a sign that the manager needs to
feedback: Vacancy rates are directly related to demand and competitive supply in the market. If vacancy rates in the managed property are too high, the manager may have to lower rates or identify problems in the property or its management that are contributing to vacancy level. On the other hand, if the property's vacancy rate is significantly lower than market rates, the manager may conclude that higher rental rates are called for

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements about the property manager’s responsibility for security and safety is true?
option_a: The manager has no responsibilities for building safety beyond ensuring that fire doors and sprinklers are working
option_b: The manager’s security responsibilities are limited to the common areas
option_c: A court may hold the manager responsible for the physical safety of tenants, employees, and customers in leased premises
option_d: The manager’s security responsibilities are limited to tenants and their employees in their leased premises
feedback: A court may hold a manager and owner responsible for the physical safety of employees, tenants, and customers in leased premises. In addition to standard life safety and security systems such as sprinklers, fire doors, smoke alarms, fire escapes, and door locks, a manager may have to provide electronic and human monitoring systems (security cameras, security guards) and be prepared to take action against tenants who allow, conduct or contribute to dangerous criminal activities such as assault and drug use

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Commercial fire and hazard insurance policies usually require coverage to equal at least 80 percent of the property’s
feedback: The amount of coverage provided by certain types of policies may be based on whether the property is insured at depreciated value or current replacement value. Depreciated value is original value minus the loss in value over time. Current replacement value, which is more expensive, is the amount it would cost to rebuild or replace the property at current rates. Commercial policies include coinsurance clauses requiring the insured to bear a portion of the loss. Fire and hazard policies usually require the coverage to be in an amount equal to at least 80 percent of the replacement value

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The building commissioner informed the developer of a new 20-story luxury property that, by city ordinance, each unit must have an installed sprinkler system. The risk management approach enforced by the city is
feedback: Reduction involves taking steps to reduce the probability or the severity of a potential loss. However, this strategy may result in reducing risk in one area only to increase it in another. A familiar example is a sprinkler system that dispenses water to reduce the risk of fire but at the same time increases the risk of water damage

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Entering into an activity in spite of known risks and taking full responsibility for the consequences is known as
feedback: Retention of risk means entering into an activity in spite of known risks and taking full responsibility for the consequences. This is, in effect, self-insurance, the only strategy left when risk cannot be reduced or transferred and one has decided not to avoid it because of the desirability of the potential benefits

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following documents should spell out how licensees should handle essential aspects of the company’s business?
feedback: A company policy & procedures manual should spell out how to handle every aspect of the company’s business that agents and brokers need to know—from handling consumers’ funds and documents, conducting consumer transactions, dealing with MLS-related matters, and placing signage, to all procedures prescribed by state or federal law, especially license, banking and fair housing laws

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following provides coverage against losses resulting from criminal or negligent acts of an employee?
feedback: A surety bond provides coverage against losses resulting from criminal activities or negligent acts of an employee

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following reduces the risk that clients and customers will accuse a licensee of misleading or inducing them to make a decision with incomplete information?
feedback: By ensuring that all parties have the information they are entitled to, proper disclosure reduces the risk that clients and customers will accuse a licensee of misleading or inducing them to make a decision with incomplete information. Further, laws in every state require disclosures of one kind or another

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The policy manual should cover the company’s rules. For example,
feedback: The policy manual should cover the company’s rules in such areas as floor duty procedures, assignment of relocation properties to agents, referrals between agents within the company, and requirements for continuing education, sales meeting participation, and property tours

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following can be more difficult to document?
feedback: It is always difficult to document telephone or face-to-face conversations, especially with the constant use of cell phones from a variety of locations. It is a good practice to make brief notes at the time and then write them up later for mailing or faxing to the other party

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: The duty to exercise skill, care and diligence means that licensees
option_a: must put the client’s interests above those of everyone else
option_b: are required to act on the principal’s wishes
option_c: are required to hold in confidence any information that would harm the client
option_d: may not be casual or negligent in their actions
feedback: The duty to exercise skill, care and diligence means that licensees may not be casual or negligent in their actions. Licensee negligence is actionable when principals are harmed by the licensee’s failure to be diligent or competent

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following statements is true regarding the Sherman Antitrust Act?
option_a: The Sherman Antitrust Act prohibits mergers or acquisitions that are likely to lessen competition and increase prices to consumers
option_b: The Sherman Antitrust Act forbids unfair competition in interstate commerce but establishes no criminal penalties
option_c: The Sherman Antitrust Act authorized searches of a business, monitoring phone calls, and employing informants equipped with secret listening devices
option_d: The Sherman Antitrust Act makes illegal all contracts, agreements, and conspiracies among competitors that would unfairly restrict interstate trade
feedback: The Sherman Antitrust Act makes illegal all contracts, agreements, and conspiracies among competitors that would unfairly restrict interstate trade by fixing prices, rigging bids, or other means

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is “cash flow?”
option_a: The total of rents plus revenues from other sources generated by a property
option_b: Fixed operating expenses minus variable operating expenses
option_c: Effective gross income minus net operating income
option_d: Net operating income minus debt service and reserves
feedback: The total of scheduled rents plus revenues from such sources as vending services, storage charges, late fees, utilities, and contracts is the potential gross income. Subtracting losses caused by uncollected rents, vacancies and evictions gives effective gross income. Operating expenses are subtracted from this total to show net operating income. When debt service and reserves (which are not counted as operating expenses) are subtracted, the result is cash flow

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If the assessed valuation of a property is $20,000, with a tax rate of $4.00 per $100, what is the annual tax?
feedback: Remember that the $4.00 per $100 means that the $20,000 must be divided by 100, giving a total of $200. That, multiplied by $4.00 gives the total tax of $800.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What are “tenant improvements?”
option_a: Alterations to a rental space made to fit a particular tenant
option_b: Marketing programs that yield a higher quality of tenant
option_c: Increased revenue resulting from a rise in rental rates
option_d: Increased occupancy resulting from a population increase in the market area
feedback: Alterations made specifically for certain tenants are called build-outs or tenant improvements. The work may involve merely painting and re-carpeting a rental space, or erecting new walls and installing special electrical or other systems. In new buildings, spaces are often left incomplete so that they can be finished to an individual tenant's specifications. In such cases, it is important to clarify which improvements will be considered tenant property (trade fixtures) and which will belong to the building

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: One of a manager’s common environmental responsibilities is
option_a: ensuring that the building is properly waterproofed
option_c: seeing that all hazardous materials are hidden from view
option_d: remodeling the building every year to keep up with changing standards
feedback: A variety of environmental concerns confronts a property manager, ranging from air quality to waste disposal, tenant concerns, and federal, state and local environmental regulations. The managed property may contain asbestos, radon, mold, lead, and other problematic substances. Tenants may produce hazardous waste. The manager must be aware of the issues and see that proper procedures are in place to deal with them, including providing means for proper disposal of hazardous materials, arranging for environmental audits and undertaking possible remediation. For instance, an audit may show that a building is causing tenants to become sick because of off-gassing from construction materials combined with a lack of ventilation. Remediation may consist of nothing more than replacing carpets and improving ventilation, and the manager, if empowered to do so, should take the necessary steps

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: $140 is 3.5% of what amount?
feedback: Remember to always divide to find the value or rate, so in this case, $140/.035 = $4,000.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: $140 is 3.5% of what amount?
feedback: Remember to always divide to find the value or rate, so in this case, $140/.035 = $4,000.

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: Which of the following is one of the landlord’s rights under most residential leases?
option_a: Enter the tenant’s property at any time without notice
option_b: Raise the rent at any time during the lease term after giving due notice
option_d: Retake possession of the premises at any time after giving due notice
feedback: The landlord has the right to expect prompt payment of rent and adherence to building rules

subject: B6 Governmental Controls
question_text: What documents grant the homeowners’ association the right to develop binding rules for the association members?
feedback: Covenants, conditions, and restrictions grant the homeowners’ association the right to develop binding rules for the association members

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A homeowner constructs an eight-bedroom brick house with a tennis court, a greenhouse, and an indoor pool in a neighborhood of modest two-bedroom and three-bedroom frame houses on narrow lots. The value of this house is MOST likely to be affected by what principle?
feedback: Larger lavish homes in a modest neighborhood will be drawn down in value by the modest, less lavish homes. Regression is the opposite of progression. Under progression, the value of a modest home would be higher if it were located among larger, fancier homes. The principle of substitution states value is determined by what the buyer can find to buy that is similar or equal to the property under consideration. The principle of contribution is used to determine the value of adding improvement to the property.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the valuation of a large apartment complex, the MOST weight would be given to which approach to value?
feedback: The income approach is the primary approach used for income producing properties such as an apartment complex. The cost approach would most likely not be used due to the difficulty of determining a replacement or reproduction cost for the property. The sales comparison approach is most commonly used in the appraisal of single family residences. The process of reconciliation requires the appraiser to weigh which of the three approaches is most appropriate, as they do not have equal weight and may not be averaged together to determine a value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In computing the square footage of a single family home, an appraiser measures the external dimensions of the gross living area, which includes all of the following EXCEPT:
feedback: To compute the square footage of a single family home, the external dimensions of the building are measured. A garage, porch, patio, or unfinished areas are not used when computing the square footage of a property. Unfinished areas are included in another section of an appraisal report.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The term depreciation refers to the:
option_a: capitalized value of lost rental income.
option_b: costs incurred to renovate or modernize a building.
option_c: value of real estate after the expiration of its useful life.
option_d: loss of value in real estate from any cause.
feedback: The term ''depreciation'' refers to the loss of value in real estate from any cause either on the property or off the property, or a combination of the two.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The purpose of an appraisal is to:
option_a: set the amount of earnest money the seller should accept from a purchaser.
option_b: determine the projected income of a property.
option_c: set the market price of a property.
option_d: estimate the market value of a property.
feedback: In an appraisal, the appraiser estimates the market value of the property. They do not determine the market price, which is the price for which the property actually sells, which may be higher or lower than the appraised value or estimated market value. An appraiser may determine the projected income of a property as part of the income approach. Earnest money and other contract considerations do not have an impact on the market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraisal differs from a competitive market analysis conducted by a broker, in that an appraisal is based solely on an analysis of properties:
option_d: which are currently on the market.
feedback: An appraisal is based on the analysis of properties that have actually sold. A comparative market analysis (CMA) features properties similar to the subject property in location, size, and amenities. A CMA includes analysis of properties currently on the market; those properties currently listed that compete for buyers with the subject property; properties that have actually sold (recently closed properties); and properties whose listings have expired.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The appraised value of a residence with four bedrooms and one bathroom would probably be reduced because of:
feedback: Functional obsolescence exists when a property has outmoded or unacceptable physical or design features. A four-bedroom home with one bath is not desirable for buyers. External obsolescence exists due to economic, environmental, or social forces outside a property. Physical deterioration refers to a loss of value because a property has not been maintained properly (deferred maintenance).

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The ability of a community to attract income and business is known as the community's:
feedback: The economic base measures the ability of a community to attract business and income. Life cycle refers to the expected useful lifetime of equipment or property. Market value is the most probable price a property would bring in an arm's-length transaction under normal conditions on the open market. A zoning plan determines the types of improvements permitted on specific properties in a community.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A CMA or BPO would be acceptable for all of the following EXCEPT:
option_c: setting the selling price of a home.
option_d: being used to determine what to offer for a property.
feedback: A CMA (competitive market analysis) or a BPO (brokers price opinion) can be used to determine the asking price of a property or for determining rent. But because CMA's and BPO's are opinions of real estate agents or brokers, and are not performed by licensed appraisers, they are generally not acceptable for determining conventional loan values. They are also not acceptable for determining federal loan amounts.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Defined as a loss in value from any cause, depreciation is generally divided into three categories. The loss of value due to the normal wear and tear on a property is called:
feedback: Physical deterioration is the loss of value based on the physical wear and tear on the property. It can be either curable or incurable deterioration. External and economic obsolescence are a reduction in a property's value caused by factors outside of the property lines. Functional obsolescence is a loss in value due to such factors as outmoded or unacceptable features of the property itself.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In doing a market analysis, an appraiser found a recently sold property where the owners had just gone through a divorce. The property had been listed for $160,000 for 3 months but was purchased for $140,000 by one of the spouses. Should the appraiser use this as a comparable?
option_a: No, because it had only been listed for 3 months
option_b: No, because the divorce makes it not an "at arms' length" transaction
option_c: Yes, the appraiser would use the actual sale price of $140,000
option_d: Yes, because it was a comparable type property
feedback: "At arm's length" means transactions involving independent and objective parties with no unrelated motivations that might distort the true value. In this case, the relationship between the owners might have skewed the final sales price, making it an unreliable comparable.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which appraisal licensing category allows an appraiser to appraise residential property of one unit to four units without regard to transaction value or complexity?
feedback: A Certified Residential Appraiser is qualified to appraise one-to-four residential units without regard to value or complexity.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A normal size section in the Government Rectangular Survey System contains:
feedback: The Government Rectangular Survey System is a rectangular system of land survey that divides a district into 24-square mile quadrangles from the meridian (north-south line) and the baseline (east-west line). The tracts are divided into 6-mile-square parts called townships, which are in turn divided into 36 tracts of 1 mile by 1 mile squares called sections.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A real estate agent should tell the buyer, a customer, about which of the following?
option_a: A pending or recent zoning change
option_b: The lowest price the seller is willing to accept
option_c: The seller's motivation for marketing his property
option_d: The seller is getting a divorce
feedback: In this case, because the buyer is the agent's customer (and not a client), the agent doesn't owe fiduciary duty to the buyer and must only share material facts about the property. A zoning change is a "material fact" and something that can have a direct impact on the property's value or desirability. For example, if the zoning in a middle class neighborhood on the edge of a growing city was changed from residential to mixed use, it means that a number of homes could be turned into businesses, which would make the property less desirable to people seeking a quiet place to raise a family.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The first step in an appraisal is:
feedback: Every appraisal is for a certain purpose (i.e. homeowner request, loan purposes, insurance claims, etc.). Defining the problem in an appraisal would be for the appraiser to identify why the valuation was requested, and move forward with their methodology in regards to proceeding with the appraisal.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A property has a net income of $30,000. An appraiser decides to use a 12% capitalization rate rather than a 10% rate on this property. What is the result of using the higher capitalization rate?
option_b: No change in the appraised value
feedback: To find the answer here, divide the net income by each cap rate- $30,000 ÷ .10 = $300,000 and $30,000 ÷ .12 = $250,000. So using a 12% cap rate rather than 10% resulted in the appraised value dropping from $300,000 to $250,000, a decrease of $50,000.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The capitalization rate on a property reflects which of these factors?
feedback: Capitalization rate is a real estate valuation measure used to compare different real estate investments. Although there are many variations, a cap rate is often calculated as the ratio between the net operating income produced by an asset and the original capital cost or alternatively its current market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of these is an example of external obsolescence?
option_a: Leaks in the roof of a warehouse, making the premises unusable and therefore unrentable
option_b: Numerous pillars supporting the ceiling in a store
option_c: Coal cellar in a house with central heating
option_d: Vacant, abandoned, and run-down buildings in an area
feedback: External obsolescence, also known as economic obsolescence, is a factor that reduces the value of an improvement because of something external to the property itself. It's not about whether the house is outdated or not, but rather something outside of the home that is causing a lower value. It's usually something that cannot be cured.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Physical deterioration is considered curable whenever:
option_a: it does not result in loss of economic utility.
option_b: it can be repaired regardless of the cost.
option_c: it is caused by lack of maintenance.
option_d: the cost to correct the issue is less than the resulting value increase.
feedback: Physical deterioration is considered "curable" whenever it makes financial sense to fix a problem. For example, let's say an older office building needs to update electric and add bathrooms, repartition offices and fire safety features to meet code for resale. The cost to complete the work is estimated at $2.2 million. However, the building is fully paid for and can easily be sold for $4 million. In this case, curing the defects makes sense. However, if those numbers were reversed, as is often the case, demolishing the building and starting over with a new structure or different use (e.g., retail space, a parking garage, etc.) would be the wiser choice.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A suburban home that lacks indoor plumbing suffers from:
feedback: Functional obsolescence, a term commonly used in real estate, is a reduction of an object's usefulness or desirability because of an outdated design feature that cannot be easily changed.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When an appraiser correlates the three approaches into a final estimate, he:
option_a: accords the greatest weight to the median value.
option_b: reconciles the differences according to the type of property being appraised and the quantity and quality of data available.
option_d: selects the estimate nearest that desired by the employer.
feedback: In order to maintain accuracy and "transparency" of his calculations, an appraiser reconciles differences and does not average comparable sales to arrive at a final value.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If Jamie's quarterly interest payments are $150 on a $12,000 loan, then what is her annual interest rate?
feedback: To figure out a percentage, divide the interest by the investment. So, $150 x 4 (quarters a year) = $600, and $600/$12,000 = 5%

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A feature found in a comparable property that is not present in the subject property will result in:
option_a: a reduction adjustment to the comparable's selling price.
option_b: an increase adjustment to the subject property's selling price.
option_c: an increase adjustment to the comparable's selling price.
option_d: the reduction adjustment to the subject property's selling price.
feedback: It's important to remember that adjustments are made to the comparable, not the subject property. For example, let's assume two homes are identical in every respect, except the comparable property has a swimming pool the subject home lacks. If the subject property is on the market for $175,000 and a swimming pool typically adds $8,000 to the selling price of homes in that area, the appraiser would deduct $8,000 from the sale price of the comparable rather than adding it to the subject property to make his comparison.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The economic life of an investment property can be described as:
option_a: the time over which yield is attributable to the land itself.
option_b: the actual age of the property.
option_c: the remaining chronological life of the improvements, unadjusted for depreciation.
option_d: the time over which value generated by the investment exceeds its cost of operation.
feedback: The economic life of an investment property is simple- how long can it make money? Once a property costs more to operate than it earns, it reaches the end of its economic life.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A recorded subdivision plat is used in the:
feedback: Lot and block is the "final" survey of property being readied for development and identifies each individual parcel in a subdivision.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Market value is primarily based on:
feedback: Market value is the price at which something will sell within a reasonable period of time. It is also thought of as the price a willing buyer would pay and which a willing seller would accept to transfer ownership of an asset.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the cost approach to value, the appraiser makes use of the:
option_a: Owner's original cost of the building
option_c: Sales prices of similar buildings in the area
option_d: Estimated replacement cost of the building.
feedback: The cost approach is used to estimate the replacement or reproduction cost of the building. Estimated replacement or reproduction cost less accrued depreciation plus estimated land value equals the estimated value by the cost approach. The owner's original purchase cost is not used in any approach to value since the process is determining current value. The sale of similar buildings is used in the sales comparison approach. Assessed value is used only by taxing authorities, but never in determining market value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
