question_text: In the cost approach to value, the appraiser makes use of the:
option_a: estimated current replacement cost of the building.
option_b: sales prices of similar buildings in the area.
option_d: owner’s original cost of the building.
feedback: The cost approach is a real estate valuation method that surmises that the price a buyer should pay for a piece of property should equal the cost to build an equivalent building. In cost approach appraisal, the market price for the property is equal to the cost of land plus cost of construction, less depreciation.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser has been hired to appraise an elegant old mansion now leased as a restaurant. Which approach is most relevant?
feedback: The Income Approach is one of three major groups of methodologies, called valuation approaches, used by appraisers. It is particularly common in commercial real estate appraisal and in business appraisal. It uses the income generated by a property, currently or potentially, to determine value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraiser contracted to determine value of a large apartment building. Which method is MOST useful?
feedback: The Income Approach is one of three major groups of methodologies, called valuation approaches, used by appraisers. It is particularly common in commercial real estate appraisal and in business appraisal. It uses the income a property currently or potentially could generate to determine value.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In valuing a single-family residence by the comparison approach, an appraiser would make adjustments to:
option_a: both the comparable and the subject property.
option_d: all current properties being offered for sale.
feedback: The objective of an appraisal is to arrive at a fair estimate… The most reliable way is to make additions and subtractions from the comparables rather than the other way around.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Looking at shopping centers, the social fiber of the community and distances from schools is called:
feedback: Neighborhood analysis is a formal process of identifying, measuring and analyzing the influences that help determine a neighborhood's vitality and desirability.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Gross rent multiplier (GRM) is the ratio of the price of investment property to its:
feedback: GRM is the ratio of the price of investment property to its annual rental income before considering expenses. Multiply monthly rent by 12 to get annual rent.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When evaluating data in a neighborhood market survey, a residential property manager will be most concerned with:
feedback: Population composition (age, sex, etc.) is often compared over time using population pyramids and is useful in determining the best marketing demographic.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Susan learned the city will build a multi-unit apartment across the street. She sells before value drops. This is an example of:
feedback: Anticipation is an appraisal principle indicating that value can increase or decrease based on the expectation of some future impact.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A home is the smallest in a neighborhood of large houses. The effect on its value is called:
feedback: The principle of progression states that a property of lesser value is enhanced through proximity to higher-value properties.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When appraising a single-family home, an appraiser considers which of the following?
feedback: Considering a property's sale history helps appraisers confirm if current appraised value is reasonable and consistent with physical and market changes.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements defines absorption?
option_a: The amount of total real estate inventory of a certain type that is unoccupied at a given time
option_b: The amount of available property that becomes occupied over a period of time
option_c: The total amount that lenders are willing to absorb in loan defaults
option_d: The acceptable vacancy rate in an investment property
feedback: Absorption is the amount of available property that becomes occupied over a period of time. The absorption process continues through the equilibrium point until price and vacancy conditions are sufficiently attractive to encourage renewed construction. Then the cycle repeats

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The real estate market is __________ by definition
feedback: Since the real estate market is local by definition, local factors weigh heavily in affecting real estate market conditions

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Regional and national economic forces influence the local real estate market in the form of
feedback: Regional and national economic forces influence the local real estate market in the form of: changes in money supply, inflation, national economic cycles

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is an example of government influence over the local real estate market?
option_a: The competitive factors in the market create an increase in the interest rate
option_b: The private restrictions in the condominium bylaws prohibit renters
option_c: An economic base and fluctuations tied to foreign trade
option_d: The city’s government powers declare a moratorium on new construction
feedback: A good example of government influence over the local real estate market is a city government's power to declare a moratorium on new construction, regardless of demand. Such officially declared stoppages may occur because of water or power shortages, insufficiency of thoroughfares, or incompatibility with the master

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Real estate supply and demand, like supply and demand for other economic products, interact in the marketplace to produce
feedback: Real estate supply and demand, like supply and demand for other economic products, interact in the marketplace to produce price movements

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Real estate is a relatively __________ economic product, meaning it cannot always be readily sold for cash
feedback: Real estate is a relatively illiquid economic product, meaning it cannot always be readily sold for cash. Since it is a large, long-term investment that has no exact duplicate, buyers must go through a complex process to evaluate and purchase the right parcel of real estate

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In real estate, __________ is the amount of property available for sale or lease at any given time
feedback: In real estate, supply is the amount of property available for sale or lease at any given time.   Demand is the number of residents in a market using the inventory of residential properties

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements regarding real estate's unique traits is true?
option_b: Real estate is relatively slow to respond to market imbalances
option_c: Two parcels of real estate can be identical
option_d: Real estate responds quickly to market conditions
feedback: Real estate is relatively slow to respond to market imbalances. Because new construction is a large-scale, time-consuming process, the market is slow to respond to increases in demand. The market is similarly slow to respond to sharp declines in demand since the product cannot be moved and sold elsewhere

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If the price of an item is increasing, one can usually assume that
option_a: demand for the item is decreasing in relation to supply of the item
option_b: demand for the item is increasing in relation to supply of the item
option_c: supply of the item is increasing
option_d: demand for the item and supply of the item are increasing
feedback: In a market economy, the primary interactions between supply, demand and price are: if supply increases relative to demand, price decreases; if supply decreases relative to demand, price increases; if demand increases relative to supply, price increases; and if demand decreases relative to supply, price decreases

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following conditions would be true if the market for an item has achieved "market equilibrium?"
option_a: New suppliers will enter the market and drive the price down
option_b: Demand will slowly taper off, driving the price down
option_c: Unmet demand for the item is directed toward demand for some other item
option_d: Supply and demand are equal, and price and value are equal
feedback: A market tends toward a state of equilibrium in which supply equals demand, and price, cost, and value are identical

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: One of the economic characteristics that distinguishes real estate is
option_d: its ability to appreciate in value
feedback: Since no two parcels of real estate can be alike (each has a different location), every parcel of real property has its own appeal. Likewise, no two parcels of real estate can have exactly the same value, except by coincidence

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If Jamie's quarterly interest payments are $150 on a $12,000 loan, then what is her annual interest rate?
feedback: To figure out a percentage, divide the interest by the investment. So, $150 x 4 (quarters a year) = $600, and $600/$12,000 = 5%

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If a new company that sells its products worldwide moves into a town and hires 100 employees, it would be reasonable to expect
option_a: an immediate rise in the demand for industrial real estate, but no other changes in the real estate market
option_b: an increase in demand for all types of real estate
option_c: a housing boom, but no other changes in the real estate market
option_d: an immediate increase in the prices for industrial and office real estate, but no impact on the residential market
feedback: As employment grows, so grows the population, leading to the demand for housing and for retail support. In addition to creating demand for real estate, employment creates the purchasing power necessary for households to acquire dwellings and retail products

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is "absorption?"
option_a: The amount of new space that is added to available space over a period of time
option_b: The number of houses that are built over a period of time
option_c: The amount of space that is occupied at any given time
option_d: The number of available units that become occupied over a period of time
feedback: Absorption is the amount of available property that becomes occupied over a period of time

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: During a time of declining vacancy in a real estate market, one would expect
feedback: When vacancy levels are low, demand for housing will lead to higher prices

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: As a component of real estate value, the principle of substitution suggests that
option_a: if two similar properties are for sale, a buyer will purchase the cheaper of the two
option_b: if one of two adjacent homes is more valuable, the price of the other home will tend to rise
option_c: if too many properties are built in a market, the prices will tend to go down
option_d: people will readily move to another home if it is of equal value
feedback: According to the principle of substitution, a buyer will pay no more for a property than the buyer would have to pay for an equally desirable and available substitute property. For example, if three houses for sale are essentially similar in size, quality and location, a potential buyer is unlikely to choose the one that is priced significantly higher than the other two

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Highest and best use of a property is that use which
option_a: is physically and financially feasible, legal, and the most productive
option_b: is legal, feasible, and deemed the most appropriate by zoning authorities
option_c: entails the largest building that zoning ordinances will allow developers to erect
option_d: conforms to other properties in the area
feedback: This valuation principle holds that there is, theoretically, a single use for a property that produces the greatest income and return. A property achieves its maximum value when it is put to this use. The use must however be legal

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The concept of market value is best described as
option_a: the price a buyer will pay for a property, assuming other similar properties are within the same price range
option_b: the price an informed, unhurried seller will charge for a property assuming a reasonable period of exposure with other competing properties
option_c: the price a buyer and seller agree upon for a property assuming stable interest rates, appreciation rates, and prices of other similar properties
option_d: the price that a willing, informed, and unpressured seller and buyer agree upon for a property assuming a cash price and the property's reasonable exposure to the market
feedback: Market value is an estimate of the price at which a property will sell at a particular time. This type of value is the one generally sought in appraisals and used in brokers' estimates of value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A significant difference between an appraisal and a broker's opinion of value is
option_a: the appraiser tends to use only one or two of the approaches to value
option_b: the broker may not be a disinterested party
option_c: the broker is subject to government regulation in generating the opinion
option_d: the appraiser uses less current market data
feedback: A broker's opinion of value may resemble an appraisal, but it differs from an appraisal in that it is not necessarily performed by a disinterested third party or licensed professional

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A notable weakness of the sales comparison approach to value is that
option_a: there may be no recent sale price data in the market
option_b: the approach is not based on the principle of substitution
option_c: the approach is only accurate with unique, special purpose properties
option_d: sale prices cannot be compared, since all real estate is different
feedback: The sales comparison approach is limited in that every property is unique. As a result, it is difficult to find good comparables, especially for special-purpose properties. In addition, the market must be active; otherwise, sale prices lack currency and reliability

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The steps in the market data approach are
option_a: choose nearby comparables, adjust the subject for differences, estimate the value
option_b: gather relevant price data, apply the data to the subject, estimate the value
option_c: select comparable properties, adjust the comparables, estimate the value
option_d: identify previous price paid, apply an appreciation rate, estimate the value
feedback: The sales comparison approach, also known as the market data approach, is used for almost all properties. Steps in the Sales Comparison Approach:  1. Identify comparable sales. 2. Compare comparables to the subject and make adjustments to comparables. 3. Weight values indicated by adjusted comparables for the final value estimate of the subject

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the sales comparison approach, an adjustment is warranted if
option_a: the buyer obtains conventional financing for the property
option_b: the seller offers below-market seller financing
option_c: a comparable is located in another, albeit similar neighborhood
option_d: one property has a hip roof and the other has a gabled roof
feedback: The principal factors for comparison and adjustment are time of sale, location, physical characteristics, and transaction characteristics. An adjustment may be made for such differences as mortgage loan terms, mortgage assumability, and owner financing

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: To complete the sales comparison approach, the appraiser
option_c: discards all comparables having a lower value
option_d: identifies the subject's value as that of the nearest comparable
feedback: The last step in the approach is to perform a weighted analysis of the indicated values of each comparable. The appraiser, in other words, must identify which comparable values are more indicative of the subject and which are less indicative. However, all comparables are taken into account, not simply the nearest comparable

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: One weakness of the cost approach for appraising market value is that
option_a: builders may not pay market value for materials or labor
option_b: market value is not always the same as what the property cost
option_c: comparables used may not have similar quality of construction
option_d: new properties have inestimable costs and rates of depreciation
feedback: The limitations of the cost approach are that: the cost to create improvements is not necessarily the same as market value; and depreciation is difficult to measure, especially for older buildings

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A home is located in a neighborhood where homeowners on the block have failed to maintain their properties. This is an example of
feedback: Economic (or external) obsolescence is the loss of value due to adverse changes in the surroundings of the subject property that make the subject less desirable. Since such changes are usually beyond the control of the property owner, economic obsolescence is considered an incurable value loss

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In appraisal, loss of value in a property from any cause is referred to as
feedback: Depreciation is the loss of value in an improvement over time. The loss of an improvement's value can come from any cause, such as deterioration, obsolescence, or changes in the neighborhood

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The first two steps in the cost approach are to estimate the value of the land and the cost of the improvements. The remaining steps are
option_a: estimate depreciation, subtract depreciation from cost, and add back the land value
option_b: subtract deterioration from cost, estimate land depreciation, and total the two values
option_c: estimate depreciation of land and improvements, subtract from original cost
option_d: estimate obsolescence, subtract from the cost of land and improvements
feedback: Steps in the Cost Approach:  1. Estimate land value. 2. Estimate reproduction or replacement cost of improvements. 3. Estimate accrued depreciation. 4. Subtract accrued depreciation from reproduction or replacement cost. 5. Add land value to depreciated reproduction or replacement cost

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The roof of a property cost $10,000. The economic life of the roof is 20 years. Assuming the straight-line method of depreciation, what is the depreciated value of the roof after 3 years?

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The income capitalization approach to appraising value is most applicable for which of the following property types?
feedback: The income capitalization approach, or income approach, is used for income properties and sometimes for other properties in a rental market where the appraiser can find rental data. The approach is based on the principle of anticipation: the expected future income stream of a property underlies what an investor will pay for the property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The steps in the income capitalization approach are:
option_a: estimate gross income, multiply times the gross income multiplier
option_b: estimate effective income, subtract tax, apply a capitalization rate
option_c: estimate net income, and apply a capitalization rate to it
option_d: estimate potential income, apply a capitalization rate to it
feedback: The steps are: estimate potential gross income; estimate effective gross income (potential minus vacancy and credit losses); estimate net operating income (effective minus total operating expenses); select a capitalization rate; and apply the capitalization rate. Use the formula: income ÷ cap rate = value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Net operating income is equal to
option_a: gross income minus potential income minus expenses
option_b: effective gross income minus debt service
option_c: potential gross income minus vacancy and credit loss minus expenses
option_d: effective gross income minus vacancy and credit loss
feedback: Effective gross income is potential gross income minus an allowance for vacancy and credit losses. Net operating income is effective gross income minus total operating expenses. Potential gross income - Vacancy & credit losses - Total operating expenses = Net operating income

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If net income on a property is $20,000 and the cap rate is 5%, the value of the property using the income capitalization method is
feedback: Value = Income ÷ Cap rate. Thus, V= $40,000 ÷ .10 = $400,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The principal shortcoming of the gross rent multiplier approach to estimating value is that
option_a: numerous expenses are not taken into account
option_b: the multiplier does not relate to the market
option_c: the method is too complex and cumbersome
option_d: the method only applies to residential properties
feedback: The gross rent multiplier approach leaves many variables out of consideration such as vacancies, credit losses, and operating expenses. In addition, the appraiser must have market rental data to establish multipliers

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If the monthly rent of a property is $3,000, and the gross rent multiplier (GRM) is 80, what is the value of the property?
feedback: GRM x Subject monthly rent = estimated value. 80 x $3,000 = $240,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Real estate value in general is
option_a: the present monetary worth of benefits arising from the ownership of real estate
option_b: based solely on the gross income that the property produces
option_c: determined by the tax benefit resulting from capital gains
option_d: the difference between gross income and operating expenses
feedback: Real estate value in general is the present monetary worth of benefits arising from the ownership of real estate. The primary benefits that contribute to real estate value are: income, appreciation, use, tax benefits

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is an example of utility?
option_a: An investor who anticipates a high return
option_b: An owner who transfers subsurface rights without transferring air rights
option_c: An alligator farmer who buys a swampland
option_d: A buyer who chooses a property based on the number of amenities
feedback: The fact that a property has a use in a certain marketplace contributes to the demand for it. For instance, a swampy area may have an ecological function as a wetland, but it may have no economic utility if it cannot be put to some use that people in the marketplace are willing to pay for. An alligator farmer would find a swampland to offer utility – usefulness

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Property that is encumbered may have a value impairment due to
feedback: How readily or easily title or rights to real estate can be transferred affects the property's value. Property that is encumbered has a value impairment since buyers do not want unmarketable title

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The benefits a buyer expects to derive from a property over a holding period influence what the buyer is willing to pay for it.  This is defined as the principle of
feedback: The benefits a buyer expects to derive from a property over a holding period influence what the buyer is willing to pay for it

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which principle holds that a property's maximal value is attained when its form and use are in tune with surrounding properties?
option_b: The principle of highest and best use
feedback: This principle holds that a property's maximal value is attained when its form and use are in tune with surrounding properties and uses. For example, a two-bedroom, one-bathroom house surrounded by four-bedroom, three-bathroom homes may derive maximal value from a room addition

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The excess value created by assemblage is called
feedback: Assemblage, or the conjoining of adjacent properties, sometimes creates a combined value that is greater than the values of the unassembled properties. The excess value created by assemblage is called plottage value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An estimate of the price at which a property will sell at a particular time is defined as
feedback: Market value is an estimate of the price at which a property will sell at a particular time. This type of value is the one generally sought in appraisals and used in brokers' estimates of value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The value based on the cost of constructing a functional equivalent of the subject property's improvements is defined as
feedback: Replacement value is the value based on the cost of constructing a functional equivalent of the subject property's improvements, assuming current construction costs

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: To reconcile the value estimates produced by the three approaches to value into a final value estimate, an appraiser must do which of the following?
option_a: Calculate and reconcile the reproduction and replacement cost to the property being appraised
option_b: Identify the highest and best use by using all three approaches and planning for any significant differences
option_c: Estimate the land value using all three approaches and making adjustments for any significant differences
option_d: Weigh the appropriateness of a particular approach to the type of property being appraised
feedback: Reconciliation is the estimate produced by evaluating the results produced by the three approaches to value into a final value estimate. To do this, an appraiser must: weigh the appropriateness of a particular approach to the type of property being appraised, take into account the quality and quantity of data obtained in each method

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: To qualify as a comparable, a property must have
option_a: sold in an arm’s length transaction
feedback: To qualify as a comparable, a property must: resemble the subject in size, shape, design, utility and location, have sold recently, generally within six months of the appraisal, have sold in an arm's-length transaction

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Appraisers have specific guidelines for selecting comparables, many of which are set by
option_b: secondary market organizations such as FNMA
option_d: principal clients such as buyers or sellers
feedback: Appraisers have specific guidelines within the foregoing criteria for selecting comparables, many of which are set by secondary market organizations such as FNMA. For example, to qualify as a comparable for a mortgage loan appraisal, a property might have to be located within one mile of the subject

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If the comparable is better than the subject in some characteristic, an amount is ____________ from the sale price of the comparable
feedback: If the comparable is better than the subject in some characteristic, an amount is deducted from the sale price of the comparable. This neutralizes the comparables competitive advantage in an adjustment category

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following is one of the principal factors used as a comparison and adjustment criteria?
feedback: The principal factors for comparison and adjustment are time of sale, location, physical characteristics, and transaction characteristics

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In terms of total adjustments made to a comparable, which of the following statements is true?
option_a: The comparable with the most adjustments tends to be most similar to the subject
option_b: The comparable with the fewest adjustments tends to be most similar to the subject
option_c: If a comparable requires excessive adjustments, it is reliable indicator of value
option_d: Whenever a number of adjustments are made, the margin of error diminishes
feedback: In terms of total adjustments, the comparable with the fewest adjustments tends to be most similar to the subject, hence the best indicator of value. If a comparable requires excessive adjustments, it is increasingly less reliable as an indicator of value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following forms of depreciation is considered to be incurable?
feedback: Economic (or external) obsolescence is the loss of value due to adverse changes in the surroundings of the subject property that make the subject less desirable. Since such changes are usually beyond the control of the property owner, economic obsolescence is considered an incurable value loss

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which method for estimating the reproduction or replacement cost of improvements is being used if the appraiser uses materials cost manuals and estimates of labor costs, overhead, and builder's profit to estimate the cost of constructing separate components of the subject?
feedback: In the unit in place method, the appraiser uses materials cost manuals and estimates of labor costs, overhead, and builder's profit to estimate the cost of constructing separate components of the subject

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which method for estimating the reproduction or replacement cost of improvements is being used if the original cost of constructing the improvement is updated by applying a percentage increase factor to account for increases in nominal costs over time?
feedback: In the cost indexing method the original cost of constructing the improvement is updated by applying a percentage increase factor to account for increases in nominal costs over time

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In accounting, what type of depreciation is represented by an equal loss of value per period over the economic life of the structure
feedback: Accrued depreciation is often estimated by the straight-line method, also called the economic age-life method. This method assumes that depreciation occurs at a steady rate over the economic life of the structure

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In appraisal practice, the concept of economic life is generally defined as
option_b: the final value estimate for the property
option_c: the period during which the structure is expected to remain useful in its original use
option_d: the period during which the structure will require minimal repairs
feedback: The economic life is the period during which the structure is expected to remain useful in its original use. The cost of the structure is divided by the number of years of economic life to determine an annual amount for depreciation

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following consists of estimating annual net operating income from the subject property, then applying a capitalization rate to the income?
feedback: The income capitalization method consists of estimating annual net operating income from the subject property, then applying a capitalization rate to the income. This produces a principal amount that the investor would pay for the property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: If a comparable's total adjustments alter the indicated value only slightly, the comparable is
option_b: adjusted again to be a better match
option_d: is a good indicator of value
feedback: If a comparable's total adjustments alter the indicated value only slightly, the comparable is a good indicator of value. If total adjustments create a large dollar amount between the sale price and the adjusted value, the comparable is a poorer indicator of value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: When using the cost approach to estimate land value, the appraiser uses the
option_d: principal of highest and best use
feedback: To estimate land value, the appraiser uses the sales comparison method: find properties which are comparable to the subject property in terms of land and adjust the sale prices of the comparables to account for competitive differences with the subject property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The appraiser considers in detail all materials, labor, supplies, overhead, and profit to get an accurate estimate of the actual cost to build the improvement. This is the definition of the
feedback: The appraiser considers in detail all materials, labor, supplies, overhead and profit to get an accurate estimate of the actual cost to build the improvement

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following describes a limitation of the income approach?
option_a: It fails to account for operating expenses
option_b: It provides a weak basis for estimating market value
option_c: It is difficult to determine an appropriate capitalization rate
option_d: It is used by investors themselves to determine how much they should pay
feedback: The income capitalization approach is limited in two ways. First, it is difficult to determine an appropriate capitalization rate. This is often a matter of judgment and experience on the part of the appraiser. Secondly, the income approach relies on market information about income and expenses, and it can be difficult to find such information

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following identifies the steps in the sales comparison approach?
option_a: Land value plus depreciated reproduction or replacement cost of improvements
option_b: Value = NOI divided by the capitalization rate
option_c: Compare sale prices, adjust comparables to account for differences with the subject
option_d: Price divided by gross annual income
feedback: The steps in the sales approach are as follows: Compare sale prices, adjust comparables to account for differences with the subject

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following identifies the steps in the income approach?
option_a: Land value plus depreciated reproduction or replacement cost of improvements
option_b: Value = NOI divided by the capitalization rate
option_c: Compare sale prices, adjust comparables to account for differences with the subject
option_d: Price divided by gross annual income
feedback: The steps in the income approach are as follows: Value = NOI divided by the capitalization rate

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Title XI of FIRREA requires that competent individuals whose professional conduct is properly supervised perform
option_c: all appraisals used in federally related transactions
option_d: all CMAs used by sellers to determine the listing price
feedback: Title XI of FIRREA requires that competent individuals whose professional conduct is properly supervised perform all appraisals used in federally related transactions

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If an investment valued at $350,000 returns 12 percent annually, then what is the amount of income produced by this investment?
feedback: Remember, take the value multiplied by the return to equal the income. So, in this case: $350,000 x 12% = $42,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following organizations develop the standards for measuring gross living area?
option_a: American National Standards Institute and Fannie Mae
option_c: National Association of REALTORS & Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice
option_d: Farmer Mac and Department of Housing and Urban Development
feedback: ANSI and Fannie Mae standards. ANSI (American National Standards Institute) and Fannie Mae are the two most prominent organizations which originate and propound modern measuring standards in national real estate practice. Current living area measurement standards can be found in ANSI and Fannie Mae literature. The following guidelines are general and not intended to be rigid or comprehensive. Practitioners must always be aware of and follow local measurement standards.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is the minimum ceiling height to be considered living area?  Assume there is not a sloped ceiling.
feedback: Minimum ceiling height for living area is 7 feet. In two-story properties (or more) with sloped ceilings, at least 50% of the ceiling area must have a height of 7 feet or more.

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A person paid $150,000 for a house with the intention of renting it out for $1,000 per month.  The economic principle that led the person to pay this price based on the property's ability to generate this future income is known as
feedback: Anticipation is the value principle that a buyer will pay a price based on the benefits the buyer expects to derive from a property over a holding period. For example, if an investor anticipates an annual rental income from a leased property to be one million dollars, this expected sum has a direct bearing on what the investor will pay for the property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following situations illustrates the principle of contribution?
option_a: A homebuyer makes a down payment of 20% instead of the 10% the lender requires
option_b: A homeowner adds a third bathroom to a house and thereby increases the appraised value by $10,000
option_c: The appraised value of a house goes up by $20,000 over a two-year period because of the prices recently paid for other houses in the neighborhood
option_d: Because of a decline in mortgage interest rates, a homeowner in a certain market is able to list her house at a higher price
feedback: The principle of contribution focuses on the degree to which a particular improvement affects market value of the overall property. In essence, the contribution of the improvement is equal to the change in market value that the addition of the improvement causes. For example, adding a bathroom to a house may contribute an additional $15,000 to the appraised value. Thus the contribution of the bathroom is $15,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A property owner buys an adjacent parcel and combines it with the original parcel to create a property with a higher value than the total of the two separate property values.  The operative principle of value in this situation is called
feedback: Assemblage, or the conjoining of adjacent properties, sometimes creates a combined value that is greater than the values of the unassembled properties. The excess value created by assemblage is called plottage value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is the difference between the appraised value of a property and its mortgage value, if any?
option_b: The appraised value is an appraiser's estimate; mortgage value is the amount a lender will lend for the purchase of the property
option_c: The appraised value is an appraiser's estimate; mortgage value is the value a lender imputes to the property as collateral
option_d: Appraised value is mortgage value multiplied by a lender's loan-to-value ratio
feedback: Appraised value is an appraiser's opinion of a property's value. Mortgage value is the value of the property as collateral for a loan

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: What is the difference between market value and market price, if any?
option_b: Market value is an estimate; market price is the price at which a property is offered
option_c: Market value is an average price derived from comparable sales; market price is a price based on the cost of creating the property
option_d: Market value is an estimate; market price is the price at which a property sold
feedback: Market value is an estimate of the price at which a property will sell at a particular time. The market price, as opposed to market value, is what a property actually sells for. Market price should theoretically be the same as market value if all the conditions essential for market value are present. Market price, however, may not reflect the analysis of comparables and of investment value that an estimate of market value includes

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An appraisal is
option_a: a professional appraiser's opinion of value, supported by data and following approved methods
option_b: a professional appraiser's estimate of market price
option_c: an estimate of sale price offered by a knowledgeable real estate professional
option_d: a broker's opinion of value, based on comparison with recent comparable sales and current listings in the multiple listing service
feedback: An appraisal is distinguished from other estimates of value in that it is an opinion of value supported by data and performed by a professional, disinterested third party. Appraisers acting in a professional capacity are also regulated by state laws and bound to standards set by the appraisal industry

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The first step in the appraisal process, regardless of the appraisal method, is to
option_a: identify the highest and best use of the property to be appraised
option_c: estimate the value of the land as if it were vacant
option_d: define the appraisal problem and the purpose of the appraisal
feedback: The first step in the process is to define the appraisal problem and the purpose of the appraisal. This involves identifying the subject property by legal description; specifying the interest to be appraised; specifying the purpose of the appraisal; specifying the date for which the appraisal is valid; and identifying the type of value to be estimated

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In the final step of an appraisal, the appraiser reconciles the value estimates derived by the various appraisal approaches by
option_a: disregarding the high and low extreme results
option_b: averaging the results of all three approaches
option_c: weighing the applicability of the approaches and considering the quality of data supporting each approach
option_d: choosing the result that is closest to the average for properties in the immediate neighborhood
feedback: The final step in the appraisal process is to reconcile the value estimates produced by the three approaches to value into a final value estimate. To do this, an appraiser must weigh the appropriateness of a particular approach to the type of property being appraised and take into account the quality and quantity of data obtained in each method

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: If an investment valued at $350,000 returns 12 percent annually, then what is the amount of income produced by this investment?
feedback: Remember, take the value multiplied by the return to equal the income. So, in this case: $350,000 x 12% = $42,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements properly describes the central concept of the sales comparison approach?
option_a: Find the median price of recently sold comparable properties and add or subtract dollar amounts in the subject property to account for competitive differences
option_b: Make dollar adjustments to the sale prices of comparable properties to account for competitive differences with the subject
option_c: Find at least three comparable properties that are currently for sale and make dollar adjustments to the listing prices to account for competitive differences with the subject
option_d: Apply an appreciation factor to the price at which the subject property most recently sold and make dollar adjustments to account for competitive differences with comparable properties currently for sale
feedback: The sales comparison approach consists of comparing sale prices of recently sold properties that are comparable with the subject, and making dollar adjustments to the price of each comparable to account for competitive differences with the subject. After identifying the adjusted value of each comparable, the appraiser weighs the reliability of each comparable and the factors underlying how the adjustments were made. The weighting yields a final value range based on the most reliable factors in the analysis

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: One of the strengths of the sales comparison approach is that it
option_a: takes into account the subject property's investment value
option_b: reveals the profit margin of the builder or developer of the subject property
option_c: discovers the underlying value of the subject property apart from the influence of competing properties
option_d: takes into account the competitive value of specific amenities of the subject property
feedback: The sales comparison approach is widely used because it takes into account the subject property's specific amenities in relation to competing properties. In addition, because of the currency of its data, the approach incorporates present market realities

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: In making dollar adjustments in the sales comparison approach, the appraiser
option_a: adds value to a comparable that is inferior to the subject property
option_b: adds value to the subject property if it is inferior to a comparable
option_c: subtracts value from a comparable that is inferior to the subject property
option_d: subtracts value from the subject property if it is inferior to a comparable
feedback: If the comparable is inferior to the subject in some characteristic, an amount is added to the price of the comparable. If the comparable is better than the subject in some characteristic, an amount is deducted from the sale price of the comparable. This neutralizes the comparable's competitive advantage or disadvantage in an adjustment category. For example, a comparable has a swimming pool and the subject does not. To equalize the difference, the appraiser deducts an amount, say $6,000, from the sale price of the comparable

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The best comparable property for use in the sales comparison approach is the one that
option_a: is located closest to the subject property
option_b: requires the fewest and smallest adjustments
option_d: was built according to the same plan as the subject and at about the same time
feedback: As a rule, the fewer the total number of adjustments, the smaller the adjustment amounts, and the less the total adjustment amount, the more reliable the comparable

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A house is being appraised using the sales comparison approach.  The house has three bedrooms, two bathrooms, and a patio.  The appraiser selects a comparable house that has three bedrooms, 2.5 bathrooms, and no patio.  The comparable house just sold for $300,000. A half-bath is valued at $5,000, and a patio at $1,000.  Assuming all else is equal, what is the adjusted value of the comparable?
feedback: Since the comparable has an extra half-bath, it is adjusted downward to equalize with the subject. Conversely, since it has no patio, the appraiser adds value to the comparable. Thus, $300,000 minus $5,000 plus $1,000 equals $296,000

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements properly describes the methodology of the cost approach to appraisal?
option_a: Apply a depreciation factor to the reported actual cost of acquiring and improving the subject property
option_b: Estimate the cost of building the improvements on the subject property
option_c: Estimate the land value and add to this the actual cost of the improvements adjusted for competitive differences with similar properties
option_d: Add the estimated land value and cost of improvements and subtract the accrued depreciation of the improvements
feedback: The cost approach consists of estimating the value of the land "as if vacant;" estimating the cost of improvements; estimating and deducting accrued depreciation; and adding the estimated land value to the estimated depreciated cost of the improvements

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: One of the strengths of the cost approach is that it
option_a: takes into account the amount of money required to develop a similar property
option_b: is very accurate for a property with new improvements that represent the highest and best use
option_c: results in an actual price in dollars instead of an estimated value
option_d: reveals the owner's return on money invested in the cost of development
feedback: The strengths of the cost approach are that it: provides an upper limit for the subject's value based on the undepreciated cost of reproducing the improvements. It is also very accurate for valuing a property with new improvements which are the highest and best use of the property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The principle underlying depreciation from physical deterioration is that
option_a: eventually, a property loses all of its value
option_b: a property loses a portion of its value each year because of economic obsolescence
option_c: a property loses the same increment of value each year over the economic life of the property
option_d: the value lost to depreciation is incurable
feedback: All property improvements have an economic life, which becomes incrementally shorter year after year as physical deterioration takes its toll. The property as a whole does not lose value, since land itself does not depreciate. Similarly, an improvement can regain value if it is repaired or updated. Finally, not all properties lose value from economic obsolescence

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A property is being appraised by the cost approach.  The appraiser estimates that the land is worth $100,000 and the replacement cost of the improvements is $750,000.  Total depreciation from all causes is $70,000.  What is the indicated value of the property?

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: Which of the following statements properly describes how to apply the income capitalization approach to appraisal?
option_a: Apply a desired rate of return to the price paid for an income property
option_b: Divide the income a property generates by a desired rate of return
option_c: Estimate the amount of income a property must generate to return the capital amount invested in it
option_d: Estimate the rate of return a property owner receives from income generated by the property
feedback: An appraiser obtains an indication of value from the income capitalization method by dividing the estimated net operating income for the subject by the rate of return, or capitalization rate. The formula is: NOI / Cap rate = Value

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A strength of the income capitalization approach is that it
option_a: uses a rate of return that is required for all potential purchasers in a market
option_b: yields an accurate projection of investment income
option_c: uses a method that is also used by investors to determine how much they should pay for an investment property
option_d: can be used with any type of property in any market
feedback: The strength of the income approach is that it is used by investors themselves to determine how much they should pay for a property. Thus, in the right circumstances, it provides a good basis for estimating market value. The approach, however, does not project what an income property's future income will be. Moreover, it is not an applicable method for estimating value if the subject is a non-income producing property

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A property is being appraised using the income capitalization approach.  Annually, it has an estimated gross income of $30,000, vacancy and credit losses of $1,500, and operating expenses of $10,000.  Using a capitalization rate of nine percent, what is the indicated value (to the nearest $1,000)?

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: An apartment building that sold for $4,500,000 had monthly gross rent receipts of $30,000.  What is its monthly gross rent multiplier?
feedback: The monthly gross rent multiplier for a property is equal to the price divided by the monthly rent. Thus, ($4,500,000 ÷ $30,000) = 150

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A rental house has monthly gross income of $1,200.  A suitable gross income multiplier derived from market data is 14.1.  What estimated sale price (to the nearest $1,000) is indicated?
feedback: Multiply the monthly gross income times 12 to derive annual income. Multiply annual income times the gross income multiplier to derive the estimate of price. Thus, $1,200 times 12 equals $14,400. This times 14.1 equals $203,040, or $203,000 rounded

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: A certified appraiser is one who has received certification by
option_c: the state in which the appraiser operates
feedback: A state-certified appraiser is one who has passed the necessary examinations and competency standards as established by each state in conformance with the federal standards

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: The act that required federally-related appraisals to be conducted by a certified appraiser is known as
option_a: the Financial Institutions Reform, Recovery and Enforcement Act (FIRREA)
option_b: the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice Act (USPAPA)
option_c: the Appraisal Foundation Authorization and Reform Act (AFAR)
option_d: the Federal Institution for Regulation and Enforcement of Appraisal Act (FIREAA)
feedback: Title XI of FIRREA requires that competent individuals whose professional conduct is properly supervised perform all appraisals used in federally-related transactions. As of January 1, 1993, such federally-related appraisals must be performed only by state certified appraisers

subject: A4 Appraisal and Valuation
question_text: According to ANSI and Fannie Mae standards,  a room whose floor level is six inches below ground level
option_a: must be counted as basement area, not living area.
option_b: may be included in Gross Living Area if it meets other requirements for living area.
option_d: cannot be classified as finished space.
feedback: Gross living area is based on the dimensions of all rooms "above grade" as measured from the exterior walls of the premises, including stairwells but excluding "open areas." Space should be measured to the inch or to 1/10th of a foot and living areas calculated and totaled. Above grade is defined as above the surface of the ground. Living area below-grade (one foot or more) must be counted as basement area – thus not as 100% living area.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: A broker was paid 6% commission on the first $60,000 of a sale price and 4% for everything above that. What would the sale price be if the total commission was $4,375?
feedback:
To solve this, first find the commission made by the broker (6% or .06) up to $60,000 and subtract that from the total commission ($4,375), then divide by the remaining commission percentage (4% or .04) to find the amount paid at that rate. Finally, add that number to $60,000 to get the total selling price.
 
 .06 x $60,000 = $3,600
 $4,375 - $3,600 = $775
 $775 ÷ .04 = $19,375
 $19,375 + $60,000 = $79,375
 
 The total sale price was $79,375.

question_text: A property sells for $495,000, which represents a 10% profit for the owner. What was the original cost of the property?
feedback:
The selling price of a property is 100% of the original cost plus 10% of the original cost (the profit), so the selling price actually equals 110% of the original cost of the property. Convert 110% to decimal form, which is 1.10 (move the decimal 2 places to the left). Finally, divide the sales price by that number to get the answer:
 
 $495,000 ÷ 1.10 = $450,000

question_text: Joe's duplex has depreciated 3% per year for the 5 years he has owned it. Since the building originally cost $20,000, what is the current value of the duplex?
feedback:
To figure this out, first figure out the total depreciation:
 3% x 5 years = 15%
 
 Next, multiply that by the original cost:
 $20,000 x 15% = $3,000
 
 Finally, subtract that amount from the original cost to find the current value:
 $20,000 - $3,000 = $17,000
 
 NOTE - Most appraisal and assessment methods use straight-line depreciation, which means it doesn't compound annually. That's why we use 3% x 5 years = 15% when determining the amount of depreciation.

question_text: Bailey Broker leases a property to Susie Smiley for 5 years. Bailey will receive a 5% commission based on the annual rent. Knowing this, what will Bailey's commission be for the full 5-year period?
feedback:
To solve this problem, you must figure out the monthly rent for each of the 5 years, multiply that by 12 to find total rent for the 5-year period, then add those together and multiply that sum by 5% to arrive at the commission.
 
 $3,000 x 12 = $36,000
 $3,100 x 12 = $37,200
 $3,200 x 12 = $38,400
 $3,300 x 12 = $39,600
 $3,400 x 12 = $40,800
 
 Add the annual rent for each year to get a total of $192,000 in rent.
 Multiply $192,000 x 0.05 to get a commission of $9,600.

question_text: A house sold for $95,000 with a sales commission rate of 7.5%. The listing broker received 50% of the total commission and the selling broker received 50%. How much would the selling salesperson receive if the selling broker kept 60% and gave 40% to the salesperson?
feedback:
With total commissions of $7,125, the selling and listing broker each receive $3,562.50. At 40% of the selling broker's share ($3,562.50), the selling salesperson would receive $3,562.50 x .4 = $1,425.
 
 Although the names sound similar, a selling agent is vastly different from a seller's agent. While a listing agent takes care of selling the property, a selling broker represents the interest of the buyer. This type of real estate agent learns about what buyers are looking for and then help match them with properties.

 Step 3, find the down payment - $256,666.67 minus $220,000 = $36,666
 
 While it's not recommended to pay more than the appraised value of a home, it can be done as long as the buyer doesn't mind coming up with extra cash.

question_text: If the transfer taxes are payable at the rate of 55 cents per $500 or part thereof, what is the grantor's cost on a $49,750 sale?
feedback:
When figuring transfer tax, you are generally required by the state to round up to the nearest $500.
 
 Round $49,750 up to $50,000 and divide it by $1000. The transfer tax is $.55 per $500, but you can double it to $1.10 per $1000 because it's easier to work with:
 
 $50,000 ÷ $1000 = 50
 Now multiply that by $1.10, so:
 50 x $1.10 = $55.00

 8% is equivalent to .08

 The opposite of 8/100 is 100/8
 100 divided by 8 = 12.5

feedback: In a tax deferred exchange transaction, the cost basis of the property transferred becomes the cost basis of the property which is acquired or received. subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: A prospect is considering the purchase of an income property. The property's operating statement shows the deductions subtracted from a total gross income of $94,500. The deductions amount to 60% of the gross income. If the prospect wants a 12% return on the purchase price of any investment he makes, what should he pay for the property?
feedback:
To solve this problem, start with finding the net income. Expenses equal 60% of the gross income, so the net income is the other 40%. To find the net income, multiply the gross income by 0.4 (which is the same as 40%):
 $94,500 x 0.4 = $37,800
 
 Now, divide the net income ($37,800) by the desired return (12%) to determine the desired purchase price:
 $37,800 ÷ 0.12 = $315,000

question_text: After subtracting $140.00 escrow fees and 6% commission on gross sales price, a seller receives $13,584.00. What is the selling price?
feedback:
To find the answer, add the seller's net sales price ($13,584) to the subtracted escrow fees ($140), then divide that by .94 (100% - 6% for the commission), which is the percentage of gross sales the seller gets to keep.
 $13,584 + $140 = $13,724
 $13,724 ÷ .94 = $14,600
 The selling price is $14,600.

question_text: A house sold for $345,000, which was 9% more than the cost of the house. The original cost of the house was most nearly:
feedback:
The Original Cost (OC) is Current Cost (CC) divided by Adjusted Cost Ratio (ACR).
 The Current Cost is $345,000, the Adjusted Cost Ratio is 100% plus or minus the appreciation/depreciation of the house. Because the house is now worth 9% more than OC, the ACR is 109% (or 1.09).
 OC = CC ÷ ACR, so OC = $345,000 ÷ 1.09
 $345,000 ÷ 1.09 = $316,513.76, so the answer is "most nearly" $316,514.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: A rectangular parcel of land that measures 220' by 330' contains most nearly:
feedback:
Multiply both sides of the parcel to figure out square footage:
 220' x 330' = 72,600 square feet
 Now figure out how many acres that is:
 1 acre is 43,560 sq. ft., so 72,600 ÷ 43,560 = 1.66 acres
 Now we know it's an answer that starts with 1, but we need to figure out which fraction equals .66. Fractions equal the top number divided by the bottom number, so: 3 ÷ 5 = .6, 1 ÷ 4 = .25, and 2 ÷ 3 = .6666
 The answer that fits "most nearly" is 1 2/3 acres.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: Andrew purchased a property at 20% less than the listed price and later sold the property for the original listed price. What was the percentage of profit?
feedback:
This problem can be figured using any list price, but let's assume that the property was listed at $10,000.
 20% of that list price is 0.2 x $10,000 = $2,000.
 So the purchase price is $10,000 - $2,000 = $8,000.
 Andrew sold the property for the original list price, so his profit is $10,000 - $8,000 = $2,000.
 To find the percentage of profit, divide the profit by purchase price: $2,000 ÷ $8,000 = 0.25, or 25% profit.
 
 NOTE - Percentages are ratios, which is why you don't need the list price to solve the problem. You can substitute any list price in this problem and still get the same answer.

question_text: If $150 interest is paid in 8 months on a straight note loan of $2,500, what is the annual rate of interest?
feedback:
In 8 months, or 2/3 of a year, $150 interest was paid. Divide $150 by 2/3 to find the total paid in a year, so $150 ÷ (2/3) = $225 interest per year.
 Divide interest per year by total loan amount to find the interest rate, so $225 ÷ $2500 = .09, or 9% APR.

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: Solly bought a home for $31,680 and now wishes to sell. He is informed that the cost of selling will amount to 12% of the selling price. He wants to sell at a high enough price that he doesn't take a loss. How much does the home need to appreciate in value to offset the selling costs?
feedback:
Solly wants his original home purchase price of $31,680 to be 88% of his desired sales price. Start by finding his desired sales price, which is the original purchase price divided by 88%:
 $31,680 ÷ .88 = $36,000
 
 Now, subtract Solly's original purchase price from his desired sales price to find out how much the house needs to appreciate in value:
 $36,000 - $31,680 = $4,320
 
 Alternately, you can multiply $36,000 by 12%, which is the portion of the overall sales price that must be appreciation in value:
 $36,000 x .12 = $4,320

subject: A5 Real Estate Math
question_text: A building has interior dimensions of 26 feet by 30 feet. The walls are 6 inches thick on every side. How much square footage of land does it cover?
feedback:
To find the total square footage of land covered by a building, you'll need to add the wall thickness from each end (6 inches) of the building to the internal dimension. When you measure the width, there are two walls; when you measure the length, there are two walls. Once you have the total length by the total width, multiply them together to find the square footage:
 
 26 ft. (internal dimension) + 6 in. (one wall) + 6 in. (the other wall) = 27 ft. on one side (remember that 6 in. + 6 in. = 1 ft.)
 30 ft. (internal dimension) + 6 in. (one wall) + 6 in. (the other wall) = 31 ft. on the other.
 31 ft. x 27 ft. = 837 sq ft.
 
 NOTE - You must add the thickness of each wall (6 inches for each wall, 2 walls length-wise and 2 walls width-wise) to the interior dimensions to calculate the entire square footage of land covered by the building.

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A seller paid a $100 item in advance.  At closing, the seller has "used" only $75 of this item.  What should appear on the closing statement?
option_a: A debit to the buyer and credit to the seller for $25
option_b: A debit to the buyer for $25 and a credit to the seller for $75
option_c: A debit to the buyer for $25 and a credit to the seller for $100
option_d: A debit to the buyer and credit to the seller for $75
feedback: The excess of the buyer's debits over the buyer's credits is the amount the buyer must bring to the closing. The excess of the seller's credits over the seller's debits is the amount the seller will receive at closing.  The excess here is $25

subject: A6 Closing and Settlem
question_text: A sale transaction closes on July 4.  The day of closing belongs to the seller.  On January 1, the seller paid a hazard insurance premium of $375 for the calendar year.  According to the 12-month/30-day method, what is the seller's share of the insurance premium?
feedback: This method assumes all months are 30 days and the year is 360 days. The daily proration is therefore $375 ÷ 360, or $1.0416. The closing occurs on the 184th day of the year. Thus, ($1.0416 x 184) = $191.67. Note that if the day of closing is the buyer's, there would be 183 day's worth of prorated expense

option_a: Employment of a business opportunity to sell stock
option_b: Any real estate agreement not to be performed within one year
option_c: Employment of a broker to exchange leases
option_d: Bill of sale on real property
feedback: Statute of Frauds requires certain contracts (like real estate not performed within 1 year) to be in writing.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If a person obtains a contract through duress, that contract is:
feedback: Contracts under duress are voidable; the party can choose to enforce or rescind.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: All of the following are essentials of each contract, except:
feedback: Proper writing is not always required; essentials are mutual consent, lawful object, capable parties, and consideration.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The law that requires real estate contracts to be in writing is:
feedback: Statute of Frauds requires certain contracts, especially real estate, to be in writing.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: To "assign" a contract means to:
option_a: Permit another broker to act as agent
option_b: Allow seller and buyer to exchange positions
option_c: Transfer one's rights under the contract
feedback: Assignment transfers rights and obligations to another party (assignee).

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Listing where owner sets price and broker keeps anything above it:
feedback: Net listings allow broker to keep amount above seller's minimum; illegal in many states due to conflicts of interest.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Undue influence or duress applied to a party makes the contract:
feedback: Contracts under duress or undue influence are voidable; the affected party may rescind.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A voidable contract is:
option_a: Valid now but can be voided by one party for cause
feedback: A voidable contract is valid until rescinded by the affected party.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: According to Statute of Frauds, which must be in writing?
option_b: Purchase contract for a single-family home
feedback: Real estate purchase contracts must be in writing to be enforceable.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Buyer not informed about septic tank and system fails before possession, buyer may:
feedback: Failure to disclose material facts allows the buyer to rescind.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Contract where one party cannot revoke offer for a period:
feedback: An option contract keeps the offer open for a specific period; optionor cannot revoke.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Any advance fee contract must include all except:
feedback: No guarantee of success; must disclose services and fee details.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A listing agreement is essentially a/an:
feedback: Listing agreements are employment contracts where broker is employed to find a buyer.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Listing where owner pays commission even if she sells:
feedback: Exclusive right to sell guarantees broker commission regardless of who sells.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Prospective purchaser may withdraw offer before seller acceptance:
feedback: Buyer may cancel anytime before acceptance for any reason.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Option exercised on commercial property, when must optionee perform?
feedback: Performance is governed by terms agreed in the option contract (price and timeframe).

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Agent promised advertising but did not perform, guilty of:
feedback: Intentionally failing to perform a promised act constitutes actual fraud.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which is NOT true about an option?
option_a: Optionee has no interest until exercised
option_b: Optionor can revoke offer during period
feedback: False: Optionor cannot revoke during the option period.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Broker entitled to commission for negotiations started during listing but completed after expiration:
feedback: Protection/safety clause allows broker to earn commission for deals closing after listing term.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Necessary for valid conditional installment sales contract:
option_a: Lawful object, competent parties, offer/acceptance, writing
option_b: Consideration, offer/acceptance, lawful object, competent parties, writing
option_d: Consideration, offer/acceptance, mutuality, competent parties, writing
feedback: Essentials of a valid contract: consideration, offer/acceptance, lawful object, competent parties, writing if required.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Written agreement allowing use of property and right to buy:
feedback: Lease option allows tenant to lease and have right to buy property later.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which terminates original offer?
feedback: Counteroffer creates new offer, terminating the original.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Voidable contract is:
feedback: Voidable is enforceable unless the affected party rescinds.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Substituting one party for another in contract is:
feedback: Novation replaces parties or obligations with consent of all involved.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Under a lease for a commercial property, a tenant agrees to pay $4,000 per month plus 3% of the gross monthly sales. This type of lease is called a:
feedback: A percentage lease requires the tenant to pay a base rent plus a percentage of gross sales made on the property.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A salesperson who prepares a written listing agreement with a seller is helping to create a contractual relationship between the:
feedback: Listings belong to the broker, not the salesperson, even if the salesperson solicited the listing.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: One of the required elements of a valid real estate sales contract is that it MUST:
option_c: provide for possession of the property by the buyer
feedback: A valid real estate sales contract must be signed by the seller; buyer signatures affect enforceability.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Under a sales contract, the legal remedy that may be used to force the seller to consummate the sale is called:
feedback: Specific performance is a court order requiring the seller to complete the sale.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If a lessee pays a fixed amount of rent plus all expenses such as taxes and insurance, the lease is known as a:
feedback: A net lease requires the tenant to pay rent plus some or all operating expenses.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A lease that provides for periodic increases of rent at regular intervals is called a:
feedback: A graduated lease has rent adjustments at specified intervals during the lease term.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: When a contract states that "time is of the essence," the contract MUST be delivered and presented:
feedback: "Time is of the essence" means that all dates in the contract are mandatory and must be adhered to.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If the owners of a shopping center want a major department store's lease to provide rental payments that reflect the store's sales, they are MOST likely to use which of the following types of leases?
feedback: A percentage lease ties rent payments to a percentage of the tenant’s gross sales.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: With the exception of court-ordered sales, the amount of earnest money to be deposited by a prospective buyer is determined by the:
option_c: agreement between the seller and the buyer
feedback: Earnest money is agreed upon between buyer and seller to show good faith.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In the event that a buyer fails to perform on an agreement to purchase, the seller may be allowed to keep the earnest money deposit as:
feedback: Contracts often state that earnest money may be kept as liquidated damages if the buyer defaults.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Each party is bound to a specific date for performance if a real estate sales contract contains:
option_a: a provision that time is of the essence
option_b: a time limit for the buyer to secure mortgage financing
feedback: "Time is of the essence" makes dates and deadlines critical and mandatory in the contract.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: To be valid, an exclusive listing contract must contain all of the following except:
option_a: the signatures of all interested parties
feedback: The final contract price is determined after an offer is presented and accepted, not at the listing stage.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A promise in exchange for a promise, supported by consideration, is the basis for a:
feedback: A bilateral contract involves a mutual exchange of promises supported by consideration.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Jones, the seller, receives an offer to purchase and puts forth a counteroffer to Brown, the buyer. Until Brown notifies Jones of his acceptance or rejection, Jones:
option_a: cannot accept another offer from a different buyer
option_b: is legally bound to the terms of the counteroffer
feedback: Counteroffers can be changed or withdrawn until notice of acceptance is communicated.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Under the statute of frauds, in order to be legally enforceable, all real estate contracts must be:
feedback: Real estate contracts must be in writing to comply with the Statute of Frauds.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The earnest money accompanying a purchase offer:
option_a: is generally 7.5% of the proposed purchase price
option_b: is not necessary to establish a valid contract
option_d: must be held in escrow by the seller
feedback: A sales contract can be valid even without earnest money; its main purpose is to show good faith.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The party most likely to sue for specific performance in the purchase of real estate is the:
feedback: Buyers may sue for specific performance to compel a seller to complete a real estate sale.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Jones goes into a restaurant and orders lunch. At the end of the meal, is he legally obligated to pay the check?
option_a: Yes, but only if Jones is a legal adult
option_b: Yes, because by ordering, Jones entered into an implied contract to pay for the meal
option_c: Yes, because Jones is liable through the process of novation
option_d: No, because strictly speaking, Jones did not promise to pay
feedback: Ordering creates an implied contract to pay for goods or services received.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: What is it called when a sum of money is submitted by a buyer with an offer to purchase?
feedback: Earnest money is a deposit showing intent to perform under the contract.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The most important document in the sales process is the purchase contract because:
option_a: it tells the government why the buyer wishes to buy
option_b: the agent is not guaranteed fee without it
option_c: unless a real estate transaction is in writing, state law dictates how the money is spent and distributed
option_d: it provides the road map for the closing
feedback: The purchase contract outlines duties, rights, and closing procedures.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: One effect of a clause in a sales agreement that states that "time is of the essence" might be that:
option_a: The buyer can take care of matters at his convenience
option_b: The seller can take care of matters at his convenience
option_c: The agent must see that the contract has been fulfilled prior to closing
option_d: All parties must attend to those details called for in the agreement in a timely manner
feedback: All parties must adhere to deadlines; failure may constitute a breach.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If during negotiations one party crosses out or alters any wording in a contract:
option_c: Each party must initial the change in the margin for it to be valid
option_d: The party who didn't make the changes or omissions becomes responsible for the deal
feedback: Changes require initials from both parties to be valid.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Jones' offer to buy Brown's house has been accepted, but the actual sale will not take place for three months. Until then, their written sales contract is considered to be:
feedback: An executory contract is not fully performed yet.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In April, Jones accepted an offer to sell his house to Brown. In June, Brown paid the purchase price and received a deed to the house and the keys. After June their contract was considered to be:
feedback: An executed contract is fully performed by all parties.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: When is a contract said to be discharged?
option_a: When most of its basic terms have been accomplished
option_b: When the contract is in dispute
option_c: Once all of its terms and conditions have been met
option_d: When there are further actions to be taken
feedback: Complete performance discharges the contract and obligations.

subject: B1 Contracts
