option_c: The landlord does not have to sell, but must renew the option
option_d: The option is expired, and the tenant has no rightful claim to money paid for the option
feedback: The option automatically expires at the end of the option period. The landlord has no obligation, either to sell the property or to return the consideration, after the expiration date

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A tenant exercises an option to buy a condominium. The landlord agrees, but raises the agreed price by $3,000, claiming financial distress. The landlord does, however, offer the tenant two months of free rent before closing as an offset. Which of the following is true?
option_a: The tenant can force the sale at the original terms
option_b: The landlord has taken a fully legal action which the tenant must abide by
option_c: The option is null, and the optionee may reclaim any option money paid
option_d: The landlord must offer sufficient free rent to equal the $3,000 price increase
feedback: An option-to-buy is an enforceable contract in which a potential seller, the optionor, grants a potential buyer, the optionee, the right to purchase a property before a stated time for a stated price and terms. In exchange for the right of option, the optionee pays the optionor valuable consideration

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following is true regarding the legal nature of option contracts?
option_b: They are enforceable, whether written or oral
option_c: They give the optionee an equitable interest in the property
option_d: They must be recorded to be valid
feedback: The optionee enjoys an equitable interest in the property because the option creates the right to obtain legal title. However, the option does not in itself convey an interest in real property, only a right to do something governed by contract law

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An important distinction between a contract for deed and a contract for sale is
option_a: a contract for deed requires no action on the part of the seller
option_b: a contract for deed requires no action on the part of the buyer
option_c: the seller retains legal title in a contract for deed transaction until fully executed
option_d: the buyer acquires legal title in a contract for deed transaction
feedback: A contract for deed is a bilateral agreement between a seller, the vendor, and a buyer, the vendee, in which the vendor defers receipt of some or all of the purchase price of a property over a specified period of time. During the period, the vendor retains legal title and the vendee acquires equitable title. The vendee takes possession of the property, makes stipulated payments of principal and interest to the vendor, and otherwise fulfills obligations as the contract requires. At the end of the period, the buyer pays the vendor the full purchase price and the vendor deeds legal title to the vendee

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: While a property is under a contract for deed, the seller, or vendor, mortgages her equity in the property, and has a separate judgment lien placed on the property. Faced with financial loss, the vendor assigns the contract to another party, then leaves town. What can the vendee do in this case?
option_a: rightfully cancel the agreement and reclaim all deposits
option_b: sue the creditor for removal of the judgment lien
option_c: disclaim the lien and the mortgage due to homestead rights
option_d: comply with the contract and take legal title when its terms are fulfilled
feedback: During the contract period, the seller may sell or assign whatever interests he or she owns in the property to another party. The vendor, however, is bound to the obligations imposed by the contract for deed. In particular, the vendor may not breach the obligation to convey legal title to the vendee upon receipt of the total purchase price

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A potential danger involved in a contract for deed is that
option_a: the vendor may rightfully sell legal title to another party during the contract period
option_b: the vendor's rights to encumber the property are extinguished
option_c: the vendee may not have a right of redemption
option_d: the vendee must make an inordinately high down payment, which can be lost
feedback: In some states, a breach of the contract for deed is remedied under local contract law rather than foreclosure law. The buyer may not have the protections of a redemption period or other buyer-protection laws which accompany formal foreclosure proceedings. The vendor might sue the vendee for breach of contract for the slightest infraction of the contract terms

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: For a real estate sales contract to be enforceable, the contract must be signed by which of the following?
option_b: The principal parties in the transaction
option_d: Each broker representing the clients in the transaction
feedback: To be enforceable, a real estate sales contract must: be validly created (mutual consent, consideration, legal purpose, competent parties, voluntary act), be in writing, identify the principal parties, clearly identify the property, preferably by legal description, contain a purchase price, be signed by the principal parties

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The real estate contract may be assigned to another party
option_a: provided that the broker’s written approval is obtained
option_b: subject to the provisions and conditions contained in the contract
option_c: subject to the buyer’s and seller’s attorney approvals
option_d: provided all parties including the lender approves
feedback: Either party to a sale transaction can assign the sale contract to another party, subject to the provisions and conditions contained in the agreement. If the contract states that it may not be assigned, then assignment may not be made

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The conventional transfer of real estate ownership takes place in the following three stages identified as
feedback: The conventional transfer of real estate ownership takes place in three stages. First, there is the negotiating period where buyers and sellers exchange offers. Second, when both parties have accepted all terms, the offer becomes a binding sale contract. Third is the closing of the transaction, when the seller deeds title to the buyer, the buyer pays the purchase price, and all necessary documents are completed

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The real estate sales contract should be signed by
option_a: one spouse if married and held jointly
option_b: all the parties who hold title to the property
option_c: the attorneys representing the seller and the buyer
feedback: All owners of the property should sign the sale contract. If the sellers are married, both spouses should sign to ensure that both spouses release homestead, dower, and curtesy rights to the buyer at closing. Failure to do so does not invalidate the contract but can lead to encumbered title and legal disputes

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A real estate sales contract gives the buyer an interest in the property that is referred to as
feedback: The sales contract gives the buyer an interest in the property that is called equitable title, or ownership in equity

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The earnest money deposit provides
option_a: potential compensation for damages to the seller if the buyer fails to perform
option_b: a down payment on behalf of the buyer to the lender
option_c: potential compensation for damages to the buyer if the seller fails to perform
option_d: comfort for the broker that the buyer will move forward with the transaction
feedback: f the buyer defaults, the seller may be entitled to retain the earnest money as damages

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Any earnest money deposit will be held by
feedback: The party holding the money under a real estate contract is referred to as the escrowee or escrow agent. The sale contract provides the escrow instructions for handling and disbursing escrow funds. The earnest money is placed in a third party trust account or escrow. A licensed escrow agent employed by a title company, financial institution, or brokerage company usually manages the escrow. An individual broker may also serve as the escrow agent

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If the buyer performs according to the terms of the sales contract, the earnest money is
option_a: applied to the purchase price at closing
option_b: retained by the seller as additional compensation for signing the contract
option_c: returned to the buyer since they performed according to the contract terms
option_d: applied towards payment of the seller’s portion of the real estate taxes
feedback: If the buyer performs under the sale contract, the deposit is applied to the purchase price. The earnest money deposit is credited to the buyer at closing as having already been paid

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following contingencies is one of the common in a real estate sales contract?
feedback: The most common contingency concerns financing. A buyer makes an offer contingent upon securing financing for the property under certain terms on or before a certain date. If unable to secure the specified loan commitment by the deadline, the buyer may cancel the contract and recover the deposit

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following may occur if either party to the contract fails to perform pursuant to the terms of the contract?
option_a: The defaulting party may be sued for damages
option_b: The party in default may sue for damages
option_c: The broker may sue the defaulting party for specific performance
option_d: The broker may receive liquidated damages
feedback: The defaulting party may be sued for damages.   If the buyer, they could lose their earnest money

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The real estate sales contract states when the
option_d: buyers will be moving into the property
feedback: The contract states when title will transfer, as well as when the buyer will take physical possession. Customarily, possession occurs on the date when the deed is recorded, unless the buyer has agreed to other arrangements

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: CLUE (Comprehensive Loss Underwriting Exchange) is a claims history database used by
feedback: CLUE (Comprehensive Loss Underwriting Exchange) is a claims history database used by insurance companies in underwriting or rating insurance policies

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In an option-to-buy contract, the potential seller is known as the
feedback: An option-to-buy is an enforceable contract in which a potential seller, the optionor, grants a potential buyer, the optionee, the right to purchase a property before a stated time for a stated price and terms. In exchange for the right of option, the optionee pays the optionor valuable consideration

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In an option-to-buy contract, the potential buyer is known as the
feedback: An option-to-buy is an enforceable contract in which a potential seller, the optionor, grants a potential buyer, the optionee, the right to purchase a property before a stated time for a stated price and terms. In exchange for the right of option, the optionee pays the optionor valuable consideration

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: To be valid and enforceable, an option-to-buy must include
feedback: To be valid and enforceable, an option-to-buy must: include actual, non-refundable consideration, include price and terms of the sale, have an expiration date, be in writing, include a legal description, meet general contract validity requirements

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Representations by the brokerage in the sale contract include
feedback: The broker discloses the applicable agency relationships in the transaction and names the party who must pay the brokerage commission

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: The seller in an installment sales contract is known as the
feedback: A contract for deed is also called a land contract, an installment sale, a conditional sales contract, and an agreement for deed. It is a bilateral agreement between a seller, the vendor, and a buyer, the vendee, in which the vendor defers receipt of some or all of the purchase price of a property over a specified period of time

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate agent who is placed in a position of trust, loyalty, and confidence is a(n):
feedback: An agent acting on behalf of a principal or client is referred to legally as their fiduciary. When we discuss the duties/responsibilities they legally have to the principal or client we call those the fiduciary duties/responsibilities.

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Any document added to the contract that clarifies certain aspects of the contract or adds new terms is called:
feedback: Addenda that are appended to the sale contract become binding components of the overall agreement. The most common addendum is the seller's property condition disclosure. Examples of other addenda are: agency disclosure, asbestos / hazardous materials, liquidated damages, radon disclosure, flood plain disclosure, tenant's lease

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following identifies one of the advantages of a contract for deed for a seller?
option_a: It allows a seller to get paid upfront in cash
option_b: It may give a seller certain tax benefits
option_c: It requires the buyer to pay a large down payment
option_d: It allows the seller to continue to live in the property until full payment has been made
feedback: A contract for deed serves two primary purposes for a seller. First, it facilitates a sale that might otherwise be impossible. Second, it may give the seller certain tax benefits. Since the seller is not liable for capital gains tax until the purchase price is received, the installment sale lowers the seller's tax liability in the year of the sale

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Secondary provisions in a contract may include
feedback: Some other provisions in the contract may provide that the seller deliver a plat of survey and disclosures of any environmental hazards known to be on the property

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A real estate sale contract is an executory contract until
option_a: the completed sale transaction is recorded
option_b: the buyer and seller have agreed to all provisions and have signed the contract
option_c: all the obligations and promises are performed and the transaction is closed
option_d: the buyer's earnest money deposit and down payment have been delivered to and accepted by the seller
feedback: An executory contract is one for which the signatories have yet to perform their respective obligations and promises. Upon closing, the sale contract is fully performed and no longer exists as a binding agreement

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: If a married couple own a property that is for sale, and only one of them signs a sale contract, what is the legal status of the contract?
feedback: All owners of the property should sign the sale contract. If the sellers are married, both spouses should sign to ensure that both spouses release homestead, dower, and curtesy rights to the buyer at closing. Failure to do so does not invalidate the contract but can lead to encumbered title and legal disputes

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: In assisting a buyer or seller to complete an offer to purchase, what should an agent do to reduce the risk of committing an unauthorized practice of law?
option_a: Use a standard contract promulgated by a state agency or a real estate board
option_b: Charge no more than a nominal fee for the assistance
option_c: Offer legal advice only on points of the contract that the agent is absolutely certain about
option_d: Write contract terms that are manifestly fair to both buyer and seller
feedback: It is advisable, and legally required in most states, for a broker to use a standard contract form promulgated by state agencies or real estate boards, as such forms contain generally accepted language. This relieves the broker of the dangers of creating new contract language, which can be construed as a practice of law for which the broker is not licensed

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: To be enforceable, a contract for the sale of real estate must
feedback: A contract for the sale of real estate is enforceable only if it is in writing. A buyer or seller cannot sue to force the other to comply with an oral contract for sale, even if the contract is valid

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A buyer makes an offer to purchase a house, and the seller accepts the offer.  Both parties sign the sale contract, but the buyer fails to provide an earnest money deposit.  What are the seller's obligations to the buyer?
option_a: None. There is no valid contract
option_b: The seller must give the buyer an opportunity to make a new offer
option_c: The seller must perform under the terms of the contract
option_d: The seller must notify the buyer in writing that the buyer is in default
feedback: To be valid, a contract must meet the criteria of: competent parties, voluntary act of good faith, mutual consent, legal purpose, and valuable consideration. The buyer broke the mutual consent requirement by opting out before being told of the seller’s acceptance. Thus, there is no valid contract

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following is an essential element of a valid contract for the sale of real estate?
option_d: Social Security numbers of seller and buyer
feedback: A contract of sale is created by full and unequivocal acceptance of an offer. Offer and acceptance satisfy the mutual consent requirement for a valid contract. The offeree must accept the offer without making any changes whatsoever

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: What kind of interest does the buyer own after a real estate sale contract is signed by the principal parties?
feedback: A sale contract gives the buyer an interest in the property that is called equitable title, or ownership in equity. If the seller defaults and the buyer can show good faith performance, the buyer can sue for specific performance, that is, to compel the seller to transfer legal title upon payment of the contract price

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A contingency in a sale contract is
option_a: a promise by buyer or seller to perform a specific action
option_b: a condition that, if unmet, renders the contract unenforceable
option_c: one of several alternative actions that buyer or seller may take to satisfy contract requirements
option_d: an optional, unilateral action that either party may take at the request of the other party
feedback: A contingency is a condition that must be met before the contract is enforceable. If one party cannot meet a contingency condition by a specified deadline, the other party may cancel the contract

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A "termite" clause in a sale contract states that the seller must provide suitable evidence that the property is free of infestation.  On the day of closing, the buyer learns that the inspection service hired by the seller was not properly licensed.  The seller expresses surprise, promises to pay for another inspection and/or extermination, and insists on proceeding with the closing.  The buyer refuses, and declares that the sale is off.  Which of the following is true of this situation?
option_a: The seller is in default and is liable for damages
option_b: The buyer will be in default, and liable for damages, if he does not complete the transaction
option_c: The buyer may be able to have the contract canceled
feedback: The termite inspection clause is a contingency clause. If one party cannot meet a contingency condition by a specified deadline, the other party may cancel the contract. Did the clause state that the inspector had to be properly licensed? If so, the buyer has a good case for cancellation

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: A buyer signs an earnest money agreement and gives it to the broker who showed her the property she is buying.  After leaving the broker's office, she reconsiders and decides she prefers a different property.  How long does she have to take back her offer?
option_a: Until the seller communicates acceptance of the offer
option_c: She can take it back at any time, but must forfeit the earnest money
option_d: She cannot take it back until after the expiration date of the offer
feedback: An offeror may revoke an offer for any reason prior to communication of acceptance by the offeree

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: On Wednesday, Fred offers to sell his property to Jack for $275,000, with the offer to remain open until 5 p.m. the next day.  On Thursday morning, Sally offers Fred $280,000 for the property and Fred accepts.  At 1 p.m. on Thursday afternoon, Jack accepts.  Which of the following is true of this situation?
option_a: The acceptance by Sally creates a contract and terminates Fred's offer to Jack
option_b: Fred has entered into contracts with both Jack and Sally to sell the same property
option_c: Fred's acceptance of Sally's offer is invalidated by Jack's acceptance, because Fred's offer to Jack was made prior to Sally's offer to Fred
option_d: No contract has been created because it is impossible to have two valid sale contracts for the same property
feedback: Jack's acceptance of Fred's offer creates a valid contract, just as Fred's acceptance of Sally's offer creates a valid contract. Fred should have revoked his offer to Jack before accepting Sally's offer

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: What parties must be identified in a sale contract?
feedback: There must be at least two parties to a sale contract: one cannot convey property to oneself

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: To create an enforceable option-to-buy contract, there must be an exchange of
option_a: a promise to sell and a promise to buy
option_b: valuable consideration and a right to buy
option_c: valuable consideration and a promise to buy
option_d: a down payment and a post-dated contract for sale
feedback: An option-to-buy is an enforceable contract in which a potential seller, the optionor, grants a potential buyer, the optionee, the right to purchase a property before a stated time for a stated price and terms. In exchange for the right of option, the optionee pays the optionor valuable consideration

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Mary Carboy buys a house from Jim Schmidt and at the same time obtains an option to purchase the adjoining vacant lot for $10,000 within one year. A few months later, Carboy informs Schmidt that she is ready to exercise her option, but finds that Schmidt has received an offer of $12,000 from another party.  Schmidt states that he will accept the offer unless Carboy is willing to match the $12,000 offer.  Which of the following is true of this situation?
option_a: Schmidt must sell to Carboy for $10,000
option_b: Carboy must pay $12,000 or lose the property
option_c: Schmidt may sell to Carboy or the other party, but the price cannot exceed $10,000
option_d: If the other party delivers payment before Carboy does, the option is canceled
feedback: Mary's option-to-buy contract is enforceable, assuming she paid a consideration for it

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: An option-to-buy is
option_a: not assignable unless the contracts specifically allows assignment
option_d: assignable unless the contract prohibits assignment
feedback: A real estate contract that is not a personal service contract can be assigned to another party unless the terms of the agreement specifically prohibit assignment. An option-to-buy is therefore assignable in the absence of such a prohibiting clause

subject: B1 Contracts
question_text: Which of the following is true of a contract for deed transaction?
option_a: At the end of the contract period, the vendee receives equitable title, provided all required periodic payments have been made
option_b: The vendee has no right to possess or occupy the property during the contract period
option_c: At the end of the contract period, the vendor conveys legal title, provided the vendee has fulfilled all obligations
option_d: The vendor may cancel the contract at any time before the final payment has been received
feedback: A contract for deed is a bilateral agreement between a seller, the vendor, and a buyer, the vendee, in which the vendor defers receipt of some or all of the purchase price of a property over a specified period of time. At the end of the period, the buyer completes payment to the vendor of the full purchase price and the vendor deeds legal title to the vendee

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A salesperson takes a listing, but the terminology of the listing doesn't authorize the listing broker to accept a deposit from a potential buyer. The broker should advise the salesperson that:
option_a: it is the broker's implied right to deny the deposit for legal liability.
option_b: it is the broker's implied right to accept a deposit on behalf of the seller for any offer.
option_c: the broker will not be able to accept a deposit from the offeror.
option_d: a deposit can be accepted and retained by the broker from the offeror, but only as an agent of the offeror for that one act.
feedback: If a broker is not authorized by the seller to accept a deposit, general laws of agency allow the broker to be the agent of the buyer (offeror) for that one act. This means that the seller's broker will have fiduciary duties to the potential buyer for the deposit, but only for the deposit.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is not essential to the creation of an agency relationship?
feedback: An agreement to pay consideration is not essential to create an agency relationship.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Ethical practices of a real estate licensee most nearly means:
option_a: The licensee's honesty and fairness in dealing with the public, clients and associates and customers.
option_b: Being held in the high esteem by the peers and the public
option_d: Knowledge and respect for the law
feedback: Ethical practices is the utilization of honesty, integrity, fairness, and responsibility when working with not only clients, but with associates, customers, and the public in general.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In which of the following ways could an agency relationship not be created?
feedback: An offer by an agent is not enough to create an agency relationship- there must be some consent (implied, written or oral) by the principal.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If a principal no longer desires the broker to act for him during the period of an exclusive right to sell listing, he may:
option_a: not revoke the agency, because an exclusive right to sell listing is an irrevocable contract
option_b: revoke the agency created by the listing contract without incurring damages
option_c: be forced to sell his property
option_d: revoke the agency created by the listing contract, but may be liable for damages
feedback: A seller can terminate the relationship with a broker, but may be liable for damages.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: To have a valid contract, which of these is not necessary?
feedback: A deed transfers title to property and thereafter evidences ownership. It is not a part of the contract nor is it necessary to complete a transaction unless made a part thereof. Compensation is just another way of saying "consideration."

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If a principal no longer desires the broker to act for him during the period of an exclusive right to sell listing, he may:
option_a: Revoke the agency created by the listing contract but may be liable for damages
option_b: Not revoke the agency because an exclusive right to sell listing is an irrevocable contract
option_c: Be forced to sell his property
option_d: None of the other options are correct
feedback: A seller can terminate a broker before the expiration of the Listing, but he may be liable for damages to the broker in this instance.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In addition to the duties of diligence, care, and skill, a real estate agent owes the principal a full disclosure of all facts material to the subject of the agency. Any intentional concealment of such facts by the agent would probably be regarded as:
feedback: Misrepresentation by a licensee will subject that licensee not only to disciplinary action, but potential civil and criminal suits as well. Misrepresentation can be fraudulent, negligent, or innocent. In this case, the only answer that is correct is "misrepresentation", as constructive fraud isn't necessarily intentional and commingling/conversion do not apply.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Broker Carr, with ABC Real Estate Company, listed a property with a seller. Broker Smith, with XYZ Real Estate Company, called Broker Carr and disclosed that he was working with a buyer. Broker Smith wrote a purchase offer with his buyer for the property. What, if any, is the relationship between the buyer's broker and the listing broker?
option_b: There is not a relationship between the brokers- Broker Carr represents the seller and Broker Smith represents the buyer.
feedback: Since each broker represents separate sides in the transaction, no relationship exists.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If two parties want to create an enforceable broker-principal relationship concerning a right, title, or interest in real property, the most essential element to do so would be:
option_b: the authority to accept a deposit.
feedback: The most essential element of an enforceable broker-principal relationship is the employment contract. A listing is the most common employment contract, but licensees can broker leases and broker for buyers as well. The phrasing of this question may seem confusing. It is important to practice questions like these as the questions on the actual exam are written in a similar way.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The words "procuring cause" would have an important meaning to which of the following:
option_b: A dispute over commission on an exclusive agency listing
option_d: A lawsuit against a licensee involving fraud
feedback: Procuring cause is the act of the broker which is instrumental in effecting the meeting of minds between the seller and the buyer as to price and terms. It is one of the requisites for entitlement to commission. Procuring cause basically means that you are the reason the buyer is there to purchase the property.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following represents the minimum time period for an exclusive right to sell listing?
option_c: Whatever is agreed upon by the seller and the broker
feedback: Often listings are for 6 months, sometimes you see 90 day listings. However, that is an industry standard- not the law. The length of a listing can be whatever is agreed upon between the seller and the broker.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A listing broker may legally refuse to present a proper offer on the property to the seller when:
option_a: the broker is acting as a gratuitous agent.
option_b: the broker is a dual agent.
option_c: the broker is acting on the express instructions from the seller.
option_d: the broker plans to purchase the property himself.
feedback: A licensee is obligated to present all written offers to a seller immediately, unless it is a frivolous offer or if the licensee has been instructed by the seller not to present certain offers.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If a broker is an agent of a seller, he owes to the buyer:
option_a: Only a duty to disclose any items which relate directly to the sales price;
option_b: Only the duty to answer questions honestly;
option_d: A duty of fair and honest dealing
feedback: A broker acting as an agent of the seller should be fair and honest with the buyer, but the fiduciary duty still lies solely with the seller.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When serving as a buyer's agent, according to the Civil Code sections dealing with agency, a licensee owes a fiduciary duty:
feedback: An agent who is representing the buyer owes a fiduciary duty to the buyer only, not to anybody else.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The broker who most qualifies for a commission is the one who:
option_a: gave the offer to the seller.
option_b: received the acceptance of an offer.
option_c: received the offer from the buyer.
option_d: communicated the acceptance to the buyer.
feedback: The broker who most qualifies for a commission is the one who has communicated the acceptance to the buyer, as this is the broker who "consummated" (or finished) the deal.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In some cases, a broker's commission can be earned if he can prove he set in motion a series of events which resulted in the sale of a property. If a broker produces a ready, willing and able buyer he would most likely:
option_a: be considered the procuring cause of the sale.
option_b: have secured the buyer's signature on the sales contract.
feedback: Procuring cause is the act of the broker which is instrumental in effecting the meeting of minds between the seller and the buyer as to price and terms. It is one of the requisites for entitlement to commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: ypically a listing broker is usually considered to be:
feedback: A "fiduciary" relationship involves more than just acting on the behalf of a client. It is a set of responsibilities that can be summed up as safeguarding the client's best interests as if they were one's own.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agency agreement could be terminated by all EXCEPT:
option_a: when the owner cancels the listing in order to contact the potential buyer and sell to him or her directly.
option_b: when the performance of the broker is suspect.
option_d: when the performance of the seller is lacking.
feedback: Sellers are free to terminate contracts at any time and, more or less, for any good faith reason, such as changing their minds about selling. However, contracts may not be cancelled simply to avoid paying a commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An actual agency relationship that arises by the actions of the parties rather than by expressed written agreement is called:
feedback: Ostensible agency, a form of implied agency, is an agency relationship in which the actions of the parties imply to a third-party that they have mutually consented to an agency relationship, even if there was never a confirmation of that relationship.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In the formation of contracts, "valuable," "good," " sufficient," and "adequate" are terms most closely associated with which of the following?
feedback: Consideration can be anything of value that is being exchanged for the purpose of the contract. This is often money, but it doesn't need to be. Anything "valuable," "good," " sufficient," and "adequate" can be used as consideration as long as it's acceptable to the other party in the contract.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements BEST shows the difference between an exclusive right-to-sell and either an open or an exclusive agency?
option_a: An exclusive right-to-sell agreement guarantees a contracted broker commission even if the seller procures a buyer, while the others do not.
option_b: An exclusive right-to-sell agreement enables the seller to negotiate a commission, while the others do not.
option_c: An exclusive right-to-sell agreement permits the seller to sell his house without paying a fee, while the others do not.
option_d: An exclusive right-to-sell appoints only one agent, while the others do not.
feedback: An exclusive agency agreement means that the seller will owe no commission if she finds her own buyer, while an exclusive right-to-sell agreement means the broker earns commission if any buyer is procured- even if the seller brings her own buyer. Open listings are risky for brokers because they will lose the commission if the seller, or any other broker or salesperson, provides a buyer.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In a typical agency relationship between the broker and the client, the broker's commission is determined by:
option_c: minimums based on the property type.
feedback: The commission will be determined by mutual agreement between the parties, however it must be disclosed that the commission is negotiable

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which is an example of ostensible agency?
option_a: A selling broker acts as a sub-agent of a listing broker
option_b: A broker is able to sell all of an owner's properties and is granted power of attorney for all the owner's affairs
option_c: The principal allows a licensee to act as the listing broker in front of a prospective buyer
option_d: A broker sells only one of an owner's properties
feedback: Ostensible agency is similar to implied agency in that the relationship is created by the actions of the parties, rather than by written contract. Ostensible agency occurs when a third party would "reasonably" believe an agency agreement exists between a principal and an agent, even if no such agreement was ever made. Even in those states that recognize ostensible and implied agency relationships as binding, a written agency agreement should be obtained as soon as possible to clarify the understandings of all parties and protect interests.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In order to be entitled to a commission, a broker must show that he/she was the procuring cause of the sale under all of the following types of listings, except:
option_b: Exclusive authorization and right to sell
feedback: Exclusive Authorization and Right to Sell Listing is a contract where the Owner agrees to sell the property in question through the Listing Broker. The Listing Broker does not need to show that he is the procuring cause of the sale to earn a commission. NOTE - This question says "all of the following types of listings EXCEPT" which means it's looking for a false answer, and it's false that an exclusive authorization and right to sell requires procuring cause.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agent who exclusively represents the buyer:
option_a: is prohibited from advising the buyer on offer prices.
option_b: should advise the buyer to make offers directly through the seller to avoid conflicts
option_c: may present offers to the seller or to a seller's agent.
option_d: can also represent the seller in the same transaction without disclosure.
feedback: An agent who exclusively represents the buyer, known as a buyer's agent, has the ability to present offers to the seller or the seller's agent on behalf of the buyer. The role of the buyer's agent is to act in the best interest of the buyer, negotiating the purchase price and terms, and facilitating the transaction process for them. This could involve communicating directly with the seller if it's a FSBO (For Sale By Owner) situation, or more commonly, with the seller's agent if the seller is also represented by an agent.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The final decision in establishing the listing price is made by the:
feedback: A listing is a written agreement between a property owner and a real estate broker authorizing the broker to find a buyer. The broker can help the owner decide the listed price, but it is the seller's sole decision as it is their house.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements about the law of agency is CORRECT?
option_b: It does not require a high degree of loyalty and fidelity from an agent.
feedback: A fiduciary relationship is a relationship of trust and confidence, as between principal and agent, trustee and beneficiary, attorney and client.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Within the agency agreement is a statement that binds the buyer to payment of the broker's fee if, within a stated number of days, the buyer purchases a property that was shown to the buyer during the agency period. This clause is called the:
feedback: Unless the buyer subsequently hires another buyer agent and purchases this property, the broker protection clause obligates the buyer to pay the broker's fee.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When a written agreement spells out the responsibilities of the principal and the agent, the relationship created is called a(n):
feedback: When the terms of the agency relationship are formalized in writing, it is known as an express (or "expressed") agency.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What happens if an agent has not located a buyer by the end of the listing period?
option_a: The listing is automatically extended for an additional 90 days
option_c: The listing price is reduced by 7.5%
option_d: The broker can sue for reimbursement of marketing expenses
feedback: Real estate agents have a "special agency", which means they are hired to perform a specific act for their client. If the object of the special agency is not performed by its expiration date, the listing expires and the agency relationship terminates.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Under what circumstances might a broker collect a commission from both buyer and seller?
option_a: If both parties give informed consent.
option_c: Only with approval from the state licensing authorities.
option_d: If doing so does not violate company policy.
feedback: Particularly in commercial transactions, payment by both parties does occur if each party agrees to the arrangement (although a few states specifically prohibit payment by both sides to a transaction).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following describes a representative with full authority to bind the principal to all agreements in which the representative has been authorized to act?
feedback: A general agent has the power to act on behalf of the principal in an authorized area of business. Property managers are an example of a general agent, as they are authorized to perform all tasks related to the managing of the property.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The fiduciary duty of full disclosure requires the seller's agent to:
option_a: obey all the seller's lawful instructions.
option_b: tell the seller anything that might be of use in negotiations.
option_c: advise the buyer about how much the seller is likely to accept for the property.
feedback: The agent is obliged to pass on to their client anything useful that is learned during the transaction. NOTE - this question is specifically asking about the fiduciary duty of disclosure.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In a real estate transaction, both the seller's agent and the buyer's agent are legally required to put whose interest first?
feedback: An agent is always required to put the interests of their principal first, above anyone else's.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The situation with the most potential for conflict is when a real estate licensee acts as a:
feedback: Dual agency poses problems because it requires equal but limited representation to two different principals. Fiduciary duties give an agent the obligation to put a principal's interests first, and it is always a challenge as a dual agent not to put one party's interests above the other.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In a real estate transaction, fiduciary duties are owed to the:
feedback: Specific fiduciary duties are owed to the agent's principal.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate licensee is the agent of the:
option_d: party with whom an agency relationship agreement has been entered into
feedback: Real estate licensees represent the person with whom they entered into an agency relationship agreement (contract), whether it be the seller or the buyer. The person paying the agent's commission is not always the agent's principal. Often a seller will pay a buyer's broker simply to facilitate the sale.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Jones agrees to represent Brown in the sale of a property. The listing contract states that Jones will receive a fee of $25,000 from Brown at closing, regardless of the sales price. Is this a valid form of compensation?
option_a: Yes. Compensation can be determined in any number of ways, as long as the seller and broker agree
option_b: No. The seller should pay on a basis of a stated percentage of contract price
option_c: No. A flat fee exposes the seller to being cheated
option_d: Yes. The broker is supposed to charge what the traffic will bear
feedback: Compensation is negotiated between broker and seller and may be stated in many ways including as a percentage of price, hourly rate, by the transaction, or by a menu of services. In this case, Brown is paying Jones a flat fee, which is an acceptable form of compensation. NOTE - A flat fee is different than a net listing, in which the seller gets a set amount and the broker's compensation varies based on the selling price.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The obligation of protecting any deposits entrusted to an agent is an example of the fiduciary duty of:
feedback: The agent must be able to account for and produce anything of value entrusted to their care. This includes advances of marketing fees, earnest money deposits, and escrows.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Jones lists his house and tells his listing agent, Brown, "I'm listing for $480,000 but I might take less." Brown may properly:
option_a: Share Jones' statement only with the buyer's broker.
option_b: Explain Jones' position to prospective buyers in an effort to obtain an offer.
option_c: Advertise the property for "$480,000 or less."
feedback: The duty of confidentiality forbids Brown's sharing any information that might damage his client's bargaining position.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Once an agency relationship is ended, any confidential information learned by the agent during the relationship is:
option_b: sealed in boxes and stored with an affidavit.
option_d: a part of the MLS historical data base.
feedback: Confidential information remains confidential forever, with few exceptions.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The relationship of a property manager with the owner is a:
feedback: Since a property manager makes decisions on behalf of the owner, such as tenant selection, budget preparation, and employee relations, the property manager is a general agent.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When an agency relationship ends, which of the following duties does NOT continue?
feedback: In real estate terms, loyalty means doing nothing that might weaken the seller's bargaining position, and that responsibility ends when the sale is complete.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following would not terminate an agency relationship?
feedback: Agency relationships can expire, be terminated by mutual agreement, or end because the agreement resulting in an agency relationship has been performed. Renewal would result in the agency relationship continuing.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following would not terminate an agency relationship?
option_d: An agent submitting two offers at the same time
feedback: Submitting offers doesn't end the relationship. Fulfillment of the agency purpose by the owner's acceptance of one, and ultimately closing on the transaction, terminates an agency relationship. Revocation by the principal or abandonment by the agent also terminates an agency relationship, but can result in breach of contract if there's a listing agreement.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If a salesperson acts as an independent contractor, then:
option_a: his broker must withhold income tax from his commission checks.
option_b: his broker will set his hours and marketing methods.
option_c: he controls his own working hours.
option_d: all of his listings belong to him.
feedback: Whether a licensee is considered an employee or independent contractor can have an enormous impact on a licensee's work style and cash flow. Independent contractors have almost unlimited latitude in determining how and when they work, as long as the results of that work are satisfactory to the broker. Listings, however, belong to the broker. Additionally, taxes are not withheld from commission checks. That means, however, that the licensee is responsible for paying estimated taxes (penalties for late payments can be substantial) as well as higher "Social Security" taxes in the form of a self-employment tax.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A broker is negligent while preparing a sales contract and allows the purchaser to void the agreement. The broker has breached the duty she owes her client of:
feedback: "Due care" refers to the licensee's obligation to ensure that all steps in the transaction process are followed with professionalism and accuracy. Errors that a broker or salesperson could reasonably have been expected to avoid and that damage a client (such as the loss of a sale) may be legally actionable.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A seller has listed her home with a broker for $90,000, and the broker tells a prospective buyer to submit a low offer because the seller is desperate to sell. The buyer offers $85,000 and the seller accepts it. In this situation:
option_a: The broker was unethical, but the seller did get to sell her property
option_b: The broker has violated his agency relationship with the seller.
option_c: Any broker is authorized to encourage such bids for the property.
option_d: The broker acted properly to obtain a quick offer on the property.
feedback: Licensees have a fiduciary responsibility to act in the best interests of their clients. By disclosing the owner's sense of urgency and suggesting a lower price, the broker was acting as an "advocate" for the opposite side in this transaction. Moreover, an implied conflict of interest can be inferred because the action creates a perception that the broker may have been looking out for his or her best interests (a quick commission), which increases the level of malpractice.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What is a "client" during a real estate transaction?
option_a: Any person who signs an agreement for the sale or purchase of a property with a licensee.
option_b: Any person who views property in the company of a licensed salesperson or broker.
option_c: Any person who phones or visits a company seeking more information about specific properties.
option_d: Any person who expresses interest in working with an individual firm or licensee.
feedback: Much as it makes us feel better to think of all people who express interest or phone us as "clients," the fact is no one is a client until he or she affixes a signature to a formal agreement.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In an agency relationship, the person identified as the client is usually the:
feedback: The person who retains an agent is known as the client, or principal. For example, the buyer and seller are principals in the purchase of real property.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is a requirement for a valid agency relationship?
feedback: From those choices, only mutual consent is required. Agency is sometimes formed by the behavior of the parties, so it does not need to be written, while compensation (payment) generally happens after the task assigned in the agreement has been performed, and having a brokerage license isn't required to form an agency relationship.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following listings is illegal in most states and open to fraudulent dealings?
feedback: Net listings, in which the agent keeps any part of the sale price above a given amount, are illegal in most states.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The main obligation the principal owes a broker is:
feedback: The principal's primary obligation in an agency agreement is usually payment of the agreed-upon commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A principal terminates the listing agreement during the listing period. What can the broker NOT do?
option_a: continue to cooperate with any subsequent listing broker.
option_b: encourage the termination of any contract in process.
option_c: collect a commission for a transaction in process.
feedback: A broker should not encourage the termination of any contract in process. This is unethical and could potentially be illegal, depending on the circumstances. Encouraging the termination of contracts could harm the principal's or other parties' positions and is not a legitimate course of action for a broker. Agents cannot interfere with any transactions that originated during their relationship with a principal, even if the principal has terminated the listing agreement. The terms of the listing agreement will often detail any damages an agent can recover under these circumstances, like marketing expenses or the full commission for a sale, and an agent has the right as an independent businessperson to cooperate with any subsequent listing broker in order to complete a transaction.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Agency is the act of representing someone in the purchase, sale, or lease of a property. Subagency is the act of representing:
option_c: A principal who is represented by an agent.
option_d: A department of the federal government.
feedback: A subagency may be authorized by the terms of a listing agreement.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When negotiating a lease on behalf of a commercial real property landlord, the broker would usually calculate his commission as:
option_a: a percent of the first and last month's rent.
option_b: a percent of the rent due in the first year of the lease.
option_c: a percent of the rent due for the entire lease period.
option_d: a percent of the rent due for the first six months of the lease.
feedback: The broker's commission is normally calculated as a percent of the total amount of lease payments. This is negotiable, however and not a hard and fast rule of law.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The maximum commission a broker may charge for the sale of residential property is:
option_a: set forth in the Real Estate Law.
option_b: six percent of the total sales price of a residence.
option_c: determined by the broker's contract with his principal.
feedback: There are currently no laws to control the commission amount charged by any broker, but the commission amount must be included in the employment agreement.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A broker has been in the business for many years. He currently has 40 exclusive listings in his office. The broker dies and his daughter who is also a broker, takes over for him. What should she do about the current listings?
option_a: She should attempt to renegotiate all of the listings, if possible
option_b: Just inform all sellers that she is taking over as broker
option_c: Do nothing, a change in the broker of record doesn't affect listings
option_d: She need only inform the Bureau of Real Estate that she is now the broker of record
feedback: Listings are personal service contracts and terminate with the death of either party. The listing agreement is between the seller and the broker. When the broker dies, the listing terminates (in non-corporate instances). In this case, the new broker should attempt to renegotiate the listings with the sellers.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate broker should regard himself/herself as a:
feedback: A real estate broker who becomes an agent of a seller or buyer, either intentionally through the execution of a written agreement, or unintentionally by a course of conduct, is deemed to be a fiduciary. Fiduciary duties are the highest duties known to the law. Classic examples of fiduciaries are trustees, executors, and guardians. As a fiduciary, a real estate broker will be held under the law to owe certain specific duties to his principal, in addition to any duties or obligations set forth in a listing agreement or other contract of employment.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When selling a business, the rate of commission is determined by the:
option_a: agreement between the broker and owner.
feedback: Real estate commissions are always negotiable and are determined by the agreement between the broker and owner.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship may be created by all of the following EXCEPT:
feedback: Subornation (to "suborn") means to bribe or induce someone to unlawfully (or secretly) perform some misdeed, or to commit a crime. Subornation won't create a lawful agency relationship, but the rest of the answers do.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If you are a subagent of the listing broker, you are representing the:
feedback: "Subagent" means a person to whom an agent delegates agency powers. In a case like this, the subagent may be "working for" the listing agent, but they are still representing the seller. This is common in real estate, as the marketing of real property by real estate agents is often a cooperative effort. The listing broker usually makes an offer of compensation to a cooperating broker (subagent) by placing the property on the Multiple Listing Service. Some MLS's make the offer of subagency automatic, while for others, the offer of subagency is optional and requires an affirmative act by the listing broker. If the principal (seller) specifically authorizes his/her broker to appoint a subagent and thus establishes a new contractual and fiduciary relationship, the subagent represents the principal in the same manner as the listing broker.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When is a properly licensed real estate salesperson most likely to get paid their commission?
option_a: When the buyer signs the contract
option_b: When the grant deed is signed
option_d: When the grant deed is recorded
feedback: Be careful when reading this question- it is not asking when the commission is EARNED, but when it's PAID. The salesperson is most likely to get paid their commission when escrow closes. They have earned a commission once acceptance of the offer is communicated back to the buyer.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: As the agent for the seller, a real estate broker negotiated a sale. By agreement, the brokerage can be paid his commission in the form of:
feedback: Commissions are usually paid with money, but a commission can be nearly anything of value (as long as it's legal). All of the choices are possible ways to pay a real estate transaction commission, as long as it's agreed upon in advance.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Seller "A" lets buyer "B" assume that Broker "C" was his agent. This type of agency relationship is an example of:
option_a: none of the other options are correct.
feedback: Ostensible authority happens when a third-party is allowed to believe there is agency relationship between an agent and a principal through the actions of the principal. If a broker acts like a seller's agent and the seller doesn't say otherwise, but the broker's actions result in a sale, the broker will most likely be considered to have an ostensible authority.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When acting as an agent for seller Jones, real estate broker Smith:
option_a: can modify the terms of any contract that Jones enters into.
option_c: can eliminate any offers on Jones' property that Smith believes are not good enough.
option_d: is obligated to render faithful service to Jones.
feedback: As a fiduciary, the broker (as well as the salesperson) has a duty to be faithful to the principal.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A power of attorney can be terminated by:
option_b: all of the other options are correct.
option_c: incapacitation of the party being represented.
feedback: A power of attorney is a legal document that grants one person (the agent or attorney-in-fact) the authority to act on behalf of another person (the principal). This authority can be terminated in several ways: Revocation by the principal means the principal decides to cancel or withdraw the power of attorney. Incapacitation of the party being represented (the principal) can terminate certain types of power of attorney, but there are "durable" powers of attorney that remain in effect even if the principal becomes incapacitated. Death of the attorney-in-fact or the principal terminates the power of attorney because the legal authority granted cannot extend beyond the life of the individuals involved. Since each of these situations results in the termination of the power of attorney, the correct answer is that all of the other options are correct.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Johnson, a real estate broker, listed a property. Suzie, a salesperson working for broker Sims, received an offer. Donna, a salesperson working for broker Johnson, got the offer accepted. Who will the commission be paid to?
feedback: As the listing broker, Johnson earned the commission under the terms of the listing agreement. Remember: listing agreements are between a broker/principal broker and a client, not the salesperson/associate broker who may be servicing the client. A commission split may occur since the offer came in through another broker.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Owner Joe tells his listing broker, Stephen, that he wants to sell his house as soon as possible and would accept $350,000. Stephen tells buyer Selwyn that Joe has financial troubles and will accept a much lower price, possibly as low as $300,000. Joe, ultimately, accepts an offer from Selwyn for $300,000. Broker Stephen is guilty of:
option_a: unethical behavior, but not a serious breach of his fiduciary responsibility to Joe.
option_b: unethical behavior and a violation of the fiduciary responsibility.
option_c: nothing, since his job is to find a buyer no matter what.
option_d: violating the fiduciary duty to buyer Selwyn.
feedback: Broker Stephen's actions are a clear violation of his fiduciary responsibility to the seller. This would also be a violation of the Realtors Code of Ethics since the seller did not authorize broker Stephen to disclose such information to the buyer. If owner Joe's financial troubles were in fact true and he authorized broker Stephen to inform the buyer of such, it would not be a violation.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Commissions rate price fixing is a violation of the:
feedback: Price fixing is a violation of the Sherman Anti-trust laws.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agent becomes the agent of the seller when both the seller and the agent sign a(n):
feedback: The Listing Agreement is the employment contract that creates the agency relationship between the seller and the listing agent.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Typically when leasing real property, the commission of the leasing agent is based on a percentage of:
option_a: initial deposit paid by the lessee
option_b: the total rent to be collected during the full term of the lease
feedback: Usually, the commission of a leasing agent is figured on the total rent to be collected over the term of the lease. The commission can be any amount negotiated and specified in the employment contract.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: All of the following are essential to the creation of an agency relationship, EXCEPT:
feedback: The payment of consideration need not be involved in an agency relationship. One may gratuitously undertake to act as an agent and will be held to the standards of agency upon assumption of those duties.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A listing agent received two offers in the morning and immediately presented the offers to the seller. In the evening the agent received two more offers, both with a lower price. What should the agent do?
option_a: Wait until the seller has made a decision on the morning offers before presenting the evening offers.
option_b: Wait until the next day to present the lower evening offers.
option_c: Don't present the evening offers to the seller because the offers were low.
option_d: Let the seller know of the two new offers immediately.
feedback: Even though it is a temptation to favor the higher offers, it is the listing agent's fiduciary duty to notify the seller of the new offer immediately.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An attorney-in-fact can best be described as a(n):
option_a: attorney appointed by the court to administer an estate of a deceased person.
option_b: legally competent person who has been given the power of attorney by another competent person.
option_c: duly authorized person who has been granted both actual and implied powers to act as a principal for another.
option_d: properly authorized party who is acting as a dual agent.
feedback: An attorney-in-fact is a competent and disinterested person who is authorized by another person to act in his/her place.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Who is more likely to earn the commission?
option_a: The one who secured the biggest down payment from the buyer.
option_b: The one who communicated the seller's acceptance of the offer to the buyer.
option_c: The one who secured the offer.
option_d: The one who communicated the offer to the seller.
feedback: Until the seller's acceptance is communicated to the buyer, there is no contract. Therefore, the one who communicates the seller's acceptance to the buyer is more likely to earn the commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship may be created by:
option_b: all of the other options are correct.
feedback: Most agency relationships are created by written agreement, but agency relationships can also be created by ratification (consenting to acts of the agent), and estoppel (conduct of a principal leads a third-party, like a buyer, to reasonably believe an agent has an agency agreement so the principal cannot go back on that conduct).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agency relationship can be created by all of the following, EXCEPT:
feedback: A real estate licensee cannot become your agent by simply volunteering. An agency relationship can be created by written agreement or it may be implied by the acts of the principal and agent. It can also be created by oral agreement.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate sales commission is usually based on the:
feedback: Real estate commissions are usually based on a percentage of the selling price, but that is not always the case. Be sure to notice on your exam if they are using absolute words such as "usually", "sometimes", or "almost" versus words like "always" or "never" because there's almost always an exception to every rule.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A "safety clause" is found in a:
feedback: A "safety clause" (also called a "protection period clause") is found in a listing contract. It provides for payment of a commission to the listing broker if a sale is consummated after the listing expires, but the property is sold to a buyer originally procured by the listing broker.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If the owner enters into an exclusive agency listing and thereafter sells the property through their own efforts:
option_b: the broker is entitled to 25 percent commission.
option_c: the broker is entitled to 50 percent of his commission.
option_d: the broker is entitled to full commission.
feedback: Under an Exclusive Agency Listing, the seller reserves the right to sell the property themselves and not pay a commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following does not have to be included in a written listing agreement?
option_b: the time duration of the listing
option_d: the complete legal description of the property being sold
feedback: The legal description is not required in most areas, and an adequate description such as the property address is satisfactory.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In a new counter-offer:
option_a: the terms of the offer can never be changed.
option_c: the counter-offeror can change the terms of the offer unilaterally.
option_d: none of the other options are correct.
feedback: When a new counter-offer is made, the roles are reversed and the person who made the counter-offer initially (the counter-offeror) becomes the person receiving the new counter-offer (counter-offeree).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A net leasing agreement would require the lessee to pay his share of all of the following EXCEPT:
feedback: With a net lease, the lessee (tenant) not only pays rent for occupancy but also pays maintenance and operating expenses such as taxes, insurance, utilities, maintenance, and repairs. The lessor (owner) would pay the leasing commission. In a true net lease, the tenant is responsible for expenses relating to the premises exactly as if the tenant were the owner. The rent paid is "net" to the lessor. This kind of lease is popular with investors who want to obtain a steady stream of income without having to handle the problems associated with management and maintenance. Commercial or industrial leases, ground leases, and long-term leases are typically net leases.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: On the last day of a 90-day exclusive agency listing, broker Jones submits a list of names of people with whom he negotiated to the seller. Broker Jones accidentally left out Adams' name. One week later, the owner sells to Adams. Which of the following is correct?
option_a: The owner is liable for half of the commission.
option_b: The owner is not liable for any commission.
option_c: Adams is liable for the full commission.
option_d: The owner is liable for the full commission.
feedback: A safety clause in an exclusive agency listing requires the broker to submit the names of all prospective buyers with whom he/she negotiated prior to the expiration of the listing. If the owner sells to a name that is not submitted per the safety clause, the seller will generally not be liable for a commission to the broker. NOTE - If Adams' name was included, a commission would be owed, however since it was omitted the seller would not owe any commission.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A contract signed under duress is:
feedback: Duress is a threat whereby a person is forced to do some act against his/her will. The person who was forced to perform under a threat has the right to void the agreement or let it stand.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A contract signed under duress is:
feedback: Duress is a threat whereby a person is forced to do some act against his/her will. The person who was forced to perform under a threat has the right to void the agreement or let it stand. NOTE -- When a contract is said to be voidable one person who is a party to the contract has the right to void the contract.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A listing that does not require a broker to use diligence in obtaining a purchaser is:
feedback: An exclusive listing is an agreement in which the seller agrees to appoint only one broker to sell the property. For this reason, it must include a promise by the broker to use diligence in finding a buyer. Therefore if a listing is not exclusive such as the case in an open listing, the broker is then not required to use diligence in finding a buyer.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A licensed real estate broker is successful in securing a buyer for a property who is ready, willing and able to buy. The offer is accepted and signed by both parties and escrow is opened. During the escrow period, the title company discovers that the seller is under 18 years of age and notifies the broker and the escrow holder. Under these circumstances, the contract is:
feedback: Contracts entered into by minors (individuals under the age of 18 in most jurisdictions) are generally considered voidable.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The lessee of an apartment and the owner of a condominium have many things in common. Which of the following is true for both?
option_a: They each hold an estate of inheritance
option_b: They each hold a less than freehold estate
option_c: They each hold a fee interest
option_d: They each hold an estate in real property
feedback: Although the type of estates are different, the thing they have in common is that they each have an estate in real property. Remember that an "estate" is simply an "interest in real property." A condo owner has a fee interest and an estate of inheritance. The lessee has a less-than-freehold estate. They each have an estate. A lease is personal property, it is also less-than-freehold estate.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: To whom does a listing belong?
feedback: This is true even if the listing was prepared by a salesperson. Representation contracts are always created at the broker level. If the salesperson moved to another brokerage, the listing would stay with the broker who sponsored that salesperson when the listing contract was entered into.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A lease where the rent is based on a percentage of the monthly or annual gross sales of the lessee is called a:
feedback: Percentage Lease is a lease whose rental is based on a percentage of the monthly or annual gross sales made on the premises.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Broker Williamsburg obtained an exclusive authorization and right to sell listing from the owner of a residence. During the listing period, Williamsburg found a buyer who made an offer that was accepted by the owner. Neither the offer nor the escrow instructions made any mention of Williamsburg being the agent, or of the payment of a commission. Under these circumstances:
option_a: Williamsburg is still legally entitled to a commission.
option_b: Williamsburg will have no legal right to enforce commission collection.
option_c: Williamsburg is subject to disciplinary action by the commission for negligence.
option_d: Williamsburg must prove he was the client in the relationship.
feedback: Williamsburg has earned his commission because the terms of the exclusive authorization and right-to-sell listing contract with the seller have been met. Exclusive right-to-sell listings guarantee the broker a commission, regardless of who finds a buyer.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Thinking that he is acting properly regarding the safety clause in a listing, a broker notifies the owner verbally of a prospective buyer with whom he negotiated. The day after the listing expires, the buyer purchases the property. Which of the following is correct?
option_a: The broker is entitled to one half of the commission
option_b: The broker is not entitled to any commission
option_c: The broker is entitled to a commission
option_d: The matter would need to go to board arbitration to be resolved
feedback: A safety clause in a listing requires the broker to submit the names of all prospective buyers with whom he/she negotiated in writing, not verbally.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Brown leases a residence from Baker on a three-month written lease. When the written lease expires, Brown retains possession of the property without the consent of Baker. Brown now holds a(n):
feedback: An estate at sufferance is defined as one in which a lessee rightfully comes into possession of the land and retains possession after the expiration of the lease.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Broker Jack listed Jill's home under an exclusive right-to-sell listing and later received an offer from another licensee, Amanda, that met all of the listing terms and conditions. After considering the offer, Jill informed Jack that she no longer wished to sell, and asked to be released from the listing agreement immediately. Which of the following is a TRUE statement about Jack's position in this situation?
option_a: Jack may keep the earnest money that accompanied the offer as liquidated damages
option_b: Jack must tell Jill that Amanda may sue for specific performance
option_c: Jack may succeed in collecting an earned commission from Jill
option_d: Jack must release Jill without obligation
feedback: In an exclusive right-to-sell listing, the listing broker gets paid a commission if the property is sold by anyone, including another broker or the seller, during the term of the listing agreement. If a valid offer is made that meets all the terms and conditions of the listing agreement and the seller then refuses to sell, the broker (Jack in this scenario) has technically fulfilled their end of the contractual agreement by producing a ready, willing, and able buyer. Therefore, Jack may be entitled to the agreed-upon commission, regardless of whether the sale completes, because he has performed his duties under the terms of the exclusive right-to-sell listing agreement. While Amanda, as the buyer, might have grounds to sue for specific performance to enforce the sale, this statement does not reflect Jack's position regarding the listing agreement with Jill. Earnest money disputes typically occur between the buyer and seller, not between the seller and the listing broker. The earnest money is not automatically entitled to the broker; it is intended as a good faith deposit to be credited toward the purchase price or returned depending on the terms of the purchase agreement and applicable state laws. Jack is not obligated to release Jill without obligation, especially since a valid offer was received that met the listing agreement's terms. While he might choose to release her for various reasons (e.g., maintaining a good relationship or avoiding legal disputes), he is not legally required to do so without any consequences.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A listing broker agrees verbally to split a commission with a selling broker. The listing broker now refuses to do so. The selling broker should:
option_b: notify the Bureau of Real Estate
option_c: do nothing since the agreement was verbal.
feedback: A verbal agreement between two brokers to split a commission is enforceable. The selling broker's recourse would be to sue the listing broker. The "selling broker" is the one who brings in the offer. NOTE - in real estate the term "selling broker" is a commonly used verbiage that refers to the buyers agent.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The word "laches" most nearly pertains to:
option_a: failure to do something within a prescribed time.
option_d: doing something within a prescribed time
feedback: Laches means failing to do something which should have been done, i.e., to file a claim or to enforce a right, all within a prescribed time. Closely related to this, the Statute of Limitations outlines actions that can be brought within a certain time frame.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A verbal agreement between two brokers to split a commission is:
option_c: void under the Statute of Frauds.
feedback: A verbal agreement between two brokers to split a commission is enforceable and is one of the exceptions to the Statute of Frauds.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following lists the essential elements of a valid contract?
option_c: Lawful object, competency, mutual consent, consideration
feedback: There are four essentials to all contracts: A lawful object: It must be legal. Competency: Parties capable of contracting. Mutual consent: Their consent which includes the offer and acceptance. Consideration: A sufficient consideration. This can be love or affection, but it's usually money.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which type of lease requires the lessee to pay a flat rent?
feedback: A gross lease is a lease where the lessee pays a fixed rent and the lessor pays the taxes, insurance, and other charges regularly incurred through ownership.

subject: B2 Agency
