question_text: An agent acquired a listing for a house and orally promised to hold an open house every week until the house was sold, but knew it was unrealistic to hold one every week. The agent held two open houses during the 90 day listing. This is an example of:
feedback: Constructive fraud is a breach of duty as by a person in a fiduciary capacity, without an actual fraudulent intent. Actual fraud is an act intended to deceive another; e.g., making a false statement or false promise without intending to perform it.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The broker's promise to use diligence in finding a buyer during an exclusive right-to-sell listing:
option_a: makes the listing a unilateral contract.
option_b: makes the listing voidable by the owner.
option_c: None of the other options are correct.
option_d: makes the listing a bilateral contract.
feedback: A bilateral contract is a contract in which each party promises to perform an act in exchange for the other party's promise to perform. An exclusive right-to-sell listing is an example of a bilateral contract with the broker agreeing to use their best efforts (diligence) to locate a ready, willing, and able buyer, while the seller promises to pay the broker a commission if the property is sold.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Antitrust laws prohibit all of the following EXCEPT for:
option_a: two real estate firms agreeing to not cooperate with another brokerage because of their low fees.
option_b: competing property management companies agreeing to standardized management fees.
option_c: competing brokers allocating market shares based on the value of homes.
option_d: a broker setting a company-wide commission schedule.
feedback: Real estate brokers can independently determine commission rates or fees for their own firms, but they can't create agreements with competing firms to do the same- this is called "collusion". Decisions about commission rates must be based on a broker's business judgment and revenue requirements and without input from other competing brokers. Standardizing management fees between competing brokerages and allocating market shares between competing brokerages based on home value also violate anti-trust laws.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Douglas, a broker, has entered into a listing agreement with a seller. Oscar is a different broker who has been working with the buyer and learns of the property through the MLS. Who would Oscar represent?
feedback: Because Oscar, the second cooperating broker in this case, has been working with the buyer, he would represent her as a buyer's agent.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A seller had a listing with ABC firm. The listing expired after 90 days and the seller never renewed the contract. Six months after the listing expired, a new agent started with ABC firm and came across the unsold listing. He wanted to call the seller but noticed the seller's number on the National Do Not Call Registry. Can the agent contact the seller?
option_a: No, because the seller's number is on the National Do Not Call Registry- even if the seller is currently active
option_b: Yes, because the firm has had a relationship with the seller in the last 18 months
option_c: Yes, because the firm has had a relationship with the seller in the last 3 months
option_d: No, because the seller's number is on the National Do Not Call Registry and the seller never contacted the agent to re-list the house
feedback: A vendor (in this case, the real estate agent) may call a consumer with whom it has an established business relationship for up to 18 months after the consumer's last contact - even if the consumer's number is on the National Do Not Call Registry. There is one caveat: if a consumer asks a specific company not to call, the company may not call, even if there is an established business relationship. A company may not call a consumer - regardless of whether the consumer's number is on the registry - if the consumer has asked to be put on the company's own do not call list. NOTE - This question is asking about customers who have had an established business relationship with a company- they can be contacted up to 18 months later. Consumers who have only made an inquiry (no established relationship) may be contacted up to 3 months later.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following must be done before placing a "For Sale" sign on a listed property?
option_a: All of the other options are correct
feedback: A broker may install a "for Sale" sign on a property only after obtaining permission from the owner of the property.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The position of trust assumed by the broker as an agent for the client is described most accurately as a
feedback: A fiduciary is a person who holds a legal or ethical relationship of trust with one or more other parties (person or group of persons). Typically, a fiduciary prudently takes care of money or other assets for another person.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The prime obligation of an agent to their client is:
feedback: The relationship between a real estate agent and a client is called a fiduciary relationship. A fiduciary is a person who acts on behalf of another person. The core fiduciary duties are: Loyalty, Confidentiality, Disclosure, Obedience, Reasonable care, and Accounting.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The principal to whom a real estate agent gives advice and counsel is a:
feedback: The real estate agent has an "agency" relationship with a client, which generally means there is a contractual agreement. In this relationship the agent is the fiduciary, not the other way around. A customer has no contractual agreement with the agent, while a subagent refers to the relationship an agent can have with the buyer (if they aren't contracted directly by the buyer). When looking at questions like this it is important to go for the best option that is available.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What is the latest time at which a broker must establish their agency relationship with a buyer or seller and execute all such agreements?
option_b: At any time after a purchase and sales agreement is executed
option_c: At the time of the first business contact
option_d: No later than the time at which a purchase and sale agreement is prepared
feedback: Usually a broker's agency relationship with a buyer or seller is established early on in the process. But if for some reason it's not, the agency relationship between broker and client must be established, and all agreements executed, by the time a purchase and sale agreement is prepared.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Procuring cause would not be required for a broker to receive a commission in a(n):
feedback: An "exclusive right to sell" listing is an agreement under which a real estate agent has the sole right to sell a specified property, usually within three months. During this period the seller cannot list the property with any other agent and (unlike in exclusive listing) must pay the agent's commission even if he or she (and not the agent) finds a buyer.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If Carol wishes to sell her own house, does she need a real estate license?
option_a: She does not need a license
option_b: She may obtain a temporary license to do this
option_c: If she is an attorney she will not need a license
option_d: She must have a license issued by the real estate commission
feedback: An owner selling her own property is exempt from real estate licensing requirements.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Vivian had listed her property under an exclusive agency agreement with David, but when the agreement expired, she changed to a new agency. Now, David has a buyer who is interested in the property. Which of the following statements is correct?
option_a: David is a dual agent and can represent both.
option_c: David cannot give the buyer any information about the physical condition of the property.
option_d: David cannot disclose any offers received on the property while he was the listing agent.
feedback: Because David has prior information on the property, he must be careful about what information he shares with the potential buyer. David must maintain confidentiality regarding any offers received on this property while he was the listing agent, although he must disclose anything he knows about the material condition of the property. And since David no longer has an agency relationship with Vivian, he is free to represent the new buyer as a buyer's agent without getting into dual agency.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The real estate broker representing a seller is usually:
feedback: In real estate, a special agent (or "specific agent") is an agent hired to perform a specific duty for a client. The real estate agent's authority is limited to the specific job for which they are hired. This is in contrast to "universal" or "general" agents which are responsible for several duties (show and rent apartments, supervise maintenance, do the bookkeeping, etc).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A valid contract employs someone to do specific legal tasks which involve representing the interests of the client to a third party. What kind of relationship is created by the contract?
feedback: The principal and agent create an agency relationship. This is a business relationship where a principal gives legal authority to an agent to act on the principal's behalf when dealing with a third party. An agency relationship is a fiduciary relationship.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A broker and seller terminate the listing contract. An offer is received in the mail by the broker after the termination of the listing contract. The offer is for full price and includes all of the terms and conditions of the seller. Why is this NOT a valid contract?
option_a: There is no current listing agreement.
option_b: No earnest money has been enclosed.
feedback: It has not been presented to or accepted by the owner. Remember, contracts aren't valid until both parties agree. However, even though the listing agreement has expired, the offer should be presented. If it's accepted and the transaction closes, the broker will generally be entitled to his or her full commission. NOTE- while there's no listing agreement here, that's not what makes the offer "NOT a valid contract"- it's the lack of 1 of the 4 essentials of a valid contract that makes it so (consideration has been offered, but no acceptance of the offer has been made).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Ivonne represents the Sparks who are selling their property. Which of the following actions must Ivonne take when prospective buyers ask to see the property?
option_a: Inform them, either orally or in writing, that she represents a certain brokerage.
option_b: She may show them the property without any required disclosures or agreements.
option_c: Inform them that she represents the seller's interests.
option_d: Inform them that they must complete a buyer representation agreement.
feedback: Ivonne must disclose that she represents the seller so the buyer correctly understands the agent's relationship in a real estate transaction.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Arthur, a licensed principal real estate broker, was sued for conversion. This means Arthur allegedly did which of the following?
option_a: Failed to maintain a positive balance in his clients' trust account.
option_c: Failed to reconcile his clients' trust account in a timely manner.
option_d: Commingled the client's trust account funds with his own.
feedback: Conversion is the misappropriation of client funds, and misappropriation is the intentional, illegal use of the property or funds of another person for one's own use or other unauthorized purpose. This is different than commingling, which is the inappropriate mixing of client funds with broker funds.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What type of a listing agreement allows the owner to appoint an exclusive agent to sell his property, but retains the right to sell the property himself without paying a commission?
feedback: Open listings mean that if the owner or any other broker or salesperson produces the buyer, the broker will lose his or her commission. Exclusive Right to Sell gives the broker his or her commission regardless of who actually sells the property, even if it is the owner. Exclusive Agency allows the seller to appoint an exclusive agent, but retain the right to sell the property himself.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Broker Jim with ABC Realty listed a property for a seller. Broker Bill with XYZ Real Estate Co. called Broker Jim and disclosed that he was a buyer's agent. Broker Bill wrote a contract with the buyer for the purchase of the property. What, if any, is the relationship between Broker Bill and Broker Jim?
option_c: There is no relationship- broker Jim represents the seller and broker Bill represents the buyer separately
feedback: Since each broker represents separate sides in the transaction and each works for a different real estate firm, no relationship exists between the two brokers.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If the buyer defaulted some time ago on a written contract to purchase a seller's real estate, the seller can still sue for damages, if he is not prohibited from doing so by the:
feedback: Statutes of limitations exist to keep the legal system from getting bogged down in old disputes and allow for evidence and recollections to remain reasonably fresh. Civil limitations typically range from one to six years, though in some cases up to twenty-five years.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Dan is house shopping and contacts a real estate office. He tells the agent, Stephen, that he is interested in the area but is not ready to sign any agreements just yet. Stephen can be helpful and accommodating with all of the following actions except:
option_a: providing information on properties for sale in the area.
option_b: providing information on mortgage interest rates and terms.
option_d: explaining to Dan about buyer agency, seller agency, and dual agency.
feedback:
Stephen cannot obtain confidential information about Dan without first entering into a buyer agency relationship, so Stephen may NOT prequalify Dan for a mortgage.

NOTE - This question says "Stephen can be helpful... with many actions EXCEPT" which means it's looking for a false answer, and it's false that Stephen can pre-qualify Dan for a mortgage.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: When must an agency disclosure statement be given to prospective purchasers/tenants by a real estate licensee?
option_b: immediately after the purchase or lease agreement is signed
option_c: at the first substantive meeting of the licensee and prospective purchasers/tenants, before any offer to purchase is prepared or presented
option_d: when the client asks for it
feedback:
The general rule for when a real estate licensee must give prospective purchasers or tenants an agency disclosure statement is at the first substantive contact. This means before entering into a formal agency relationship or exchanging confidential information, which could materially affect the prospective purchaser's or tenant's decision-making process regarding a real estate transaction.

This requirement aims to ensure transparency and protect consumers by informing them about the nature of their relationship with the real estate professional. This clarification includes explaining whether the agent is working as a representative for the seller (seller's agent), the buyer (buyer's agents), both parties (dual agency), or providing a transactional service without agency representation. This early disclosure helps establish clear expectations and allows consumers to make informed decisions about how they proceed in their real estate transactions.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What law prescribes the rights and duties between a property manager and his clients?
feedback: The laws of agency are an area of contractual or commercial law dealing with fiduciary relationships that involve a person, called the agent, who is authorized to act on behalf of another (called the principal).

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The primary focus of ethical behavior regulations does not generally include
option_d: standards of conduct in every facet of the business
feedback: The handling of earnest money is a legal issue, usually covered in state legislation

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate licensee has a buyer agency agreement. What is the relationship of the seller to the licensee in this situation?
feedback: There's an important distinction between client and customer. Unless there is a specific agreement to the contrary, licensees represent only one side in a transaction and the person they represent is their client - everyone the licensee doesn't represent is a customer. In this case, the licensee represents the buyer so the seller is a customer to the licensee.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A real estate licensee has a buyer agency agreement. What is the relationship of the seller to the licensee in this situation?
feedback: There's an important distinction between client and customer. Unless there is a specific agreement to the contrary, licensees represent only one side in a transaction and the person they represent is their client- everyone the licensee doesn't represent is a customer. In this case, the licensee represents the buyer so the seller is a customer to the licensee.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The principal theme of federal fair housing laws is to
option_a: ensure all Americans a fair chance to own a home
option_c: ensure that housing transactions are negotiated fairly
option_d: prohibit agents from dealing unfairly with clients and customers
feedback: Federal and state governments have enacted laws prohibiting discrimination in the national housing market

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: It is illegal to discriminate in selling a house based on race, color, religion, or national origin. This is provided for through
option_d: the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
feedback: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968, known today as the Fair Housing Act, prohibits discrimination in housing based on race, color, religion, or national origin

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following laws or rulings extended discrimination to include gender, handicapped status, and family status?
option_c: the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
feedback: The Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988 prohibited discrimination based on sex and discrimination against handicapped persons and families with children. Executive Order 11063 concerned racial discrimination in housing where federal funding was involved; the Civil Rights Act of 1968 concerned discrimination based on race, color, religion, and national origin; Jones v Mayer concerned racial discrimination

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agent informs numerous families in a neighborhood that several minority families are planning to move into the immediate area, and that the trend could have adverse effects on property values. This activity is
feedback: Blockbusting is the practice of inducing owners in an area to sell or rent to avoid an impending change in the ethnic or social makeup of the neighborhood that will cause values to go down

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A minority family would like to buy a home in a certain price range. The agent shows the family all available properties in a neighborhood of families with similar backgrounds. The agent did not mention a number of homes in the family's price range in other neighborhoods. This agent could be liable for
option_d: nothing; his services were legal and acceptable
feedback: Steering is the practice of directly or indirectly channeling customers toward or away from homes and neighborhoods. Broadly interpreted, steering occurs if an agent describes an area in a subjective way for the purpose of encouraging or discouraging a buyer about the suitability of the area

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agent does not like a particular minority buyer, and is very short with the person, refusing to engage in lengthy conversation or show him any properties. A second minority party visits the office the next day. The agent is very forthcoming, and shows the person five prospective properties. This agent could be liable for
option_d: nothing; both parties were minorities, and therefore no discrimination occurred
feedback: The agent may be illegally providing unequal services by altering the nature or quality of brokerage services to a party based on race, color, sex, national origin, or religion

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Following the client's recommendation, an agent conceals the availability of a property from an employed but pregnant and unmarried minority woman. This agent could be liable for
option_d: nothing: an agent may show or not show any property at his or her discretion
feedback: Discriminatory misrepresentation means an agent may not conceal available properties, represent that they are not for sale or rent, or change the sale terms for the purpose of discriminating

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A condominium complex prohibits ownership of any unit by persons under 55 years of age. The association claims it has made the prohibition properly. Which of the following is true?
option_a: They are violating the Civil Rights Act of 1866
option_b: They are violating the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
option_c: They are guilty of age discrimination
feedback: Federal fair housing laws do not prohibit age and family status discrimination under the following circumstances:  •in government-designated retirement housing  •in a retirement community if all residents are 62 years of age or older  •in a retirement community if 80 % of the dwellings have one person who is 55 years of age or older, provided there are amenities for elderly residents  •in residential dwellings of four units or less, and single family houses if sold or rented by owners who have no more than three houses

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An owner suddenly pulls a property off the market after hearing from the agent on the phone that the agent had received a full-price offer from a minority party. The agent then informs the offeror that the home has been removed from the market and is unavailable. Which party or parties, if any, have violated fair housing laws?
feedback: The owner has illegally discriminated, and the agent, by going along with the owner, is equally guilty of violating fair housing laws

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Real estate trade associations promote a code of ethics primarily to establish
option_a: minimum standards of practice as imposed by law
option_c: high standards of practice for all facets of the business
feedback: Trade associations representing the real estate industry have instituted their own codes of ethics and professional practices covering every facet of brokerage activity. The standards of most of these codes concern the following general areas of practice

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The parts of the Americans with Disabilities Act that most concern real estate agents are those that deal with
feedback: Real estate practitioners are most likely to encounter Titles I and III and should acquire familiarity with these.  Title I (Employment) concerns equal employment opportunity. It is enforced by the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission.  Title III (Public Accommodations) concerns nondiscrimination in public accommodations and commercial facilities. It is enforced by the U.S. Department of Justice

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: How does CERCLA concern real estate agents?
option_a: It may cause them to be held liable for improper disclosure of potential violations
option_b: It requires them to conduct Phase II Environmental Site Assessments
option_c: It makes them subject to Environmental Protection Agency orders
option_d: It absolves them of any responsibility for  knowing about environmental hazards
feedback: Under CERCLA and the Superfund Amendment of 1986, current landowners as well as previous owners of a property may be held liable for environmental violations, even if “innocent” of a violation. Sellers often carry the greatest exposure, and real estate licensees may be held liable for improper disclosure

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is a fact about home warranties that an agent should be sure a client knows?
option_a: If the homeowner has paid an annual fee for the warranty, there are no additional charges for service
option_b: Conditions that pre-existed the coverage date of the warranty will not be covered
option_c: Any item that is not specifically excluded in the warranty contract may be assumed to be covered
option_d: Warranties are usually purchased to cover the full term of the mortgage loan
feedback: Purchasers of home warranties need to fully understand their coverage limitations in order to have realistic expectations of the warranty. Similarly, it is important for a homeowner to understand what may not be covered in the warranty. These items include conditions that existed prior to the effective coverage of the warranty

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The original fair housing statute prohibits discrimination in housing based on race.  It relates to selling, renting, inheriting, and conveying real estate.  This statute is known as the
feedback: The original fair housing statute, the Civil Rights Act of 1866, prohibits discrimination in housing based on race. The prohibition relates to selling, renting, inheriting, and conveying real estate

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In 1962, the President of the United States issued an executive order to prevent discrimination in residential properties financed by FHA and VA loans and facilitated the enforcement of fair housing where federal funding was involved.  The executive order was known as
option_c: Presidential Executive Order for Fair Housing
feedback: While the Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibited discrimination, it was only marginally enforced. In 1962, the President issued Executive Order 11063 to prevent discrimination in residential properties financed by FHA and VA loans. The order facilitated enforcement of fair housing where federal funding was involved

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The Fair Housing Act prohibits discrimination in housing based on which of the following protected classes?
option_a: income, number of children, ancestry, and gender
option_c: family size, marital status, credit score, and age
option_d: race, color, religion, national origin, sex, familial status, and disability
feedback: The Fair Housing Act prohibits discrimination based on the following categories of protection: race, color, religion, national origin, sex, familial status and disability.  The states and local entities may have additional protected classes

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of discriminatory advertising?
option_a: Advertising only in a newspaper that is written in Polish
option_b: Advertising that a listed property is located in a great school district
option_c: Advertising that a retail shopping center offers a wide variety of goods and services
option_d: Advertising that highlights the positive features of the property
feedback: Advertising exclusively in this way would restrict availability to a prospective buyer or tenant that does not have access to the Polish publication and does not speak Polish

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of discriminatory misrepresentation?
option_a: An agent informs a prospective tenant who is disabled that the property is no longer available when it is
option_b: An agent informs a prospective tenant who is disabled that the property is handicap accessible
option_c: An agent informs a prospective tenant that their application has been denied due to their poor credit scores
option_d: An agent informs a prospective buyer that the property has many nearby amenities
feedback: An agent may not conceal available properties, represent that they are not for sale or rent, or change the sale terms for the purpose of discriminating. For example, an agent who lies about the availability of a unit to a disabled person is violating fair housing laws

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of steering?
option_a: An agent shows a person homes that are only located in minority neighborhoods
option_b: An agent shows a person homes that they only qualify for and refuses to show higher-priced properties that they do not qualify for
option_c: An agent shows a disabled person homes that are handicap accessible and have specific amenities
option_d: An agent shows a couple expecting their first child a 3-bedroom home, although they requested to see 2-bedroom homes
feedback: Steering is the practice of directly or indirectly channeling customers toward or away from homes and neighborhoods. An agent who shows a person homes that are located only near certain places of worship is steering

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Redlining is defined as
option_a: identifying a community by indicating the outer boundary in red
option_b: identifying the area that a client has an interest in purchasing properties
option_c: refusing to make loans in specific neighborhoods regardless of financial qualifications
option_d: refusing to show million-dollar listings to someone who does not qualify
feedback: Redlining is the residential financing practice of refusing to make loans on properties in a specific neighborhood regardless of a mortgagor's qualifications. In effect, the lender draws a red line around an area on the map and denies all financing to applicants within the encircled area

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: There are exemptions to the fair housing laws for certain properties.  For example,
option_c: an owner-occupied building with four units if advertising is not discriminatory
option_d: a religious organization with discriminatory membership requirements
feedback: The Fair Housing Act allows for exemptions under a few specific circumstances. These are: a privately owned single-family home where no broker is used and no discriminatory advertising is used, with certain additional conditions, rental of an apartment in a 1-4 unit building where the owner is also an occupant, provided the advertising is not discriminatory, facilities owned by private clubs and leased non-commercially to members, facilities owned by religious organizations and leased non-commercially to members, provided membership requirements are not discriminatory

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Federal Fair Housing laws do not prohibit age and familial status discrimination under which of the following circumstances?
option_a: A gated townhome community that does not allow school buses to enter
option_b: A retirement community if all residents are 62 years of age and older
option_c: A condominium building that lacks a pool and play area
option_d: A 3-flat apartment building that is not owner-occupied
feedback: Federal fair housing laws do not prohibit age and family status discrimination under the following circumstances: in government-designated retirement housing, in a retirement community if all residents are 62 years of age or older, in a retirement community if 80 % of the dwellings have one person who is 55 years of age or older, provided there are amenities for elderly residents, in residential dwellings of four units or less, and single-family houses if sold or rented by owners who have no more than three houses

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Amendments to the original fair housing laws were made with the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988.  The amendments included discrimination against
option_b: age and families with more than four children
option_c: all occupants under the age of 21
feedback: The Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988 included handicapped persons and families with children as additional categories of protection

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If a real estate agent encounters discriminatory behavior on the part of the client, the agent should
option_a: avoid a confrontation and go along with a client’s discriminatory act
option_b: pretend as if the discrimination never occurred
option_c: withdraw from any relationship where client discrimination occurs
feedback: If the real estate agent goes along with a client’s discriminatory act, the agent is equally liable for the violation of fair housing laws. Thus it is imperative to avoid complicity with client discrimination and withdraw from any relationship where client discrimination occurs

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Complaints alleging fair housing violations may be filed within one year of the violation with the
option_d: Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity
feedback: Persons who feel they have been discriminated against under federal fair housing laws may file a complaint with the Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO) within HUD, or they may file suite in a federal or state court. Complaints alleging fair housing violations must be filed with the Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity within one year of the violation. HUD then initiates an investigation in conjunction with federal or local enforcement authorities

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Instead of or in addition to filing a complaint, a party may file suit in state or federal court
option_a: within one year of the violation
option_b: within two years of the allegation
option_c: if the one filing the complaint is the aggrieved party
option_d: if the allegation is directly related to the purchase of real estate
feedback: In addition to or instead of filing a complaint with HUD, a party may file suit in state or federal court within two years of the alleged violation

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following may occur if it is found that a fair housing complaint warrants further action?
option_a: The aggrieved parties will automatically be awarded fair compensation
option_b: The aggrieved parties may file suit in state or federal court
option_c: The aggrieved parties must wait two years before re-filing the complaint
feedback: If HUD decides that the complaint merits further action, it will attempt to resolve the matter out of court.  If efforts to resolve the problem fail, the aggrieved party may file suit in state or federal court

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: If discrimination is confirmed in court, which of the following may apply?
option_a: The respondent may be ordered to pay compensation for damages
option_b: The aggrieved parties may be awarded $50,000 for each confirmed violation
option_c: The respondent’s penalty may include jail time
option_d: The aggrieved parties may decide the penalties
feedback: If discrimination is confirmed in court, the respondent may be enjoined to cease practicing his or her business.   Also, the plaintiff may be compensated for damages, including humiliation, suffering and pain.  The courts may also impose civil penalties

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The statute requiring lenders involved with federally guaranteed or insured loans to exercise impartiality and non-discrimination in the geographical distribution of their loan portfolio is known as the
feedback: The Home Mortgage Disclosure Act is designed to prohibit redlining.  It is enforced in part by requiring lenders to report to authorities where they have placed their loans

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The civil rights law prohibiting discrimination against individuals with disabilities in all areas of public life, including employment, education, transportation, and facilities that are open to the general public is known as the
feedback: The ADA, which became law in 1990, is a civil rights law that prohibits discrimination against individuals with disabilities in all areas of public life, including employment, education, transportation and facilities that are open to the general public

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An amendment to the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) became effective on January 1, 2009, and, among other things, clarified the definition of disability to be as a
option_a: physical or mental condition that affects one’s ability to work
option_b: temporary or long-term condition that limits one’s movement
option_c: mental impairment that substantially limits one or more major life activities
option_d: condition that impairs the individual’s ability to run, jump, see, or hear
feedback: The Americans with Disabilities Act Amendments Act (ADAAA) became effective on January 1, 2009. Among other things, the ADAAA clarified that a disability is “a physical or mental impairment that substantially limits one or more major life activities.”

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) applies to which of the following?
option_a: All private employers with 15 or more employees
option_c: All properties with two or more stories
feedback: The ADAAA clarified that it applies to “all titles of the ADA and covers private employers with 15 or more employees state and local governments, employment agencies, labor unions, agents of the employer, joint management labor committees, and private entities considered places of public accommodation“.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The Americans with Disabilities Act Amendments Act (ADAAA) applies to which of the following categories?
feedback: The ADAAA also clarified that it applies to “state and local governments, employment agencies, labor unions, agents of the employer, joint management labor committees, and private entities considered places of public accommodation.”

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of a wrongful way for a landlord to deal with a disabled tenant?
option_a: A landlord requests that modifications made to a unit by the tenant be restored
option_b: A landlord installs a handicap ramp without the tenant’s consent
option_c: A landlord refuses to install a handicap ramp
option_d: A landlord shows the 5th-floor unit to a person with disabilities
feedback: The ADA requires that landlords modify housing and facilities so that disabled persons can access them without hindrance.  Modifications to a building would include the installation of a ramp

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Property disclosures are required in many states and require sellers to make a written disclosure about the condition of the property for the benefit of the buyer.  The required disclosures may include
option_a: seller disclosure regarding property condition and material facts
option_b: disclosure of the seller’s financial status concerning the property
option_c: seller disclosure regarding whether pets are living or have lived in the home
option_d: seller disclosure as to the stigmatization of the property
feedback: The seller property condition disclosure is the seller’s written summary of the property’s condition at the time of the contract.  It must be completed by the seller and must be truthful

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The Lead-Based Paint Act requires a seller to disclose known lead problems in properties built before:
feedback: Lead-based paint can be found in most homes built before 1978

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following has been linked to lung cancer?
feedback: Radon is an odorless, colorless, tasteless, and radioactive gas that is created in the ground where uranium and radium exist. Prolonged exposure to radon can cause lung cancer

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following disclosures are required if the property is part of a homeowner association?
option_a: The names and contact information of all residents
feedback: A typical HOA disclosure package includes: declarations, articles of incorporation, bylaws, articles of organization, operating agreements, rules and regulations, party wall agreements, minutes of annual owners’ meeting, minutes of directors’ or managers’ meetings, financial documents: balance sheet, income and expenditures, budget, reserve study, unpaid assessments, audit report, list of fees and charges, list of insurance policies, list of assessments by unit type

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The ethical practices that licensees must follow in serving the needs of consumers have emerged from three primary sources.  Which of the following is not one of the primary sources for real estate professionals?
option_b: Industry self-regulation through trade associations and institutes
feedback: The sources for real estate professionals include the following: federal and state legislation, state real estate licensing regulation, industry self-regulation through trade associations and institutes

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In compliance with applicable laws and to promote respect for the real estate profession, licensees should be careful to disclose certain information to potential buyers and sellers.  Which of the following is not required to be disclosed?
option_a: the licensee will receive compensation from more than one party in the transaction
option_b: any interest the licensee has in a listed property
option_c: in what year they received their real estate license
option_d: any profits made on a client’s money
feedback: This is usually public information that can be found on the state licensing board web site but is not a required disclosure. licensees should be careful to disclose: that the agent is going to receive compensation from more than one party in a transaction, property defects if they are reasonably apparent; however there is no duty to disclose a defect which it would require technical expertise to discover, any interest the agent has in a listed property if the agent is representing a party concerning the property, any profits made on a client's money, the agent's identity in advertisements, the agent’s representation of both parties in a transaction, the existence of accepted offers, identity of broker and firm in advertising as required by state law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 always prohibits discrimination, without exception or exemption, to which of the following categories of protection?
feedback: ALL discrimination in selling or renting residential property based on race is prohibited under the provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1866.  Even though the federal fair housing laws exempt certain restrictions from discrimination status, It is NEVER ok to discriminate based on race

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Buyer Jessica wishes to purchase seller Ryan's house.  Jessica includes a handwritten note along with her full price offer saying how much she loves the property and how she can imagine living at the home for the rest of her life.  This personal note included with the offer is typically referred to as a
feedback: Buyers sometimes try to enhance the attractiveness of a purchase offer by attaching a personal note to the offer. This appeal to the seller, called a “love letter,” typically mentions such things as why the buyer particularly likes the home, how the buyer imagines family life in the home, and how well the buyer will care for the home.

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The fair housing law that first protected people against discrimination in housing based on race was the
option_d: Title VIII amendment to the Fair Housing Act
feedback: The original fair housing statute, the Civil Rights Act of 1866, prohibits discrimination in housing based on race. The prohibition relates to selling, renting, inheriting, and conveying real estate

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The classes protected against discrimination by the Fair Housing Act of 1968 are
option_c: race, color, religion, and national origin
feedback: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968, known today as the Fair Housing Act, prohibits discrimination in housing based on race, color, religion, or national origin

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: An agent tells a prospective buyer that a seller will not provide any assistance in financing, when, in fact, seller financing is available.  If the buyer belongs to one of the groups protected by fair housing laws, the agent may be guilty of which type of illegal discrimination?
feedback: Discriminatory misrepresentation means an agent may not conceal available properties, represent that they are not for sale or rent, or change the sale terms for the purpose of discriminating

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following actions represents discriminatory advertising?
option_a: Telling prospective buyers about the positive and negative aspects of a certain neighborhood
option_b: Telling a prospective seller that now would be a good time to put a property on the market
option_c: Advertising a property as available to individuals of a particular race
option_d: Telling a prospective buyer that the agent is too busy to show the buyer properties personally on a given day
feedback: The prohibition against discriminatory advertising states that an agent may not advertise residential properties in such a way as to restrict their availability to any prospective buyer or tenant

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is an example of blockbusting?
option_a: An agent shows a minority home buyer properties located in a neighborhood where there are no other minority home owners
option_b: An agent persuades a minority home buyer to avoid looking in a neighborhood where there are no minority home owners
option_c: An agent persuades a family to put their house on the market because ethnic minority families are beginning to move into the neighborhood
option_d: An agent persuades a minority home buyer to buy a property located in an area where most of the home owners belong to minority groups
feedback: Blockbusting, a prohibited activity, is the practice of inducing owners in an area to sell or rent to avoid an impending change in the ethnic or social makeup of the neighborhood that will cause values to go down

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What is the significance of the Jones v. Mayer Supreme Court decision?
option_a: It provides that anyone seeking to buy or rent a residential property who feels victimized by racial discrimination may take legal action under the 1866 Civil Rights Act
option_b: It allows for certain exemptions to the Title VIII prohibitions against discrimination provided there is no broker involved in the sale or lease transaction
option_c: It established that brokers and lenders must indicate their compliance with fair housing laws by displaying a standard HUD poster
option_d: It added gender, age, and family status to the groups protected against discrimination in housing
feedback: In 1968, the Supreme Court ruled in Jones v. Mayer that all discrimination in selling or renting residential property based on race is prohibited under the provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1866. Thus, while the Federal Fair Housing Act exempts certain kinds of discrimination, anyone who feels victimized by discrimination based on race may seek legal recourse under the 1866 law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968 applies to the sale of
option_c: privately owned single-family residences listed with a broker
option_d: privately owned single-family residences "for sale by owner."
feedback: Among the circumstances where the Fair Housing Act might allow for an exemption is: a privately owned single-family home where no broker is used, with certain additional conditions. In other words, as soon as a broker is used in the sale, the law applies

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: How is an intentional misrepresentation penalized?
option_a: License discipline, fines, and possible incarceration
option_b: License discipline and fines, but no incarceration
feedback: Intentional misrepresentation, as a type of fraud, is a criminal act that may result in fines and incarceration, in addition to discipline from state regulators and professional organizations

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A broker signs a listing agreement to sell a home for $100,000.  An immigrant couple are interested in the house and ask the agent the price.  The agent states the price as $110,000.  According to the fair housing laws, such an action is
option_a: illegal, because the agent changed the terms of the sale to discourage this particular couple
option_b: illegal, because the agent violated the listing agreement
option_c: legal, because the quoted price increase did not exceed 10% of the listing price
option_d: legal, because the increased price does not necessarily exclude the couple
feedback: The Fair Housing Act prohibition against discriminatory misrepresentation states that an agent may not conceal available properties, represent that they are not for sale or rent, or change the sale terms for the purpose of discriminating

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following actions is allowed under federal fair housing laws?
option_a: A broker, following the instructions of the seller, advertises the property as for sale to Christian families only
option_b: A home seller, acting without a broker, places a "for sale-- mature, single men only" sign in front of the house
option_c: The owner of four rental houses advertises one of the properties for rent "to married couples, no children, no pets."
option_d: The owner of a duplex who resides in one of the units refuses to rent the other unit to a non-Christian
feedback: The Fair Housing Act would exempt the owner in this situation because it involves rental of an apartment in a 1-4 unit building where the owner is also an occupant and there is no discriminatory advertising

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: May Southfeldt believes a real estate agent has kept her from seeing a certain property for rent because she is a woman.  What actions should she take if she wants legal satisfaction for her complaint?
option_a: File charges of illegal discrimination with the police department that has jurisdiction over the local area
option_b: File a complaint with HUD and/or file suit against the offending parties in a state or federal court within the prescribed time period
option_c: Wait two years and then file a civil suit in federal court
option_d: File a civil suit in federal court to force HUD to enforce the Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988
feedback: The Fair Housing Amendments Act of 1988 prohibits discrimination based on sex. If May feels she has been discriminated against in this way, she may file a complaint with the Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (OFHEO) in HUD within one year or file suit in a federal or state court within two years of the alleged violation

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: John Dunbar hires Betty Jones to sell his house, with the condition that he will not be the first one in the neighborhood to sell to members of a certain ethnic group.  What should Betty do about this condition?
option_a: Inform Dunbar that the condition is illegal and that she cannot comply with it
option_b: Note the condition on the listing agreement and have Dunbar initial it
option_c: Pretend that she did not hear the condition and proceed to market the property to all groups
option_d: Inform Dunbar that she will try to discourage members of that group from looking at the property, but that she cannot control cooperating brokers
feedback: Fair housing laws apply to home sellers as well as to agents, with the exception of the exemptions. If an agent goes along with a client's discriminatory act, the agent is equally liable for violation of fair housing laws. It is thus imperative to avoid complicity with client discrimination. Further, an agent should withdraw from any relationship where client discrimination occurs

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Under federal fair housing laws, the owner of a ten-unit apartment building may legally
option_a: advertise that the property is not available to anyone requiring wheelchair access
option_c: require families without children to pay the same security deposit that families with children must pay
option_d: require tenants to move out when they become 62 years old
feedback: Fair housing laws prohibit discriminatory advertising, discrimination on the basis of national origin, discrimination based on age in dwellings of more than four units, and discrimination against families with children. The fact that the owner is requiring the same deposit from tenants with and without children does not discriminate against families with children but actually favors them

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Under what conditions would federal fair housing laws allow a home owner to refuse to rent to a family because they have children?
option_a: The owner has a consistent no-children policy in all her rental properties
option_b: The owner can prove that costs to repair damage caused by previous tenants with children exceeded the tenants' security deposit
option_c: It is a single-family house that is part of a federally-designated planned unit development
option_d: It is a single-family house, and the owner owns only one other rental home in addition to his own residence
feedback: Federal fair housing laws do not prohibit age and family status discrimination in residential dwellings of four units or less and in single family houses if sold or rented by owners who have no more than three houses

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What is the purpose of a home warranty?
option_a: Protect the lender against loss of collateral
option_b: Protect the homeowner against costs arising from major system failures
option_c: Provide funds to rebuild the home in case of natural disaster
option_d: Ensure the lender that the homeowner will make mortgage payments
feedback: Home warranties, or home service contracts, cover service, repair, or replacement of a home's major systems and appliances

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Of the following risk management strategies, the one that aims at minimizing both the severity and the likelihood of loss is
feedback: Reduction involves taking steps to reduce the probability or the severity of a potential loss. In real estate practice, one risk reduction tactic is to share responsibility for making a decision. The agent provides the consumer with expertise, and perhaps some advice, but lets the consumer decide how much to offer

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Insurance is a method of
feedback: Transference (outsourcing, insuring) means passing the risk to another party, by contract or other means. An insurance policy is the common example, but sometimes the wording of a sales or personal services contract can transfer risk without resorting to insurance

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: How does disclosure manage risk?
option_a: It deflects and reduces risk by preventing others from claiming they were misled
option_b: It eliminates the risk of committing an error
option_c: It accepts the risk of revealing confidential information
option_d: It transfers the risk of a code violation to the client
feedback: By ensuring that all parties have the information they are entitled to, proper disclosure reduces the risk that clients and customers will accuse a licensee of misleading or inducing them to make a decision with incomplete information

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following statements about a company procedures manual as a risk management device is true?
option_a: It places all the responsibility for compliance on the broker
option_b: It relieves the licensee of responsibility for knowing and obeying the law
option_c: It can be a guide to compliance with the law
option_d: It adds to the risk of misleading the public
feedback: Adherence to a procedures manual reduces the risk that an individual will inadvertently commit an unlawful act

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In what sense is the use of standardized forms a risk management procedure?
option_a: It eliminates the risk of being unable to draft a contract correctly
option_b: It reduces the risk of losing a client by having to make a referral to an attorney
option_c: It reduces the risk of committing an unauthorized practice of law
option_d: It eliminates the risk of wasting valuable time in creating custom forms
feedback: Standard forms save time and protect against the unauthorized practice of law. Since they are most often prepared by lawyers familiar with the market area, they can address contingencies that are common in the area in a manner that reflects the real estate laws of the state

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Keeping thorough records of every transaction is not only a risk management technique, it is
option_c: a technique for discovering market trends
feedback: State laws require licensees to document transactions. Firms are required to keep written records of all real estate transactions for a number of years (usually three to five) after closing or termination

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following communication records must (as opposed to should) be kept?
option_b: Copies of required communications to principals
option_d: Business cards of licensees one meets at open houses
feedback: Some communications with transaction parties are good and necessary for business. Others are required by law, and records of them must be maintained for a statutory period. Required records typically include listing agreements, offers, contracts, closing statements, agency agreements, disclosure documents, correspondence and other communication records, notes and any other relevant information

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The standard E & O policy covers damages resulting from
option_a: failure to disclose an environmental condition
option_d: negligence, error or omission in carrying out professional services
feedback: A standard E&O policy provides coverage for “damages resulting from any negligent act, error or omission arising out of Professional Services.”

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following is a common risk relating to the agency relationship?
option_a: Failing to inform and disclose properly
option_b: Failing to take a personal interest in a transaction
option_c: Acting as an exclusive agent without an oral agency agreement
option_d: Forgetting to record the listing agreement
feedback: Agency risks commonly concern the requirement to inform and disclose. Most states require agency relationships to be in writing and to be disclosed to all parties to a transaction. In states that do not use agency, there is still the obligation to explain and disclose the nature of the relationship

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Even after giving buyer and seller the required information about property condition disclosures, the licensee may still be subject to legal action for
option_b: failing to disclose known adverse facts
option_c: relying on publicly available market information
option_d: advising the purchaser to exercise due diligence
feedback: Depending on the state, the licensee may have no further duty to disclose property condition after properly informing parties of their rights and obligations. However, the licensee may still be subject to legal action for failing to disclose adverse facts the licensee knew about or should have known about

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In performing a comparative market analysis, a licensee must be careful to
option_a: use the term "market value" whenever possible in the report
option_b: show a low suggested selling price to avoid a complaint of misrepresenting the value
option_c: include the results of a certified appraisal in the analysis
option_d: avoid creating a false impression that the licensee is a certified appraiser
feedback: In preparing a Comparative Market Analysis, licensees should guard against using the terms “appraisal” and “value,” which are reserved for the use of certified appraisers. Misuse of these terms could lead to a charge of misrepresenting oneself as an appraiser. In discussing listed properties with clients or customers, real estate licensees should be careful to use guarded terms such as “recommended listing price,” “recommended purchase price,’ and “recommended listing price range.”

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In fulfilling a listing agreement, one of the major risk areas is
option_a: finding a buyer who turns out to be unqualified
option_b: exceeding the authority of the agreement
option_c: showing the property without the presence of the owner
feedback: Licensees should stay within the bounds of the authority granted by the agency agreement or must not do anything requiring permission without first getting that permission in writing

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A simple way of reducing the risk of committing an error or omission in the contracting process is
option_a: use a checklist of all items, contingencies, dates and responsibilities that must be met
option_b: delegate some of your responsibilities to the licensee who represents the other party in the contract
option_c: call the buyer and seller daily to check on progress
option_d: cut the list of necessary tasks down to a few essentials and concentrate on tracking those
feedback: One way to reduce risk in the contracting process is to use a checklist that covers all the contract items such as inclusions and exclusions, contingency periods, documents, forms, data checking, and milestone dates

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Regarding contracts and forms,
option_a: once written and signed they cannot be changed except by a lawyer
option_b: real estate licensees may alter forms but not contracts
option_c: whoever originates them can make changes without the risk of unauthorized practice of law
option_d: the principals may make changes as long as they sign or initial each change
feedback: Real estate professionals who are not attorneys are usually limited to filling in blanks or making deletions on a preprinted contract form prepared by a lawyer. While a licensee may make deletions, additions to a form should be drafted by an attorney. The principals themselves can make changes as long as each change is signed or initialed by all signers

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The best way to minimize the risk of violating fair housing laws is to
option_a: deal only with consumers who do not belong to a protected class
option_b: obtain education in the content and intent of the laws
option_c: make sure there is always a witness present at all meetings with consumers
option_d: stay away from transactions involving public housing
feedback: The risk of violating fair housing laws can be minimized through ongoing education that addresses both the content and the intent of the laws

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A licensee risks violating antitrust law by
option_a: being present at a conversation where the setting of commission rates is discussed
option_b: being present at a discussion of antitrust laws
option_c: charging a commission rate that happens to be the same as that charged by another firm
option_d: cooperating with another firm to do market research
feedback: Antitrust laws forbid brokers to band together to set a price on their services in listing and selling property. Even being overheard discussing commission rates or being present at such a conversation can lead to charges of price fixing

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: All of the following are areas of risk for unintentional misrepresentation EXCEPT
option_c: stating that a client should seek legal counsel
option_d: making statements about the presence or absence of hazardous substances
feedback: Risky areas for unintentional misrepresentation include:  •making and reporting measurements  •describing property  •offering opinions about future growth and development of a neighborhood or neighboring property  •making declarative statements about the presence or absence of hazardous materials

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: To reduce risks inherent in reporting transaction progress to a client, the licensee should
option_a: make reports orally only, never in writing
option_b: leave progress reporting to the inspectors and other experts
option_c: advise the client that it is company policy to make no progress reports until the contingency period is over
option_d: avoid statements of opinion and speculation in all reports
feedback: Progress reports should be accurate, timely, in writing, and free of speculation. Speculation may encourage the consumer to believe the agent has expertise that in fact is non-existent. If a consumer has a question about the meaning of something in an inspection report, the licensee should refer the consumer to the person who wrote the report rather than trying to explain it

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: How does sharing the qualifying function with a lender protect a licensee?
option_a: It guarantees that a buyer will have a loan
option_b: It reduces the chance of presenting an offer from an unqualified buyer
option_c: It relieves the licensee of his or her due diligence responsibilities
option_d: It allows the licensee to avoid asking embarrassing questions
feedback: Using a lender to qualify the buyer protects the agent against leading a seller to believe a purchaser is fully qualified when this may not be the case. Also, lenders and loan agents are better able to look into the buyer’s qualifications than a real estate licensee is. If it is necessary to show a property to a potential buyer who has not been qualified by a lender, the licensee can gain some protection by performing an informal qualification and documenting the fact that it was based on the information provided by the buyer

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Regulations affecting real estate practice at the state level are
option_a: the same throughout the United States
option_c: implemented by the state’s department of education
option_d: heavily focused on license laws and license maintenance
feedback: Virtually every real estate practitioner in the United States is subject to license requirements according to the laws of the state in which the practitioner resides and does business. Regulations vary from state to state, but license laws originate primarily from the state government level

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Real estate licensing applies to
option_a: any person over the age of 21 who provides any level of real estate services to the public
option_b: any person who performs a real estate service on behalf of another party for consideration
option_c: any property owner who wishes to sell their property
option_d: the executor of an estate when selling properties
feedback: Any person who performs a real estate service on behalf of another party for consideration must be duly licensed under state law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following parties are exempt from licensing if practicing within his or her designated profession?
feedback: Real estate license laws commonly exempt the following from licensing requirements when discharging their occupational responsibilities: employees of a property owner who are not paid on a per-transaction basis, such as an onsite rental agent in an apartment complex, employees of transient lodging facilities such as motels and hotels, managers of residential condominiums or cooperatives, dealers in cemetery lots, attorneys performing regular legal duties for a principal, persons acting in a court-appointed capacity

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: While license requirements vary from state to state, they commonly focus on which of the following?
option_a: College degree and 21 years of age or older
feedback: Requirements vary from state to state, but generally, an applicant must be of legal age and mentally competent, be of good moral character and may be required to have a high school diploma

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Most states have two or more levels of licensure for real estate brokerage. These may include
feedback: Most states have a 2-level license system for salespeople and brokers (or brokers who manage)

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The requirement to advance from a salesperson license to a broker license in most states includes
option_a: a specific amount of time as a salesperson before applying to become a broker
option_b: a minimum of 10 years in the real estate industry
option_c: a recommendation from the applicant’s broker
option_d: only a passing grade on the broker exam
feedback: Most states require an applicant for a broker’s license to have experience as an actively licensed salesperson within a certain period of time preceding the date of the application

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Every state has an entity that administers and enforces licensing regulation.  This entity is most often known as
option_a: the state’s real estate license committee
option_b: the state’s commission on consumer relations
option_d: the state’s real estate finance commission
feedback: State license laws provide for the establishment of a real estate regulatory entity to administer and enforce licensing legislation.  This entity is most often known as the state’s real estate commission

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The regulatory authority is charged with enacting enforceable guidelines with regard to the ethical and professional behavior of licensees.    These guidelines may include specific sanctions against
option_a: use of profanity when meeting with a client
option_b: fraud, negligence, failure to disclose as required, kickbacks, misleading advertising
option_c: marketing yourself instead of the client’s property
feedback: The guidelines may also include concealment, collusion, violations of fiduciary duty, practicing law in completing or interpreting contracts, mismanaging escrow funds and/or earnest money deposits, or making improper commission arrangements with clients, customers or other licensees

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The practice of being licensed in one state and obtaining a second license in another state -- without having to fulfill all the requirements existent in the second state -- is referred to as
feedback: A broker's license from one state does not confer the authority to operate in another state since a real estate license only permits practice in a given state.  For example, a broker who holds a Virginia real estate license has no authority to operate in Wyoming. However, a licensed broker in a state having reciprocity with another state may be excused from some of the licensing requirements that an unlicensed applicant has to meet in that state

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: State education and research foundations are established in order to:
option_a: carry out various real estate and research projects
option_b: assist consumers by answering questions and accepting complaints
option_c: confirm that applicants for a license have met the educational requirements
option_d: provide the various state legislatures with information regarding the passing of real estate laws
feedback: The foundation serves the real estate industry and the public by carrying out these projects

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Any state may establish recovery funds for the purpose of
option_a: paying for the licensee’s fees and costs for attorneys to defend the licensee
option_b: having a reserve account funded through license fees and fines to reimburse consumers
option_c: compensating licensees for loss when a complaint has been filed against them
option_d: allowing the licensee to recover from the investigation
feedback: The recovery fund is a reserve account funded through license fees and collected fines. The fund pays portions of a money judgment on behalf of a licensee, up to a prescribed amount

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In which risk management strategy does a party take full responsibility for consequences and enter into an activity in spite of known risks?
feedback: Retention of risk means entering into an activity in spite of known risks and taking full responsibility for the consequences

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Brokerage firms can best reduce the risks that licensees will commit errors and violate laws by
option_b: hiring only agents with law degrees
feedback: Education is the first line of defense against risk. When agents are familiar with the forms provided by the office, how and when to complete them and where to send them, the likelihood of errors is reduced. Likewise, agents need to be able to identify and understand common contract elements, complete contract forms developed by attorneys, and evaluate offers received from co-op agents on their listings without committing a license violation or breach of law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: How does documentation manage risk?
option_a: It shows the public that the office is well-managed
option_b: It serves as evidence that laws have been complied with
option_c: It relieves agents of the need to remember details
option_d: It keeps office staff too busy to interfere with transactions
feedback: Documentation provides evidence of compliance with laws and regulations. It proves what clients and customers and licensees said and did in a transaction. Some documentation is required by law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: A good way of reducing the risk that brokers and sales affiliates will commit errors or inadvertent law violations is to
option_a: establish a company-administered system of fines and penalties
option_b: maintain a comprehensive and up-to-date procedures manual
option_c: hire only licensees with at least five years’ experience
option_d: have licensees sign an agreement that they take sole responsibility for their actions
feedback: Adherence to a procedures manual reduces the risk that an individual will inadvertently commit an unlawful act

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The risk of committing an unauthorized practice of law can be practically reduced by
option_a: having customers and clients provide their own legal forms
option_b: avoiding situations that require the use of legal forms
option_c: using standard forms and contracts prepared by attorneys
option_d: having on on-staff attorney be present at the signing of any legal form
feedback: Standard forms save time and protect against the unauthorized practice of law. Since they are most often prepared by lawyers familiar with the market area, they can address contingencies that are common in the area in a manner that reflects the real estate laws of the state

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Transaction records required to be maintained typically include
feedback: Required transaction records typically include:  •listing agreements  •offers  •contracts  •closing statements  •agency agreements  •disclosure documents  •correspondence and other communication records  •notes and any other relevant information

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: What kind of coverage is provided by general liability insurance?
option_a: Coverage for any financial harm caused by a covered licensee
option_b: Coverage for a property owner’s risks incurred when the public enters the insured premises
option_c: Coverage for property damage caused by a property manager
option_d: Coverage for financial harm caused by an illegal action of a licensee
feedback: General liability insurance provides coverage for risks incurred by a property owner when the public or a licensee enters the owned property (public liability)

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Standard E & O policies exclude coverage of damages resulting from
option_b: negligent performance of a professional service
option_c: failing to carry out a company policy
feedback: A standard E&O policy does NOT cover violations of law

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Failure to maintain client confidentiality is a risk associated primarily with
feedback: The duty of confidentiality requires the agent to hold in confidence any information that would harm the client’s interests or bargaining position or anything else the client wishes to keep secret, unless the law requires disclosure. The duty of confidentiality survives the termination of the listing contract

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: The lead-based paint disclosure requirement
option_b: applies to residential properties built before 1978
option_c: applies to the seller of a property, not to the seller’s agent
option_d: is mandatory for the seller of a property but optional for the seller’s agent
feedback: Federal law requires sellers of houses built before 1978 to make a lead-based paint disclosure before accepting an offer to purchase

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: One of the major risk areas in advertising a listed property is that an advertisement will
option_a: describe the property in excessively glowing terms
option_b: fail  to appear at the same time in all available media
option_c: omit any mention of the owner’s main selling points
feedback: The license laws of most states list as illegal advertising activities subject to discipline such actions as making any substantial and intentional misrepresentation, making false promises, and making misleading or untruthful statements in any advertising

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: Which of the following actions can a real estate licensee perform without risking the commission of an unauthorized practice of law?
option_b: Fill in blanks on a lawyer-prepared contract form
option_c: Charge a fee for preparing a purchase contract
option_d: Draft an addition to a preprinted form
feedback: Licensees may fill in blanks or make deletions on a preprinted contract form prepared by a lawyer

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: In assisting a party to prepare a purchase contract, it is essential for a licensee to
option_a: use the services of an attorney
option_b: make sure that the buyer and seller do not make any changes to the preprinted form
option_c: sign the contract along with the buyer and seller
option_d: make sure the information in the contract is accurate and that all agreed terms are included
feedback: Contracts that contain incorrect information or are inadequately prepared can pose a serious liability for a licensee. To avoid such a situation, it is imperative for the contract to reflect the terms that the parties have agreed upon in the most accurate and honest manner. The agent must also be careful to comply with the letter of the real estate law. Violations can jeopardize the enforceability of a listing or sales contract, in addition to resulting in criminal prosecution

subject: B2 Agency
question_text: To minimize the risk of violating fair housing laws, a licensee should
option_a: refuse to use terms that refer to or describe any of the classes of persons protected by the laws
option_b: avoid working in neighborhoods that are predominantly occupied by a single ethnic group
option_c: make discriminatory or derogatory remarks in conversation only, never in writing
option_d: give better service to members of a protected class than is standard for other clients or customers
feedback: Before entering into a listing agreement, a licensee should explain that it is necessary to comply with fair housing laws and obtain the potential client's acknowledgment and agreement. The agent should make it clear that the agent will reject the use of terms indicating race, religion, creed, color, national origin, sex, handicap, age or familial status to describe prospective buyers.
