option_c: No, active participation in real estate transactions requires a license.
option_d: Yes, because she is being paid a salary, not commissions.
feedback: Tasks such as answering phones or making copies are considered support and don't require any licensing or course work. However, activities such as soliciting listings or providing information about a property are part of the transaction process and may be handled only by licensed real estate professionals.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A listing broker had a signed sales contract on a house. The broker then noticed a crumbling wall in the basement. The broker should inform:
option_c: both the buyer and the seller.
option_d: the buyer, seller and mortgage company.
feedback: Licensees owe honesty and disclosure of all material facts to both buyer and seller. Material facts do not need to be disclosed to mortgage companies because their primary interest in the property is value, and any material defects will already have been factored into the price before closing.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What is RESPA?
option_d: A standard for access and accommodation for persons with disabilities.
feedback: The Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) provides consumers with improved disclosures of settlement costs and to reduce the costs of closing by the elimination of referral fees and kickbacks.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which act requires that "reasonable accommodation" be made in public accommodations?
feedback: The American with Disabilities act, passed by Congress in 1990, this act requires that "reasonable accommodation" be made in public accommodations, including the workplace, for those with physical or mental disability. As a result it would require the removal of architectural barriers on some commercial properties.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Of the following, which is considered a violation of fair housing laws in regards to periodic tenancy:
option_a: A landlord requiring that each and every tenant have a good credit rating and the assurance of a steady source of income
option_b: A landlord that requires all tenants pay first, second and last month's rent in advance
option_c: The requirement of a landlord of a co-signer exclusively for tenants who are single
option_d: The requirement of a landlord that all tenants furnish references from their previous landlord
feedback: A landlord who requires a co-signor only for single tenants would be in violation of fair housing laws

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Listing a home at an inflated price requiring minority prospects to pay that price, but accepting a lower price from other prospects:
option_a: Is not a violation of fair housing law because the property is still available to minority prospects.
option_b: is illegal only if an FHA or VA loan is being used to purchase the property.
option_c: could cause the listing salesperson and broker to be liable for money damages in a fair housing suit.
option_d: is a violation of fair housing laws by the seller only.
feedback: Listing of a property at an inflated price and requiring that price for minority prospects, but accepting a lower price from other prospects could cause the listing salesperson and broker to be liable for money damages in a fair housing suit.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A developer, to avoid discrimination, instructed his sales staff: (1) Give special preference to women who want to purchase; and (2) set aside the same number of homes for each ethnic group. Which answer best describes these policies:
option_a: Both policies are discriminatory and therefore illegal
feedback: Selling properties based on gender preferences or to fill ethnic or racial quotas is discrimination.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Alicia is a licensee with Foul Real Estate Inc. She placed the following ad in a newspaper: "Big bedroom, sun deck and fireplace - $500,000. 273-1321". Which of the following would describe this ad?
feedback: Blind Advertising is any real estate advertisement that does not properly identify the broker. When advertising real property as an agent, a salesperson must always remember to include the employing broker's name in the advertisement.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Federal Fair housing law is a United States policy which:
option_a: builds housing units designed primarily for minority groups throughout the United States.
option_b: prohibits discrimination in housing for all persons throughout the United States.
option_c: eliminates prejudice wherever it exists in the United States.
option_d: guarantees separate but equal housing units for all people in all of the states.
feedback: The Federal Fair Housing Law creates a policy of providing fair housing for all persons through the United States by prohibiting discrimination based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, and disability.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In which of the following situations may a real estate licensee lawfully refuse to show a home to a minority prospect who has requested to see it?
option_a: The agent may not refuse to show any home to a minority person at any time
option_b: When the owner is out of town and has instructed the licensee that no showing may be made in his absence
option_c: When the owner is exempt under the 1968 Fair Housing law and has stated that his single-family home is not available to minority persons
option_d: When the licensee sincerely believes that showing the property to minorities would cause panic in the neighborhood
feedback: It is permitted for an agent to refuse to show a property to a minority buyer if the owner is out of town, as long as the owner left instructions that the house was not to be shown to anyone until his return. Basically, an agent may refuse to show a property to a minority prospect only if the agent is refusing to show that property to everybody, and that is only likely to happen upon owner request.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: If an advertisement is placed in a newspaper advertising a house for sale and only the APR is stated:
option_a: Number of payments must be included
option_b: Total finance charges must be included
option_c: The amount of the down payment must be included
feedback: The APR is the cost of credit that consumers pay, expressed as a simple annual percentage. If the ad only states the APR, then other disclosures are not necessary.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: RESPA would apply to which of the following?
feedback: RESPA (Real Estate Settlement Procedures ACT) applies to loans secured with a mortgage placed on one-to-four family residential dwellings. It is primarily there to provide consumers with enough information to enable them to shop for settlement services.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A land developer offers a free prize to anyone who replies to his direct mail advertisement and visits the site of his subdivision. The sub-divider requires the prize winner to attend a sales presentation at the subdivision site prior to awarding the prize. The sales presentation requirement was not mentioned in the advertisement. This kind of promotion is:
option_a: Legal if the prize is actually awarded.
option_b: Legal if the prizewinner purchases the property.
option_c: Illegal because free prizes cannot be offered under any circumstances.
option_d: Illegal because it did not disclose the sales presentation attendance requirement.
feedback: Prizes are legal provided that the advertisement states that to receive the prize requires attendance at a sales presentation. Failure to disclose attendance requirements makes it illegal.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: When a buyer gives the agent a post dated check, it:
option_a: May be retained but must be disclosed to the seller.
option_b: Must be delivered to the seller within one day.
option_c: May not be accepted by the agent.
feedback: An agent may accept a post dated check from a buyer as long as it is disclosed to the seller.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Jackson leased his home to Sullivan with a verbal agreement to sell the property to Sullivan. Jackson knows that Sullivan has been making significant improvements to the property in reliance on that verbal agreement. Jackson now declines to sell the property to Sullivan. Which of the following best describes the rights of the parties?
option_a: Jackson made an ongoing ostensible agreement and must sell the property
option_b: The doctrine of estoppel will apply in this case and Jackson will most likely have to sell
option_c: Jackson does not have to sell because of the statute of frauds
option_d: Jackson does not have to sell because verbal agreements are unenforceable
feedback: The doctrine of estoppel states that a person cannot take a position that contradicts a prior position. In other words, a person can't go back on their word, especially when others have taken action based on that word. In this situation, Jackson made a verbal agreement to sell the property and Sullivan has made improvements to the property based on that verbal agreement. If Jackson refused to sell and Sullivan took him to court over it, Sullivan would likely win the court suit based on the doctrine of estoppel. The statute of frauds is only applicable to the actual purchase agreement (not a verbal agreement to sell at some time in the future) and an ostensible agreement is implied through actions only, whereas this situation has a prior verbal agreement.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A visually-impaired person with a Seeing Eye dog makes application for rental of an apartment that has a vigorously enforced "no pets" policy in the building. According to the Federal Fair Housing Act as amended in 1988, the owner may:
option_b: require the person to house the dog outside of the apartment
option_c: require the person to restore any damage to the apartment that results from the dog
option_d: refuse to rent to the applicant as a result of the policy
feedback: The Fair Housing Act, as amended in 1988, prohibits housing discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, and national origin. It is unlawful to discriminate in any aspect of selling or renting housing or to deny a dwelling to a buyer or renter because of the disability of that individual, The Fair Housing Act requires owners of housing facilities to make reasonable exceptions in their policies and operations to afford people with disabilities equal housing opportunities. For example, a landlord with a "no pets" policy may be required to grant an exception to this rule and allow an individual who is blind to keep a guide dog in the residence. The Fair Housing Act also requires landlords to allow tenants with disabilities to make reasonable access-related modifications to their private living space, as well as to common use spaces. (The landlord is not required to pay for the changes.) The Act further requires that new multifamily housing with four or more units be designed and built to allow access for persons with disabilities. This includes accessible common use areas, doors that are wide enough for wheelchairs, kitchens and bathrooms that allow a person using a wheelchair to maneuver, and other adaptable features within the units.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Steering, which is prohibited by fair housing laws, is the practice of:
option_a: denying membership in a brokers' organization because of the applicant's race or other protected class.
option_b: inducing panic-selling because a neighborhood is changing.
option_c: subtly guiding buyers to areas that match their race or religion.
option_d: refusing to make loans in certain inner city areas.
feedback: Steering occurs when the licensee channels buyers either to or away from certain neighborhoods based on race, religion, or some other characteristic.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The owners of a house sign a listing agreement and later tells the salesperson that the garage roof leaks. If they tell the salesperson not to reveal the roof's condition to any prospective buyers, the salesperson MUST:
option_a: refer all prospective buyers who have questions about the roof's condition to the sellers
option_b: advise only those prospective buyers who ask about the roof's condition
option_c: advise the sellers that they must disclose the condition of the roof to prospective buyers
feedback: A licensee must reveal material facts in a house, such as a leaky roof or poor insulation. A failure to disclose material facts concerning a property may allow the innocent buyer to rescind the contract and possibly take legal action.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A minority couple asks a broker to show them a listed house. What is the broker guilty of if the broker suggests that they look at houses in areas with larger minority populations than that of the listed house?
feedback: Steering is a form of racial discrimination in which brokers or salespeople direct interested purchasers away from and toward certain neighborhoods to control racial composition. If an agent only shows minority buyers houses that are located in segregated areas, the agent would be guilty of Steering. An Agent should select the houses that he would show to a minority the same way that he would select houses for any other prospective buyer.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Responsibilities of the seller at closing usually include paying the:
feedback: At closing fees are incurred known as "closing costs" these Fees incurred in a real estate or mortgage transaction are paid by borrower and/or seller at the closing of the transaction. One of the fees is the commissions. The commission is paid from the proceeds of the seller. NOTE - This question, like many other you may see on the test is asking what usually, or generally happens. Commission is negotiable, and can be arranged any number of ways. It is most common in most states, and most closings that the seller pays the commission.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A homeowner who plans to travel for a year asks a real estate firm to rent the house for this period and indicates a preference for a non-minority family rather than a minority family "if at all possible." Under the Federal Fair Housing Act, the broker should inform the owner that these instructions:
option_a: will be followed because normally the house is owner-occupied.
option_b: cannot be followed because they violate the Act.
option_c: will be followed only if the owner puts them in writing.
option_d: will be followed only if the owner accepts responsibility in case of a lawsuit.
feedback: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968 (Fair Housing Act), as amended, prohibits discrimination in the sale, rental, and financing of dwellings, and in other housing-related transactions, based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status (including children under the age of 18 living with parents of legal custodians, pregnant women, and people securing custody of children under the age of 18), and handicap (disability). Real estate firms, brokers and agents are prohibited from participating in discrimination during the sale or purchase of a home.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In a real estate closing, which of the following is generally paid for by the buyer?
feedback: Recording is the act of officially recording legal instruments that affect title to real property. This is accomplished by entering them into a county book of public records, which provides notice to creditors, buyers, and all others who might have interest in the subject property. The buyer generally pays for recording a new mortgage.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In order for a property manager to determine net operating income on a property, which of the following fees is subtracted from the effective gross income?
feedback: Operating expenses are the costs associated with operating income producing property usually before interest and income tax expense, but including property taxes, insurance, repairs and maintenance, replacement reserves. This would be subtracted from the effective gross income.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Davilla is a salesperson associated with broker Brown, and is serving as a buyer's agent for Sam. After Sam has bought a house, Davilla can expect payment of a commission from:
option_a: either Sam or the seller, depending on how the sales agreement reads.
feedback: A salesperson may never accept payment from anyone except his or her own supervising broker. In this case, Davilla is a salesperson associated with broker Brown so her commission will come from Brown.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibits discrimination in real estate based on:
feedback: The law that followed the close of the Civil War extended equal rights in real property to members of all races.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The IRS requires that an independent contractor be:
option_a: Strictly regulated in the matter of attendance at sales meetings.
option_c: Paid on the basis of successful transactions rather than hours worked.
option_d: Allowed at least one week of paid vacation annually.
feedback: Yes, the IRS requires that an independent contractor be paid on the basis of successful transactions rather than hours worked.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Real estate agents are licensed by:
option_d: The United States Attorney General's office.
feedback: Each state sets its own requirements for real estate licensing.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibits discrimination regarding real property and provides:
option_b: Some exceptions for the conveyance of real property only.
feedback: The fact that no exceptions are provided for discrimination based on color is the most important aspect of this act, as many later laws that prohibit discrimination do include specific exceptions. The Act specifically states “All citizens of the United States shall have the same right in every state and territory as is enjoyed by white citizens thereof to inherit, purchase, lease, sell, hold, and convey real and personal property.”

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: When presenting offers to the seller, the agent should always be:
feedback: In contract negotiations, the agent should always be diligent and accountable in trying to bring the parties together.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What type of insurance protects real estate brokers against claims of inadequate work or negligent actions?
feedback: Many brokers and salespersons carry errors and omission insurance to cover their real estate practice. This is also called Professional Liability Insurance and is a broker's equivalent to medical malpractice insurance.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibits:
option_c: discrimination against intellectually disabled persons, specific religious groups, and race.
feedback: Discrimination based upon race was made illegal in 1866 when Congress passed the Civil Rights Act. Later versions of the Civil Rights Act (1964, 1968, and the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990) added protections for other classes of US citizens.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What is a person called who has been given the right to sign agreements or contracts on behalf of another?
feedback: An "attorney in fact" acts on another's behalf in certain defined circumstances and does not need to be an actual attorney.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Fair Housing laws are not applicable in EVERY real estate transaction. If an owner chooses to discriminate in housing, he or she must do which of the following?
option_a: The owner must NOT use a real estate professional to conduct the transaction & The owner must NOT discriminate in advertising the property.
option_b: The owner must NOT discriminate in advertising the property.
option_c: None of the other options are correct
option_d: The owner must NOT use a real estate professional to conduct the transaction.
feedback: Homeowners wishing to discriminate in a real estate transaction cannot use a real estate professional, and cannot discriminate in advertising.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following are covered under the Fair Housing Act?
option_a: All of the other options are covered under the Fair Housing Act.
option_b: Vacant land intended for residential use
feedback: Condominiums and cooperatives, rental mobile homes and their sites, and boarding houses and dormitories also fall under the Fair Housing Act.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: If a contract for sale provides that the owner is selling his home in an "as-is" condition, then:
option_a: The broker must assist the buyer in making a thorough inspection.
option_b: The owner is generally immune from liability.
option_c: Known latent defects must still be disclosed to the purchaser.
feedback: By definition, latent defects are not obvious to the untrained observer. If a seller is aware of any such defects, they must be disclosed so that potential buyers know exactly what "as is" constitutes for this particular property.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following actions by a salesperson would be grounds for the real estate commission to revoke his or her salesperson's license?
option_a: entering into an exclusive-listing contract with a seller
option_b: depositing a buyer's earnest money into a personal bank account
option_c: showing a property listed with a broker with whom the salesperson has no affiliation
feedback: A salesperson may not place funds into a personal bank account. Since salespersons are not authorized to have brokerage trust accounts, the real estate commission does have the power to revoke a salesperson's license for depositing a buyer's earnest money into a personal account.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In this telephone directory listing, "Mr. James Lowe, Real Estate Salesperson, Residential Property My Specialty," which of the following pieces of information would also be required?
option_b: the name of his employing broker
option_c: the expiration date of his license
feedback: This listing must also include the name of the employing broker.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following is true regarding a real estate conservation area?
option_a: in order to conserve green space and natural settings, development is limited
option_b: trees larger than six inches in diameter may not be removed without approval from the conservation committee
option_c: certain real estate activities are prohibited in order to maintain the stability of an area
option_d: conservation buffer zones have been developed
feedback: A real estate conservation area is one where specific real estate activities are prohibited, such as no "For Sale" signs, in order to stabilize the neighborhood.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A buyer defaulted several months ago on a written contract to purchase a seller's real estate. The seller can still sue for damages, unless he is prohibited from doing so by the:
feedback: Virtually all avenues of legal recourse, including suits for breaking a contract, have "statutes of limitation". A statute of limitation is a time limits for taking legal action against another person or entity.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A successful property manager would NOT:
option_a: appease their tenants by repairing tenant-owned equipment.
option_b: study rental rates in the area.
option_c: screen the tenants' ability to pay.
option_d: consider the type of business the tenant has.
feedback: Property managers represent the owner's interests, not the tenants. Although they should certainly make residents feel welcome, repairing their personal property takes "customer service" too far.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A lease agreement is signed by a lessee who is sixteen years of age. Which of the following is true?
option_a: A sixteen-year-old person cannot sign a lease
option_d: The lease agreement is valid provided the security deposit is increased
feedback: Although minors may sign contracts, they are not legally enforceable. Courts will generally allow minors the option of continuing with performance of the contract or voiding the contract, making it "voidable" by the minor.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: RESPA would prohibit which of the following acts?
feedback: RESPA is concerned with educating consumers about the true costs of borrowing and standardizing lending practices and closing practices. As such, referral fees (or "kickbacks") are prohibited between the different entities involved in the home loan process.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In most states, by paying the debt after a foreclosure sale, the mortgagor has the right to regain the property. What is this right called?
feedback: In most states, after a foreclosure sale, the mortgagor (the person who took out the mortgage) may have the right to regain the property by paying off the debt, including any additional costs and interest. This right is known as the "statutory right of redemption." It is defined by state law rather than arising out of the mortgage contract itself (which makes it "statutory"). The statutory right of redemption allows the original property owner a specified amount of time after the foreclosure sale to redeem the property by paying the sale price and any other charges. The duration of the redemption period varies by state, and not all states offer this right. This right exists to protect homeowners from losing their property for less than its fair market value and to provide an additional opportunity to retain their property even after foreclosure. Equitable Right of Redemption allows the mortgagor (the borrower) to redeem the property before the foreclosure sale takes place. It is based on equitable principles, meaning it's recognized as a fair opportunity for the borrower to keep their property by paying off the full amount owed, including any interest and fees, up until the time of the foreclosure auction or sale. The equitable right of redemption exists in virtually all states and is available up until the foreclosure sale. The purpose is to give the borrower a last chance to save their property by settling their debts.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In most states, by paying the debt after a foreclosure sale, the mortgagor has the right to regain the property. What is this right called?
feedback: This right of redemption is called "statutory" because it is legally mandated by law, as opposed to a custom lenders could follow or not at their option. The statutory right of redemption is applicable after a foreclosure sale. An equitable right of redemption is applicable before a foreclosure sale. NOTE -Not all states offer statutory or equitable rights of redemption, but it is still important to understand what they are.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The lender is required, under TRID, to provide a detailed Loan Estimate at the time of loan application or within three business days to:
option_a: neither the buyer nor the seller.
feedback: When a homebuyer applies for a home loan, the TILA/RESPA Integrated Disclosure rule requires the lender to provide a Loan Estimate to the buyer within 3 days of receipt of the loan application, and all revisions have to be delivered at least 7 days before closing. The loan estimate details the loan terms, mortgage payments, costs at closing and other costs and fees related to the loan, many of which cannot change.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The maximum permissible "loan to value ratios" are:
option_a: based on the banker's competitive market analysis.
option_b: fixed by law for conventional loans.
option_c: not determined by federal statute in the case of FHA loans.
option_d: based on sale price or appraised value, whichever is lower.
feedback: Although various banks, mortgage companies and programs have different LTVs, the lower, more conservative number is used.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: An owner advertised "beautiful acreage; only $5,000 down; owner will personally finance down payment." Would this be in violation of the Truth in Lending Act?
option_a: No, because owners are not covered by Regulation Z
option_b: Yes, since a down payment was stated
option_c: No, because brokers can advertise the down payment
option_d: Yes, because acreage is not exempt from Regulation Z
feedback: Regulation Z requires disclosure of all financing terms and conditions if a low interest rate, down payment or other enticement is featured in an ad. This does not apply in this case, however, because Regulation Z applies only to institutions, not individuals selling their own property.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A written purchase agreement has been signed by both a buyer and a seller. The Uniform Vendor and Purchaser Risk Act states that until the buyer has either possession of or title to a property, responsibility for the physical condition of the property:
option_a: is assumed by the seller's homeowner's insurance company
option_b: is delegated to the buyer, who upon signing has equitable title to the property
option_d: is given to a third party
feedback: Responsibility for hazards or damage remains with the seller until the buyer has possession or title.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A written purchase agreement has been signed by both a buyer and a seller. The Uniform Vendor and Purchaser Risk Act states that until the buyer has either possession of or title to a property, responsibility for the physical condition of the property:
option_a: Is given to a third party.
option_b: Is assumed by the seller's homeowner's insurance company.
option_d: Is delegated to the buyer, who upon signing has equitable title to the property.
feedback: When a contract for sale is ratified, by virtue of equitable and insurable title being conveyed to the buyer, responsibility for any hazard damage to the property rests with the seller. This is only relevant to states who have adopted the Uniform Vendor and Purchaser Risk Act.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What is redlining?
option_a: When a lending institution refuses a loan solely on sound economic grounds
option_b: The practice of lining out portions of mortgage documents so that the document becomes invalid
option_c: When a lending institution does not place a branch office in a neighborhood for reasons other than economic impact
option_d: The practice of refusing to make mortgage loans or issue insurance policies in specific areas for reasons other than the economic qualifications of the applicants
feedback: Redlining is the practice of refusing to make mortgage loans or issue insurance policies in specific areas for reasons other than the economic qualifications of the applicants.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What is steering?
option_a: Channeling of prospective homebuyers to or away from particular neighborhoods, thereby limiting their choices
option_b: All of the other options are correct
option_c: The practice of driving prospective home buyers to see homes for sale
option_d: The practice of guiding a prospective home buyer to look at homes within his or her price range
feedback: Steering is the assumption and channeling of prospective home seekers to or away from certain neighborhoods or properties thereby limiting their choices.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The federal law requiring unrestricted access to public accommodations is the:
feedback: The Americans with Disabilities Act gives civil rights protection to individuals with disabilities, similar to those provided to individuals on the basis of race, color, sex, national origin, age, and religion. It guarantees equal opportunity for individuals with disabilities in public accommodations, employment, transportation, state and local government services, and telecommunications services.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Repair or remodeling of a property generally adds to its worth. In recognition of the role contractors and suppliers play in increasing the value of a property, in many states they are assured payment for their goods and services by being able to file a:
feedback: Contractors and others are permitted to file a mechanic's lien against the improved property. A mechanics lien is a statutory remedy for a contractor, subcontractor, or supplier of labor, services or materials when it has performed work but not received compensation for the work. In that instance, the contractor or subcontractor may file a mechanics lien against the property where he performed the work.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In order to reach the beach, the Jones family has a permanent right of way across their neighbor's property. They most likely own:
feedback: An easement appurtenant allows an owner a particular use of an adjoining parcel's land and is legally permanent until terminated. A license could be revoked at any time (so it's not permanent) and the other answers don't apply.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Charge for the preparation of a settlement statement under the Uniform Settlement Act is:
feedback: There is no charge for the preparation of the settlement statement.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In the practice of real estate, what is the common term used if a licensee brags that a property located near a large park is "in the best part of town"?
feedback: Puffing is considered making extravagant claims about the good points of real property and is based on opinion rather than fact. As long as the statement is not an outright lie (or a Fair Housing violation, etc.), puffing is not illegal.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Several brokerage firms were accused of violating antitrust laws. Of the following, they were MOST likely accused of:
option_c: not having an equal housing opportunity sign in an office window.
feedback: Antitrust violations are violations of laws designed to protect trade and commerce from abusive practices such as price-fixing, restraints, price discrimination, and monopolization. The principal federal antitrust laws come from the Sherman Act.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Usury MOST nearly means:
option_a: lending money at fluctuating interest rates.
option_c: making loans without the benefit of co-signors.
feedback: Each state sets its own ceiling for the maximum interest rate lenders may charge. Rates above that ceiling are considered usurious and illegal. No reputable lender exceeds those rates and those that make a practice of it are commonly known as loan sharks.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The illegal practice of directing minority buyers to areas populated by the same race or religion is called:
feedback: "Steering" happens when agents "steer" buyers towards particular neighborhoods. Blockbusting and panic peddling are on the opposite side of the same coin. They refer to agents coercing sellers to "panic-sell" their homes because a minority group is moving into the neighborhood.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: When a principal or property manager signs a contract to manage an owner's property he becomes a
feedback: A fiduciary is a person who holds a legal or ethical relationship of trust with one or more other parties. Typically, a fiduciary prudently takes care of money or other assets for another person.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A lease for an apartment in a residential apartment project is usually which type of lease?
feedback: A gross lease is a type of lease where the tenant pays a flat rental amount, and the landlord pays for all property charges regularly incurred by the ownership. Most apartment leases resemble gross leases.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Equitable title is transferred to the buyer upon the execution of which of the following documents?
feedback: An installment contract is between the buyer and the seller of real property, in which the seller provides the buyer financing in the purchase, and the buyer repays the resulting loan in installments.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The right of a tenant to use leased premises is called
feedback: A leasehold estate is an ownership of a temporary right to hold land or property in which a lessee or a tenant holds rights of real property by some form of title from a lessor or landlord. Although a tenant does hold rights to real property, a leasehold estate is typically considered personal property.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The owner of real property who leases it to another is called
feedback: a lessor is a person who leases or lets a property to another; a landlord.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A listing broker receives his authority to accept an earnest money deposit in the
feedback: A listing broker receives his authority to accept an earnest money deposit in the listing. Earnest money is a deposit made to a seller that represents a buyer's good faith to buy a home. The money serves as a “promise” to the home seller and gives the buyer extra time to get financing and conduct the title search, property appraisal and inspections before closing.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Federal anti-discriminatory laws apply to which of the following?
option_b: A broker finding a buyer for a single-family home
option_c: A private club not open to the general public
feedback: Civil rights laws apply to owners of residential property, rental units, hotels and virtually any other building offering housing or accommodations to the general public. A broker finding a buyer for a single-family home is subject to anti-discrimination laws, but private clubs, commercial real estate, and industrial rentals are not.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following is true regarding condominiums?
option_a: A condominium unit may be mortgaged like any other parcel of real estate
option_b: If one unit owner defaults on property taxes, those taxes will be owed by the remaining unit owners
option_c: The covenants and restrictions define the property taxes to be paid by the condo community
option_d: If a unit owner does not pay the monthly maintenance fee, the entire community will be foreclosed
feedback: A mortgage is a debt instrument, secured by the collateral of specified real estate property, that the borrower is obliged to pay back with a predetermined set of payments. Condominiums units are treated like any other real property and can be mortgaged like a house.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a strict legal term for a type of employment contract or listing?
feedback: A multiple listing service (MLS, also multiple listing system or multiple listings service) is a suite of services that real estate brokers use to establish contractual offers of compensation (among brokers) and accumulate and disseminate information to enable appraisals. This isn't an employment contract, but listing agreements are.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A developer built a six-story structure. Several years later, an ordinance was passed in that area banning any building six stories or higher. This building is a
option_b: situation in which the structure would have to be demolished.
feedback: A nonconforming use is a use of property that was allowed under the zoning regulations at the time the use was established but which, because of subsequent changes in those regulations, is no longer a permitted use. ... State law does not regulate nonconforming uses, structures, or lots.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A percentage lease is calculated on a percentage of which of the following?
feedback: A percentage lease is a type of lease where the tenant pays a base rent plus a percentage of any revenue earned while doing business on the rental premises. It is a term mostly used in commercial real estate.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following statements regarding counteroffers is true?
option_a: A counter offer is not binding if either party wants to return to the original offer.
option_b: Both the buyer and the seller are allowed to write two different counter offers during negotiations or the entire transaction will become void.
option_c: If there is a slight change, such as a change in the closing date, it is not considered a counter offer.
option_d: A counter offer is a rejection of the first offer and becomes the new offer.
feedback: A real estate counteroffer is generated by a home seller after a buyer has submitted an offer to purchase and the offer includes terms that aren't agreeable. Typically, a counteroffer states that the seller has accepted the buyer's offer subject to one or more changes.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: "Reversion" best describes the property interest of a:
option_b: beneficiary under a deed of trust.
feedback: In property law, "reversion" is a future interest retained by the grantor after the conveyance of a lesser estate to another person or entity, such as the owner of a fee simple granting a life estate or a leasehold estate.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A man had a one-year lease on a residence which expired July 31, 2019. He stayed on after that date without the consent of the landlord and did not pay any rent. He now holds:
feedback: A tenancy at sufferance is the situation when a tenant of real estate continues to occupy the premises without the landlord's consent after the original lease or rental agreement between the landlord and the tenant has expired. A tenant in this situation is also referred to a holdover tenant.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A lessee assigns or sells and transfers total interest in the leasehold to a third party. The third party becomes a:
feedback: The new third party took over whatever previous interest the lessee had.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A court order to a properly designated individual to sell property in order to satisfy a judgment is called:
feedback: A writ of execution is a court order granted to put in force a judgment of possession obtained by a plaintiff from a court. When issuing a writ of execution, a court typically will order a sheriff or other similar official to take possession of property owned by a judgment debtor.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Statements regarding which of the following are prohibited under anti-discrimination laws?
option_b: Asking whether or not a person is planning to have a larger family
option_d: All of the other options are correct
feedback: All of the given options are prohibited by anti-discrimination laws.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibits discrimination in housing based on which of the following reasons?
feedback: Although surprising to many, the original civil rights legislation was passed in 1866 by one vote, over the veto of President Andrew Johnson. It prohibited discrimination based on race, although later amendments added protections for religion, sex, and marital status.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Civil Rights Act of 1866 prohibits discrimination in housing based on which of the following reasons?
feedback: Although surprising to many, the original civil rights legislation was passed in 1866 by one vote, over the veto of President Andrew Johnson. It prohibited discrimination based on race, although later amendments added protections for religion, sex, and marital status.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In a real estate sales transaction, the amount of the earnest money deposit is determined by
option_b: a minimum of 5% of sales price.
feedback: An earnest payment is a specific form of security deposit made in some major transactions such as real estate dealings or required by some official procurement processes to demonstrate that the applicant is serious and willing to demonstrate an earnest of good faith about wanting to complete the transaction

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following best describes conversion?
option_a: Accepting and depositing money into a clients' trust account
option_b: Using funds from a business account for personal use
option_c: Adding personal funds into a business account
option_d: Using funds from a clients' trust account for personal use
feedback: Conversion is the act of using your clients money for personal use.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A buyer properly revoked his offer after receiving the property condition disclosure and requests the return of his earnest money. The broker:
option_a: is required to hold onto the earnest money until a legal authority intervenes
option_b: can transfer the earnest money to the seller as damages for contract termination
option_c: may, with the seller's permission, deduct part of his commission from the earnest money.
option_d: must promptly return the earnest money.
feedback: When a buyer properly revokes their offer after receiving the property condition disclosure, according to most real estate purchase agreements and state laws, they are typically entitled to a full refund of their earnest money. The broker handling the earnest money, in this scenario, has the obligation to promptly return these funds to the buyer without unnecessary delay. This is because the earnest money is meant to be a good faith deposit to secure the transaction, not a payment that the buyer forfeits improperly. Since the revocation of the offer was proper and presumably allowed under the terms of the agreement for reasons such as unsatisfactory property condition disclosures, there is neither a legal nor ethical basis for withholding or deducting from the earnest money deposit.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: An exclusive agency listing guarantees the broker his/her commission:
option_a: only if the sales price exceeds the listed price.
option_b: if the property changes ownership for any reason.
option_c: if the broker is the procuring cause of the buyer of the property.
option_d: if the seller sells the property.
feedback: An Exclusive Agency Listing is an agreement between a seller and a real estate firm or agent granting them the right to be the only firm or agent to market and sell a property. An exclusive agency listing guarantees the broker a commission if any licensee procures a buyer- that is why it's called an Exclusive Agency listing. If a licensee from another agency finds a buyer, the holder of the Exclusive Agency listing is still owed a commission. However, the seller retains the right to find their own buyer without having to pay a commission. NOTE - This is different than an Exclusive Authorization and Right-to-Sell listing, which guarantees the broker a commission if anyone (including the seller) finds a buyer during the terms of the listing agreement.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The form of listing agreement that provides the most benefit and protection to a broker is the:
feedback: An exclusive right to sell listing is a formal agreement under which a real estate broker has the sole right to sell a specified property. For example, even if an owner sells a property herself, an exclusive right to sell listing would guarantee her broker a commission.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What records are found in a profit and loss statement?
option_a: The amount of sales per broker
option_b: A record of both fixed and variable operating expenses
option_c: The number of failed sales in the company
feedback: An income statement or profit and loss account is one of the financial statements of a company and shows the company’s revenues and expenses during a particular period. It indicates how the revenues are transformed into the net income. The expenses included in the report can be both fixed (like taxes, insurance, maintenance) and variable (like unexpected repairs or monthly ulitities).

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Mr. and Mrs. Thomas Martin want to sell their house. They want a broker to show the property, but they would like to sell the house themselves without the obligation of paying a broker a commission. What form of listing would allow them to use the services of several brokers and not pay a commission if they sell the house themselves?
feedback: An open listing lets an owner sell her home by herself as a "For Sale By Owner." It is a non-exclusive agreement, meaning the owner may execute open listings with more than one real estate broker and pay only the broker who brings an able buyer whose offer the owner accepts.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A tenant complained to HUD about his landlord's discriminatory practices in his/her building. A week later the landlord gave the tenant an eviction notice. Under which of the following situations would the Federal Fair Housing Act be violated?
option_a: when the tenant is conducting an illegal use on the premises
option_b: when the tenant is two months behind in his/her rent
option_c: when the tenant has damaged the premises
option_d: when the landlord evicts the tenant for reporting him to HUD
feedback: Anti-discrimination laws do not apply to situations that are in violation of generally accepted policies such as paying rent on time, maintaining the premises and abiding by use agreements.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of these is legally required in a real estate brokerage?
option_a: Displaying the RESPA Lending Discrimination poster in a brokerage
option_b: Displaying the TILA APR Explanation poster in a brokerage
option_c: Displaying an explanation of dual agency in a brokerage
option_d: Displaying the Equal Housing Opportunity poster in a brokerage
feedback: Any business, real estate broker, agent or person in the real estate business must post the HUD Equal Housing Opportunity Poster in accordance with the Federal Fair Housing Act. It contains legal definitions of discrimination in real estate. Not posting it may be seen as evidence of a discriminatory housing practice.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Blockbusting occurs when
option_a: one lending institution buys all the houses on a block.
option_b: a lending institution does not make loans in a certain part of the city.
option_c: a broker shows houses to prospective buyers only in certain areas.
option_d: the broker uses racial fears to prompt people in a changing neighborhood to sell their homes.
feedback: Blockbusting is the practice of persuading owners to sell property cheaply because of the fear of people of another race or class moving into the neighbourhood, and then profiting by reselling at a higher price.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following are listed in a profit and loss statement?
option_b: The annual gross sales of each broker
option_c: The profit from an advertising campaign
option_d: Items that make a profit and those causing a loss
feedback: Both fixed and variable operating expenses are listed in a profit and loss statement. An income statement, or profit and loss statement, is one of several financial statements a company might have. It shows the company’s revenues and expenses during a particular period to indicate how the revenues are transformed into net income. While a profit and loss statement might reflect the overall revenue and expenses associated with advertising, it does not break down the profit from specific advertising campaigns. The annual gross sales of each broker is too specific and would not typically appear on a company-wide profit and loss statement. Such information might be part of internal sales performance reports but not on the profit and loss statement itself. While a profit and loss statement does show revenue (indicative of profitable activities) and expenses (which could be seen as causing a loss if revenues do not exceed them), it does not specifically list items as "making a profit" or "causing a loss." Instead, it categorizes all revenues and expenses broadly to calculate net profit or loss.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Within months of buying their home, the Jackson family learned that the roof leaked during heavy rains. When the house was listed with the Bankers Agency, the seller had told the Bankers' broker that the roof leaked, but Bankers claims the Jackson's did not ask about the roof. What actions can be taken in this situation?
option_a: The buyer cannot do anything because this fact should have been discovered during home inspection
option_b: The buyer can sue the broker for non-disclosure
option_c: The buyer can sue the seller under the license law
option_d: The buyer cannot sue the broker under the license law
feedback: Disclosure IS required by license law, thus the Jacksons can sue the broker for nondisclosure. The broker is required by law to disclose any fact that would materially affect the buyer's decision to make an offer, such as a leaky roof.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Part of the consideration offered to a seller when the buyer signs an agreement of sale is called:
feedback: Earnest money is a deposit made to a seller that represents a buyer's good faith to buy a home. The money serves as a “promise” to the home seller and gives the buyer extra time to get financing and conduct the title search, property appraisal and inspections before closing. It is commonly transferred to the seller as part of the overall consideration for the home, but this can vary state-to-state.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: An agent working as a subagent of the seller would suggest that the buyer hire an inspector from an outside service in all of the following cases EXCEPT:
option_a: when there is a slow drain in the toilet.
option_b: when there is sawdust in the kitchen cabinets.
option_c: when they smell gas in the basement.
option_d: when a hinge is off the door.
feedback: Home inspectors are hired to find significant and often hidden property defects, such as signs of a leaking roof, termites (which leave behind sawdust), foundation cracking and so forth. Hinges and other "wear and tear" items are obvious and not among the reasons for hiring an inspector.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: An aggrieved party with a Fair Housing violation claim has how long to file a complaint with the Department of Housing and Urban Development?
feedback: If the complaint is not filed within one year, a person may still file a civil suit in a Federal Court.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: In a usual listing agreement, the broker is authorized to:
option_b: find a purchaser and accept a deposit.
option_c: promise the buyer that any offer on the listed terms will be accepted.
option_d: find a purchaser and bind his client to a contract.
feedback: In a usual listing agreement, the broker is authorized to find a purchaser and accept an earnest money deposit. Earnest money is a deposit made to a seller indicating the buyer's good faith in an arrangement. Often used in real estate transactions, earnest money allows the buyer additional time when seeking financing. Earnest money is typically held jointly by the seller and buyer in a trust or escrow account.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Federal Fair Housing Act states that a prima facie (at first view) case against a broker for discrimination be established after a complaint has been received because the broker has failed to do which of the following?
option_a: The broker has failed to join the HUD anti-discriminatory task force
option_b: The broker has failed to display a fair housing poster
option_c: The broker has failed to join an affirmative marketing program
option_d: The broker has failed to attend classes on fair housing
feedback:
Fair Housing regulations require that a HUD fair housing poster be prominently displayed in brokerages.
Reference: https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-24/part-110

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The illegal process of a banker refusing to approve loans for a neighborhood based on the racial composition of the area is:
feedback: Loans may only be approved or denied on the basis of whether a specific individual and property meet established standards. Thus lenders are well within their rights to deny a loan to a particular person because he or she lacks sufficient income or has poor credit. Additionally, a loan for a partially completed home or one that doesn't meet code can also be denied. However, "macro" issues such as race or neighborhood cannot be considered.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Miller, a licensed principal real estate broker, was sued for conversion. This means Miller allegedly did which of the following?
option_a: Failed to reconcile his clients' trust account in a timely manner
option_b: Failed to maintain a positive balance in his clients' trust account
option_d: Commingled the client's trust account funds with his own
feedback: Conversion (or misappropriation) is the intentional, illegal use of the property or funds of another person for one's own use or other unauthorized purpose.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Discrimination is prohibited in lending practices under which of these acts?
feedback: Passed in 1992, the Equal Credit Opportunity Act prohibits a broad spectrum of discriminatory lending practices, including the granting or denial of credit or the costs associated with borrowing based on race, gender, marital status, source of income (e.g., public assistance) and other factors.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Statements by a real estate licensee that somewhat exaggerate the intangibles of a property without committing fraud are called:
feedback: Puffing is generally an expression or exaggeration made by a salesperson or found in an advertisement that concerns the quality of goods offered for sale. It presents opinions rather than facts and is usually not considered a legally binding promise.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A broker was found guilty of steering. What did the broker do?
option_a: Before showing properties, she asked the buyers what areas they wanted to see properties in
option_b: She told the buyers what price to offer
option_c: She insisted on driving the buyers to each property shown
option_d: She showed buyers properties in a predominately minority community because the buyers were of the same race
feedback: Racial steering refers to the practice of advising customers to purchase homes in particular neighborhoods on the basis of race — the broker "steers" the buyer towards a certain neighborhood based on their race. Fair housing laws prohibit steering and other discriminatory practices.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A second offer made on a property prior to the seller's decision on the first offer should be:
option_a: held pending a decision on the first offer.
option_c: returned to the offeror if it does not provide better terms than the first offer.
feedback: The agent/broker owes the seller fiduciary responsibilities of loyalty and reasonable care (among others). To fulfill these responsibilities, all reasonable offers must be submitted to the seller as soon as possible to allow the seller to make the best possible decision in the sale of the property.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A broker was found guilty of steering. What did the broker do?
option_a: She told the buyers what price to offer
option_b: Before showing properties, she asked the buyers in what areas they wanted to see properties
option_c: She insisted on driving the buyers to each property shown
option_d: Without asking, she showed properties in a predominately minority community because the buyers were of the same race
feedback: Racial steering refers to the practice of advising customers to purchase homes in particular neighborhoods on the basis of race- the broker "steers" the buyer towards a certain neighborhood based on their race. Fair housing laws prohibit steering and other discriminatory practices.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A real estate licensee only shows minority clients properties in several traditionally minority neighborhoods. She bases this practice on the need to preserve the valuable cultural integrity of immigrant communities. Which of these statements is TRUE?
option_a: The licensee’s policy is steering, but it does not violate the fair housing laws because she is motivated by cultural preservation, not by exclusion or discrimination
option_b: The licensee’s policy has the effect, but not the intent, of steering
option_c: Because the licensee is not attempting to restrict the rights of any single minority group, the practice does not constitute steering
option_d: The licensee’s policy is steering and violates the fair housing laws regardless of her motivation
feedback: Racial steering refers to the practice in which real estate brokers guide prospective home buyers towards or away from certain neighborhoods based on their race. The use of race for ANY reason as a qualifying factor when determining what neighborhoods to show a prospective buyer would be in violation of fair housing laws.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which activity requires a real estate license?
option_a: A service that, for a fee, matches individuals who want to buy and sell properties
option_b: A resident manager who collects rent on behalf of a building owner
option_c: An executor selling a decedent’s building
option_d: A homeowner selling their own home
feedback: Real estate licenses, authorizations issued by state governments, give agents and brokers the legal ability to represent a home seller or buyer in the process of buying or selling real estate.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A provision in an offer to purchase which requires the completion of a certain act before the contract becomes binding is known as a:
feedback: Sometimes a contingency clause is attached to an offer to purchase real estate and included in the real estate contract. Essentially, a contingency clause gives parties the right to back out of the contract under certain circumstances that must be negotiated between the buyer and seller. A common contingency clause might pledge to purchase the house "contingent" on obtaining financing.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: When planning a subdivision, a developer should determine the kinds of land uses to be involved and the amounts of land to be allocated to each use by considering:
option_a: which are the most profitable types of buildings to construct.
option_b: what he considers an ideal development.
option_c: the comprehensive plan of the local government.
option_d: the customs of the area and what other developers have already done.
feedback: A subdivision is the act of dividing land into pieces that are easier to sell or otherwise develop, usually via a plat. The most effective way to determine land use and the amount of land to allocate is to consult the local government or municpality's comprehensive plan.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A mobility impaired person was renting a unit in an apartment complex. Half the units had been assigned parking spaces near the door, while the other half had not. The owner:
option_a: must give a parking space near the door to the handicapped person, if one is available and a need is demonstrated.
option_b: must allow the handicapped person to live there for a month and if a space becomes available during that time, give the parking space to the handicapped person.
option_c: may charge extra money to the handicapped person for providing the parking space near the door.
option_d: must take a vote of all tenants to see if they want to allow the handicapped person a parking space.
feedback: The "equal access" aspects of fair housing legislation do not necessarily mean equal treatment. "Reasonable accommodation" must also be made to meet the needs of handicapped people, including exceptions to standard policies such as convenient parking and guide dogs.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The balance sheet for a real estate office would show:
option_a: the firm's assets, liabilities and net worth.
option_b: the balance the firm will retain after paying commissions or transactions currently in escrow.
option_c: net earnings for the firm for the year after taxes.
option_d: the balance that should be in the firm's business checking account after the monthly bank statement reconciliation.
feedback: The balance sheet for a real estate office would show the firm's assets, liabilities and net worth.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A seller engages a broker to find a buyer ready, willing and able to purchase a seller's parcel of real property. The seller executes and delivers to the broker an exclusive right-to-sell employment contract. A few weeks prior to the expiration date of the listing, the seller decides to revoke the contract before any sale occurred and prior to the broker finding a buyer ready, willing and able to buy the property. Which of the following statements is most likely to apply in this situation?
option_a: The owner can revoke his contract but could be liable for damages
option_b: The owner can revoke his contract and will not be liable for damages to the broker
option_c: The owner could not revoke his contract because it is irrevocable
option_d: The owner would have to sell the real property at the listed price if the broker found a buyer ready, willing and able to buy at this price
feedback: An owner can revoke an exclusive right-to-sell listing, but this could be considered "breach of contract" by a court. If the property is sold before the original contract would have ended, the owner can still be liable for damages.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: A seller refuses to provide a seller's property disclosure to the buyer. What could the listing agent do in this situation?
option_a: Offer to provide a filled out disclosure to the best of their ability.
option_b: Provide the buyer with a disclaimer without the seller's signature.
option_c: Provide the buyer with a disclaimer signed by the supervising principal broker.
option_d: Provide the buyer with a disclaimer signed by the seller.
feedback:
A seller disclosure is a document completed by the seller that lists any known issues and "material facts" known about the property during the time they owned the home. In most states, the seller is required to provide this disclosure within a few days of mutual acceptance.

NOTE - There is only one action provided as an answer that could be taken by the listing agent, which is to get the seller to sign a disclaimer and deliver that to the buyer.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Federal regulations on unsolicited email:
option_a: require commercial emails to include a physical address for the sender.
option_b: require prior permission of recipients in order to send email to them.
option_c: exempt phone calls to individuals with whom the office has a prior business relationship.
option_d: require that email lists be scrubbed every 31 days.
feedback:
The CAN-SPAM Act, a law that sets the rules for commercial email, establishes requirements for commercial messages- for example, they must include a physical address for the sender. It also gives recipients the right to have you stop emailing them and spells out tough penalties for violations.

The CAN-SPAM Act covers all commercial messages, which the law defines as “any electronic mail message the primary purpose of which is the commercial advertisement or promotion of a commercial product or service,” including email that promotes content on commercial websites. The law makes no exception for business-to-business email. That means all email - for example, a message to former customers announcing a new product line - must comply with the law.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The Fair Housing Act of 1968 prohibits discrimination based on:
feedback: The Civil Rights Act of 1968 defines housing discrimination as the "refusal to sell or rent a dwelling to any person because of his race, color, religion, or national origin". Title VIII of this Act is commonly referred to as the Fair Housing Act of 1968. Gender, familial status, and disability were added to the list of identities protected by the Fair Housing Act through amendments in 1974 and 1988.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968, also known as the Federal Fair Housing Act, prohibits discrimination in which of the following categories?
option_a: The sale of homes, home loan lending, and title insurance
option_b: Title insurance, home loan lending, and selling single family homes
option_c: The sale of homes and home loan lending
option_d: Escrow closing and title insurance, and selling single family residences
feedback:
The Civil Rights Act of 1968 defines housing discrimination as the "refusal to sell or rent a dwelling to any person because of his race, color, religion, or national origin". Title VIII of this Act is commonly referred to as the Fair Housing Act of 1968, and it specifically prohibited discrimination concerning the sale, rental and financing of housing based on race, religion, national origin. Gender, familial status, and disability were added to the list of identities protected by the Fair Housing Act through amendments in 1974 and 1988.

Whatever you do, remember the year 1968 when taking the real estate exam!

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968, also known as the Federal Fair Housing Act, prohibit discrimination in which of the following?
option_a: The sales and lending of residential properties
option_b: The leasing of commercial and industrial properties
option_c: The sales, leasing and lending of commercial and industrial properties
option_d: The sales, leasing and lending of residential properties
feedback:
The Civil Rights Act of 1968 defines housing discrimination as the "refusal to sell or rent a dwelling to any person because of his race, color, religion, or national origin". Title VIII of this Act is commonly referred to as the Fair Housing Act of 1968.
NOTE - Gender, familial status, and disability were added to the list of identities protected by the Fair Housing Act through amendments in 1974 and 1988.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which situation violates the Federal Fair Housing Act of 1968?
option_a: The general policy of a loan company to grant home improvement loans to qualified individuals living in transitional neighborhoods
option_b: The intentional neglect of a broker to show a minority family any property listings in non-minority neighborhoods
option_c: The refusal of a property manager to rent an apartment to a Catholic couple who is not otherwise qualified
option_d: A widowed woman’s insistence on renting her spare bedroom only to another widowed woman
feedback: The Fair Housing Act (Title VIII of the Civil Rights Act of 1968) introduced meaningful federal enforcement mechanisms to prohibit discrimination in the selling or renting of a dwelling to any person because of race, color, disability, religion, sex, familial status, or national origin. Using race as a qualifying factor when deciding what neighborhood to show a potential buyer is what violates the FFHA in this question.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, lenders are prohibited from doing which of the following?
option_a: Refusing credit due to down payment requirements
option_b: Refusing credit due to the condition of the property
option_c: Refusing credit due to the gender of an applicant
option_d: Refusing credit due to the applicant's FICO score
feedback: The Federal Trade Commission (FTC), the nation's consumer protection agency, enforces the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA), which prohibits credit discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, or because you get public assistance. Lenders are still allowed to refuse loans to any person for financial reasons that would disqualify anyone from getting that same loan.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: When can a landlord evict a blind or otherwise disabled tenant from the premises?
option_a: If the tenant makes modifications to the unit at his own expense
option_b: If the tenant gets a service dog and the apartment policy does not allow pets
option_c: If the tenant has loud parties and makes too much noise
option_d: If the tenant insists on a handicapped parking place
feedback: The law requires "reasonable accommodation" for disabled tenants. For example, a landlord must allow a guide dog for a blind person even if there's a "no pets" policy. This doesn't mean that all rules are suspended for that person, however, and noise, safety, and "use of premises policies" may still be enforced.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: One general rule of the federal do-not-call regulations is that:
option_a: real estate offices are exempt from the laws because they are not telemarketers.
option_b: states must maintain separate do-not-call lists.
option_c: it is illegal to make an unsolicited phone call to a number listed on the national registry.
option_d: the national registry must be searched once a year.
feedback: The National Do Not Call Registry gives you a choice about whether to receive telemarketing calls. It is illegal to make an unsolicited phone call to any number listed on the national registry.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The basic goal of a professional real estate property manager is to:
option_a: maintain the property in as perfect condition as humanly possible.
option_b: generate the highest possible net return over the long run for the property owner.
option_c: obtain tenants who will stay for long periods of time.
option_d: keep administration costs to a minimum.
feedback: The overall goal of a property manager is simple- generate the highest net return over the long run for the owner of the property. To accomplish this, the property manager will want to do things like find long-term tenants, maintain the property well, and keep administration costs to a minimum, but each of these is only one step toward the greater goal of generating more profits.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: The National Do Not Call Registry provides that:
option_a: licensees may not contact previous customers who are on the registry.
option_b: consumers who have made an inquiry to a licensee may be contacted up to three months later.
option_c: licensees may never contact consumers without written authorization.
option_d: consumers with whom a licensee has had a business relationship can be contacted for up to 12 months after termination of that relationship.
feedback:
The National Do Not Call Registry is a database maintained by the United States federal government, listing the telephone numbers of individuals and families who have requested that telemarketers not contact them. Certain callers are required by federal law to respect this request, but it allows for licensees to contact potential customers for up to 3 months after the customer has made an inquiry with them.

NOTE - Consumers who have only made an inquiry- they may be contacted up to 3 months later. Customers who have had an established business relationship with a company can be contacted up to 18 months later.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following activities is a violation of the Federal Fair Housing Act?
option_a: Discrimination in the sale of a warehouse based on the prospective purchaser's gender.
option_b: A nonprofit church that denies access to its retirement home to any person because of race.
option_c: A nonprofit private club that gives preference in renting units to its members at lower rates.
option_d: The owner of a single-family residence who is selling his/her own home (without a broker) and gives preference to a buyer based on his/her sex.
feedback: The private club is exempt because its preferential treatment is based on its membership; the homeowner is exempt, so as long as he is selling his home without a broker; the warehouse is exempt because it's not a housing unit. The church can not discriminate on the basis of race, as it is a protected class.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of these phrases, when placed in a print advertisement, would comply with the requirements of the Truth in Lending Act?
feedback: The Truth in Lending Act (TILA) of 1968 is a United States federal law designed to promote the informed use of consumer credit, by requiring disclosures about its terms and cost to standardize the manner in which costs associated with borrowing are calculated and disclosed. It requires finance charges to be stated as an “annual percentage rate,” using that term.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: An agreement that ends all future lessor-lessee obligations under a lease is called:
feedback: The word "surrender" is appropriate, as the lessee is "surrendering" their rights to a leased property. In landlord-tenant law, a surrender occurs when a tenant agrees to return the leased premises to the landlord before the expiration of the lease and the landlord agrees to accept the return of the premises.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: What is the time expectation in "Time is of the Essence"?
option_a: 5 business days from the time of receipt
feedback: "Time is of the Essence" is a phrase in a contract which usually says that performance of a specific act within a specified period is necessary for the contract to properly move forward. Failure to act within the time required constitutes a breach of contract. It is commonly said that the expectation is "as soon as possible", but it doesn't mean "drop everything else to finish this".

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Where no time period for acceptance by the offeree is stipulated in an offer, the offer:
option_b: will lapse after a reasonable time.
option_c: will not lapse; it must be revoked by the offeror.
feedback: Where no time period for acceptance by the offeree is stipulated in an offer, the offer will lapse after a reasonable time. If there is any dispute regarding the offer, a court will decide what "a reasonable time" is.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: If an office building lease does NOT prohibit assignment or subletting, under what conditions may the tenant assign the space?
option_a: After hiring legal counsel to obtain full rights to the lease
option_c: Only with any rent increase being split with the owner
feedback: You can sublet a space under any standard lease, unless the lease specifically prohibits sub-letting.

subject: B4 Disclosure
question_text: Which of the following best describes an earnest money agreement?
option_a: A legally enforceable contract binding both the buyer and seller to all known and unknown facts attached to the property
option_b: A legally enforceable contract binding the buyer and their heirs to the conditions of the contract
option_c: a legally enforceable contract requiring the seller's signature to be valid
option_d: a legally enforceable contract requiring the signatures of all parties to be valid
feedback: While an earnest money deposit is not a contract, an earnest money AGREEMENT is a legally enforceable contract that requires the signature of all parties to be valid. Earnest money agreements are contracts written up during the exchange of the earnest money which often outline the conditions for refunding the deposit.
